kikuyu: Nake mũthamaki agĩcookeria arori a njata acio, akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Ũndũ ũyũ nĩguo nduĩte ngwatĩirie mũno: Mũngĩaga kũnjĩĩra kĩroto kĩrĩa ndootete na mũgĩtaũre-rĩ, nguuga mũtemangwo mũkue, nacio nyũmba cianyu imomorwo ituĩke hĩba cia mahiga.
english: The king answered the Chaldeans, “The thing has gone from me. If you don’t make known to me the dream and its interpretation, you will be cut in pieces, and your houses will be made a dunghill.

kikuyu: Nacio ndaimono igĩkĩrĩrĩria gũthaitha Jesũ ndagaciathe ithiĩ Irima-rĩrĩa-Rĩtarĩ-Gĩturi.
english: They begged him that he would not command them to go into the abyss.

kikuyu: andũ acio moigaga atĩrĩ, ‘Njeherera, tiga kũnguhĩrĩria, tondũ niĩ ndĩ mũtheru gũgũkĩra!’ Andũ ta acio mahaana ta ndogo maniũrũ-inĩ makwa, na ta mwaki wakanaga mũthenya wothe.
english: who say, ‘Stay by yourself, don’t come near to me, for I am holier than you.’ These are smoke in my nose, a fire that burns all day.

kikuyu: Wee wanaciirĩra kũniinwo kwa ndũrĩrĩ nyingĩ, ũgaconorithia nyũmba yaku mwene, na ũkoorwo nĩ muoyo waku.
english: You have devised shame to your house by cutting off many peoples, and have sinned against your soul.

kikuyu: Ũguo nĩguo ngeeruta mangʼũrĩ makwa na rũthingo rũu, na njũkĩrĩre acio maarũhakire coka. Ngaakwĩra atĩrĩ, “Rũthingo nĩ rweherete, o na arĩa maarũhakire coka,
english: Thus I will accomplish my wrath on the wall, and on those who have plastered it with whitewash. I will tell you, ‘The wall is no more, nor those who plastered it—

kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa wĩkaga ũũru nĩathiĩ na mbere gwĩka ũũru, nake ũrĩa kĩmaramari athiĩ na mbere na kũmaramara; na ũrĩa wĩkaga ũrĩa kwagĩrĩire athiĩ na mbere gwĩka ũrĩa kwagĩrĩire; ningĩ ũrĩa mũtheru agĩthiĩ na mbere gũtuĩka mũtheru.”
english: He who acts unjustly, let him act unjustly still. He who is filthy, let him be filthy still. He who is righteous, let him do righteousness still. He who is holy, let him be holy still.”

kikuyu: Nĩwaũthereirie mũgũnda, naguo ũkĩgwata, na ũkĩiyũra bũrũri wothe.
english: You cleared the ground for it. It took deep root, and filled the land.

kikuyu: Hooyaga atĩ Ngai wa Mwathani witũ Jesũ Kristũ, o we ithe witũ mwene riiri, amũhe Roho, ũrĩa ũkamũhe ũũgĩ na ũguũrio nĩguo mũmenye Ngai wega.
english: that the God of our Lord Jesus Christ, the Father of glory, may give to you a spirit of wisdom and revelation in the knowledge of him,

kikuyu: Rĩrĩa Jobabu aakuire, Hushamu wa kuuma bũrũri wa Atemani agĩtuĩka mũthamaki ithenya rĩake.
english: Jobab died, and Husham of the land of the Temanites reigned in his place.

kikuyu: No nĩngarigiicĩria itũũra rĩa Arieli; nĩrĩkarĩra na rĩcakaye, na rĩgaatuĩka ta riiko rĩa kĩgongona harĩ niĩ.
english: then I will distress Ariel, and there will be mourning and lamentation. She shall be to me as an altar hearth.†

kikuyu: Jairu aarĩ na ariũ mĩrongo ĩtatũ arĩa mathiiaga mahaicĩte ndigiri mĩrongo ĩtatũ. Maathaga matũũra mĩrongo ĩtatũ kũu Gileadi, marĩa nginya ũmũthĩ metagwo Havothu-Jairu.
english: He had thirty sons who rode on thirty donkey colts. They had thirty cities, which are called Havvoth Jair to this day, which are in the land of Gilead.

kikuyu: Inyuĩ atumia-rĩ, mwĩnyiihagĩriei athuuri anyu o taarĩ Mwathani mũrenyiihĩria.
english: Wives, be subject to your own husbands, as to the Lord.

kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa ũtũmaga kũgĩe na nyamũkano-rĩ, mũkaanagie riita rĩa mbere, na rĩa keerĩ. Thuutha wa ũguo, regana nake.
english: Avoid a factious man after a first and second warning,

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo Jehova ombire igũrũ na thĩ, na iria rĩrĩa inene na indo ciothe iria irĩ kuo, na mĩthenya ĩtandatũ, no akĩhurũka mũthenya wa mũgwanja. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Jehova akĩrathima mũthenya ũcio wa Thabatũ, akĩwaamũra.
english: for in six days Yahweh made heaven and earth, the sea, and all that is in them, and rested the seventh day; therefore Yahweh blessed the Sabbath day, and made it holy.

kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, Ngai arogoocwo, o we ũrĩa ũtũtongoragia hĩndĩ ciothe na ũhootani thĩinĩ wa Kristũ, nake agatũhũthagĩra ta tũrĩ mũtararĩko mwega wa kũmemerekia ũhoro wa kũmenya Kristũ kũndũ guothe.
english: Now thanks be to God who always leads us in triumph in Christ, and reveals through us the sweet aroma of his knowledge in every place.

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Zadoku na Abiatharu magĩkuua ithandũkũ rĩa Ngai, makĩrĩcookia Jerusalemu na magĩikara kuo.
english: Zadok therefore and Abiathar carried God’s ark to Jerusalem again; and they stayed there.

kikuyu: Nĩngameta ndĩmacookanĩrĩrie. Ti-itherũ nĩngamakũũra; nao nĩmakaingĩha o ta ũrĩa maarĩ mbere.
english: I will signal for them and gather them, for I have redeemed them. They will increase as they were before.

kikuyu: Mwana ũcio agĩkũra, na mũthenya ũmwe akiumagara, agĩthiĩ kũrĩ ithe, ũrĩa warĩ hamwe na agethi.
english: When the child was grown, one day he went out to his father to the reapers.

kikuyu: Andũ othe nĩmakaigua ũhoro ũcio nao metigĩre, na matigacooka gũkorwo marĩ na kĩnyararo rĩngĩ.
english: All the people shall hear and fear, and do no more presumptuously.

kikuyu: Nĩũgũtuĩka mũira wake kũrĩ andũ othe wa kũmoimbũrĩra ũrĩa wonete na ũrĩa ũiguĩte.
english: For you will be a witness for him to all men of what you have seen and heard.

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Gideoni agĩakĩra Jehova kĩgongona hau, agĩgĩĩta Jehova nĩ Thayũ. Nakĩo gĩtũire kũu Ofira kwa Aabiezeri nginya ũmũthĩ.
english: Then Gideon built an altar there to Yahweh, and called it “Yahweh is Peace.”§ To this day it is still in Ophrah of the Abiezrites.

kikuyu: Rĩrĩa Firaũni omirie mũtwe akĩrega gũtũrekereria-rĩ, Jehova nĩooragire marigithathi ma bũrũri wa Misiri mothe, andũ o na nyamũ. Ũndũ ũcio nĩguo ũtũmaga ndutĩre Jehova igongona rĩa njamba ciothe iria ciambaga kuuma nda, na ngakũũra mũriũ wakwa wothe wa irigithathi.
english: When Pharaoh stubbornly refused to let us go, Yahweh killed all the firstborn in the land of Egypt, both the firstborn of man, and the firstborn of livestock. Therefore I sacrifice to Yahweh all that

kikuyu: No Nineve nĩ kũrĩ na andũ makĩria ma ngiri igana rĩa mĩrongo ĩĩrĩ arĩa matangĩkũũrana guoko kwa ũrĩo kana guoko kwa ũmotho, o na ningĩ rĩrĩ na mahiũ maingĩ. Githĩ o na niĩ ndiagĩrĩirwo nĩkũiguĩra tha itũũra rĩu inene?”
english: Shouldn’t I be concerned for Nineveh, that great city, in which are more than one hundred twenty thousand persons who can’t discern between their right hand and their left hand, and also many animals?”

kikuyu: Ningĩ Jehova akĩĩra Musa atĩrĩ, “Tambũrũkia guoko ũkũroretie matu-inĩ nĩgeetha mbura ya mbembe yure bũrũri wa Misiri guothe; yurĩre andũ, na nyamũ na yurĩre kĩndũ gĩothe kĩrakũra mĩgũnda-inĩ ya bũrũri wa Misiri.”
english: Yahweh said to Moses, “Stretch out your hand toward the sky, that there may be hail in all the land of Egypt, on man, and on animal, and on every herb of the field, throughout the land of Egypt.”

kikuyu: Matũũra macio matandatũ magaatuĩka kũndũ gwa kũũrĩrwo nĩ andũ a Isiraeli, na ageni, o na andũ arĩa angĩ matũũraga thĩinĩ wao, nĩgeetha mũndũ ũraganĩte ataciirĩire gwĩka ũguo ooragĩre kuo.
english: These six cities shall be refuge for the children of Israel, for the stranger, and for the foreigner living among them, that everyone who kills any person unwittingly may flee there.

kikuyu: No maũndũ macio mothe, matiratũma njiganĩre rĩrĩa rĩothe ndĩrona Moridekai, Mũyahudi ũcio, aikarĩte kĩhingo-inĩ kĩa mũciĩ wa mũthamaki.”
english: Yet all this avails me nothing, so long as I see Mordecai the Jew sitting at the king’s gate.”

kikuyu: Nake Lea akiuga atĩrĩ, “Hĩ, kaĩ ũyũ nĩ mũnyaka-ĩ!” Nĩ ũndũ ũcio agĩgatua Gadi.
english: Leah said, “How fortunate!” She named him Gad.

kikuyu: Nĩngamũrehithĩria rũhiũ rwa njora rũrĩhĩrie kĩrĩkanĩro kĩrĩa mũthũkĩtie. Muorĩra matũũra-inĩ marĩa manene manyu, nĩngamũrehithĩria mũthiro thĩinĩ wanyu, na ndĩmũrekererie moko-inĩ ma thũ cianyu.
english: I will bring a sword upon you that will execute the vengeance of the covenant. You will be gathered together within your cities, and I will send the pestilence among you. You will be delivered into the hand of the enemy.

kikuyu: Hũndũka nginya rĩrĩa kũrĩĩrura, nacio ciĩruru ciirĩrie. Hũndũka mwendwa wakwa, tuĩka ta thiiya, kana ta thwariga nyanake, ĩrĩ irĩma-inĩ cia Betheri.
english: Until the day is cool, and the shadows flee away, turn, my beloved, and be like a roe or a young deer on the mountains of Bether.

kikuyu: Akiuga atĩrĩ, “Mwathi wakwa, angĩkorwo nĩndetĩkĩrĩka maitho-inĩ maku, ndũkahĩtũke ndungata yaku.
english: and said, “My lord, if now I have found favor in your sight, please don’t go away from your servant.

kikuyu: Ciugo cia kanua gakwa na matarania ma ngoro yakwa irogũkenia maitho-inĩ maku, Wee Jehova, o Wee Rwaro rwakwa rwa Ihiga, na Mũkũũri wakwa.
english: Let the words of my mouth and the meditation of my heart be acceptable in your sight, Yahweh, my rock, and my redeemer.

kikuyu: Nao makĩĩrana atĩrĩ, “Ĩĩrĩa nĩ thakame! Athamaki acio no nginya makorwo nĩmarũĩte na makooragana o ene. Andũ a Moabi, nĩtũthiĩi tũgatahe indo!”
english: They said, “This is blood. The kings are surely destroyed, and they have

kikuyu: Haarĩriai handũ ha kũũragĩra ciana ciake, nĩ ũndũ wa mehia ma maithe mao ma tene; nĩguo itikanarahũke ciĩgaĩre bũrũri, na ciĩyakĩre matũũra maiyũre thĩ.
english: Prepare for slaughter of his children because of the iniquity of their fathers, that they not rise up and possess the earth, and fill the surface of the world with cities.

kikuyu: Thigari cia mbarathi irahithũka, ikahenũkia hiũ cia njora na matimũ marahenia! Andũ aingĩ nĩmooragĩtwo, gũkagĩa na hĩba cia arĩa makuĩte, na ciimba itangĩtarĩka, nao andũ makahĩngwo nĩ ciimba icio:
english: the horseman charging, and the flashing sword, the glittering spear, and a multitude of slain, and a great heap of corpses, and there is no end of the bodies. They stumble on their bodies

kikuyu: ee Jehova, o Wee Mwathani witũ, kaĩ rĩĩtwa rĩaku nĩ inene gũkũ thĩ yothe-ĩ! Nĩũigĩte riiri waku o igũrũ matu-inĩ.
english: ahweh, our Lord, how majestic is your name in all the earth! You have set your glory above the heavens!

kikuyu: Acio nĩo ndĩrahoera. Ndirahoera andũ a gũkũ thĩ, no ndĩrahoera arĩa ũũheete, nĩgũkorwo nĩ aku.
english: I pray for them. I don’t pray for the world, but for those whom you have given me, for they are yours.

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo Jehova Ngai Mwene-Hinya-Wothe ekuuga atĩrĩ, “Nĩngarahũra rũrĩrĩ rũgũũkĩrĩre, wee nyũmba ya Isiraeli, rũrĩa rũgakũhinyĩrĩria kuuma itoonyero rĩa Hamathu, o nginya mũkuru wa Araba.”
english: For, behold, I will raise up against you a nation, house of Israel,” says Yahweh, the God of Armies; “and they will afflict you from the entrance of Hamath to the brook of the Arabah.”

kikuyu: a thikĩrĩriai mũigue ũhoro ũrĩa Jehova ekũmwĩra, inyuĩ andũ a nyũmba ya Isiraeli.
english: ear the word which Yahweh speaks to you, house of Israel!

kikuyu: ‘Niĩ ndimatũmĩte,’ nĩguo Jehova ekuuga. ‘Mararatha ũhoro wa maheeni, makĩgwetaga rĩĩtwa rĩakwa. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio nĩngamũingata, na inyuĩ mũniinwo biũ, wee hamwe na anabii acio makũrathagĩra mohoro.’ ”
english: For I have not sent them,” says Yahweh, “but they prophesy falsely in my name; that I may drive you out, and that you may perish, you, and the prophets who prophesy to you.”

kikuyu: Andũ arĩa marĩmaga na mũraũ marĩmĩte ngʼongʼo wakwa, magatema mĩtaro mĩraihu.
english: The plowers plowed on my back. They made their furrows long.

kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, canjamũkai na mũkenere indo iria ngũmba tene na tene, nĩgũkorwo nĩngũmba itũũra rĩa Jerusalemu rĩtuĩke rĩa gũkenerwo, na andũ a rĩo matuĩke a gũkenania.
english: But be glad and rejoice forever in that which I create; for, behold, I create Jerusalem to be a delight, and her people a joy.

kikuyu: Mũthamaki na anene ake na kĩũngano kĩu gĩothe kũu Jerusalemu magĩtua atĩ megũkũngũĩra Bathaka mweri wa keerĩ.
english: For the king had taken counsel with his princes and all the assembly in Jerusalem to keep the Passover in the second month.

kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa warehire maruta make mũthenya wa mbere aarĩ Nahashoni mũrũ wa Aminadabu wa mũhĩrĩga wa Juda.
english: He who offered his offering the first day was Nahshon the son of Amminadab, of the tribe of Judah,

kikuyu: “No inyuĩ mũtikanetwo ‘Rabii’, nĩgũkorwo Mũnene wanyu no ũmwe, na inyuothe mũrĩ a ithe ũmwe.
english: But you are not to be called ‘Rabbi’, for one is your teacher, the Christ, and all of you are brothers.

kikuyu: lawii makĩrehe ithandũkũ rĩa Ngai, makĩrĩtoonyia thĩinĩ wa hema ĩrĩa yaambĩtwo nĩ Daudi nĩ ũndũ warĩo, na makĩrutĩra Ngai maruta ma njino na maruta ma ũiguano.
english: hey brought in God’s ark, and set it in the middle of the tent that David had pitched for it; and they offered burnt offerings and peace offerings before God.

kikuyu: Matigethaahia rĩngĩ na mĩhianano yao, kana mĩhiano mĩũru, kana waganu wao o wothe, nĩgũkorwo nĩngamahonokia matige gũcooka na thuutha nĩ ũndũ wa mehia mao, na nĩngamatheria. Magaatuĩka andũ akwa, na niĩ nduĩke Ngai wao.
english: They won’t defile themselves any more with their idols, nor with their detestable things, nor with any of their transgressions; but I will save them out of all their dwelling places in which they have sinned, and will cleanse them. So they will be my people, and I will be their God.

kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, thiĩi mũkeerute ũrĩa ciugo ici ciugĩte: ‘Kũiguanĩra tha nĩkuo nyendaga, no ti magongona.’ Nĩgũkorwo niĩ ndiokire gũcaria arĩa athingu, no ndokire nĩ ũndũ wa arĩa ehia.” Jesũ Kũũrio Ũhoro wa Kwĩhinga Kũrĩa
english: But you go and learn what this means: ‘I desire mercy, and not sacrifice,’* for I came not to call the righteous, but sinners to repentance.”†

kikuyu: Ningĩ nĩmũgathaahia mĩhianano yanyu ya kũhooywo ĩrĩa ĩgemetio na betha igũrũ, na mĩhiano
english: You shall defile the overlaying of your engraved images of silver, and the plating

kikuyu: Mũrangĩri ũcio wa njeera akĩmatwara gwake nyũmba, na akĩmahe irio. Akĩiyũrwo nĩ gĩkeno tondũ nĩeetĩkirie Ngai, we o hamwe na andũ a nyũmba yake yothe.
english: He brought them up into his house and set food before them, and rejoiced greatly with all his household, having believed in God.

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo Jehova akĩĩra Joshua atĩrĩ, “Thondeka tũhiũ twa mahiga ma nyaigĩ, ũroke kwerũhia ũhoro wa irua harĩ andũ a Isiraeli.”
english: At that time, Yahweh said to Joshua, “Make flint knives, and circumcise again the sons of Israel the second time.”

kikuyu: Atĩrĩrĩ, nĩũkaingatwo weherio kuuma kũrĩ andũ, ũgatũũranagie na nyamũ cia gĩthaka; ũkaarĩĩaga nyeki ta ngʼombe, na
english: You will be driven from men and your dwelling shall be with the animals of the field. You will be made to eat grass as oxen, and will be wet with the dew of the sky, and seven times shall pass over you, until you know that the Most High rules in

kikuyu: Ũrĩa mũnene thĩinĩ wanyu nĩatuĩkage ndungata yanyu.
english: But he who is greatest among you will be your servant.

kikuyu: Gũtirĩ ũrogi ũngĩũraga Jakubu, gũtirĩ ũragũri ũngĩthũkia Isiraeli. Rĩu ha ũhoro wa Jakubu na wa Isiraeli kũrĩĩranagwo atĩrĩ, ‘Ta kĩonei ũrĩa Ngai ekĩte!’
english: Surely there is no enchantment with Jacob; neither is there any divination with Israel. Now it shall be said of Jacob and of Israel, ‘What has God done!’

kikuyu: Ndaamũrutire bũrũri wa Misiri, ngĩmũkũũra kuuma bũrũri wa ũkombo. Ngĩtũma Musa amũtongorie, marĩ na Harũni na Miriamu.
english: For I brought you up out of the land of Egypt, and redeemed you out of the house of bondage. I sent before you Moses, Aaron, and

kikuyu: akiuga atĩrĩ: “Wee Jehova, Ngai wa maithe maitũ, githĩ tiwe Ngai ũrĩa ũrĩ igũrũ? Nĩwe ũthamakagĩra mothamaki mothe ma ndũrĩrĩ. Hinya na ũhoti irĩ guoko-inĩ gwaku, na gũtirĩ mũndũ ũngĩgwĩtiiria.
english: and he said, “Yahweh, the God of our fathers, aren’t you God in heaven? Aren’t you ruler over all the kingdoms of the nations? Power and might are in your hand, so that no one is able to withstand you.

kikuyu: Jehova ekuuga atĩrĩ, “Nĩmwarĩtie maũndũ mooru ma kũnjambia. “No mũũragia atĩrĩ, ‘Tũgũcambĩtie atĩa?’
english: “Your words have been harsh against me,” says Yahweh. “Yet you say, ‘What have we spoken against you?’

kikuyu: Nake akĩrora arutwo ake, akiuga atĩrĩ: “Kũrathimwo-rĩ, nĩ inyuĩ athĩĩni, nĩgũkorwo ũthamaki wa Ngai nĩ wanyu.
english: He lifted up his eyes to his disciples, and said: “Blessed are you who are poor, for God’s Kingdom is yours.

kikuyu: na mũndũ-wa-nja warĩ na cuba ya maguta ma goro mũno na manungi wega, agĩũka kũrĩ Jesũ, akĩmũitĩrĩria maguta mũtwe arĩ metha-inĩ akĩrĩa.
english: a woman came to him having an alabaster jar of very expensive ointment, and she poured it on his head as he sat at the table.

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa wee ũgaakorwo ũthaahĩte maitho-inĩ ma ndũrĩrĩ, nĩũkamenya atĩ niĩ nĩ niĩ Jehova.’ ”
english: You will be profaned in yourself in the sight of the nations. Then you will know that I am Yahweh.” ’ ”

kikuyu: Kuuma kũu magĩthiĩ na mbere nginya bũrũri ũrĩa ũrĩ irĩma wa Efiraimu na magĩkinya mũciĩ wa Mika.
english: They passed from there to the hill country of Ephraim, and came to the house of Micah.

kikuyu: Akĩgayania andũ acio magana matatũ ikundi ithatũ, akĩmanengera othe o mũndũ karumbeta na nyũngũ theri, ĩrĩ na kĩmũrĩ thĩinĩ.
english: He divided the three hundred men into three companies, and he put into the hands of all of them trumpets and empty pitchers, with torches within the pitchers.

kikuyu: Nake mwana ũcio ti Samũeli agĩkũra, akĩnenehaga, akĩendagwo nĩ Jehova o na andũ. Ũrathi wa Gũũkĩrĩra Nyũmba ya Eli
english: The child Samuel grew on, and increased in favor both with Yahweh and also with men.

kikuyu: “Edomu arĩ kũu, hamwe na athamaki ake, na anene ake othe; o na maarĩ na hinya-rĩ, makometio hamwe na arĩa maanooragwo na rũhiũ rwa njora. Makomete hamwe na arĩa mataruĩte, o hamwe na arĩa maharũrũkagio irima.
english: “There is Edom, her kings, and all her princes, who in their might are laid with those who are slain by the sword. They will lie with the uncircumcised, and with those who go down to the pit.

kikuyu: nĩgũkorwo inyuĩ mwĩĩtaga atũũri a itũũra rĩrĩa inene itheru,
english: for they call themselves citizens of the holy city, and rely on the God of Israel; Yahweh of Armies is his name.

kikuyu: Na mwanake ũmwe ũteehumbĩte kĩndũ tiga taama wa gatani akĩrũmĩrĩra Jesũ. Na rĩrĩa maamũnyiitire,
english: A certain young man followed him, having a linen cloth thrown around himself over his naked body. The young men grabbed him,

kikuyu: Hooyai nĩguo gwĩthara kwanyu gũtikanakorwo kũrĩ hĩndĩ ya heho kana mũthenya wa Thabatũ.
english: Pray that your flight will not be in the winter nor on a Sabbath,

kikuyu: ĩ niĩ Petero, mũtũmwo wa Jesũ Kristũ, Ngũmwandĩkĩra marũa maya, inyuĩ arĩa Ngai eyamũrĩire, na mũtũũrĩte mũrĩ ageni gũkũ thĩ, na mũkahurunjũkĩra mabũrũri ma Ponto, na Galatia, na Kapadokia, na Asia, na Bithinia,
english: eter, an apostle of Jesus Christ,† to the chosen ones who are living as foreigners in the Dispersion in Pontus, Galatia, Cappadocia, Asia, and Bithynia,

kikuyu: Nake Hezekia agĩcookia atĩrĩ, “Kiugo kĩa Jehova kĩrĩa waria nĩ kĩega,” tondũ eeciiririe atĩrĩ, “Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa ngũtũũra muoyo nĩgũgũkorwo kũrĩ na thayũ na ũgitĩri.”
english: Then Hezekiah said to Isaiah, “Yahweh’s word which you have spoken is good.” He said moreover, “For there will be peace and truth in my days.”

kikuyu: Ũrĩa wothe ũrĩĩaga mwĩrĩ wakwa na akanyua thakame yakwa aikaraga thĩinĩ wakwa na niĩ ngaikara thĩinĩ wake.
english: He who eats my flesh and drinks my blood lives in me, and I in him.

kikuyu: Nake Hezekia agĩtũũra ehokete Jehova, Ngai wa Isiraeli. Gũtiarĩ mũndũ ũngĩ take harĩ athamaki othe a Juda, mbere yake kana arĩa mookire thuutha wake.
english: He trusted in Yahweh, the God of Israel, so that after him was no one like him among all the kings of Judah, nor among them that were before him.

kikuyu: Mũndũ mũkarĩ nĩeriragĩria mũno gũtonga, na ndooĩ atĩ etereirwo nĩ ũthĩĩni.
english: A stingy man hurries after riches, and doesn’t know that poverty waits for him.

kikuyu: “Ũthamaki wa igũrũ ũhaanaine na mũthiithũ wahithĩtwo mũgũnda. Na rĩrĩa mũndũ aawoonire, akĩũhitha rĩngĩ, na nĩ ũndũ wa ũrĩa akenete, agĩthiĩ akĩendia indo ciothe iria arĩ nacio akĩgũra mũgũnda ũcio.
english: “Again, the Kingdom of Heaven is like treasure hidden in the field, which a man found and hid. In his joy, he goes and sells all that he has and buys that field.

kikuyu: Jeremia nĩandĩkĩte ibuku-inĩ rĩa gĩkũnjo ũhoro wothe ũkoniĩ mwanangĩko ũrĩa ũgaakinyĩrĩra Babuloni: maũndũ marĩa mothe maandĩkĩtwo makoniĩ Babuloni.
english: Jeremiah wrote in a book all the evil that should come on Babylon, even all these words that are written concerning Babylon.

kikuyu: Ĩhuragia thĩ na ũcamba ĩgĩkenagĩra hinya wayo, ĩkaguthũka ĩtoonyete mbaara-inĩ.
english: He paws in the valley, and rejoices in his strength. He goes out to meet the armed men.

kikuyu: Mũcirutanĩrie na thenge ĩmwe ĩrĩ igongona rĩa kũhoroheria mehia, yongererwo harĩ igongona rĩa horohio, naigongonarĩanjinorĩahĩndĩciothe, hamwenairutarĩamũtu, namarutamaomakũnyuuo. Gĩathĩ gĩa Ithũnũ
english: one male goat for a sin offering, in addition to the sin offering of atonement, and the continual burnt offering, and its meal offering, and their drink offerings.

kikuyu: ndũmĩrĩri ya mũndũ ũrĩa ũiguaga ciugo cia Ngai, ũrĩa ũrĩ na ũmenyo uumĩte kũrĩ Ũrĩa-ũrĩ-Igũrũ-Mũno, ũrĩa wonaga kĩoneki kuuma kũrĩ Mwene-Hinya-Wothe, ũrĩa ũgũũaga agakoma thĩ, namo maitho make makahingũka, ekuuga ũũ:
english: he says, who hears the words of God, knows the knowledge of the Most High, and who sees the vision of the Almighty, falling down, and having his eyes open:

kikuyu: Guoko gwaku kũroikara na mũndũ ũrĩa ũrĩ guoko-inĩ gwaku kwa ũrĩo, ũcio mũrũ wa mũndũ wĩrereire wee mwene.
english: Let your hand be on the man of your right hand, on the son of man whom you made strong for yourself.

kikuyu: Nao anene arĩa maamũũragire nĩ Jozakaru mũrũ wa Shimeathu, na Jehozabadu mũrũ wa Shomeru. Joashu agĩkua na agĩthikwo hamwe na maithe make itũũra-inĩ rĩa Daudi. Nake mũriũ Amazia agĩtuĩka mũthamaki ithenya rĩake.
english: For Jozacar the son of Shimeath, and Jehozabad the son of Shomer, his servants, struck him, and he died; and they buried him with his fathers in David’s city; and Amaziah his son reigned in his place.

kikuyu: Nake Asa akĩoya betha na thahabu kuuma igĩĩna-inĩ cia hekarũ ya Jehova o na kuuma nyũmba-inĩ yake ya ũthamaki, na agĩcitũmĩra Beni-Hadadi mũthamaki wa Suriata ũrĩa wathamakaga Dameski.
english: Then Asa brought out silver and gold out of the treasures of Yahweh’s house and of the king’s house, and sent to Ben Hadad king of Syria, who lived at Damascus, saying,

kikuyu: ingĩ Ayubu agĩcookia atĩrĩ:
english: hen Job answered,

kikuyu: Mũthamaki akĩĩra mũndũ ũcio wa Ngai atĩrĩ, “Ũka tũthiĩ mũciĩ ũkarĩe, na nĩngũkũhe kĩheo.”
english: The king said to the man of God, “Come home with me and refresh yourself, and I will give you a reward.”

kikuyu: O na ningĩ nĩngaitĩrĩria ndungata ciakwa cia arũme na cia andũ-a-nja Roho wakwa matukũ-inĩ macio, nacio nĩikarathaga mohoro.
english: Yes, and on my servants and on my handmaidens in those days, I will pour out my Spirit, and they will prophesy.

kikuyu: Ũguo noguo, mũtĩ wothe mwega ũciaraga maciaro mega, no mũtĩ mũũru ũciaraga maciaro mooru.
english: Even so, every good tree produces good fruit, but the corrupt tree produces evil fruit.

kikuyu: Andũ arĩa maathitangĩte matianyonire ngĩkararania na mũndũ o na ũrĩkũ hekarũ-inĩ, kana ngĩtũma kĩrĩndĩ kĩgĩe na ngũĩ thunagogi-inĩ, o na kana handũ hangĩ o hothe itũũra-inĩ.
english: In the temple they didn’t find me disputing with anyone or stirring up a crowd, either in the synagogues or in the city.

kikuyu: Mũndũ mũrũme o wothe ũrĩa ũhooyaga kana akaratha ehumbĩrĩte mũtwe-rĩ, nĩ mũtwe wake aconorithagia.
english: Every man praying or prophesying, having his head covered, dishonors his head.

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo rĩu ingĩkomete ndĩ na thayũ; rĩu ingĩrĩ toro hurũkĩte
english: For now I should have lain down and

kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa wathĩkagĩra maathani-rĩ, nĩ muoyo wake amenyagĩrĩra, no ũrĩa ũtoonaga bata wa kũmenyerera mĩthiĩre yake nĩagaakua.
english: He who has pity on the poor lends to Yahweh; he will reward him.

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ inyuĩ, Alawii, mũtiarĩambatirie ihinda rĩa mbere, nĩkĩo Jehova Ngai witũ aatũũkĩrĩire na marakara. Tũtiigana gũtuĩria ũhoro wa ũrĩa tũngĩekire kũrĩ we, kũringana na ũrĩa gwatuĩtwo.”
english: For because you didn’t carry it at first, Yahweh our God broke out in anger against us, because we didn’t seek him according to the ordinance.”

kikuyu: Nĩtũthuthuurie-i mĩthiĩre iitũ na tũmĩtuĩrie, tũcokerere Jehova.
english: Let us search and try our ways, and turn again to Yahweh.

kikuyu: Nĩgũkaara riũa rĩa kũhũithia maaĩ makuo! Namo nĩmakaahũa biũ. Nĩgũkorwo ũcio nĩ bũrũri ũiyũrĩte ngai cia mĩhianano, nao andũ akuo nĩmagũrũkĩtio nĩyo.
english: A drought is on her waters, for it is a land of engraved images, and they are mad over idols.

kikuyu: O ũrĩa athĩnjĩri-Ngai maingĩhaga, noguo maakĩragĩrĩria kũnjĩhĩria; nao magĩkũũrania riiri wao na kĩndũ gĩa thoni.
english: As they were multiplied, so they sinned against me. I will change their glory into shame.

kikuyu: Ĩno nĩyo ndũmĩrĩri ĩrĩa tũiguĩte kuuma kũrĩ we, na nĩyo tũramũmenyithia, atĩ: Ngai nĩ ũtheri; thĩinĩ wake gũtirĩ nduma o na hanini.
english: This is the message which we have heard from him and announce to you, that God is light, and in him is no darkness at all.

kikuyu: Ũcooke ũmĩare mĩhari ĩĩrĩ, o mũhari mĩgate ĩtandatũ, ũmĩigĩrĩre igũrũ rĩa metha ya thahabu therie hau mbere ya Jehova.
english: You shall set them in two rows, six on a row, on the pure gold table before Yahweh.

kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa ũrĩcinaga moori ĩyo no nginya athambie nguo ciake na ethambe mwĩrĩ na maaĩ, na o nake atiinde arĩ na thaahu nginya hwaĩ-inĩ.
english: He who burns her shall wash his clothes in water, and bathe his flesh in water, and shall be unclean until the evening.

kikuyu: Jesũ akĩmacookeria atĩrĩ, “Musa aamwandĩkĩire watho ũcio tondũ wa ũrĩa muomĩtie ngoro cianyu.
english: But Jesus said to them, “For your hardness of heart, he wrote you this commandment.

kikuyu: rĩrĩa ngaanoora rũhiũ rwakwa rwa njora rũkahenia na ndĩrũnyiite na guoko gwakwa ngĩtua ciira, nĩngerĩhĩria kũrĩ thũ ciakwa, na ndĩĩrĩhĩrie kũrĩ arĩa maathũire.
english: if I sharpen my glittering sword, my hand grasps it in judgment; I will take vengeance on my adversaries, and will repay those who hate me.

kikuyu: “Kũgĩa na mbaũ nyingĩ mũno cia mĩtarakwa-rĩ, no gũtũme ũtuĩke mũthamaki? Githĩ thoguo ndaarĩ o na gĩa kũrĩa na gĩa kũnyua? Ekaga maũndũ marĩa maagĩrĩire na ma kĩhooto, nĩ ũndũ ũcio maũndũ make mothe makĩgaacĩra.
english: “Should you reign because you strive to excel in cedar? Didn’t your father eat and drink, and do justice and righteousness? Then it was well with him.

kikuyu: “Na nĩ kĩĩ gĩtũmaga mwĩtange nĩ ũndũ wa nguo? Ta rorai itoka cia gĩthaka ũrĩa ikũraga. Itirutaga wĩra kana ikogotha ndigi,
english: Why are you anxious about clothing? Consider the lilies of the field, how they grow. They don’t toil, neither do they spin,

kikuyu: rũtuĩke rũũri gatagatĩ-inĩ kanyu. Mahinda marĩa magooka, rĩrĩa ciana cianyu ikaamũũria atĩrĩ, ‘Gĩtũmi kĩa mahiga maya nĩ kĩĩ?’
english: that this may be a sign among you, that when your children ask in the future, saying, ‘What do you mean by these stones?’

kikuyu: Ũrĩa wothe waiguaga akĩaria nĩagegagio nĩ ũmenyo wake na ũrĩa aacookagia.
english: All who heard him were amazed at his understanding and his answers.

kikuyu: Nake Simoni Petero akĩhaica marikabu akĩguucia wabu ũcio nginya hũgũrũrũ-inĩ. Naguo waiyũrĩte thamaki iria nene, 153, no o na irĩ nyingĩ ũguo, wabu ũcio ndwatuĩkire.
english: Simon Peter went up, and drew the net to land, full of one hundred fifty-three great fish. Even though there were so many, the net wasn’t torn.

kikuyu: Atĩrĩrĩ, mangʼũrĩ ma Jehova nĩmagatuthũka mahaana ta kĩhuhũkanio; ti-itherũ makaahaana ta rũhuho rũkũhurutana mũno, igũrũ rĩa andũ arĩa aaganu.
english: Behold, Yahweh’s storm, his wrath, has gone out, a sweeping storm; it will burst on the head of the wicked.

kikuyu: Wee Jehova-rĩ, ĩtĩkĩra kũũhonokia, Wee Jehova-rĩ, ũka narua ũndeithie.
english: Be pleased, Yahweh, to deliver me. Hurry to help me, Yahweh.

kikuyu: Ta ũrĩa kwandĩkĩtwo atĩrĩ: “Nĩngũtuĩte ithe wa ndũrĩrĩ nyingĩ.” We nĩwe ithe witũ maitho-inĩ ma Ngai, o ũcio Iburahĩmu eetĩkĩtie, o we Ngai ũrĩa ũriũkagia arĩa makuĩte, na waragia ũhoro wa maũndũ marĩa matarĩ ho ta marĩ ho.
english: As it is written, “I have made you a father of many nations.”* This is in the presence of him whom he believed: God, who gives life to the dead, and calls the things that are not, as though they were.

kikuyu: Nĩakaarĩria andũ handũ haku, na agaatuĩka taarĩ we kanua gaku, nawe ũtuĩke ta ũrĩ Ngai harĩ we.
english: He will be your spokesman to the people. It will happen that he will be to you a mouth, and you will be to him as God.

kikuyu: No Petero akĩaria ahinyĩrĩirie, akiuga atĩrĩ, “O na angĩkorwo ndĩrĩkuanĩra nawe, niĩ nd- ingĩgũkaana!” O na acio angĩ othe makiuga o ũguo.
english: But he spoke all the more, “If I must die with you, I will not deny you.” They all said the same thing.

kikuyu: Nake akĩĩhĩta na ũrĩa ũtũũraga muoyo nginya tene na tene, o we ũrĩa wombire igũrũ na indo ciothe iria irĩ kuo, na akĩũmba thĩ na indo ciothe iria irĩ kuo, o na akĩũmba iria na indo ciothe iria irĩ thĩinĩ warĩo, akiuga atĩrĩ, “Hatirĩ na ihinda rĩngĩ rĩa gweterera!”
english: and swore by him who lives forever and ever, who created heaven and the things that are in it, the earth and the things that are in it, and the sea and the things that are in it, that there will no longer be delay,

kikuyu: Jehova akĩnyiitwo nĩ kĩeha nĩ ũndũ wa kũũmba mũndũ gũkũ thĩ, na ngoro yake ĩkĩiyũrwo nĩ ruo.
english: Yahweh was sorry that he had made man on the earth, and it grieved him in his heart.

kikuyu: Ũndũ mũtaanye ũngĩikario mũno nĩũrwaragia mũndũ ngoro, no kĩĩrĩgĩrĩro kĩhingĩtio gĩtuĩkaga ta mũtĩ wa muoyo.
english: Hope deferred makes the heart sick, but when longing is fulfilled, it is a tree of life.

kikuyu: Icio nĩcio ciarĩ mbarĩ cia Simeoni; nao maarĩ andũ 22,200.
english: These are the families of the Simeonites, twenty-two thousand two hundred.

kikuyu: Thikĩrĩria mataaro na wĩtĩkĩre ũrutani, marigĩrĩrio-inĩ nĩũgaatuĩka mũndũ mũũgĩ.
english: Listen to counsel and receive instruction, that you may be wise in your latter end.

kikuyu: No-o magĩcookia atĩrĩ, “Ithuĩ tũtinyuuaga ndibei, nĩ ũndũ ithe witũ wa tene, Jonadabu mũrũ wa Rekabu, nĩatũtigĩire watho, akiuga atĩrĩ, ‘Mũtikananyue ndibei, inyuĩ ene kana njiaro cianyu.*
english: But they said, “We will drink no wine; for Jonadab the son of Rechab, our father, commanded us, saying, ‘You shall drink no wine, neither you nor your children, forever.

kikuyu: “Wee Efiraimu-rĩ, ingĩgwĩka atĩa? Na wee Juda-rĩ, ngũgwĩka atĩa? Wendo waku ũhaana ta kĩbii kĩa rũciinĩ, ũkahaana ta ime rĩa rũciinĩ rĩrĩa rĩcookaga rĩgathira.
english: “Ephraim, what shall I do to you? Judah, what shall I do to you? For your love is like a morning cloud, and like the dew that disappears early.

kikuyu: Ehĩtũkĩri othe nĩmatahĩte indo ciake; atuĩkĩte wa kũnyararwo nĩ andũ arĩa mariganĩtie nake.
english: All who pass by the way rob him. He has become a reproach to his neighbors.

kikuyu: ũhaandagwo ũrĩ mwĩrĩ wa ndũire, ũgacooka ũkariũkio ũrĩ mwĩrĩ wa kĩĩroho. Angĩkorwo nĩ kũrĩ mwĩrĩ wa ndũire-rĩ, o na mwĩrĩ wa kĩĩroho ũrĩ kuo.
english: It is sown a natural body; it is raised a spiritual body. There is a natural body and there is also a spiritual body.

kikuyu: “Na rĩrĩ, hinya wa Mwathani ũrokĩonekana o ta ũrĩa ugĩte, atĩrĩ:
english: Now please let the power of the Lord† be great, according as you have spoken, saying,

kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, thuutha-inĩ nĩmericũkirwo, makĩnyiita andũ acio mohorete na makĩmatua ngombo rĩngĩ.
english: but afterwards they turned, and caused the servants and the handmaids whom they had let go free to return, and brought them into subjection for servants and for handmaids.

kikuyu: Nĩngananga matũũra marĩa manene ma bũrũri wanyu, na tharũrie ciĩhitho iria nũmu cianyu ciothe.
english: I will cut off the cities of your land and will tear down all your strongholds.

kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, inyuĩ andũ a nyũmba ya Isiraeli, mugaga atĩrĩ, ‘Njĩra ya Mwathani ti ya kĩhooto.’ No rĩrĩ, niĩ ngaatuĩra o mũndũ wanyu ciira kũringana na mĩthiĩre yake we mwene.” Gũtaarĩria gwa Kũgũa kwa Jerusalemu
english: Yet you say, “The way of the Lord is not fair.” House of Israel, I will judge every one of you after his ways.’ ”

kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, aciarithania acio tondũ nĩmetigĩrĩte Ngai, matiigana gwĩka ũrĩa mũthamaki wa bũrũri wa Misiri aamerĩte meke; makĩreka tũhĩĩ tũu tũtũũre muoyo.
english: But the midwives feared God,‡ and didn’t do what the king of Egypt commanded them, but saved the baby boys alive.

kikuyu: Rĩrĩa yarahũka, arĩa marĩ hinya nĩmamakaga; mooraga ĩtanamahũũra.
english: When he raises himself up, the mighty are afraid. They retreat before his thrashing.

kikuyu: “ ‘Ningĩ mũndũ angĩkaamũrĩra Jehova mũgũnda ũrĩa agũrĩte, na ti mũgũnda wa nyũmba yao,
english: “ ‘If he dedicates a field to Yahweh which he has bought, which is not of the field of his possession,

kikuyu: Jesũ akĩmarora, akĩmooria atĩrĩ, “Maandĩko marĩa mandĩkĩtwo atĩrĩ: “ ‘Ihiga rĩrĩa aaki maaregire, nĩrĩo rĩtuĩkĩte ihiga inene rĩa koine’, ũguo nĩ kuuga atĩa?
english: But he looked at them and said, “Then what is this that is written, ‘The stone which the builders rejected was made the chief cornerstone’?*

kikuyu: No rĩrĩa aathitangirwo nĩ athĩnjĩri-Ngai arĩa anene na athuuri, ndarĩ ũndũ aacookirie.
english: When he was accused by the chief priests and elders, he answered nothing.

kikuyu: Nake akĩoya mĩgate ĩyo ĩtano na thamaki icio igĩrĩ, akĩrora na igũrũ, agĩcookia ngaatho na agĩcienyũranga. Agĩcooka agĩcinengera arutwo ake magaĩre andũ.
english: He took the five loaves and the two fish, and looking up to the sky, he blessed them, broke them, and gave them to the disciples to set before the multitude.

kikuyu: Nĩngahaanda rũciaro rwake nginya tene, na gĩtĩ gĩake kĩa ũnene ndĩkĩhaande rĩrĩa rĩothe igũrũ rĩgũtũũra.
english: I will also make his offspring endure forever, and his throne as the days of heaven.

kikuyu: Nake Naomi akĩoya kaana kau, agĩkaigĩrĩra ciero-inĩ ciake, agĩkarera.
english: Naomi took the child, laid him in her bosom, and became nurse to him.

kikuyu: Makĩmĩtwarĩra Jesũ, magĩcooka makĩigĩrĩra nguo ciao igũrũ rĩa njaũ ĩyo, na magĩikarĩria Jesũ igũrũ rĩayo.
english: Then they brought it to Jesus. They threw their cloaks on the colt and sat Jesus on them.

kikuyu: Ndaabataragio nĩ ũira wa mũndũ, ũkoniĩ mũndũ ũrĩa ũngĩ, nĩgũkorwo nĩamenyaga kĩrĩa kĩrĩ thĩinĩ wa mũndũ.
english: and because he didn’t need for anyone to testify concerning man; for he himself knew what was in man.

kikuyu: na Nibushani, na itũũra inene rĩa Cumbĩ, na Eni-Gedi; mothe maarĩ matũũra matandatũ na tũtũũra twamo.
english: Nibshan, the City of Salt, and En Gedi; six cities with their villages.

kikuyu: a rĩrĩ, mũraika wa Jehova nĩambatire agĩkinya Bokimu oimĩte Giligali, akiuga atĩrĩ, “Niĩ nĩ niĩ ndaamwambatirie ngĩmũruta bũrũri wa Misiri na ngĩmũtongoria nginya bũrũri-inĩ ũrĩa ndeerĩire maithe manyu na mwĩhĩtwa atĩ nĩngamahe. Ngiuga atĩrĩ, ‘Niĩ ndirĩ hĩndĩ ngaathũkia kĩrĩkanĩro giitũ na inyuĩ,
english: ahweh’s angel came up from Gilgal to Bochim. He said, “I brought you out of Egypt, and have brought you to the land which I swore to give your fathers. I said, ‘I will never break my covenant with you.

kikuyu: Cooka woe gakundi kayo ũgaikie mwaki-inĩ ũgacine. Mwaki nĩũgatheerema kuuma hau ũiyũre nyũmba yothe ya Isiraeli.
english: Of these again you shall take, and cast them into the middle of the fire, and burn them in the fire. From it a fire will come out into all the house of Israel.

kikuyu: Ningĩ Barizilai, ũrĩa Mũgileadi agĩikũrũka kuuma Rogelimu akaringe Rũũĩ rwa Jorodani me hamwe na mũthamaki, na amumagarie njĩra-inĩ ya kuuma kũu.
english: Barzillai the Gileadite came down from Rogelim; and he went over the Jordan with the king to conduct him over the Jordan.

kikuyu: kũrĩa gwatũmire ndĩmookĩrĩre nginya ngĩmatwarithia bũrũri wa thũ ciao, thuutha ũcio rĩrĩa ngoro icio ciao nyũmũ ikenyiihia na ciĩtĩkĩre ihũũra rĩa mehia mao-rĩ,
english: I also walked contrary to them, and brought them into the land of their enemies; if then their uncircumcised heart is humbled, and they then accept the punishment of their iniquity,

kikuyu: na makĩria ma ũguo ũkoiga atĩ marakara make matiherithanagia na ndarũmbũyagia waganu o na hanini!
english: But now, because he has not visited in his anger, neither does he greatly regard arrogance,

kikuyu: Na rĩrĩa rĩothe ngoma thũku ciamuonaga, ciegũithagia thĩ mbere yake, igakaya ikiugaga atĩrĩ, “Wee nĩwe Mũrũ wa Ngai.”
english: The unclean spirits, whenever they saw him, fell down before him and cried, “You are the Son of God!”

kikuyu: Nowe Jesũ ndaigana gũcookia ũndũ o na ũmwe wa macio; nake barũthi akĩgega mũno.
english: He gave him no answer, not even one word, so that the governor marveled greatly.

kikuyu: na ngʼombe 72,000,
english: seventy-two thousand head of cattle,

kikuyu: ũrũ wakwa, ũngĩkorwo ũigĩte kĩndũ gĩa kũrũgamĩrĩra thiirĩ wa mũndũ wa itũũra rĩaku, ũkorwo atĩ nĩwe ũtuĩkĩte wa kũmũrĩhĩra thiirĩ harĩ mũndũ ũngĩ-rĩ,
english: y son, if you have become collateral for your neighbor, if you have struck your hands in pledge for a stranger,

kikuyu: na aarie ndeto cia kũmũcambia, nake amũthũkĩrie rĩĩtwa, oige atĩrĩ, “Ndahikirie mũtumia ũyũ, no rĩrĩ, ndamũkuhĩrĩria ndionire imenyithia cia atĩ nĩ mũirĩtu gathirange,”
english: accuses her of shameful things, gives her a bad name, and says, “I took this woman, and when I came near to her, I didn’t find in her the tokens of virginity;”

kikuyu: Inyuĩ mwaciirĩte kũnjĩka ũũru, no Ngai aanjiirĩire ũndũ mwega nĩguo akinyanĩrie ũrĩa kũrekĩka rĩu, ũhoro wa kũhonokia mĩoyo ya andũ aingĩ.
english: As for you, you meant evil against me, but God meant it for good, to save many people alive, as is happening today.

kikuyu: Mũkaahũthĩra hinya wanyu o tũhũ, tondũ tĩĩri wanyu ndũkagethwo kĩndũ, kana mĩtĩ ya bũrũri wanyu ĩciare maciaro mayo.
english: Your strength will be spent in vain; for your land won’t yield its increase, neither will the trees of the land yield their fruit.

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ngoro-inĩ nĩkuo kuumaga meciiria mooru, na ũragani, na ũtharia, na ũhũũri maraya, na ũici, na kũiganĩrĩra igenyo, na njambanio.
english: For out of the heart come evil thoughts, murders, adulteries, sexual sins, thefts, false testimony, and blasphemies.

kikuyu: Nao Alawii maarĩ: Jeshua mũrũ wa Azania, na Binui wa ariũ a Henadadi, na Kadimieli,
english: The Levites: Jeshua the son of Azaniah, Binnui of the sons of Henadad, Kadmiel;

kikuyu: We mũthaka kũrĩ andũ-a-nja othe, akorwo ndũũĩ-rĩ, umagara ũrũmĩrĩre makinya ma ngʼondu, ũkarĩithĩrie tũũri twaku hakuhĩ na hema cia arĩithi. Mwendani
english: If you don’t know, most beautiful among women, follow the tracks of the sheep. Graze your young goats beside the shepherds’ tents.

kikuyu: Nake mũnabii akĩũria atĩrĩ, “Nĩ kĩĩ moonire nyũmba-inĩ yaku ya ũthamaki.” Nake Hezekia agĩcookia atĩrĩ, “Moonire indo ciothe cia nyũmba yakwa ya ũthamaki. Gũtirĩ kĩndũ o na kĩmwe kĩrĩ igĩĩna-inĩ ciakwa itanamoonia.”
english: Then he asked, “What have they seen in your house?” Hezekiah answered, “They have seen all that is in my house. There is nothing among my treasures that I have not shown them.”

kikuyu: Nake Jehu akĩmaandĩkĩra marũa ma keerĩ akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Angĩkorwo mũrĩ mwena wakwa na nĩmũkũnjathĩkĩra, ũragai ariũ a mwathi wanyu, mũgooka na ciongo ciao gũkũ Jezireeli ihinda o ta rĩĩrĩ rũciũ.” Na rĩrĩ, ariũ a mũthamaki mĩrongo mũgwanja maarĩ na atongoria a itũũra arĩa maamareraga.
english: Then he wrote a letter the second time to them, saying, “If you are on my side, and if you will listen to my voice, take the heads of the men who are your master’s sons, and come to me to Jezreel by tomorrow this time.” Now the king’s sons, being seventy persons, were with the great men of the city, who brought them up.

kikuyu: “Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa maũndũ macio mothe makaahinga, na ti-itherũ nĩmakahinga, hĩndĩ ĩyo nĩmaka- menya atĩ thĩinĩ wao nĩ kwarĩ na mũnabii.”
english: “When this comes to pass—behold, it comes—then they will know that a prophet has been among them.”

kikuyu: Manase akĩhurũka hamwe na maithe make na agĩthikwo nyũmba-inĩ yake ya ũthamaki. Nake mũriũ Amoni agĩtuĩka mũthamaki ithenya rĩake. Amoni Mũthamaki wa Juda
english: So Manasseh slept with his fathers, and they buried him in his own house; and Amon his son reigned in his place.

kikuyu: “Na rĩrĩ, mwĩciirĩtie ũrĩa mũkũregana na ũthamaki wa Jehova, ũrĩa ũrĩ moko-inĩ ma njiaro cia Daudi. Ti-itherũ mũrĩ mbũtũ nene ya ita, na mũrĩ na njaũ cia thahabu iria ciathondekirwo nĩ Jeroboamu ituĩke ngai cianyu.
english: “Now you intend to withstand the kingdom of Yahweh in the hand of the sons of David. You are a great multitude, and the golden calves which Jeroboam made you for gods are with you.

kikuyu: Ningĩ akĩĩra mũndũ-wa-nja ũcio atĩrĩ, “Nĩngũkũingĩhĩria ruo mũno hĩndĩ ya gũciara; ũrĩciaraga ciana na ruo. Merirĩria maku marĩkoragwo marĩ harĩ mũthuuriguo,
english: To the woman he said, “I will greatly multiply your pain in childbirth. You will bear children in pain. Your desire will be for your husband, and he will rule over you.”

kikuyu: Rĩrĩa Samũeli aarehire mĩhĩrĩga yothe ya Isiraeli hakuhĩ-rĩ, mũhĩrĩga wa Benjamini nĩguo wathuurirwo.
english: So Samuel brought all the tribes of Israel near, and the tribe of Benjamin was chosen.

kikuyu: “Efiraimu nĩetukanĩtie na ndũrĩrĩ;
english: Ephraim mixes himself among the nations. Ephraim is a pancake not turned over.

kikuyu: “ ‘Mũisiraeli o wothe, kana mũgeni o wothe ũtũũranagia na inyuĩ, ũkaaguĩma nyamũ kana nyoni o yothe ĩngĩrĩĩo, no nginya agaita thakame yayo thĩ, acooke amĩthike na tĩĩri,
english: “ ‘Whatever man there is of the children of Israel, or of the strangers who live as foreigners among them, who takes in hunting any animal or bird that may be eaten, he shall pour out its blood, and cover it with dust.

kikuyu: Na angĩkorwo ũhoro ũcio wathiraga wokire na riiri-rĩ, ũcio ũngĩ wa gũtũũra ndũngĩgĩkorwo na riiri makĩria!
english: For if that which passes away was with glory, much more that which remains is in glory.

kikuyu: Ũtũtuĩte kĩndũ gĩa kũrũagĩrwo nĩ arĩa tũriganĩtie nao, nacio thũ ciitũ igatũnyũrũria.
english: You make us a source of contention to our neighbors. Our enemies laugh among themselves.

kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, athĩnjĩri-Ngai arĩa anene na Kĩama kĩrĩa Kĩnene gĩothe nĩmacarĩrĩirie ũira wa maheeni nĩguo mamũũragithie.
english: Now the chief priests, the elders, and the whole council sought false testimony against Jesus, that they might put him to death,

kikuyu: Geithiai Turufena na Turufosa, andũ-a-nja acio marutaga wĩra na hinya thĩinĩ wa Mwathani. Na mũgeithie mũrata wakwa Perisi, mũndũ-wa-nja ũngĩ o nake ũrutĩte wĩra na hinya mũno thĩinĩ wa Mwathani.
english: Greet Tryphaena and Tryphosa, who labor in the Lord. Greet Persis, the beloved, who labored much in the Lord.

kikuyu: ingĩ Jehova akĩnjĩĩra atĩrĩ, “Thiĩ, wonie mũtumia waku wendo rĩngĩ, o na gũtuĩka nĩendetwo nĩ mũndũ ũngĩ na nĩ mũtharia. Mwende o ta ũrĩa Jehova endete andũ a Isiraeli, o na gũtuĩka nĩmagarũrũkagĩra ngai ingĩ na makenda keki iria nyamũre cia thabibũ nyũmũ.”
english: ahweh said to me, “Go again, love a woman loved by another, and an adulteress, even as Yahweh loves the children of Israel, though they turn to other gods, and love cakes of raisins.”

kikuyu: Kĩu nĩkĩo gĩtũmaga gĩthaka kĩu gĩĩtwo Gĩthaka gĩa Thakame nginya ũmũthĩ.
english: Therefore that field has been called “The Field of Blood” to this day.

kikuyu: Wee Jehova, tũhonokie; Wee Jehova, tũma tũgaacĩre.
english: Save us now, we beg you, Yahweh! Yahweh, we beg you, send prosperity now.

kikuyu: No nĩngacookia Isiraeli ũrĩithio-inĩ wake mwene, nake akaarĩithagĩria kũu Karimeli na Bashani; akaarĩĩaga akahũũna kũu irima-inĩ cia Efiraimu, na cia Gileadi.
english: I will bring Israel again to his pasture, and he will feed on Carmel and Bashan. His soul will be satisfied on the hills of Ephraim and in Gilead.

kikuyu: Mũgambo ũkĩmwarĩria riita rĩa keerĩ ũkĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Ndũkanoige kĩndũ o na kĩrĩkũ kĩrĩa Ngai atheretie atĩ ti kĩamũre.”
english: A voice came to him again the second time, “What God has cleansed, you must not call unclean.”

kikuyu: Nyũmba ĩrĩa nini ĩkongerereka ĩtuĩke andũ ngiri, nayo ĩrĩa ĩtarĩ andũ aingĩ ĩtuĩke rũrĩrĩ rũrĩ hinya mũno. Niĩ nĩ niĩ Jehova. Nĩngeeka ũndũ ũcio narua ihinda rĩaguo rĩakinya.”
english: The little one will become a thousand, and the small one a strong nation. I, Yahweh, will do this quickly in its time.”

kikuyu: Tigagaikũheenaniamũndũnaũrĩaũngĩ, kuonaatĩnĩmwĩyaũrĩte mũndũ ũrĩa wa tene na ciĩko ciake,
english: Don’t lie to one another, seeing that you have put off the old man with his doings,

kikuyu: Kaba mũthenya ũmwe nja-inĩ ciaku, gũkĩra gũikara mĩthenya ngiri handũ hangĩ o hothe; kaba ngorwo ndĩ mũrangĩri wa kĩhingo kĩa nyũmba ya Ngai wakwa, gũkĩra ndũũre thĩinĩ wa hema cia andũ arĩa aaganu.
english: For a day in your courts is better than a thousand. I would rather be a doorkeeper in the

kikuyu: Atĩrĩrĩ, nũũ ũngĩmwĩka ũũru mũngĩkorwo mũrĩ na kĩyo gĩa gwĩka wega?
english: Now who will harm you if you become imitators of that which is good?

kikuyu: No Jehova akĩũmia ngoro ya Firaũni, nake akĩrega kũmarekereria mathiĩ.
english: But Yahweh hardened Pharaoh’s heart, and he wouldn’t let them go.

kikuyu: wa njiaro cia Shekania; na kuuma njiaro cia Paroshu, aarĩ Zekaria, hamwe na andũ 150 arĩa meyandĩkithĩtie;
english: Of the sons of Shecaniah, of the sons of Parosh, Zechariah; and with him were listed by genealogy of the males one hundred fifty.

kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, megũtuĩka ndungata ciake, nĩgeetha mamenye ũtiganu wa kũndungatĩra na gũtungatĩra athamaki a mabũrũri ma kũngĩ.”
english: Nevertheless they will be his servants, that they may know my service, and the service of the kingdoms of the countries.”

kikuyu: Nĩngumbũra atĩ wendo waku ũtũũraga ũrĩ mũrũmu nginya tene, nyumbũre atĩ wĩhokeku waku nĩũũhaandĩte o kũu igũrũ.
english: I indeed declare, “Love stands firm forever. You established the heavens. Your faithfulness is in them.”

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ wa gĩtũmi gĩkĩ nĩngũmũtũmĩra Timotheo, mũrũ wakwa ũrĩa nyendete, na ũrĩa mwĩhokeku thĩinĩ wa Mwathani. Nĩekũmũririkania mũtũũrĩre wakwa thĩinĩ wa Kristũ Jesũ, ũrĩa ũringaine na ũrĩa ndutanaga makanitha-inĩ kũndũ guothe.
english: Because of this I have sent Timothy to you, who is my beloved and faithful child in the Lord, who will remind you of my  Job 5:13  Psalms 94:11

kikuyu: Nake Jesũ agĩcookia atĩrĩ, “Tiganai nake, araigĩte maguta macio nĩ ũndũ wa mũthenya ũrĩa ngaathikwo.
english: But Jesus said, “Leave her alone. She has kept this for the day of my burial.

kikuyu: Nake Jesũ agĩthiĩ, akĩoya mũgate akĩmahe, agĩcooka akĩmahe thamaki o nacio.
english: Then Jesus came and took the bread, gave it to them, and the fish likewise.  or, Twin  TR adds “Thomas,”  or, Twin  200 cubits is about 100 yards or about 91 meters

kikuyu: “Kũu gũtirĩ mwana wa rũkenge ũgaacooka gũkua arĩ na matukũ manini, o na kana mũndũ mũkũrũ akue atatũũrĩte mĩaka yake yothe; mũndũ ũrĩa ũgaakua arĩ na mĩaka igana akoonagwo taarĩ mũndũ mwĩthĩ; nake ũrĩa ũtagaakinyia mĩaka igana akoonagwo ta arumĩtwo na kĩrumi.
english: “No more will there be an infant who only lives a few days, nor an old man who has not filled his days; for the child will die one hundred years old, and the sinner being one hundred years old will be accursed.

kikuyu: Ũũgĩ ũrĩ igũrũ mũno, ndũngĩkinyĩrĩka nĩ mũndũ mũkĩĩgu; rĩrĩa kwagomanwo kĩhingo-inĩ, ndangĩona wa kuuga.
english: Wisdom is too high for a fool. He doesn’t open his mouth in the gate.

kikuyu: Maũndũ macio mothe meekirwo Jerusalemu na Juda nĩ tondũ wa marakara ma Jehova, na mũthia-inĩ akĩmaingata mehere mbere yake. Kũgũa kwa Jerusalemu Nake Zedekia akĩremera mũthamaki wa Babuloni.
english: For through the anger of Yahweh, this happened in Jerusalem and Judah, until he had cast them out from his presence. Then Zedekiah rebelled against the king of Babylon.

kikuyu: Ningĩ mũgeithie Asunikirito, na Felegoni, na Herime, na Pateroba, na Herima, o na ariũ na aarĩ a Ithe witũ arĩa marĩ hamwe nao.
english: Greet Asyncritus, Phlegon, Hermes, Patrobas, Hermas, and the brothers‡ who are with them.

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo nĩũkaririkana mĩthiĩre yaku, nawe ũconoke rĩrĩa ũkaamũkĩra aarĩ a maitũguo arĩa akũrũ gũgũkĩra na arĩa anini gũgũkĩra. Nĩngamaneana kũrĩ we marĩ ta aarĩ aku, no ti kũringana na kĩrĩkanĩro giitũ nawe.
english: Then you will remember your ways and be ashamed when you receive your sisters, your elder sisters and your younger; and I will give them to you for daughters, but not by your covenant.

kikuyu: “Jehova nĩ mũthingu, no rĩrĩ, nĩndaremeire watho wake. Thikĩrĩriai, inyuĩ andũ othe, na mũrore gũthĩĩnĩka gwakwa. Aanake akwa na airĩtu akwa nĩmatahĩtwo magatwarwo thĩ ngʼeni.
english: “Yahweh is righteous, for I have rebelled against his commandment. Please hear all you peoples, and see my sorrow. My virgins and my young men have gone into captivity.

kikuyu: Matirĩ hĩndĩ marĩ maheenania; o na matirĩ ũcuuke. Araika Atatũ
english: In their mouth was found no lie, for they are blameless.†

kikuyu: Nake Ngai agĩathana, akiuga atĩrĩ, “Nĩkũgĩe wariĩ mũnene gatagatĩ ka maaĩ ũgayũkanie maaĩ na marĩa mangĩ.”
english: God said, “Let there be an expanse in the middle of the waters, and let it divide the waters from the waters.”

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo niĩ nĩ niĩ Jehova, Ngai waku, ũrĩa ũkũnyiitaga guoko kwa ũrĩo, na agakwĩra atĩrĩ, Tiga gwĩtigĩra; nĩngũgũteithia.
english: For I, Yahweh your God, will hold your right hand, saying to you, ‘Don’t be afraid. I will help you.’

kikuyu: Atĩrĩrĩ, inyuĩ inyuothe arĩa mũnyootiĩ-rĩ, ũkaai mũnyue maaĩ; na inyuĩ mũtarĩ na mbeeca-rĩ, ũkaai mũgũre irio, mũrĩe! Ũkaai, mũgũre ndibei na iria mũtarĩ na mbeeca mũkũruta, na hatarĩ na thogora.
english: Hey! Come, everyone who thirsts, to the waters! Come, he who has no money, buy, and eat! Yes, come, buy wine and milk without money and without price.

kikuyu: Jusufu akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Ũtaũri wakĩo nĩ ũyũ: Ikabũ icio ithatũ nĩ mĩthenya ĩtatũ.
english: Joseph answered, “This is its interpretation. The three baskets are three days.

kikuyu: Ĩthekagĩrĩra inegene rĩrĩ thĩinĩ wa itũũra; ndĩiguaga mũkaĩrĩrio wa mũtwarithia.
english: He scorns the tumult of the city, neither does he hear the shouting of the driver.

kikuyu: andũ arĩa anene maatigaga kwaria, makahumbĩra tũnua twao na moko;
english: The princes refrained from talking, and laid their hand on their mouth.

kikuyu: Rutanĩriai mĩgate ĩyo na tũtũrũme mũgwanja, o kamwe karĩ na ũkũrũ wa mwaka ũmwe, na tũtarĩ na kaũũgũ, na gategwa kamwe, na ndũrũme igĩrĩ. Nacio igaatuĩka iruta rĩa njino rĩrutĩirwo Jehova, hamwe na maruta macio ma mũtu na ma kũnyuuo, rĩĩrĩ nĩ iruta rĩa gũcinwo na mwaki, rĩrĩ na mũtararĩko mwega wa gũkenia Jehova.
english: You shall present with the bread seven lambs without defect a year old,  1 ephah is about 22 liters or about 2/3 of a bushel  A hin is about 6.5 liters or 1.7 gallons.  1 ephah is about 22 liters or about 2/3 of a bushel

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio gĩthaka kĩu na ngurunga ĩyo yarĩ kuo ikĩgarũrĩrwo Iburahĩmu nĩ Ahiti irĩ handũ ha gũthikanaga.
english: The field, and the cave that is in it, were deeded to Abraham by the children of Heth as a possession for a burial place.

kikuyu: Rũkĩraiha nginya rũgĩkinyĩra mbũtũ cia igũrũ, na rũkĩrutũrũra mbũtũ imwe rũgĩcigũithia thĩ na rũgĩcirangĩrĩria.
english: It grew great, even to the army of the sky; and it cast down some of the army and of the stars to the ground and trampled on them.

kikuyu: Acio nĩ arĩa mateethũkĩtie na andũ-a-nja, nĩgũkorwo nĩmeigĩte marĩ atheru. Nao maarũmagĩrĩra Gatũrũme o kũrĩa guothe aathiiaga. Nĩo maagũrirwo kuuma kũrĩ andũ, na makĩrutwo marĩ maciaro ma mbere kũrĩ Ngai o na kũrĩ Gatũrũme.
english: These are those who were not defiled with women, for they are virgins. These are those who follow the Lamb wherever he goes. These were redeemed by Jesus from among men, the first fruits to God and to the Lamb.

kikuyu: Jehova agĩcooka akĩĩra Musa atĩrĩ,
english: Yahweh spoke to Moses, saying,

kikuyu: Mũriũ wa Jonathani: eetagwo Meribu-Baali, na nĩwe warĩ ithe wa Mika.
english: The son of Jonathan was Merib-baal. Merib-baal became the father of Micah.

kikuyu: i-itherũ Ngai nĩ mwega harĩ andũ a Isiraeli, harĩ andũ arĩa atheru ngoro.
english: urely God† is good to Israel, to those who are pure in heart.

kikuyu: Mũndũ kĩgũũta o na ndahĩhagia nyama cia ũguĩmi wake, no mũndũ ũrĩ kĩyo onaga indo ciake irĩ cia goro.
english: The slothful man doesn’t roast his game, but the possessions of diligent men are prized.

kikuyu: Andũ acio metuaga ta meyamũrĩire Ngai no nĩmakaanaga hinya wake. Wĩthemage andũ ta acio.
english: holding a form of godliness but having denied its power. Turn away from these, also.

kikuyu: Nĩmacinire handũ-haku-harĩa-haamũre hakĩmomoka, magĩthaahia gĩikaro kĩa Rĩĩtwa rĩaku.
english: They have burned your sanctuary to the ground.  The word translated “Lord” is “Adonai.”  “Yahweh” is God’s proper Name, sometimes rendered “LORD” (all caps) in other translations.

kikuyu: Goocai Jehova Ngai wanyu atanarehe nduma, na mbere ya magũrũ manyu mahĩngwo kũu irĩma-inĩ kũragĩa nduma. Inyuĩ mwĩrĩgĩrĩire ũtheri, nowe-rĩ, nĩakaũgarũra ũtuĩke nduma ndumanu, aũgarũre ũtuĩke ta kĩeha kĩnene.
english: Give glory to Yahweh your God, before he causes darkness, and before your feet stumble on the dark mountains, and while you look for light, he turns it into the shadow of death, and makes it deep darkness.

kikuyu: No tũngĩĩtuĩra ciira ithuĩ ene, tũtingĩtuĩka a gũciirithio.
english: For if we discerned ourselves, we wouldn’t be judged.

kikuyu: mahaandĩtwo kũu nyũmba-inĩ ya Jehova, marĩtheeremaga kũu nja-inĩ cia Ngai witũ.
english: They are planted in Yahweh’s house. They will flourish in our God’s courts.

kikuyu: Tũkonyo na honge ciarĩ kĩndũ kĩmwe na mũtĩ ũcio wa kũigĩrĩrwo matawa, iturĩtwo kuuma thahabu therie.
english: Their buds and their branches were of one piece with it. The whole thing was one beaten work of pure gold.

kikuyu: Abigaili ndaateire ihinda. Akĩoya mĩgate magana meerĩ, na mondo igĩrĩ cia ndibei, na ngʼondu ithano ĩrĩ thĩnje, na ibaba ithano* cia ngano hĩhie, na imanjĩka igana cia thabibũ nyũmũ, na ikũmba magana meerĩ cia ngũyũ, agĩciigĩrĩra ndigiri igũrũ.
english: Then Abigail hurried and took two hundred loaves of bread, two containers of wine, five sheep ready dressed, five seahs† of parched grain, one hundred clusters of raisins, and two hundred cakes of figs, and laid them on donkeys.

kikuyu: Korwo ndariĩ ũrĩa ndatariĩ matukũ-inĩ marĩa ndagaacĩire biũ, hĩndĩ ĩrĩa kuumĩrania ngoro na Ngai kwarathimaga nyũmba yakwa,
english: as I was in my prime, when the friendship of God was in my tent,

kikuyu: Memukani agĩcookia arĩ mbere ya mũthamaki na andũ arĩa maarĩ igweta atĩrĩ, “Vashiti mũtumia wa mũthamaki nĩekĩte mahĩtia, na to mũthamaki wiki ahĩtĩirie, no ahĩtĩirie o na andũ othe arĩa marĩ igweta na andũ othe a mabũrũri ma Mũthamaki Ahasuerusu.
english: Memucan answered before the king and the princes, “Vashti the queen has not done wrong to just the king, but also to all the princes, and to all the people who are in all the provinces of the King Ahasuerus.

kikuyu: a rĩrĩ, Paũlũ agĩkinya Deribe agĩcooka akiuma kuo agĩthiĩ Lisitera, kũrĩa mũrutwo wetagwo Timotheo aatũũraga, ũrĩa nyina aarĩ Mũyahudi mũtumia wetĩkĩtie Ngai, no ithe aarĩ Mũyunani.
english: e came to Derbe and Lystra; and behold, a certain disciple was there, named Timothy, the son of a Jewess who believed, but his father was a Greek.

kikuyu: Nĩngeera mwena wa gathigathini atĩrĩ, ‘Marekererie!’ na njĩĩre mwena wa gũthini atĩrĩ, ‘Tiga kũmagirĩrĩria.’ Ariũ akwa marehei moime kũndũ kũraya, o nao airĩtu akwa marehei moime ituri-inĩ ciothe cia thĩ:
english: I will tell the north, ‘Give them up!’ and tell the south, ‘Don’t hold them back! Bring my sons from far away, and my daughters from the ends of the earth—

kikuyu: Makiuga atĩrĩ, “Mũndũ ũyũ araringĩrĩria andũ kũhooya Ngai na njĩra ĩrĩa ĩreganĩte na watho.”
english: saying, “This man persuades men to worship God contrary to the law.”

kikuyu: Ndũkanagĩe ũrata na mũndũ ũrĩa ũrĩ marakara mahiũ, ndũkanagĩe thiritũ na mũndũ ũrĩa ũhiũhaga kũrakara,
english: Don’t befriend a hot-tempered man. Don’t associate with one who harbors anger,

kikuyu: Amuramu nĩahikirie mwarĩ wa nyina na ithe wetagwo Jokebedi, ũrĩa wamũciarĩire Harũni na Musa. Amuramu aatũũrire muoyo mĩaka 137.
english: Amram took Jochebed his father’s sister to himself as wife; and she bore him Aaron and Moses. The years of the life of Amram were one hundred thirty-seven years.

kikuyu: Wega wa Mwathani witũ Jesũ Kristũ ũrogĩa na inyuĩ inyuothe.
english: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you all. Amen.

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ wa gwĩtĩkia-rĩ, nĩtũmenyaga atĩ thĩ yothe yombirwo na kiugo kĩa Ngai, na nĩ ũndũ ũcio indo iria cionagwo itiathondekirwo kuuma kũrĩ indo iria cionekanaga.
english: By faith we understand that the universe has been framed by the word of God, so that what is seen has not been made out of things which are visible.

kikuyu: Joabu agĩcookia atĩrĩ, “Ti-itherũ o ta ũrĩa Ngai atũũraga muoyo, korwo ndũnaaria-rĩ, andũ aya mangĩathiĩ o na mbere gũtengʼeria ariũ a ithe wao nginya rũciinĩ.”
english: Joab said, “As God‡ lives, if you had not spoken, surely then in the morning the people would have gone away, and not each followed his brother.”

kikuyu: Nĩtũrehei ithandũkũ rĩa kĩrĩkanĩro rĩa Ngai witũ tũrĩcookie kũrĩ ithuĩ, nĩgũkorwo tũtiatuĩragia ũhoro harĩ rĩo hĩndĩ ya ũthamaki wa Saũlũ.”
english: Also, let’s bring the ark of our God back to us again, for we didn’t seek it in the days of Saul.”

kikuyu: Aragania njĩra iria magũrũ maku marĩthiiagĩra, na ũthiiagĩre o njĩra iria ngiindĩrĩku.
english: Make the path of your feet level. Let all of your ways be established.

kikuyu: No Jehova akiuga atĩrĩ, “Ndũngĩona ũthiũ wakwa, nĩgũkorwo gũtirĩ mũndũ ũngĩona ũthiũ wakwa na acooke atũũre muoyo.”
english: He said, “You cannot see my face, for man may not see me and live.”

kikuyu: Ũngĩtoonya mũgũnda wa mĩthabibũ wa mũndũ ũngĩ, no ũrĩe thabibũ nginya ũhũũne, no ndũgekĩre o na ĩmwe kĩondo-inĩ gĩaku.
english: When you come into your neighbor’s vineyard, then you may eat your fill of grapes at your own pleasure; but you shall not put any in your container.

kikuyu: wana mũũgĩ atũmaga ithe akene, no mwana mũkĩĩgu atũũragia nyina na kĩeha.
english: he proverbs of Solomon. A wise son makes a glad father; but a foolish son brings grief to his mother.

kikuyu: Ciana ciake irotuĩka njara-rũhĩ cia gũcangacanga; iroingatwo ciume mĩciĩ yao ĩyo mĩanangĩku.
english: Let his children be wandering beggars. Let them be sought from their ruins.

kikuyu: Nĩ kaba gũtũũra nyũmba-igũrũ, koine-inĩ, gũkĩra gũtũũra nyũmba thĩinĩ na mũtumia wa haaro.
english: It is better to dwell in the corner of the housetop than to share a house with a contentious woman.

kikuyu: ci nĩcio irĩra cia watho wa kũrũmĩrĩrwo, na mawatho marĩa mwagĩrĩirwo nĩ kũmenyerera mũmarũmagĩrĩremũrĩbũrũriũrĩaJehovaNgaiwamaithemanyuamũheetemũwĩgwatĩre, rĩrĩarĩothe mũgũtũũra bũrũri-inĩ ũcio.
english: hese are the statutes and the ordinances which you shall observe to do in the land which Yahweh, the God of your fathers, has given you to possess all the days that you live on the earth.

kikuyu: Gĩthaka kĩu nĩkĩo gĩgaatuĩka igai rĩake thĩinĩ wa bũrũri wa Isiraeli. Nao anene acio akwa matigacooka kũhinyĩrĩria andũ akwa, no nĩmagetĩkĩria andũ a nyũmba ya Isiraeli megwatĩre bũrũri ũcio kũringana na mĩhĩrĩga yao.
english: In the land it shall be to him for a possession in Israel. My princes shall no more oppress my people, but they shall give the land to the house of Israel according to their tribes.”

kikuyu: Mũraika wa Jehova agĩkora Hagari hakuhĩ na handũ haatherũkaga maaĩ werũ-inĩ; gathima kau kaarĩ mũkĩra-inĩ wa njĩra ĩrĩa ya gũthiĩ Shuri.
english: Yahweh’s angel found her by a fountain of water in the wilderness, by the fountain on the way to Shur.

kikuyu: Nĩtũũĩ atĩ Ngai ndathikagĩrĩria andũ ehia. Athikagĩrĩria mũndũ ũrĩa ũrĩ na ũngai na ũrĩa wĩkaga wendi wake.
english: We know that God doesn’t listen to sinners, but if anyone is a worshiper of God and does his will, he listens to him.*

kikuyu: Ningĩ akĩndehe itoonyero-inĩ rĩa kĩhingo gĩa gathigathini kĩa nyũmba ya Jehova, na niĩ ngĩona andũ-a-nja maikarĩte thĩ ho magĩcakaĩra Tamuzu.*
english: Then he brought me to the door of the gate of Yahweh’s house which was toward the north; and I saw the women sit there weeping for Tammuz.

kikuyu: Ndigũtũũra ndũithanagia nginya tene, o na kana ndũũre ndakarĩte hĩndĩ ciothe, tondũ roho wa mũndũ wahota kũringĩka mbere yakwa, mĩhũmũ ĩyo ya mũndũ ũcio ndoombire ĩhwererekere.
english: For I will not contend forever, neither will I always be angry; for the spirit would faint before me, and the souls whom I have made.

kikuyu: Jehova akĩnjĩĩra atĩrĩ, “Nĩndambĩrĩria kũneana Sihoni na bũrũri wake kũrĩ inyuĩ. Ambĩrĩriai kũmahũũra mũmatoorie, nĩgeetha mwĩgwatĩre bũrũri ũcio wake.”
english: Yahweh said to me, “Behold, I have begun to deliver up Sihon and his land before you. Begin to possess, that you may inherit his land.”

kikuyu: “Nĩ kĩĩ gĩtũmaga andũ arĩa marĩ na mĩnyamaro maheo ũtheri, naguo muoyo ũkaheo arĩa marĩ na ruo rwa ngoro,
english: “Why is light given to him who is in misery, life to the bitter in soul,

kikuyu: ingĩ mũndũ ũcio akĩnginyia kĩhingo-inĩ kĩrĩa kĩangʼetheire mwena wa irathĩro,
english: fterward he brought me to the gate, even the gate that looks toward the east.

kikuyu: Ndaiguire ũhoro ũcio, ngoro yakwa ĩgĩtumatuuma, nacio iromo ciakwa ikĩinaina nĩ mũgambo ũcio; mahĩndĩ makwa makĩonja, namo magũrũ makwa makĩinaina. No niĩ nĩngweterera ngirĩrĩirie, nginya mũthenya ũrĩa wa kũnyariirĩka ũgaakinya, ũkore rũrĩrĩ rũrĩa rũtũtharĩkagĩra.
english: I heard, and my body trembled. My lips quivered at the voice. Rottenness enters into my bones, and I tremble in my place because I must wait quietly for the day of trouble, for the coming up of the people who invade us.

kikuyu: Niĩ nĩngendia ariũ anyu na airĩtu anyu kũrĩ andũ a Juda, nao mamenderie andũ a Sheba, rũrĩrĩ rwa kũraya mũno.” Nĩ Jehova warĩtie ũndũ ũcio.
english: and I will sell your sons and your daughters into the hands of the children of Judah, and they will sell them to the men of Sheba, to a faraway nation, for Yahweh has spoken it.”

kikuyu: Thuutha wa kwaranĩria na andũ, Jehoshafatu agĩthuura andũ a kũinĩra Jehova na a kũmũgooca nĩ ũndũ wa riiri wa ũtheru wake, nao hĩndĩ ĩrĩa megũtongoria mbũtũ ĩyo ya ita, makiugaga atĩrĩ: “Cookeriai Jehova ngaatho, nĩgũkorwo wendo wake ũtũũraga nginya tene.”
english: When he had taken counsel with the people, he appointed those who were to sing to Yahweh and give praise in holy array as they go out before the army, and say, “Give thanks to Yahweh, for his loving kindness endures forever.”

kikuyu: akĩheana bũrũri wao ũtuĩke igai, ũtuĩke igai kũrĩ andũ ake a Isiraeli.
english: and gave their land for a heritage, a heritage to Israel, his people.

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo Abisalomu nĩakoretwo orĩte. Na rĩrĩ, mũndũ ũrĩa warangagĩra itũũra agĩcũthĩrĩria na akĩona andũ aingĩ njĩra-inĩ mwena wake wa ithũĩro, maikũrũkĩire mwena-inĩ wa kĩrĩma. Nake mũrangĩri agĩthiĩ akĩĩra mũthamaki atĩrĩ, “Nĩndĩrona andũ mwena ũũrĩa wa Horonaimu, mwena-inĩ wa kĩrĩma.”
english: But Absalom fled. The young man who kept the watch lifted up his eyes and looked, and behold, many people were coming by way of the hillside behind him.

kikuyu: makĩona arutwo ake amwe makĩrĩa irio na moko maarĩ na thaahu, ũguo nĩ ta kuuga, matethambĩte moko.
english: Now when they saw some of his disciples eating bread with defiled, that is unwashed, hands, they found fault.

kikuyu: Ruo ta rwa mũtumia ũkũrũmwo nĩrũramũnyiita, nowe nĩ mwana ũtarĩ na ũũgĩ; ihinda rĩakinya-rĩ, akarega gũikũrũka nĩguo aciarwo.
english: The sorrows of a travailing woman will come on him.

kikuyu: Andũ arĩa othe mendaga kũnjũraga maroconorithio na manyiitwo nĩ kĩrigiicano; arĩa othe mendaga nyanangwo marocooka na thuutha maconokete.
english: Let them be disappointed and confounded together who seek after my soul to destroy it. Let them be turned backward and brought to dishonor who delight in my hurt.

kikuyu: Na ũrĩa wothe mũkahooya, nĩngawĩka thĩinĩ wa rĩĩtwa rĩakwa, nĩgeetha Mũriũ arehere Baba riiri.
english: Whatever you will ask in my name, I will do it, that the Father may be glorified in the Son.

kikuyu: No Ngai nĩoonire andũ marĩ na mahĩtia, akiuga atĩrĩ: “Matukũ nĩmarooka, rĩrĩa ngaarĩkanĩra kĩrĩkanĩro kĩerũ na nyũmba ya Isiraeli, o hamwe na nyũmba ya Juda, nĩguo Mwathani ekuuga.
english: For finding fault with them, he said, “Behold,† the days are coming”, says the Lord, “that I will make a new covenant with the house of Israel and with the house of Judah;

kikuyu: tondũ Johana nĩamwĩrĩte atĩrĩ: “Watho ndũgwĩtĩkĩrĩtie ũmũhikie.”
english: For John said to him, “It is not lawful for you to have her.”

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio, agĩũkĩra, akĩoya kaana na nyina, magĩthiĩ bũrũri wa Isiraeli.
english: He arose and took the young child and his mother, and came into the land of Israel.

kikuyu: Mĩromo yaku ĩhaana ta uuthi mũtune; kanua gaku kagĩrĩte mũno. Thĩa ciaku ihaana ta ciatũ igĩrĩ cia itunda rĩa mũkomamanga, ikĩonerwo hau taama-inĩ ũcio ũkũhumbĩrĩte ũthiũ.
english: Your lips are like scarlet thread. Your mouth is lovely. Your temples are like a piece of a pomegranate behind your veil.

kikuyu: Gũtirĩ mũndũ mũthingu gũkũ thĩ, ũrĩa wĩkaga o wega mũtheri na ndehagia.
english: Surely there is not a righteous man on earth who does good and doesn’t sin.

kikuyu: Nao matiatigire kũrũmĩrĩra watho ũrĩa mũthamaki aathĩte athĩnjĩri-Ngai kana Alawii ũndũ-inĩ o wothe hamwe na ũmenyereri wa igĩĩna.
english: They didn’t depart from the commandment of the king to the priests and Levites concerning any matter or concerning the treasures.

kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, ndũgamĩte haha ũmũthĩ njiirithio nĩ ũndũ wa kwĩrĩgĩrĩra kĩĩranĩro kĩrĩa Ngai eerĩire maithe maitũ.
english: Now I stand here to be judged for the hope of the promise made by God to our fathers,

kikuyu: ũmwe wao aarĩ Ahija, ũrĩa wehumbĩte ebodi. Aarĩ mũrũ wa Ahitubu mũrũ wa nyina na Ikabodu, mũrũ wa Finehasi, ũrĩa mũrũ wa Eli, mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai wa Jehova kũu Shilo. Gũtiarĩ mũndũ wamenyete atĩ Jonathani nĩathiĩte.
english: including Ahijah the son of Ahitub, Ichabod’s brother, the son of Phinehas, the son of Eli the priest of Yahweh in Shiloh, wearing an ephod. The people didn’t know that Jonathan was gone.

kikuyu: Tondũ wa ũrĩa Isiraeli oonaga ũmaraya wake ũtarĩ ũndũ mũnene harĩ we, nĩathaahirie bũrũri ũcio, agĩkĩrĩrĩria kũhũũra ũmaraya na ũndũ wa kũhooya mahiga na mĩtĩ.
english: Because she took her prostitution lightly, the land was polluted, and she committed adultery with stones and with wood.

kikuyu: Akĩanĩrĩra na mũgambo mũnene, akiuga atĩrĩ, “Ũrenda atĩa na niĩ, wee Jesũ, Mũrũ wa Ngai-Ũrĩa-Ũrĩ-Igũrũ-Mũno? Ndagũthaitha na Ngai ndũkaanyariire!”
english: and crying out with a loud voice, he said, “What have I to do with you, Jesus, you Son of the Most High God? I adjure you by God, don’t torment me.”

kikuyu: Nake Jesũ akĩmacookeria atĩrĩ, “Wĩra wa Ngai nĩ ũyũ: nĩ mwĩtĩkie ũrĩa we atũmĩte.”
english: Jesus answered them, “This is the work of God, that you believe in him whom he has sent.”

kikuyu: Andũ aingĩ nĩmathiire kũrĩ Johana akamabatithie, nake akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Inyuĩ rũciaro rũrũ rwa nduĩra! Nũũ ũmũtaarĩte mũũrĩre marakara marĩa makiriĩ gũũka?
english: He said therefore to the multitudes who went out to be baptized by him, “You offspring of vipers, who warned you to flee from the wrath to come?

kikuyu: Guoko gwake kwa ũmotho akũigĩte rungu rwa mũtwe wakwa, nakuo guoko gwake kwa ũrĩo gũkaahĩmbĩria.
english: His left hand would be under my head. His right hand would embrace me.

kikuyu: Jehova Mwene-Hinya-Wothe ekuuga atĩrĩ, “Andũ aya moigaga atĩrĩ, ‘Ihinda ti ikinyu rĩa gwakĩra Jehova nyũmba.’ ”
english: “This is what Yahweh of Armies says: These people say, ‘The time hasn’t yet come, the time for Yahweh’s house to be built.’ ”

kikuyu: Nao athuuri arĩa mĩrongo ĩĩrĩ na ana, na ciũmbe iria inya irĩ muoyo, makĩĩgũithia thĩ makĩhooya Ngai, ũrĩa waikarĩire gĩtĩ kĩa ũnene. Nao makĩanĩrĩra atĩrĩ: “Ameni, Haleluya!”
english: The twenty-four elders and the four living creatures fell down and worshiped God who sits on the throne, saying, “Amen! Hallelujah!”

kikuyu: Nake Jesũ akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Mũgambo ũcio ndũnaaria nĩ ũndũ wakwa, no waria nĩ ũndũ wanyu.
english: Jesus answered, “This voice hasn’t come for my sake, but for your sakes.

kikuyu: Njiaro cia Manase ciarĩ: Asirieli, ũrĩa aaciarĩirwo nĩ thuriya yake ĩrĩa yarĩ Mũaramiti. Ningĩ thuriya ĩyo ĩkĩmũciarĩra Makiru, ũrĩa warĩ ithe wa Gileadi.
english: The sons of Manasseh: Asriel, whom his concubine the Aramitess bore. She bore Machir the father of Gilead.

kikuyu: No ariũ ake nĩmakehaarĩria kũrũa mbaara, macookanĩrĩrie mbũtũ nene ya ita hamwe ĩrĩa ĩkaahaata ta kĩguũ kĩa maaĩ kĩrĩa gĩtangĩhingĩrĩrio, na makinyie mbaara ĩyo nginya itũũra-inĩ rĩake rĩrĩa rĩirigĩre.
english: Behold, a hand touched me, which set me on my knees and on the palms of my hands.

kikuyu: Agĩthiĩ na mbere, akiuga atĩrĩ, “Ĩtĩkĩra kũũria Mũthamaki Solomoni, nĩ ũndũ wee ndan- gĩkũregera, aahe Abishagi ũrĩa Mũshunami atuĩke mũtumia wakwa.”
english: Bathsheba said, “All right. I will speak for you to the king.”

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo ndiaririe ndĩĩrĩte niĩ mwene, no Baba ũrĩa wandũmire nĩwe wanjathire ũrĩa ingiuga, na ũrĩa ingĩaria.
english: For I spoke not from myself, but the Father who sent me gave me a commandment, what I should say and what I should speak.

kikuyu: nakĩo kĩrĩndĩ kĩnene kĩa andũ gĩkĩmũrũmĩrĩra tondũ nĩkĩoneete ciama iria aaringĩire andũ arĩa maarĩ arũaru.
english: A great multitude followed him, because they saw his signs which he did on those who were sick.

kikuyu: Macookaga hwaĩ-inĩ makĩraramaga ta ngui, makĩũrũraga itũũra-inĩ inene.
english: At evening let them return. Let them howl like a dog, and go around the city.

kikuyu: “Kuuma o kĩambĩrĩria-rĩ, ũrĩ waathana atĩ rũciinĩ rũgĩe ho, kana ũkĩonereria ũtheri wa rũciinĩ harĩa ũkuumĩrĩra,
english: “Have you commanded the morning in your days, and caused the dawn to know its place,

kikuyu: Arũme othe a ũkũrũ wa kuuma mĩaka mĩrongo ĩtatũ nginya mĩaka mĩrongo ĩtano arĩa mookire gũtungata wĩra-inĩ wa Hema-ya-Gũtũnganwo,
english: from thirty years old and upward even to fifty years old, everyone who entered into the service for work in the Tent of Meeting.

kikuyu: Mũgoocei mũkĩhuhaga karumbeta, mũmũgooce na kĩnanda kĩa mũgeeto, na gĩa kĩnũbi,
english: Praise him with the sounding of the trumpet! Praise him with harp and lyre!

kikuyu: nĩgũkorwo tũrĩ kũu andũ arĩa maatũtahĩte nĩmatwĩraga tũmainĩre nyĩmbo, o acio atũnyariiri magatwĩra tũmainĩre nyĩmbo tũkenete, magatwĩra atĩrĩ, “Tũinĩrei rwĩmbo rũmwe rwa iria mwainaga mũrĩ Zayuni!”
english: For there, those who led us captive asked us for songs. Those who tormented us demanded songs of joy: “Sing us one of the songs of Zion!”

kikuyu: Ningĩmakĩgarũrũka, makĩambata na njĩra marorete Bashani, nake Ogu mũthamaki wa Bashani na mbũtũ ciake ciothe cia ita makiumagara makahũũrane na Isiraeli kũu Edirei.
english: They turned and went up by the way of Bashan. Og the king of Bashan went out against them, he and all his people, to battle at Edrei.

kikuyu: Nake Jesũ akiuma kũu agĩthiĩ gũkuhĩ na Turo. Agĩtoonya nyũmba ĩmwe na ndeendaga mũndũ o na ũrĩkũ amenye atĩ arĩ kũu; no ndangĩahotire kwĩhitha.
english: From there he arose and went away into the borders of Tyre and Sidon. He entered into a house and didn’t want anyone to know it, but he couldn’t escape notice.

kikuyu: Jehova Mwene-Hinya-Wothe ekuuga atĩrĩ, “Mũthenya ũcio nĩnganiina marĩĩtwa ma mĩhianano ya kũhooywo mathire bũrũri-inĩ, na matigacooka kũririkanwo rĩngĩ. Nĩngeheria anabii hamwe na roho ũcio wa thaahu, mathire bũrũri-inĩ.
english: It will come to pass in that day, says Yahweh of Armies, that I will cut off the names of the idols out of the land, and they will be remembered no more. I will also cause the prophets and the spirit of impurity to pass out of the land.

kikuyu: Narĩo riũa rĩkĩmũrathĩra akĩhĩtũka hau Penieli, na nĩathuaga nĩ ũndũ nĩathenyũkĩtwo nĩ ihĩndĩ rĩa gĩtina gĩa kũgũrũ.
english: The sun rose on him as he passed over Peniel, and he limped because of his thigh.

kikuyu: ũciĩre atĩrĩ: ‘Inyuĩ irĩma cia Isiraeli, iguai kiugo kĩa Mwathani Jehova. Ũũ nĩguo Mwathani Jehova ekwĩra irĩma na tũrĩma, na mĩkuru na cianda: Niĩ ngirie kũmũũkĩrĩra na rũhiũ rwa njora, na nyanange kũndũ kwanyu kũrĩa gũtũũgĩru.*
english: and say, ‘You mountains of Israel, hear the word of the Lord Yahweh! The Lord Yahweh says to the mountains and to the hills, to the watercourses and to the valleys: “Behold, I, even I, will bring a sword on you, and I will destroy your high places.

kikuyu: na cia Jora ciarĩ 112,
english: The children of Jorah, one hundred twelve.

kikuyu: Mwaka-inĩ wa ikũmi na ĩĩrĩ wa Mũthamaki Ahasuerusu, mweri wa mbere, mweri wa Nisani, magĩcuuka puri (ũguo nĩ ta kuuga gũcuuka mĩtĩ) mbere ya Hamani nĩguo mathuure mũthenya na mweri. Naguo mũtĩ ũkĩgwĩra mweri wa ikũmi na ĩĩrĩ, nĩguo mweri wa Adari.
english: In the first month, which is the month Nisan, in the twelfth year of King Ahasuerus, they cast Pur, that is, the lot, before Haman from day to day, and from month to month, and chose the twelfth month, which is the month Adar.

kikuyu: Nake Jesũ akĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Wee ndũngĩrĩ na ũhoti igũrũ rĩakwa korwo ndũheetwo guo kuuma igũrũ. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio ũrĩa ũũneanire kũrĩwe nĩwe ũrĩ na mehia marĩa manene.”
english: Jesus answered, “You would have no power at all against me, unless it were given to you from above. Therefore he who delivered me to you has greater sin.”

kikuyu: Mĩthenya ĩtatũ thuutha wa kũgĩa na kĩrĩkanĩro kĩu na andũ acio a Gibeoni, andũ a Isiraeli makĩigua atĩ andũ acio maahakanĩte nao, na maatũũraga o hakuhĩ nao.
english: At the end of three days after they had made a covenant with them, they heard that they were their neighbors, and that they lived among them.

kikuyu: Nake mũrutwo ũrĩa Jesũ eendete akĩĩra Petero atĩrĩ, “Nĩ Mwathani!” Na rĩrĩa Simoni Petero aiguire mũrutwo ũcio aamwĩra atĩ, “Nĩ Mwathani”, akĩĩoha nguo yake ya igũrũ (tondũ nĩamĩrutĩte), na akĩrũga maaĩ-inĩ.
english: That disciple therefore whom Jesus loved said to Peter, “It’s the Lord!” So when Simon Peter heard that it was the Lord, he wrapped his coat around himself (for he was naked), and threw himself into the sea.

kikuyu: Ningĩ agĩcooka akiumĩrĩra Jakubu na thuutha wa ũguo agĩcooka akiumĩrĩra atũmwo othe,
english: Then he appeared to James, then to all the apostles,

kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, o matiigana gũũthikĩrĩria kana kũrũmbũiya ũhoro ũcio. No nĩmoomirie ngoro ciao na makĩagana gũkĩra maithe mao.’
english: Yet they didn’t listen to me or incline their ear, but made their neck stiff. They did worse than their fathers.

kikuyu: nĩwe watongoririe andũ ake magĩtuĩkanĩria werũ-inĩ, Wendo wake nĩ wa tene na tene.
english: to him who led his people through the wilderness, for his loving kindness endures forever;

kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, we mwene o na kana arĩa maamũtungatagĩra, na andũ a bũrũri ũcio matiarũmbũirie ciugo iria Jehova aarĩtie na kanua ka Jeremia ũcio mũnabii.
english: But neither he, nor his servants, nor the people of the land, listened to Yahweh’s words, which he spoke by the prophet Jeremiah.

kikuyu: jĩtĩka rĩrĩa ndagũkaĩra, Wee Ngai wakwa, o Wee mũthingu. Ũndeithagie rĩrĩa ndĩ mĩnyamaro-inĩ; njiguĩra tha na ũigue mahooya makwa.
english: 568 Psalms 6:6 4 For the Chief Musician; on stringed instruments. A Psalm by David. 1 Answer me when I call, God of my righteousness. Give me relief from my distress. Have mercy on me, and hear my prayer.

kikuyu: Ũcio mũrũ wa mũtumia Mũisiraeli nĩarumire Rĩĩtwa rĩa Ngai na kĩrumi; nĩ ũndũ ũcio makĩmũtwarĩra Musa. (Nyina wa mũndũ ũcio eetagwo Shelomithu, mwarĩ wa Dibiri ũrĩa Mũdani.)
english: The son of the Israelite woman blasphemed the Name, and cursed; and they brought him to Moses. His mother’s name was Shelomith, the daughter of Dibri, of the tribe of Dan.

kikuyu: Kũngĩgaakorwo na mũndũ mũthĩĩni gatagatĩ-inĩ ka ariũ a ithe wanyu itũũra-inĩ o rĩothe kũu bũrũri-inĩ ũcio Jehova Ngai wanyu ekũmũhe-rĩ, mũtikanamũmĩrie ngoro, kana mũtuĩke akarĩ kũrĩ mũrũ wa ithe wanyu ũcio mũthĩĩni.
english: If a poor man, one of your brothers, is with you within any of your gates in your land which Yahweh your God gives you, you shall not harden your heart, nor shut your hand from your poor brother;

kikuyu: Niĩ, Johana, mũrũ wa ithe wanyu, na mũgwatanĩri na inyuĩ thĩinĩ wa mĩnyamaro, na ũthamaki, na gũkirĩrĩria tũũmĩrĩirie, o iria irĩ ciitũ thĩinĩ wa Jesũ, ndaarĩ gĩcigĩrĩra-inĩ kĩa iria kĩrĩa gĩĩtagwo Patimo tondũ wa Ũhoro wa Ngai na ũira wa Jesũ.
english: I John, your brother and partner with you in the oppression, Kingdom, and perseverance in Christ Jesus, was on the isle that is called Patmos because of God’s Word and the testimony of Jesus Christ.

kikuyu: Agĩcooka agĩtoonya gatarũ kaumaarĩ, naruorũhuhorũgĩtuĩka. Naomakĩgegamũno,
english: He got into the boat with them; and the wind ceased, and they were very amazed among themselves, and marveled;

kikuyu: akainũka mũciĩ. Agacooka ageeta arata ake na andũ a itũũra hamwe, akameera atĩrĩ, ‘Ũkai tũkenanĩre nĩ ũndũ nĩnyonete ngʼondu yakwa ĩrĩa ĩrorĩte.’
english: When he comes home, he calls together his friends and his neighbors, saying to them, ‘Rejoice with me, for I have found my sheep which was lost!’

kikuyu: Balamu agĩcooka akĩaria ndũmĩrĩri yake, akiuga atĩrĩ: “Arahũka, wee Balaki na ũigue; Thikĩrĩria, wee mũrũ wa Ziporu.
english: He took up his parable, and said, “Rise up, Balak, and hear! Listen to me, you son of Zippor.

kikuyu: Arũme othe a ũkũrũ wa kuuma mĩaka mĩrongo ĩtatũ nginya mĩaka mĩrongo ĩtano arĩa mookire gũtungata wĩra-inĩ wa Hema-ya- Gũtũnganwo,
english: from thirty years old and upward even to fifty years old—everyone who entered into the service for work in the Tent of Meeting,

kikuyu: Ningĩ nĩũkoiga ũrĩ hau mbere ya Jehova Ngai waku atĩrĩ: “Baba aarĩ Mũsuriata watũũraga orũũraga,* nake agĩikũrũka agĩthiĩ bũrũri wa Misiri marĩ na andũ mataarĩ aingĩ, nao magĩtũũra kuo na magĩtuĩka rũrĩrĩ rũnene rũrĩ hinya na rwa andũ aingĩ.
english: You shall answer and say before Yahweh your God, “My father† was a Syrian ready to perish. He went down into Egypt, and lived there, few in number. There he became a great, mighty, and populous nation.

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Nebukadinezaru agĩcinwo nĩ marakara, nake agĩtũmanĩra Shadiraka, na Meshaki, na Abedinego mareehwo harĩ we. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio andũ acio makĩreehwo mbere ya mũthamaki,
english: Then Nebuchadnezzar in rage and fury commanded that Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego be brought. Then these men were brought before the king.

kikuyu: Ndaamahũũrire makĩhinya ta rũkũngũ rũmbũrĩtwo nĩ rũhuho; ngĩmaita njĩra-inĩ ta ndooro.
english: Then I beat them small as the dust before the wind. I cast them out as the mire of the streets.

kikuyu: Njathĩkagĩra mawatho maku na kĩrĩra gĩaku, nĩgũkorwo mĩthiĩre yakwa yothe nĩũmĩũĩ. Ihooya rĩa kũũria Ũteithio
english: I have obeyed your precepts and your testimonies, for all my ways are before you. TAV

kikuyu: “Kũrathimwo-rĩ, nĩ inyuĩ hĩndĩ ĩrĩa andũ mekũmũruma, na makamũnyariira, na makamũigĩrĩra maũndũ mothe mooru nĩ ũndũ wakwa.
english: “Blessed are you when people reproach you, persecute you, and say all kinds of evil against you falsely, for my sake.
