kikuyu: Wenda gwĩtana, ĩtana, no rĩrĩ, nũũ ũngĩgwĩtĩka? Nĩ ũrĩkũ wa arĩa atheru ũngĩũrĩra kũrĩ we?
english: 536 Job 6:5 5 1 “Call now; is there any who will answer you? To which of the holy ones will you turn?

kikuyu: Rezoni aarĩ thũ ya Isiraeli rĩrĩa rĩothe Solomoni aatũũrire muoyo, akĩongerera thĩĩna ũrĩa warehetwo nĩ Hadadi. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Rezoni agĩathana kũu Suriata na nĩathũire Isiraeli mũno. Jeroboamu Kũremera Solomoni
english: He was an adversary to Israel all the days of Solomon, in addition to the mischief of Hadad. He abhorred Israel, and reigned over Syria.

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Afarisai makĩĩrana atĩrĩ, “Ta kĩonei tũtirĩ ũndũ tũreeka. Onei ũrĩa andũ othe mamũrũmĩrĩire!” Jesũ Kũratha Ũhoro wa Gĩkuũ Gĩake
english: The Pharisees therefore said among themselves, “See how you accomplish nothing. Behold, the world has gone after him.”

kikuyu: Mũrungu nĩatũmĩte ngoro yakwa yũrwo nĩ hinya; ũcio Mwene-Hinya-Wothe nĩatũmĩte nyiitwo nĩ guoya.
english: For God has made my heart faint. The Almighty has terrified me.

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio-rĩ, nĩtumagarei tũthiĩ kũrĩ we kũu nja ya itũũra, tũkuuanĩire nake gĩconoko kĩrĩa we aakuuire.
english: Let’s therefore go out to him outside of the camp, bearing his reproach.

kikuyu: Mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai aarĩkia kũrũgamia mũtumia ũcio mbere ya Jehova, nĩakamuohora njuĩrĩ yake, acooke amũnengere iruta rĩu rĩa kĩririkano rĩa mũtu nĩ ũndũ wa ũiru arĩnyiite na moko, nake mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai we mwene akorwo anyiitĩte maaĩ macio marũrũ marĩa marehaga kĩrumi.
english: The priest shall set the woman before Yahweh, and let the hair of the woman’s head go loose, and put the meal offering of memorial in her hands, which is the meal offering of jealousy. The priest shall have in his hand the water of bitterness that brings a curse.

kikuyu: a rĩrĩ, rĩrĩa Jabini mũthamaki wa Hazoru aiguire ũhoro wa maũndũ macio, agĩtũma ũhoro kũrĩ Jobabu mũthamaki wa Madoni, kũrĩ athamaki a Shimuroni na Akishafu,
english: hen Jabin king of Hazor heard of it, he sent to Jobab king of Madon, to the king of Shimron, to the king of Achshaph,

kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, Jesũ aacooka kuuma mũrĩmo ũũrĩa ũngĩ wa iria, andũ aingĩ nĩmamwamũkĩrire tondũ nĩmamwetereire.
english: When Jesus returned, the multitude welcomed him, for they were all waiting for him.

kikuyu: ũrĩa werutire nĩ ũndũ wa mehia maitũ nĩguo atũhonokie kuuma kũrĩ mahinda maya tũrĩ, marĩa maiyũrĩtwo nĩ maũndũ mooru, kũringana na wendi wa Ngai Ithe witũ,
english: who gave himself for our sins, that he might deliver us out of this present evil age, according to the will of our God and Father—

kikuyu: “Mũndũ ũrĩ na mũrimũ ta ũcio ũngĩgwatanio-rĩ, no nginya ehumbe nguo ndarũku, na arekererie njuĩrĩ yake, na ahumbĩre kanua gake, athiĩ akĩanagĩrĩra akiugaga atĩrĩ, ‘Ndĩ na thaahu! Ndĩ na thaahu!’
english: “The leper in whom the plague is shall wear torn clothes, and the hair of his head shall hang loose. He shall cover his upper lip, and shall cry, ‘Unclean! Unclean!’

kikuyu: Na o rĩngĩ agĩtũma ndungata ĩngĩ, nayo makĩmĩũraga. Nĩatũmire ndungata ingĩ nyingĩ, imwe ciacio magĩcihũũra, na iria ingĩ magĩciũraga.
english: Again he sent another, and they killed him, and many others, beating some, and killing some.

kikuyu: “No kũhoteke Ngai atũũre gũkũ thĩ? Igũrũ, o na kũrĩa igũrũ mũno-rĩ, wee ndũngĩiganĩra kuo. Hekarũ ĩno njakĩte-rĩ, githĩ ndĩkĩrĩ nini makĩria!
english: But will God in very deed dwell on the earth? Behold, heaven and the heaven of heavens can’t contain you; how much less this house that I have built!

kikuyu: Ikaragiai Thabatũ ciakwa irĩ theru, nĩguo ituĩke kĩmenyithia gatagatĩ gakwa na inyuĩ. Hĩndĩ ĩyo nĩmũkamenya atĩ niĩ nĩ niĩ Jehova Ngai wanyu.”
english: Make my Sabbaths holy. They shall be a sign between me and you, that you may know that I am Yahweh your God.’

kikuyu: Wee Ngai, o Wee Ngai ũrĩa ũũhonokagia-rĩ, honokia kuumana na ũiti thakame, naruo rũrĩmĩ rwakwa nĩrũkaina ũhoro wa ũthingu waku.
english: Deliver me from the guilt of bloodshed, O God, the God of my salvation. My tongue will sing aloud of your righteousness.

kikuyu: Jehova, Ngai wa maithe mao, akĩmatũmĩra ndũmĩrĩri na tũnua twa atũmwo ake rĩngĩ na rĩngĩ, nĩgũkorwo nĩaiguagĩra andũ ake tha, o na gĩikaro gĩake.
english: Yahweh, the God of their fathers, sent to them by his messengers, rising up early and sending, because he had compassion on his people and on his dwelling place;

kikuyu: na wa ikũmi na ũmwe kũrĩ Eliashibu, na wa ikũmi na ĩĩrĩ kũrĩ Jakimu,
english: the eleventh to Eliashib, the twelfth to Jakim,

kikuyu: nĩgũkorwo maũndũ ma Ngai marĩa mangĩmenyeka nĩo matiagĩte kũmenyeka nĩo, tondũ Ngai nĩwe wamamenyithirie.
english: because that which is known of God is revealed in them, for God revealed it to them.

kikuyu: Nake mũthamaki akĩmacookeria atĩrĩ, “Nĩndĩramenya kũna atĩ mũrenda o kũraiharaihia ihinda, tondũ nĩmũramenya atĩ ũndũ ũyũ nĩguo nduĩte ngwatĩirie mũno:
english: The king answered, “I know of a certainty that you are trying to gain time, because you see the thing has gone from me.

kikuyu: Nĩmũkagĩa na irio nyingĩ cia kũrĩa, o nginya mũhũũne, na inyuĩ nĩmũkagoocaga rĩĩtwa rĩa Jehova Ngai wanyu, ũrĩa ũmwĩkĩire maũndũ ma magegania; andũ akwa matigacooka gũconorithio rĩngĩ.
english: You will have plenty to eat and be satisfied, and will praise the name of Yahweh, your God, who has dealt wondrously with you; and my people will never again be disappointed.

kikuyu: Andũ arĩa moigaga maũndũ ta macio monanagia atĩ nĩ gwetha methaga bũrũri ũrĩa ũrĩ wao kĩũmbe.
english: For those who say such things make it clear that they are seeking a country of their own.

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo maaragia ndeto cia tũhũ na cia mwĩtĩĩo, makaguucagĩrĩria na merirĩria mao mooru ma mwĩrĩ o na ũũra-thoni andũ arĩa marethara moime thĩinĩ wa arĩa matũũraga mahĩtagia.
english: For, uttering great swelling words of emptiness, they entice in the lusts of the flesh, by licentiousness, those who are indeed escaping from those who live in error;

kikuyu: na akĩona rũciaro rwa gatatũ rwa ciana cia Efiraimu. O na ciana cia Makiru mũrũ wa Manase ciaciarwo nĩciaigĩrĩirwo maru-inĩ ma Jusufu.
english: Joseph saw Ephraim’s children to the third generation. The children also of Machir, the son of Manasseh, were born on Joseph’s knees.

kikuyu: Macio nĩmo mawatho makoniĩ mũrimũ o ro wothe wa ngoothi ũrĩa ũngĩgwatanio, na ũhere,
english: This is the law for any plague of leprosy, and for an itch,

kikuyu: na ndegwa igĩrĩ, na ndũrũme ithano, na thenge ithano na tũtũrũme tũtano twa mwaka ũmwe, irutwo irĩ igongona rĩa ũiguano. Ici nĩcio indo iria ciarutirwo nĩ Eliabu mũrũ wa Heloni.
english: and for the sacrifice of peace offerings, two head of cattle, five rams, five male goats, and five male lambs a year old. This was the offering of Eliab the son of Helon.

kikuyu: Rĩrĩa andũ amwe a arĩa marũngiĩ hau maiguire ũguo, makiuga atĩrĩ, “Ta thikĩrĩriai agĩĩta Elija.”
english: Some of those who stood by, when they heard it, said, “Behold, he is calling Elijah.”

kikuyu: Jehova nĩwe hinya wakwa na nowe ngo yakwa; ngoro yakwa nĩwe ĩĩhokete, na nĩandeithĩtie. Ngoro yakwa nĩĩrarũũhia nĩ gĩkeno, na nĩngũmũinĩra rwĩmbo rwa kũmũcookeria ngaatho.
english: Yahweh is my strength and my shield. My heart has trusted in him, and I am helped. Therefore my heart greatly rejoices. With my song I will thank him.

kikuyu: mũrũ wa Ethani, mũrũ wa Zima, mũrũ wa Shimei,
english: the son of Ethan, the son of Zimmah, the son of Shimei,

kikuyu: “Mũthenya ũrĩa mũnene wa Jehova ũrĩ hakuhĩ, ũrĩ hakuhĩ na nĩũrooka narua. Ta thikĩrĩriai! Kĩrĩro kĩa mũthenya ũcio wa Jehova gĩgaakorwo kĩrĩ kĩnene, nacio njamba cia ita nĩikaarĩra mũno irĩ kũu.
english: The great day of Yahweh is near. It is near and hurries greatly, the voice of the day of Yahweh. The mighty man cries there bitterly.

kikuyu: O na gũtuĩka maarĩ na guoya wa andũ arĩa maamathiũrũrũkĩirie, nĩmakire kĩgongona mũthingi-inĩ wakĩo, na makĩrutĩra Jehova magongona ma njino ho, magongona ma rũciinĩ na ma hwaĩ-inĩ.
english: In spite of their fear because of the peoples of the surrounding lands, they set the altar on its base; and they offered burnt offerings on it to Yahweh, even burnt offerings morning and evening.

kikuyu: Ahazia aarĩ na ũkũrũ wa mĩaka mĩrongo ĩĩrĩ na ĩĩrĩ rĩrĩa aatuĩkire mũthamaki, na agĩthamaka arĩ Jerusalemu mwaka ũmwe. Nyina eetagwo Athalia, mwarĩ wa mwana wa Omuri mũthamaki wa Isiraeli.†
english: Ahaziah was twenty-two years old when he began to reign; and he reigned one year in Jerusalem. His mother’s name was Athaliah the daughter of Omri king of Israel.

kikuyu: Makĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Twagũthaitha mwathi witũ, nĩ tuokire gũkũ hĩndĩ ya mbere kũgũra irio.
english: and said, “Oh, my lord, we indeed came down the first time to buy food.

kikuyu: Ũndũ wĩgiĩ Asheri oigire atĩrĩ: “Mũriũ ũrĩa mũrathime gũkĩra arĩa angĩ othe nĩ Asheri; ariũ a ithe maroomwendaga, na arothambagia nyarĩrĩ ciake na maguta.*
english: About Asher he said, “Asher is blessed with children. Let him be acceptable to his brothers. Let him dip his foot in oil.

kikuyu: Rũmagiai irĩra ciakwa cia watho na mũcirũmagĩrĩre. Niĩ nĩ niĩ Jehova, ũrĩa ũmũtheragia.
english: You shall keep my statutes, and do them. I am Yahweh who sanctifies you.

kikuyu: No Musa akĩĩra Jehova atĩrĩ, “Angĩkorwo andũ a Isiraeli matingĩnjigua-rĩ, nĩkĩ kĩngĩtũma Firaũni anjigue, kuona njaragia ngĩtondoiraga?” Andũ a Nyũmba ya Musa na Harũni
english: Moses spoke before Yahweh, saying, “Behold, the children of Israel haven’t listened to me. How then shall Pharaoh listen to me, when I have uncircumcised lips?”

kikuyu: Mwĩnyiihĩtie biũ na mũhooreire na mũkirĩrĩirie, na ningĩ mũkiranagĩrĩrie mũndũ na ũrĩa ũngĩ mũrĩ na wendani.
english: with all lowliness and humility, with patience, bearing with one another in love,

kikuyu: Solomoni aarĩ na ciugũ cia mbarathi cia ita ngiri inya, na mbarathi ngiri ikũmi na igĩrĩ.
english: Solomon had forty thousand stalls of horses for his chariots, and twelve thousand horsemen.

kikuyu: Shalumu mũrũ wa Jabeshi nĩaciirĩire gũũkĩrĩra Zekaria. Akĩmũtharĩkĩra mbere ya andũ, akĩmũũraga, nake agĩtuĩka mũthamaki ithenya rĩake.
english: Shallum the son of Jabesh conspired against him, and struck him before the people and killed him, and reigned in his place.

kikuyu: na nĩ ũndũ ũcio Ayahudi makĩĩra mũndũ ũcio wahonetio atĩrĩ, “Ũmũthĩ nĩ mũthenya wa Thabatũ; watho ndũgwĩtĩkĩrĩtie ũkuue kĩbarĩ gĩaku.”
english: So the Jews said to him who was cured, “It is the Sabbath. It is not lawful for you to carry the mat.”

kikuyu: Nĩngaikia mĩguĩ ĩtatũ mwena-inĩ warĩo, taarĩ wathi ngwĩgera.
english: I will shoot three arrows on its side, as though I shot at a mark.

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio gatũrũme kau, na iruta rĩu rĩa ngano, na maguta macio nĩirĩrutagwo rũciinĩ o rũciinĩ rĩrĩ iruta rĩa njino rĩa hĩndĩ ciothe.
english: Thus they shall prepare the lamb, the meal offering, and the oil, morning by morning, for a continual burnt offering.”

kikuyu: Nake Ishibi-Benobu ũrĩa warĩ ũmwe wa njiaro cia Rafa, ũrĩa mũthia wa itimũ rĩake warĩ na ũritũ wa cekeri magana matatũ,* na aarĩ na rũhiũ rwa njora rwerũ, ũcio akiuga nĩekũũraga Daudi.
english: and Ishbibenob, who was of the sons of the giant, the weight of whose spear was three hundred shekels of bronze in weight, he being armed with a new sword, thought he would kill David.

kikuyu: Riũa rĩathũa nĩagathirwo nĩ thaahu, na thuutha ũcio no arĩe maruta marĩa maamũre, tondũ nĩcio irio ciake.
english: When the sun is down, he shall be clean; and afterward he shall eat of the holy things, because it is his bread.

kikuyu: esũ na arutwo ake magĩkinya mũrĩmo ũũrĩa ũngĩ wa iria, magĩthiĩ bũrũri wa Agerasi.
english: hey came to the other side of the sea, into the country of the Gadarenes.

kikuyu: Petero akĩmũrũmĩrĩra, makiuma nja ya njeera, no ndaamenyaga atĩ ũndũ ũcio mũraika eekaga warĩ ũndũ wa ma; eeciiragia atĩ nĩ kĩoneki oonaga.
english: And he went out and followed him. He didn’t know that what was being done by the angel was real, but thought he saw a vision.

kikuyu: Mehia ma andũ amwe nĩmeyonanĩtie, makamatongoragia ciira-inĩ no mehia ma andũ angĩ mamoimaga thuutha.
english: Some men’s sins are evident, preceding them to judgment, and some also follow later.

kikuyu: Bũrũri ũngĩĩhia, nĩũgĩaga na aathani aingĩ, no mũndũ ũrĩ na ũtaũku na akagĩa na ũmenyo nĩatũmaga bũrũri ũkindĩrie.
english: In rebellion, a land has many rulers, but order is maintained by a man of understanding and knowledge.

kikuyu: “ ‘Nĩ kũrĩ nyamũ imwe icookagia o ithagumia tu, na ingĩ irĩ o mahũngũ marekanĩtie; icio mũtikanacirĩe. Ngamĩĩra o na aakorwo nĩĩcookagia ithagumia-rĩ, mahũngũ mayo matirekanĩtie; ĩyo ĩrĩ thaahu harĩ inyuĩ.
english: “ ‘Nevertheless these you shall not eat of those that chew the cud, or of those who part the hoof: the camel, because it chews the cud but doesn’t have a parted hoof, is unclean to you.

kikuyu: Nĩtwatahire matũũramothemarĩamaarĩkũndũkwaraganu, naGileadiguothe, naBashaniguothe, onginyaSaleka na Edirei, matũũra ma kũrĩa Ogu aathanaga kũu Bashani.
english: We took all the cities of the plain, and all Gilead, and all Bashan, to Salecah and Edrei, cities of the kingdom of Og in Bashan.

kikuyu: Mũtikanareke ngoro cianyu ĩrũmĩrĩre mĩthiĩre yake, kana mũingĩre njĩra-inĩ ciake.
english: Don’t let your heart turn to her ways. Don’t go astray in her paths,

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio, makĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Ta twĩre, nũũ ũtũmĩte thĩĩna ũyũ wothe ũtũkore? Wee-rĩ, ũrutaga wĩra ũrĩkũ? Uumĩte kũ? Wĩ wa bũrũri ũrĩkũ? Wĩ wa rũrĩrĩ rũrĩkũ?”
english: Then they asked him, “Tell us, please, for whose cause this evil is on us. What is your occupation? Where do you come from? What is your country? Of what people are you?”

kikuyu: Ihinda-inĩ o rĩu no ehokaga atĩ Paũlũ nĩangĩamũheire ihaki, na nĩ ũndũ ũcio nĩaikaraga akĩmũtũmanagĩra, akaaria nake.
english: Meanwhile, he also hoped that money would be given to him by Paul, that he might release him. Therefore also he sent for him more often and talked with him.

kikuyu: MarĩĩtwamaariũaHarũnimaarĩNadabuũrĩawarĩirigithathi, naAbihu, naEleazaru, naIthamaru.
english: These are the names of the sons of Aaron: Nadab the firstborn, and Abihu, Eleazar, and Ithamar.

kikuyu: Nao makĩmũnengera iruta rĩu rĩa njino gĩcunjĩ kĩmwe gwa kĩmwe, o hamwe na mũtwe, nake agĩcicinĩra kĩgongona-inĩ.
english: They delivered the burnt offering to him, piece by piece, and the head. He burned them upon the altar.

kikuyu: Ithua nĩngacitua matigari, na arĩa maingatĩtwo ndĩmatue rũrĩrĩ rũrĩ hinya. Jehova nĩwe ũkamaathaga arĩ Kĩrĩma-inĩ gĩa Zayuni, kuuma mũthenya ũcio nginya tene.
english: and I will make that which was lame a remnant, and that which was cast far off a strong nation: and Yahweh will reign over them on Mount Zion from then on, even forever.”

kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, Iburahĩmu aatiira maitho- rĩ, akĩona andũ atatũ marũgamĩte o hakuhĩ nake. Rĩrĩa aamoonire, akĩhiũha akiuma mũromo-inĩ wa hema yake, agĩthiĩ kũmatũnga, na agĩturumithia ũthiũ wake thĩ.
english: He lifted up his eyes and looked, and saw that three men stood near him. When he saw them, he ran to meet them from the tent door, and bowed himself to the earth,

kikuyu: Jehova akĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Nĩ atĩa wĩkĩte? Ta thikĩrĩria! Thakame ya mũrũ-wa-nyũkwa nĩĩrangaĩra ĩrĩ tĩĩri-inĩ.
english: Yahweh said, “What have you done? The voice of your brother’s blood cries to me from the ground.

kikuyu: Mũndũ ũmwe wa kũu Maoni, ũrĩa warĩ na indo kũu Karimeli, aarĩ mũtongu mũno. Aarĩ na mbũri ngiri ĩmwe na ngʼondu ngiri ithatũ, iria aamuraga guoya kũu Karimeli.
english: There was a man in Maon whose possessions were in Carmel; and the man was very great. He had three thousand sheep and a thousand goats; and he was shearing his sheep in Carmel.

kikuyu: Mũtĩ wa Kĩhingo kĩa Irathĩro wagwĩrĩire Shelemia. Ningĩ magĩcuuka mĩtĩ nĩ ũndũ wa mũriũ Zekaria, ũrĩa warĩ mũheani kĩrĩra mũũgĩ, nake akĩgwĩrwo nĩ mũtĩ wa Kĩhingo gĩa Gathigathini.
english: The lot eastward fell to Shelemiah. Then for Zechariah his son, a wise counselor, they cast lots; and his lot came out northward.

kikuyu: Njetagĩrĩra Jehova, ngoro yakwa nĩĩmwetereire, niĩ ndĩĩhokaga kiugo gĩake.
english: I wait for Yahweh. My soul waits. I hope in his word.

kikuyu: akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Ũkĩra, woe kaana gaka na nyina, mũthiĩ bũrũri wa Isiraeli, nĩgũkorwo arĩa maageragia gũkooraga nĩmakuĩte.”
english: “Arise and take the young child and his mother, and go into the land of Israel, for those who sought the young child’s life are dead.”

kikuyu: Nao Afilisti makĩambata na makĩamba hema kũu Juda, makĩaragana gũkuhĩ na Lehi.
english: Then the Philistines went up, encamped in Judah, and spread themselves in Lehi.

kikuyu: Rĩrĩa ciugo ciaingĩha mehia matiagaga, no mũndũ ũrĩa ũkiraga nĩ mũũgĩ.
english: In the multitude of words there is no lack of disobedience, but he who restrains his lips does wisely.

kikuyu: Arĩithagia mbũri ciake o ta mũrĩithi: Natuo tũgondu atuoyaga na moko make na agatũkuuĩra gĩthũri-inĩ gĩake; nacio ngʼondu iria irongithia acitongoragia o kahora.
english: He will feed his flock like a shepherd. He will gather the lambs in his arm, and carry them in his bosom.

kikuyu: Na mũhooyage hĩndĩ ciothe na mahooya ma mĩthemba yothe, mũgĩthaithanaga mũrĩ na Roho. Mwarĩkia gwĩka ũguo-rĩ, ikaragai mwĩhũgĩte, na mũhooyagĩre andũ arĩa aamũre hĩndĩ ciothe. * 6:16 Ngo cia Aroma ciakoragwo ithondeketwo na njũũa. Ciarĩ nene na nyũmũ nĩguo ihote kũhoria mĩguĩ. Ciatobokagio maaĩ-inĩ nĩguo ciororoe.
english: with all prayer and requests, praying at all times in the Spirit, and being watchful to this end in all perseverance and requests for all the saints.

kikuyu: O ũmwe wanyu no nginya akarehaga kĩheo kĩringaine na ũrĩa Jehova Ngai wanyu amũrathimĩte. Aciiri
english: Every man shall give as he is able, according to Yahweh your God’s blessing which he has given you.

kikuyu: Ti-itherũ nĩnyonete kũhinyĩrĩrio kwa andũ akwa kũu bũrũri wa Misiri. Nĩnjiguĩte gũcaaya kwao, na nĩnjikũrũkĩte thĩ nĩguo ndĩmohorithie. Rĩu ũka nĩngũgũtũma ũcooke Misiri.’
english: I have surely seen the affliction of my people who are in Egypt, and have heard their groaning. I have come down to deliver them. Now come, I will send you into Egypt.’*

kikuyu: “We-rĩ, nĩarakũguucĩrĩria uume magego-inĩ ma mĩnyamaro, agũtware handũ haariĩ hatarĩ na ũkunderu, na agũtware akũhurũkie metha-inĩ yaku ĩiyũrĩte irio iria njega.
english: Yes, he would have allured you out of distress, into a wide place, where there is no restriction. That which is set on your table would be full of fatness.

kikuyu: Naguo ũyũ nĩguo wendi wa ũrĩa wandũmire, atĩ ndigaate o na ũmwe wa arĩa othe aaheete, no ngaamariũkia mũthenya wa kũrigĩrĩria.
english: This is the will of my Father who sent me, that of all he has given to me I should lose nothing, but should raise him up at the last day.

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo atongoria a nyũmba ya Juda na ya Benjamini, na athĩnjĩri-Ngai na Alawii, arĩa othe Ngai aarahũrire ngoro ciao, makĩĩhaarĩria gũthiĩ magaake nyũmba ya Jehova kũu Jerusalemu.†
english: Then the heads of fathers’ households of Judah and Benjamin, the priests and the Levites, all whose spirit God had stirred to go up, rose up to build Yahweh’s house which is in Jerusalem.

kikuyu: Gideoni akĩmacookeria atĩrĩ, “Acio maarĩ ariũ a baba, o na ariũ a maitũ. O ta ũrĩa Jehova atũũraga muoyo-rĩ, korwo nĩmwahonokirie mĩoyo yao-rĩ, ndingĩamũũraga.”
english: He said, “They were my brothers, the sons of my mother. As Yahweh lives, if you had saved them alive, I would not kill you.”

kikuyu: Ngingo yaku ĩhaana ta mũthiringo ũrĩa mũraihu wa Daudi, wakĩtwo wega mũno; nakuo gũgacuurio ngo ngiri, na ciothe nĩ ngo cia njamba cia ita.
english: Your neck is like David’s tower built for an armory, on which a thousand shields hang, all the shields of the mighty men.

kikuyu: Ũmwe wao akiuga atĩrĩ, “Mwathi wakwa, mũndũ-wa-nja ũyũ na niĩ tũikaraga nyũmba ĩmwe. Na nĩndĩraciarire mwana arĩ ho.
english: The one woman said, “Oh, my lord, I and this woman dwell in one house. I delivered a child with her in the house.

kikuyu: Gideoni agĩtoonya nyũmba, agĩthĩnja koori, ningĩ agĩthondeka mĩgate ĩtarĩ na ndawa ya kũimbia kuuma eba ĩmwe† ya mũtu. Agĩĩkĩra nyama icio gĩkabũ-inĩ, naguo thathi wacio akĩwĩkĩra nyũngũ-inĩ, agĩtwarĩra mũraika na akĩmũigĩra hau gĩtina-inĩ kĩa mũgandi.
english: Gideon went in and prepared a young goat and unleavened cakes of an ephah‡ of meal. He put the meat in a basket and he put the broth in a pot, and brought it out to him under the oak, and presented it.

kikuyu: Kuona atĩ andũ aingĩ nĩmareraha ta ũrĩa thĩ ĩĩrahaga-rĩ, o na niĩ ngwĩraha o tao.
english: Seeing that many boast after the flesh, I will also boast.

kikuyu: “Ningĩ andũ arĩa maatigaire, magĩikũrũka kũrĩ andũ arĩa maarĩ igweta; andũ a Jehova magĩũka kũrĩ niĩ hamwe na andũ arĩa maarĩ hinya.
english: “Then a remnant of the nobles and the people came down. Yahweh came down for me against the mighty.

kikuyu: ũndũ ũrĩa wothe wendete ũtaaro nĩendete ũmenyi, no ũrĩa ũthũire gũkaanio nĩ mũndũ mũkĩĩgu.
english: hoever loves correction loves knowledge, but he who hates reproof is stupid.

kikuyu: Nĩnganiina rũngʼathio rwa mwĩtĩĩo wanyu, na ndũme matu matuĩke ta cuuma, nayo thĩ yũme ĩtuĩke ta gĩcango.
english: I will break the pride of your power, and I will make your sky like iron, and your soil like bronze.

kikuyu: “ ‘Mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai nĩakandĩka irumi icio ibuku-inĩ rĩa gĩkũnjo, acooke acithambĩrie maaĩ-inĩ macio marũrũ.
english: “ ‘The priest shall write these curses in a book, and he shall wipe them into the water of bitterness.

kikuyu: Nao anabii acio angĩ othe maarathaga o ũndũ ũmwe, makoiga atĩrĩ, “Tharĩkĩra Ramothu-Gileadi na nĩũgũtooria, tondũ Jehova nĩekũrĩneana moko-inĩ ma mũthamaki.”
english: All the prophets prophesied so, saying, “Go up to Ramoth Gilead and prosper; for Yahweh will deliver it into the hand of the king.”

kikuyu: Harũni agĩĩka o ta ũguo; akĩbanga matawa macio amaroretie mwena ũrĩa ũtheri wamo watheragĩra hau mbere ya mũtĩ ũcio maaigagĩrĩrwo, o ta ũrĩa Jehova aathĩte Musa gwĩkwo.
english: Aaron did so. He lit its lamps to light the area in front of the lamp stand, as Yahweh commanded Moses.

kikuyu: “ ‘No rĩrĩ, wee nĩwehokire ũthaka waku, na ũkĩhũthĩra ngumo yaku ĩgĩtũma ũtuĩke mũmaraya. Nĩwahũũrire ũmaraya na mwĩhĩtũkĩri o wothe, naguo ũthaka waku ũgĩtuĩka wake.
english: “ ‘ “But you trusted in your beauty, and played the prostitute because of your renown, and poured out your prostitution on everyone who passed by. It was his.

kikuyu: Na rĩrĩa andũ-a-nja acio maathiiaga-rĩ, arangĩri amwe a arĩa maarangĩrĩte mbĩrĩra magĩtoonya itũũra inene na makĩĩra athĩnjĩri-Ngai arĩa anene maũndũ mothe marĩa mekĩkĩte.
english: Now while they were going, behold, some of the guards came into the city and told the chief priests all the things that had happened.

kikuyu: Nyeki ĩrĩa nyũmũ rĩrĩa yatũgũtwo na nyeki njerũ ĩkunũke-rĩ, nayo ĩrĩa ya irĩma-inĩ yũnganio na ĩinũkio-rĩ,
english: The hay is removed, and the new growth appears, the grasses of the hills are gathered in.

kikuyu: No angĩaga kũgĩa na indo cia kũmũrĩha-rĩ, gĩcunjĩ kĩrĩa eendirie gĩgũtũũra kĩrĩ kĩa mũgũri nginya Mwaka wa Jubilii. Gĩgaacookio hĩndĩ ya Jubilii, na hĩndĩ ĩyo no acooke gĩthaka-inĩ gĩake.
english: But if he isn’t able to get it back for himself, then what he has sold shall remain in the hand of him who has bought it until the Year of Jubilee. In the Jubilee it shall be released, and he shall return to his property.

kikuyu: Ta rora, andũ arĩa aaganu nĩmageetete mota mao, makahagĩka mĩguĩ yao ndigi-inĩ nĩguo mehithĩte nduma-inĩ marathe andũ arĩa atheru ngoro.
english: For, behold, the wicked bend their bows. They set their arrows on the strings, that they may shoot in darkness at the upright in heart.

kikuyu: “ ‘Mũthenya wa thabatũ-rĩ, rutagai iruta rĩa tũtũrũme twĩrĩ, o kamwe ka ũkũrũ wa mwaka ũmwe, tũtarĩ na kaũũgũ, hamwe na kĩndũ kĩa iruta rĩa kũnyuuo, na iruta rĩa mũtu icunjĩ igĩrĩ cia ikũmi cia eba ĩmwe§ ya mũtu mũhinyu mũno ũtukanĩtio na maguta.
english: “ ‘On the Sabbath day, you shall offer two male lambs a year old without defect, and two tenths of an ephah† of fine flour for a meal offering mixed with oil, and its drink offering:

kikuyu: Thuutha wa ũguo, Jesũ na arutwo ake nĩmoimagarire magĩthiĩ bũrũri wa Judea, kũrĩa aikarangire nao, na akabatithanagia.
english: After these things, Jesus came with his disciples into the land of Judea. He stayed there with them and baptized.

kikuyu: Tha ciaku ikoragwo irĩ njerũ rũciinĩ o rũciinĩ, wĩhokeku waku nĩ mũnene mũno.
english: They are new every morning. Great is your faithfulness.

kikuyu: ũwĩre atĩrĩ, ‘Jehova ekuuga ũũ: Nĩngũgũũkĩrĩra. Nĩngũcomora rũhiũ rwakwa rwa njora kuuma njora-inĩ, na ngwehererie andũ arĩa athingu na arĩa aaganu.
english: Tell the land of Israel, ‘Yahweh says: “Behold, I am against you, and will draw my sword out of its sheath, and will cut off from you the righteous and the wicked.

kikuyu: Andũ othe arĩa maatigaire kuuma kũrĩ Aamori, na Ahiti, na Aperizi, na Ahivi, na Ajebusi (andũ acio matiarĩ andũ a Isiraeli),
english: As for all the people who were left of the Amorites, the Hittites, the Perizzites, the Hivites, and the Jebusites, who were not of the children of Israel—

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩmwe nĩ kwarĩ itũũra inini, na rĩarĩ na andũ o anini thĩinĩ warĩo. Na mũthamaki warĩ na hinya mũno akĩrĩũkĩrĩra, akĩrĩthiũrũrũkĩria, na agĩaka rũirigo rwa kũrĩrigiicĩria, arĩũkĩrĩre.
english: There was a little city, and few men within it; and a great king came against it, besieged it, and built great bulwarks against it.

kikuyu: Wũi, kaĩ mũthenya ũcio ũrĩ na haaro-ĩ! Nĩgũkorwo mũthenya wa Jehova* ũrĩ hakuhĩ; mũthenya ũcio ũgooka ta mwanangĩko uumĩte kũrĩ Mwene-Hinya-Wothe.
english: Alas for the day! For the day of Yahweh is at hand, and it will come as destruction from the Almighty.

kikuyu: mũndũ ũrĩ iguku kana mũhomu, mũndũ ũrĩ na kaũũgũ ka riitho, kana mũndũ ũrĩ na ironda iratogota kana iroira, kana mũthũku nyee.
english: or hunchbacked, or a dwarf, or one who has a defect in his eye, or an itching disease, or scabs, or who has damaged testicles.

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio, ndaakũheire mũthamaki ndakarĩte, na ngĩcooka ngĩmweheria ndĩ na mangʼũrĩ.
english: I have given you a king in my anger, and have taken him away in my wrath.

kikuyu: Jehova akĩruruma arĩ kũu igũrũ; mũgambo wa Ũrĩa-ũrĩ-Igũrũ-Mũno ũkĩiguuo.
english: Yahweh also thundered in the sky. The Most High uttered his voice: hailstones and coals of fire.

kikuyu: Ningĩ Isaia akiuga atĩrĩ, “Thondekai gĩkũmba kĩa ngũyũ.” Nao magĩĩka o ũguo, na magĩkĩigĩrĩra ihũha-inĩ rĩu, nake Hezekia akĩhona.
english: Isaiah said, “Take a cake of figs.” They took and laid it on the boil, and he recovered.

kikuyu: Rĩrĩa Ayahudi acio maarĩ na Mariamu kũu nyũmba makĩmũũmĩrĩria monire ũrĩa okĩra na ihenya oimĩte nja, makĩmũrũmĩrĩra, magĩĩciiria atĩ aathiiaga mbĩrĩra-inĩ akarĩrĩre ho.
english: Then the Jews who were with her in the house and were consoling her, when they saw Mary, that she rose up quickly and went out, followed her, saying, “She is going to the tomb to weep there.”

kikuyu: Mwaka-inĩ wa mĩrongo ĩĩrĩ na mũgwanja, mũthenya wa mbere wa mweri wa mbere, kiugo kĩa Jehova nĩkĩanginyĩrĩire, ngĩĩrwo atĩrĩ:
english: It came to pass in the twenty-seventh year, in the first month, in the first day of the month, Yahweh’s word came to me, saying,

kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, rĩathani rĩrĩ nĩ rĩanyu, inyuĩ athĩnjĩri-Ngai.”
english: Now, you priests, this commandment is for you.

kikuyu: na Simoni ũrĩa Mũzelote, na Judasi Mũisikariota, ũrĩa wamũkunyanĩire.
english: Simon the Zealot; and Judas Iscariot, who also betrayed him.

kikuyu: Nake akĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Baba, wee nĩweehĩtire mwĩhĩtwa kũrĩ Jehova. Njĩka o ta ũrĩa weranĩire, kuona atĩ Jehova nĩakũrĩhĩria harĩ Aamoni thũ ciaku.”
english: She said to him, “My father, you have opened your mouth to Yahweh; do to me according to that which has proceeded out of your mouth, because Yahweh has taken vengeance for you on your enemies, even on the children of Ammon.”

kikuyu: Ũũ nĩguo mũkaameeka: Mũkoinanga igongona ciao, na mũharaganie mahiga ma igongona ciao, na mũtemange itugĩ ciao cia Ashera, o na mũcine mĩhianano yao na mwaki.
english: But you shall deal with them like this: you shall break down their altars, dash their pillars in pieces, cut down their Asherah poles, and burn their engraved images with fire.

kikuyu: Nake Jesũ agĩĩta kĩrĩndĩ kĩu gĩũke harĩ we, agĩkĩĩra atĩrĩ, “Thikĩrĩriai na mũtaũkĩrwo.
english: He summoned the multitude, and said to them, “Hear, and understand.

kikuyu: Kaĩ nĩndĩĩriragĩria mawatho maku-ĩ! Tũũria muoyo wakwa nĩ ũndũ wa ũthingu waku. Kwĩhoka Watho wa Jehova
english: Behold, I long for your precepts! Revive me in your righteousness. VAV

kikuyu: No mũndũ ũcio agĩcookia atĩrĩ, “O na ingĩthimĩrwo cekeri ngiri ĩmwe† njĩkĩrĩrwo guoko, ndingĩtambũrũkĩria mũrũ wa mũthamaki guoko ndĩmwĩke ũũru. Mũthamaki aagwathire wee, na Abishai, na Itai tũkĩiguaga, akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, ‘Menyererai mwanake ũcio ũgwĩtwo Abisalomu nĩ ũndũ wakwa.’
english: The man said to Joab, “Though I should receive a thousand pieces of silver in my hand, I still wouldn’t stretch out my hand against the king’s son; for in our hearing the king commanded you and Abishai and Ittai, saying, ‘Beware that no one touch the young man Absalom.’

kikuyu: Ngĩkiuga atĩrĩ, “Niĩ ndikona Jehova rĩngĩ, ndikona Jehova ndĩ bũrũri ũyũ wa arĩa matũũraga muoyo; gũtirĩ hĩndĩ ĩngĩ ngona mũndũ wa thĩ, kana ngorwo hamwe na arĩa marĩ gũkũ thĩ.
english: I said, “I won’t see Yah, Yah in the land of the living. I will see man no more with the inhabitants of the world.

kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa ũiyaga, nĩatige gũcooka kũiya, nĩ kaba arute wĩra, akenogagia, na ekage maũndũ mega na moko make, nĩgeetha agĩe na kĩndũ angĩgayana na arĩa mabataire.
english: Let him who stole steal no more; but rather let him labor, producing with his hands something that is good, that he may have something to give to him who has need.

kikuyu: Nake Hathaka agĩcooka, na akĩmenyithia Esiteri ũrĩa Moridekai oigĩte.
english: Hathach came and told Esther the words of Mordecai.

kikuyu: Tondũ wa hekarũ yaku ĩrĩa ĩrĩ kũu Jerusalemu-rĩ, athamaki nĩmagakũrehagĩra iheo.
english: Because of your temple at Jerusalem, kings shall bring presents to you.

kikuyu: makoiga atĩrĩ, ‘Mũndũ ũyũ aambĩrĩirie gwaka nyũmba akĩremwo nĩ kũmĩrĩkia.’
english: saying, ‘This man began to build and wasn’t able to finish.’

kikuyu: Indo iria aarutire ciarĩ thaani ĩmwe ya betha ya ũritũ wa cekeri igana rĩmwe rĩa mĩrongo ĩtatũ, na mbakũri ĩmwe ya betha ya kũminjaminjĩria ya ũritũ wa cekeri mĩrongo mũgwanja, cierĩ kũringana na gĩthimo gĩa cekeri ya harĩa haamũre, na o ĩmwe yaiyũrĩtio mũtu ũrĩa mũhinyu mũno ũtukanĩtio na maguta, ũrĩ iruta rĩa mũtu;
english: gave his offering: one silver platter, the weight of which was a hundred and thirty shekels, one silver bowl of seventy shekels, according to the shekel of the sanctuary, both of them full of fine flour mixed with oil for a meal offering;

kikuyu: Kũrathimwo-rĩ, nĩ arĩa mahũũtagĩra na makanyootera ũthingu, nĩgũkorwo nĩmakahũũnio.
english: Blessed are those who hunger and thirst for righteousness, for they shall be filled.

kikuyu: Nake Jehova akiuga atĩrĩ, “Ũndũ ũyũ nĩguo wa gũtũma metĩkie atĩ Jehova, o we Ngai wa maithe mao Ngai wa Iburahĩmu, na Ngai wa Isaaka, na Ngai wa Jakubu nĩakuumĩrĩire.”
english: “This is so that they may believe that Yahweh, the God of their fathers, the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob, has appeared to you.”

kikuyu: Nayo ndungata ĩyo ĩkĩĩra Isaaka ũrĩa wothe yekĩte.
english: The servant told Isaac all the things that he had done.

kikuyu: Na rĩrĩa aakuhĩrĩirie harĩa ndarũgamĩte, ngĩtuĩka nda, ngĩgwa ngĩturumithia ũthiũ thĩ. Nake akĩnjĩĩra atĩrĩ: “Mũrũ wa mũndũ, nĩ wega ũmenye atĩ kĩoneki kĩu gĩkoniĩ mahinda ma ithirĩro.”
english: So he came near where I stood; and when he came, I was frightened, and fell on my face; but he said to me, “Understand, son of man, for the vision belongs to the time of the end.”

kikuyu: Maũndũ marĩa mwarĩirie nduma-inĩ, makaiguuo mũthenya barigici, na ũrĩa wothe mwanahehanĩrĩra matũ-inĩ mũrĩ tũnyũmba twa thĩinĩ, nĩũkanĩrĩrwo kuuma nyũmba igũrũ.
english: Therefore whatever you have said in the darkness will be heard in the light. What you have spoken in the ear in the inner rooms will be proclaimed on the housetops.

kikuyu: Thuuna ciake nĩigakũra. Ũthaka wake ũgaatuĩka ta wa mũtĩ wa mũtamaiyũ, naguo mũtararĩko wake ũtuĩke ta mũtarakwa wa Lebanoni.
english: His branches will spread, and his beauty will be like the olive tree, and his fragrance like Lebanon.

kikuyu: “Athuuri acio a Isiraeli nĩmagagũthikĩrĩria. Ningĩ wee na athuuri acio nĩmũgathiĩ kũrĩ mũthamaki wa bũrũri wa Misiri na mũmwĩre atĩrĩ, ‘Jehova, o we Ngai wa Ahibirania, nĩarĩtie na ithuĩ. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio twĩtĩkĩrie tũthiĩ rũgendo rwa mĩthenya ĩtatũ werũ-inĩ tũkarutĩre Jehova Ngai witũ magongona.’
english: They will listen to your voice. You shall come, you and the elders of Israel, to the king of Egypt, and you shall tell him, ‘Yahweh, the God of the Hebrews, has met with us. Now please let us go three days’ journey into the wilderness, that we may sacrifice to Yahweh, our God.’

kikuyu: Aciari ake moigire ũguo nĩ ũndũ wa gwĩtigĩra Ayahudi, nĩgũkorwo Ayahudi nĩmarĩkĩtie gũtua atĩ mũndũ o wothe ũngiuga atĩ Jesũ nĩwe Kristũ nĩegũikio nja ya thunagogi.
english: His parents said these things because they feared the Jews; for the Jews had already agreed that if any man would confess him as Christ, he would be put out of the synagogue.

kikuyu: No oya rũthanju rũrũ nĩguo ũhotage kũringa ciama naruo.” Musa Gũcooka Bũrũri wa Misiri
english: You shall take this rod in your hand, with which you shall do the signs.”

kikuyu: “Nĩ ũndũ ũcio, wee mũrũ wa mũndũ-rĩ, ratha ũhoro, na ũhũũre hĩ. Naruo rũhiũ rũu rwa njora ũreke rũringe maita meerĩ, o na nĩrũringe maita matatũ. Nĩ rũhiũ rwa njora rwa kũũragana, nĩ rũhiũ rwa njora rwa kũũragana kũnene, rũramahatĩrĩria na mĩena yothe.
english: “You therefore, son of man, prophesy, and strike your hands together. Let the sword be doubled the third time, the sword of the fatally wounded. It is the sword of the great one who is fatally wounded, which enters into their rooms.

kikuyu: Ngoro yakwa nĩĩracakaĩra Moabi o ta kĩnanda kĩa mũgeeto, nayo ngoro yakwa ĩgacaĩra Kiri-Heresethu.
english: Therefore my heart sounds like a harp for Moab, and my inward parts for Kir Heres.

kikuyu: agĩthiĩ, agĩkuua mahĩndĩ ma Saũlũ o na ma mũriũ Jonathani kuuma kwa andũ a Jabeshi-Gileadi. Maamarutĩte na hitho kuuma kĩhaaro-inĩ kĩa mũingĩ kũu Bethi-Shani, harĩa Afilisti maamacuurĩtie, thuutha wa kũũragĩra Saũlũ kũu Giliboa.
english: So David went and took the bones of Saul and the bones of Jonathan his son from the men of Jabesh Gilead, who had stolen them from the street of Beth Shan, where the Philistines had hanged them in the day that the Philistines killed Saul in Gilboa;

kikuyu: na tũnyũmba twa athĩnjĩri-Ngai rĩarĩ rĩa wariĩ wa mĩkono mĩrongo ĩĩrĩ gũthiũrũrũkĩria hekarũ yothe.
english: Between the rooms was a width of twenty cubits around the house on every side.

kikuyu: na andũ a thiritũ yake arĩa maarĩ njamba, maarĩ andũ 128. Mũtongoria wao mũnene aarĩ Zabidieli mũrũ wa Hagedolimu.
english: and their brothers, mighty men of valor, one hundred twenty-eight; and their overseer was Zabdiel, the son of Haggedolim.

kikuyu: Jusufu aarĩ na mĩaka mĩrongo ĩtatũ rĩrĩa atoonyire ũtungata-inĩ wa Firaũni mũthamaki wa Misiri. Nake Jusufu akĩehera mbere ya Firaũni, agĩtuĩkania bũrũri wothe wa Misiri.
english: Joseph was thirty years old when he stood before Pharaoh king of Egypt. Joseph went out from the presence of Pharaoh, and went throughout all the land of Egypt.

kikuyu: Nake Paũlũ agĩcookia atĩrĩ, “Rĩu ndũgamĩte igooti-inĩ rĩa Kaisari, harĩa njagĩrĩirwo nĩ gũci- irithĩrio. Niĩ ndihĩtĩirie Ayahudi, o ta ũrĩa wee mwene ũmenyete o wega.
english: But Paul said, “I am standing before Caesar’s judgment seat, where I ought to be tried. I have done no wrong to the Jews, as you also know very well.

kikuyu: Nake Jesũ akĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “O na inyuĩ arutani a Watho kaĩ mũrĩ na haaro-ĩ! Tondũ mũkuuithagia andũ mĩrigo mĩritũ ĩrĩa matangĩhota gũkuua, na inyuĩ ene mũtingĩmĩhutia o na kaara kamwe mũmateithie.
english: He said, “Woe to you lawyers also! For you load men with burdens that are difficult to carry, and you yourselves won’t even lift one finger to help carry those burdens.

kikuyu: Ningĩ ngĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Mũthamaki angĩona kwagĩrĩire, reke heo marũa ndwarĩre abarũthi a Mũrĩmo-wa-Farati, nĩgeetha manjĩtĩkĩrie hĩtũkĩre kuo kinya nginye Juda.
english: Moreover I said to the king, “If it pleases the king, let letters be given me to the governors beyond the River, that they  The Hebrew word rendered “God” is “אֱֹלהִ ֑ים” (Elohim).  “Yahweh” is God’s proper Name, sometimes

kikuyu: nake Jesũ aarĩ hekarũ-inĩ agĩceraceera gĩthaku-inĩ gĩa Solomoni.
english: It was winter, and Jesus was walking in the temple, in Solomon’s porch.

kikuyu: Rũrĩrĩ ũtooĩ nĩrũkarĩa irio cia bũrũri iria ũrutĩire wĩra, na gũtirĩ kĩndũ ũkaagĩa nakĩo, tiga o kũhinyĩrĩrio gũkĩru matukũ maku mothe.
english: A nation which you don’t know will eat the fruit of your ground and all of your work. You will only be oppressed and crushed always,

kikuyu: Andũ aingĩ metuaga marĩ na wendo ũtangĩthira, no mũndũ mwĩhokeku-rĩ, nũũ ũngĩmuona?
english: Many men claim to be men of unfailing love, but who can find a faithful man?

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio, Jehova Mwene-Hinya-Wothe, o we Ngai wa Isiraeli, ekuuga atĩrĩ: ‘Jonadabu mũrũ wa Rekabu ndarĩ hĩndĩ akaaga mũndũ wa kũndungatĩra.’ ”
english: therefore Yahweh of Armies, the God of Israel, says: ‘Jonadab the son of Rechab will not lack a man to stand before me forever.’ ”

kikuyu: Thuutha wa kahinda kanini mũndũ ũngĩ akĩmuona, akiuga atĩrĩ, “O nawe ũrĩ ũmwe wao.” Petero akĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Mũndũ ũyũ, niĩ ndirĩ wao!”
english: After a little while someone else saw him and said, “You also are one of them!” But Peter answered, “Man, I am not!”

kikuyu: na makenda gũikaragĩra itĩ iria cia bata thunagogi-inĩ, na gũikara handũ ha andũ arĩa atĩĩku maruga-inĩ.
english: and to get the best seats in the synagogues and the best places at feasts,

kikuyu: Atongoria a andũ othe a mĩhĩrĩga ya Isiraeli makĩrũgama handũ hao kũu kĩũngano-inĩ kĩa andũ a Ngai, marĩ thigari ngiri magana mana meeohete hiũ cia njora.
english: The chiefs of all the people, even of all the tribes of Israel, presented themselves

kikuyu: Nao magĩthiĩ kũrĩ Balamu makĩmwĩra atĩrĩ: “Ũũ nĩguo Balaki mũrũ wa Ziporu ekuuga: Ndũgetĩkĩre ũndũ o na ũrĩkũ ũgirie ũũke kũrĩ niĩ,
english: They came to Balaam, and said to him, “Balak the son of Zippor says, ‘Please let nothing hinder you from coming to me,

kikuyu: Agĩthiĩ na mbere na kwaria, akiuga atĩrĩ, “Kĩrĩa kiumaga thĩinĩ wa mũndũ nĩkĩo kĩmũgwatagia thaahu.
english: He said, “That which proceeds out of the man, that defiles the man.

kikuyu: Itharĩkĩra rĩu rĩa mbere rĩa Jonathani marĩ na mũkuui wake wa indo cia mbaara rĩoragire andũ mĩrongo ĩĩrĩ gacigo-inĩ ka nuthu ya ĩka. Afilisti Kũhootwo nĩ Isiraeli
english: That first slaughter, which Jonathan and his armor bearer made, was about twenty men, within as it were half a furrow’s length in an acre of land.

kikuyu: “Haha harĩ kahĩĩ karĩ na mĩgate ĩtano ya mũtu wa cairi na thamaki igĩrĩ nini, no ingĩkĩigana atĩa andũ marĩ aingĩ ũũ?”
english: “There is a boy here who has five barley loaves and two fish, but what are these among so many?”

kikuyu: “Itanarĩkia kũhooya ũguo na ngoro-rĩ, Rebeka oka arĩ na ndigithũ yake kĩande. Nake aikũrũka gĩthima-inĩ kĩa maaĩ, aataha maaĩ na niĩ ndamwĩra atĩrĩ, ‘Ndagũthaitha he maaĩ nyue.’
english: Before I had finished speaking in my heart, behold, Rebekah came out with her pitcher on her shoulder. She went down to the spring, and drew. I said to her, ‘Please let me drink.’

kikuyu: Na rĩrĩa ahooyaga, mũhaanĩre wa ũthiũ wake ũkĩgarũrũka, nacio nguo ciake ikĩerũha igĩkenga o ta rũheni.
english: As he was praying, the appearance of his face was altered, and his clothing became white and dazzling.

kikuyu: Mũtikanamene Mũedomu nĩgũkorwo nĩ mũrũ wa ithe wanyu. Mũtikanamene Mũmisiri, nĩ ũndũ nĩmwatũire mũrĩ ageni bũrũri wake.
english: You shall not abhor an Edomite, for he is your brother. You shall not abhor an Egyptian, because you lived as a foreigner in his land.

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Joshua akĩũria andũ a Isiraeli atĩrĩ, “Nĩ nginya rĩ mũgweterera mũtegũthiĩ kwĩgwatĩra bũrũri ũrĩa Jehova Ngai wa maithe manyu aamũheire?
english: Joshua said to the children of Israel, “How long will you neglect to go in to possess the land, which Yahweh, the God of your fathers, has given you?

kikuyu: Njĩtĩkiai rĩrĩa nguuga atĩ ndĩ thĩinĩ wa Baba, nake Baba arĩ thĩinĩ wakwa; kana mũgĩĩtĩkie nĩ ũndũ wa ũira wa ciama iria ningaga.
english: Believe me that I am in the Father, and the Father in me; or else believe me for the very works’ sake.

kikuyu: na a Harimu maarĩ 1,017.
english: The children of Harim, one thousand seventeen.

kikuyu: kũrĩa maithe manyu maangeririe na makĩndoria, na makĩona ciĩko ciakwa, ihinda rĩa mĩaka mĩrongo ĩna.
english: where your fathers tested me and tried me, and saw my deeds for forty years.

kikuyu: O na gũtuĩka ndeheririe kũndũ kũrĩa gũtũũgĩru kuuma Isiraeli-rĩ, ngoro ya Asa nĩamĩneanĩte biũ kũrĩ Jehova mũtũũrĩre-inĩ wake wothe.
english: But the high places were not taken away out of Israel; nevertheless the heart of Asa was perfect all his days.

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Musa akĩĩra aciirithania a Isiraeli atĩrĩ, “O mũndũ wanyu no nginya oorage arũme anyu arĩa manyiitanĩire na andũ a Moabi kũhooya Baali ya Peori.”
english: Moses said to the judges of Israel, “Everyone kill his men who have joined themselves to Baal Peor.”

kikuyu: Ti-itherũ mũthenya wa ituĩro nĩũrooka, naguo ũgaakana ta icua rĩa mwaki. Arĩa othe etĩĩi, na arĩa aaganu, magaatuĩka maragara ma mahuti, naguo mũthenya ũcio wakinya magaacinwo, maniinwo biũ. Gũtirĩ mũri kana rũhonge rwao rũgaatigara,” ũguo nĩguo Jehova Mwene-Hinya-Wothe ekuuga.
english: For behold, the day comes, burning like a furnace, when all the proud and all who work wickedness will be stubble. The day that comes will burn them up,” says Yahweh of Armies, “so that it will leave them neither root nor branch.

kikuyu: duĩra ciira wakwa, Wee Ngai, na ũnjiirĩrĩre harĩ rũrĩrĩ rũrũ rũtagwĩtĩkĩtie; ndeithũra kuuma kũrĩ andũ aya aaganu na maaragia maheeni.
english: indicate me, God, and plead my cause against an ungodly nation. Oh, deliver me from deceitful and wicked men.

kikuyu: Andũ nĩmagacookia moige, atĩrĩ, ‘Nĩ tondũ nĩmatiganĩirie Jehova, o we Ngai wa maithe mao, ũrĩa wamarutire bũrũri wa Misiri, na makĩhĩmbĩria ngai ingĩ, magĩcihooya na magĩcitungatĩra, nĩkĩo amarehithĩirie mwanangĩko ũyũ wothe.’ ”
english: They shall answer, ‘Because they abandoned Yahweh, the God of their fathers, who brought them out of the land of Egypt, and took other gods, worshiped them, and served them. Therefore he has brought all this evil on them.’ ”

kikuyu: Wĩhũgũre na kũngĩ, ũtige kũndora; maitho maku nĩmarahoota. Njuĩrĩ yaku no ta rũũru rwa mbũri rũgĩikũrũka Kĩrĩma kĩa Gileadi.
english: Turn away your eyes from me, for they have overcome me. Your hair is like a flock of goats, that lie along the side of Gilead.

kikuyu: Marĩ kũu makaanagĩrĩra, makoiga atĩrĩ, ‘Firaũni mũthamaki wa bũrũri wa Misiri, nĩ inegene rĩa tũhũ; nĩorirwo nĩ mweke wake.’ ”
english: They cried there, ‘Pharaoh king of Egypt is but a noise; he has let the appointed time pass by.’

kikuyu: Ningĩ ngĩona ũũgĩ gũkũ thĩ kwaraga riũa ũrĩa wamakirie mũno, watariĩ ũũ:
english: I have also seen wisdom under the sun in this way, and it seemed great to me.

kikuyu: Jesũ akĩmooria atĩrĩ, “Mũrĩ na mĩgate ĩigana?” Nao makĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Tũrĩ na mĩgate mũgwanja.”
english: He asked them, “How many loaves do you have?” They said, “Seven.”

kikuyu: Andũ arĩa akuũ-rĩ, wee nĩũmonagia magegania maku? Acio makuĩte-rĩ, nĩmarahũkaga, magakũgooca?
english: Do you show wonders to the dead? Do the departed spirits rise up and praise you? Selah.

kikuyu: Rĩrĩa aakinyire kuo, agĩkora anene a mbũtũ cia ita maikarĩte hamwe. Akĩĩra mũnene wao atĩrĩ, “Mũnene, ndĩ na ndũmĩrĩri yaku.” Nake Jehu akĩũria atĩrĩ, “Ithuothe-rĩ, nĩ ũrĩkũ ũrĩ na ndũmĩrĩri yake?” Nake akĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Nĩwe mũnene, ndũmĩrĩri nĩ yaku.”
english: When he came, behold, the captains of the army were sitting. Then he said, “I have a message for you, captain.” Jehu said, “To which one of us?” He said, “To you, O captain.”

kikuyu: nginya rĩrĩa Jehova aameheririe mbere yake o ta ũrĩa aamerĩte na kanua ka ndungata ciake ciothe cia anabii. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio andũ a Isiraeli makĩeherio bũrũri wao, magĩthaamĩrio Ashuri, na no kuo marĩ. Samaria Gũtũũrwo Rĩngĩ
english: until Yahweh removed Israel out of his sight, as he said by all his servants the prophets. So Israel was carried away out of their own land to Assyria to this day.

kikuyu: Nake barũthi oona ũrĩa gwekĩka, agĩĩtĩkia, nĩgũkorwo nĩagegirio nĩ ũrutani ũcio ũkoniĩ Mwathani. Paũlũ arĩ Antiokia ya Bũrũri wa Pisidia
english: Then the proconsul, when he saw what was done, believed, being astonished at the teaching of the Lord.

kikuyu: na gũtirĩ mũndũ ũkaahuna kana atuĩke thaata bũrũri-inĩ wanyu. Nĩndĩĩmũkinyagĩria matukũ marĩa mwathĩrĩirio ma gũtũũra muoyo.
english: No one will miscarry or be barren in your land. I will fulfill the number of your days.

kikuyu: Rĩrĩa ũgaakorwo ũrĩkĩtie kwamũra gĩcunjĩ gĩa ikũmi kĩa maciaro maku mothe mwaka-inĩ wa gatatũ, mwaka ũcio wa kũrutwo gĩcunjĩ gĩa ikũmi, nĩũgakĩheana kũrĩ Alawii, na kũrĩ mũndũ wa kũngĩ, na kũrĩ mwana wa ngoriai, o na kũrĩ mũtumia wa ndigwa, nĩgeetha marĩe mahũũne marĩ kũu matũũra-inĩ manyu.
english: When you have finished tithing all the tithe of your increase in the third year, which is the year of tithing, then you shall give it to the Levite, to the foreigner, to the fatherless, and to the widow, that they may eat within your gates and be filled.

kikuyu: na Ebali, na Abimaeli, na Sheba,
english: Ebal, Abimael, Sheba,

kikuyu: Nake Jeremia ũcio mũnabii akĩĩra Zedekia mũthamaki wa Juda maũndũ macio mothe, arĩ kũu Jerusalemu,
english: Then Jeremiah the prophet spoke all these words to Zedekiah king of Judah in Jerusalem,

kikuyu: Roho wa mũndũ nĩguo ũmũũmagĩrĩria rĩrĩa arĩ mũrũaru, no roho mũthuthĩku nũũ ũngĩhota kũwĩtiiria?
english: A man’s spirit will sustain him in sickness, but a crushed spirit, who can bear?

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ wa kwaga thoni ciothe-rĩ, nĩmeheanĩte kũrĩ thuti
english: They, having become callous, gave themselves up to lust, to work all uncleanness with greediness.

kikuyu: Mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai nĩagathũngũthia tũtũrũme tũu tweerĩ mbere ya Jehova, tũtuĩke iruta rĩa gũthũngũthio, hamwe na mĩgate ĩyo ya maciaro ma mbere. Macio nĩ maruta matheru marutĩirwo Jehova nĩ ũndũ wa mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai.
english: The priest shall wave them with the bread of the first fruits for a wave offering before Yahweh, with the two lambs. They shall be holy to Yahweh for the priest.

kikuyu: Mwatũrutire bũrũri wa Misiri nĩkĩ, mũgĩtũrehe kũndũ gũkũ kũũru ũũ? Gũkũ gũtirĩ ngano kana ngũyũ, kana thabibũ, kana makomamanga. O na gũtirĩ maaĩ ma kũnyua!”
english: Why have you made us to come up out of Egypt, to bring us in to this evil place? It is no place of seed, or of figs, or of vines, or of pomegranates; neither is there any water to drink.”

kikuyu: Ariũ a Gerishoni maarĩ: mũriũ Libini, nake Jehathu aarĩ mũriũ wa Libini, nake Zima aarĩ mũriũ wa Jehathu,
english: Of Gershom: Libni his son, Jahath his son, Zimmah his son,

kikuyu: Nake Jehova aroria atĩrĩ, ‘Nũũ ũkũheenereria Ahabu mũthamaki wa Isiraeli nĩguo atharĩkĩre Ramothu-Gileadi, nĩgeetha athiĩ agakuĩre kuo?’ “Ũmwe akiuga ũũ, na ũrĩa ũngĩ akiuga ũũ.
english: Yahweh said, ‘Who will entice Ahab king of Israel, that he may go up and fall at Ramoth Gilead?’ One spoke saying in this way, and another saying in that way.

kikuyu: Nake mũrutwo ũngĩ akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Mwathani, reke nyambe thiĩ ngathike baba.”
english: Another of his disciples said to him, “Lord, allow me first to go and bury my father.”

kikuyu: Rĩrĩa Samũeli aagarũrũkaga athiĩ-rĩ, Saũlũ akĩnyiita ruuno rwa nguo yake, nayo ĩgĩtarũka.
english: As Samuel turned around to go away, Saul grabbed the skirt of his robe, and it tore.

kikuyu: Ũcooke ũthiĩ kũrĩ mũthamaki ũmwarĩrie ciugo ici.” Nake Joabu akĩmwĩra ũrĩa ekuuga.
english: Go in to the king and speak like this to him.” So Joab put the words in her mouth.

kikuyu: Rĩu-rĩ, hĩndĩ ya kũriũka mũtumia ũcio agaakorwo arĩ wa ũ, nĩgũkorwo othe mũgwanja nĩmamũhikĩtie?”
english: In the resurrection, when they rise, whose wife will she be of them? For the seven had her as a wife.”

kikuyu: Ĩra andũ a nyũmba ya Isiraeli atĩrĩ, ‘Ũũ nĩguo Mwathani Jehova ekuuga: Ngirie gũthaahia handũ-hakwa-harĩa- haamũre, hau gĩikaro kĩa hinya kĩrĩa mwĩtĩĩaga nakĩo, o kĩu gĩkenagia maitho manyu, kĩrĩa mwendete. Ariũ na aarĩ anyu arĩa mwatigire na thuutha makaaniinwo na rũhiũ rwa njora.
english: ‘Speak to the house of Israel, “The Lord Yahweh says: ‘Behold, I will profane my sanctuary, the pride of your power, the desire of your eyes, and that which your soul pities; and your sons and your daughters whom you have left behind will fall by the sword.

kikuyu: Mĩaka yothe ĩrĩa Lameku aatũũrire muoyo yarĩ mĩaka magana mũgwanja na mĩrongo mũgwanja na mũgwanja, agĩcooka agĩkua.
english: All the days of Lamech were seven hundred seventy-seven years, then he died.

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo nĩngoya matawa njethe andũ Jerusalemu, na herithie arĩa meiganĩtie, arĩa mahaana ndibei ĩtiganĩirio na kĩnyita kĩayo, arĩa meciiragia atĩrĩ, ‘Jehova ndarĩ ũndũ angĩĩka, mwega kana mũũru.’
english: It will happen at that time, that I will search Jerusalem with lamps, and I will punish the men who are settled on their dregs, who say in their heart, “Yahweh will not do good, neither will he do evil.”

kikuyu: Ndungata ĩyo ĩkĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Ĩ mũndũ-wa-nja ũcio angĩkaarega gũũka na niĩ bũrũri-inĩ ũyũ? Ngaacookia mũrũguo bũrũri ũrĩa woimire?”
english: The servant said to him, “What if the woman isn’t willing to follow me to this land? Must I bring your son again to the land you came from?”

kikuyu: na Jehudu, na Bene-Beraka, na Gathi-Rimoni,
english: Jehud, Bene Berak, Gath Rimmon,

kikuyu: nĩguo mahotomie ciira wa andũ arĩa athĩĩni, na magirĩrĩrie kĩhooto kĩa andũ akwa arĩa mahinyĩrĩirio, na magatua atumia a ndigwa iguĩma rĩao, o na magatunya ciana cia ngoriai indo ciacio.
english: to deprive the needy of justice, and to rob the poor among my people of their rights, that widows may be their plunder, and that they may make the fatherless their prey!

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Daudi na athuuri a Isiraeli, na anene a ikundi cia ngiri, magĩthiĩ kwambatia ithandũkũ rĩa kĩrĩkanĩro kĩa Jehova kuuma nyũmba ya Obedi-Edomu makenete.
english: So David, the elders of Israel, and the captains over thousands went to bring the ark of Yahweh’s covenant up out of the house of Obed-Edom with joy.

kikuyu: No rĩrĩa mwanake wa mwarĩ wa nyina na Paũlũ aaiguire ũhoro wa ithugunda rĩu, agĩthiĩ nyũmba ya thigari akĩĩra Paũlũ.
english: But Paul’s sister’s son heard they were lying in wait, and he came and entered into the barracks and told Paul.

kikuyu: Ihinda rĩa mĩaka mĩrongo ĩna nĩwamatũũririe werũ-inĩ; matiagire kĩndũ o nakĩ, ningĩ nguo ciao itiigana gũthira, kana magũrũ mao makĩimba.
english: “Yes, forty years you sustained them in the wilderness. They lacked nothing. Their clothes didn’t grow old, and their feet didn’t swell.

kikuyu: Nake akĩmacookeria atĩrĩ, “Mũndũ o wothe angĩtigana na mũtumia wake acooke ahikie mũtumia ũngĩ, mũndũ ũcio nĩ atharĩtie.
english: He said to them, “Whoever divorces his wife and marries another commits adultery against her.

kikuyu: akĩmatũmĩra mũnabii, ũrĩa wameerire atĩrĩ, “Ũũ nĩguo Jehova, Ngai wa Isiraeli, ekuuga: Niĩ ndamũrutire bũrũri wa Misiri, ngĩmũruta bũrũri wa ũkombo.
english: Yahweh sent a prophet to the children of Israel; and he said to them, “Yahweh, the God of Israel, says, ‘I brought you up from Egypt, and brought you out of the house of bondage.

kikuyu: Arĩa marĩkuonaga, marĩgũkũũragĩra maitho, magecũũrania ũhoro waku, makoorania atĩrĩ, “Ũyũ nĩwe mũndũ ũrĩa wenyenyagia thĩ na agatũma mothamaki mainaine,
english: Those who see you will stare at you. They will ponder you, saying, “Is this the man who made the earth to tremble, who shook kingdoms,

kikuyu: nacio ciĩga iria tuonaga ta itarĩ cia gũtĩĩo mũno-rĩ, nĩcio tũheaga gĩtĩĩo kĩa mwanya. Nacio ciĩga iria tuonaga ta itagĩrĩire kuoneka nĩimenyagĩrĩrwo mũno,
english: Those parts of the body which we think to be less honorable, on those we bestow more abundant honor; and our unpresentable parts have more abundant modesty,

kikuyu: Ũmĩgite igũrũ, no ũtigie buti ĩmwe na nuthu mwena wa igũrũ wa thingo. Wĩkĩre mũrango mwena-inĩ wa thabina, na ũmĩake ĩrĩ na ngoroba ya thĩ, na ya gatagatĩ, na ya igũrũ.
english: You shall make a roof in the ship, and you shall finish it to a cubit upward. You shall set the door of the ship in its side. You shall make it with lower, second, and third levels.

kikuyu: ningĩ nĩ aarĩ na ngʼondu 7,000, na ngamĩĩra 3,000, na ndegwa cia kũigana kuohwo macooki 500, na ndigiri 500, na nĩ aarĩ na ndungata nyingĩ mũno. Nĩwe warĩ mũndũ ũrĩa mũnene mũno kũrĩ andũ arĩa angĩ othe a mwena wa Irathĩro.
english: His possessions also were seven thousand sheep, three thousand camels, five hundred yoke of oxen, five hundred female donkeys, and a very great household; so that this man was the greatest of all the children of the east.

kikuyu: Ciĩranĩro ciaku nĩthuuranĩre o biũ, na ndungata yaku nĩĩciendete.
english: Your promises have been thoroughly tested, and your servant loves them.

kikuyu: agitagĩra mahĩndĩ make mothe, gũtirĩ o na rĩmwe rĩngiunĩka.
english: He protects all of his bones. Not one of them is broken.

kikuyu: No hĩndĩ ĩrĩa ũngĩtwo-rĩ, ikaragĩra itĩ cia thuutha, nĩgeetha ũrĩa ũgwĩtĩte ooka, akwĩre atĩrĩ, ‘Mũrata wakwa, thiĩ ũgaikare harĩa mbere.’ Hĩndĩĩyonĩũkaheogĩtĩĩo maitho-inĩma ageniarĩa angĩothe.
english: But when you are invited, go and sit in the lowest place, so that when he who invited you comes, he may tell you, ‘Friend, move up higher.’ Then you will be honored in the presence of all who sit at the table with you.

kikuyu: ,000. 5 Mũthenya ũyũ ũngĩ, aathani ao na athuuri, na arutani a watho makĩgomana kũu Jerusalemu.
english: In the morning, their rulers, elders, and scribes were gathered together in Jerusalem.

kikuyu: Nĩngatũma bũrũri ũcio wanangĩke ũkire ihooru, naguo hinya ũrĩa wĩtĩĩagĩra nĩũgaathira, na irĩma icio cia Isiraeli nĩigakirio ihooru, kwage mũndũ ũngĩtuĩkanĩria kuo.
english: I will make the land a desolation and an astonishment. The pride of her power

kikuyu: Wee Ngai nĩwe kĩirigo gĩakwa kĩa hinya. Ũndiganĩirie nĩkĩ? Ingĩthiĩ ngĩcakayaga nĩkĩ nĩ ũndũ wa kũhinyĩrĩirio nĩ thũ?
english: For you are the God of my strength. Why have you rejected me? Why do I go mourning because of the oppression of the enemy?

kikuyu: No ningĩ nĩ mũtugo wanyu hĩndĩ ya Bathaka ndĩmuohorere mũndũ ũmwe. Nĩmũkwenda ndĩmuohorere ‘mũthamaki wa Ayahudi’?”
english: But you have a custom that I should release someone to you at the Passover. Therefore, do you want me to release to you the King of the Jews?”

kikuyu: Tondũ Edomu ndaametĩkĩririe matuĩkanĩrie bũrũri wao-rĩ, andũ a Isiraeli makĩhũndũka, magĩtigana nao. Gĩkuũ kĩa Harũni
english: Thus Edom refused to give Israel passage through his border, so Israel turned away from him.

kikuyu: Musa agĩthĩnja ndegwa ĩyo, na akĩoya thakame ĩmwe yayo na kĩara gĩake, akĩmĩhaka hĩa ciothe cia kĩgongona nĩgeetha atherie kĩgongona. Agĩcooka agĩita thakameĩyoĩngĩgĩtina-inĩgĩakĩgongona. Nĩũndũũcioagĩkĩamũranĩguoakĩhoroherie.
english: He killed it; and Moses took the blood, and put it around on the horns of the altar with his finger, and purified the altar, and poured out the blood at the base of the altar, and sanctified it, to make atonement for it.

kikuyu: o arĩa matigaga njĩra iria nũngĩrĩru, magathiĩra njĩra cia nduma,
english: who forsake the paths of uprightness, to walk in the ways of darkness,

kikuyu: nĩgũkorwo mũrĩ andũ mamũrĩirwo Jehova Ngai wanyu. Kuuma ndũrĩrĩ-inĩ ciothe cia thĩ, Jehova nĩamũthuurĩte mũtuĩke andũ ake a bata.
english: For you are a holy people to Yahweh your God, and Yahweh has chosen you to be a people for his own possession, above all peoples who are on the face of the earth.

kikuyu: naruo rũrĩrĩ rũu ngaanĩtie rũngĩĩrira, rũtigane na ũũru waruo, nĩngaigua tha, njage kũrũĩka ũũru ũcio njiirĩire kũrũĩka.
english: if that nation, concerning which I have spoken, turns from their evil, I will repent of the evil that I thought to do to them.

kikuyu: Jehova nĩagakũrehithĩria irumi, na kĩrigiicano, na ikũũma ũndũ-inĩ ũrĩa wothe ũrĩĩkaga na guoko gwaku, nginya akũniine ũthire na ihenya nĩ ũndũ wa wĩhia ũrĩa wĩkĩte wa kũmũtiganĩria.
english: Yahweh will send on you cursing, confusion, and rebuke in all that you put your hand to do, until you are destroyed and until you perish quickly, because of the evil of your doings, by which you have forsaken me.

kikuyu: Na rĩrĩa maakuhĩrĩirie nyũmba ya Mika, magĩkũũrana mũgambo wa mwanake ũcio Mũlawii; nĩ ũndũ ũcio magĩtoonya kuo makĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Nũũ wakũrehire gũkũ? Nĩ kĩĩ ũreka kũndũ gũkũ? Nĩ kĩĩ gĩtũmĩte ũkorwo ũrĩ gũkũ?”
english: When they were by the house of Micah, they knew the voice of the young man the Levite; so they went over there and said to him, “Who brought you here? What do you do in this place? What do you have here?”

kikuyu: Rĩrĩa Mũthamaki Jeroboamu aaiguire ũrĩa mũndũ ũcio wa Ngai aanĩrĩire agokĩrĩra kĩgongona kĩu kĩarĩ Betheli, agĩtambũrũkia guoko gwake arĩ kĩgongona-inĩ, akiuga atĩrĩ, “Mũnyiitei!” No rĩrĩ, guoko kũu aatambũrũkĩirie mũndũ ũcio gũkĩonja, na ndangĩahotire gũgũthuna rĩngĩ.
english: When the king heard the saying of the man of God, which he cried against the altar in Bethel, Jeroboam put out his hand from the altar, saying, “Seize him!” His hand, which he put out against him, dried up, so that he could not draw it back again to himself.

kikuyu: a rĩrĩ, mũndũ ũrĩa ũkoragwo arĩ na wĩtĩkio mũhũthũ mwamũkĩrei, mũtekũmũtuĩra ciira nĩ ũndũ wa maũndũ marĩa mataiguanĩirwo.
english: ow accept one who is weak in faith, but not for disputes over opinions.

kikuyu: No nĩngũthondeka kĩrĩkanĩro nawe. Wee nĩũgatoonya thabina, wee mwene na ariũ aku na mũtumia waku, na atumia a ariũ aku.
english: But I will establish my covenant with you. You shall come into the ship, you, your sons, your wife, and your sons’ wives with you.

kikuyu: “Thũ nayo yetĩĩte, ĩkoiga atĩrĩ, ‘Nĩngamarũmĩrĩra na ihenya, ndĩmakinyĩre. Ngagayania indo iria matahĩte, ndĩĩhũũnĩrĩrie nacio. Nĩngũcomora rũhiũ rwakwa rwa njora, nakuo guoko gwakwa kũmaniine.’
english: The enemy said, ‘I will pursue. I will overtake. I will divide the plunder. My desire will be satisfied on them. I will draw my sword. My hand will destroy them.’

kikuyu: Matigacooka gũtahwo nĩ ndũrĩrĩ, kana kũrĩĩo nĩ nyamũ cia gĩthaka. Magaatũũraga na thayũ, na gũtirĩ mũndũ ũkaamamakagia.
english: They will no more be a prey to the nations, neither will the animals of the earth devour them; but they will dwell securely, and no one will make them afraid.

kikuyu: Kwarĩ mũthenya wa Ihaarĩria rĩa Kiumia gĩa Gĩathĩ kĩa Bathaka; na kwarĩ ta thaa thita. Nake Pilato akĩĩra Ayahudi atĩrĩ, “Ũyũ mũthamaki wanyu.”
english: Now it was the Preparation Day of the Passover, at about the sixth hour.† He said to the Jews, “Behold, your King!”

kikuyu: Atĩrĩrĩ, ithandũkũ rĩa kĩrĩkanĩro rĩa Mwathani wa thĩ yothe nĩrĩkũringa Rũũĩ rwa Jorodani mbere yanyu.
english: Behold, the ark of the covenant of the Lord‡ of all the earth passes over before you into the Jordan.

kikuyu: Mwaka-inĩ wa ikũmi na ũmwe, mũthenya wa mũgwanja wa mweri wa mbere, kiugo kĩa Jehova nĩkĩanginyĩrĩire, ngĩĩrwo atĩrĩ:
english: In the eleventh year, in the first month, in the seventh day of the month, Yahweh’s word came to me, saying,

kikuyu: Nake akĩheana mũigana wa ũritũ wa thahabu ya gũthondeka indo ciothe cia thahabu cia kũhũthagĩrwo ũtungata-inĩ wa mĩthemba o mĩthemba, na ũritũ wa betha ya gũthondeka indo ciothe cia betha cia kũhũthagĩrwo ũtungata-inĩ
english: of gold by weight for the gold for all vessels of every kind of service, for all the vessels of silver by weight, for all vessels of every kind of service;

kikuyu: Goocai Jehova gũkũ thĩ, inyuĩ ciũmbe cia iria-inĩ, na ũriku wothe wa maria marĩa manene,
english: Praise Yahweh from the earth, you great sea creatures, and all depths,

kikuyu: Makĩmatumĩra iremba cia gatani njega, na tũkũbia twa gatani, na thuruarĩ cia thĩinĩ cia gatani ĩrĩa yogothetwo wega.
english: the turban of fine linen, the linen headbands of fine linen, the linen trousers of fine twined linen,

kikuyu: Rĩrĩa Joshua na Isiraeli othe moonire atĩ andũ ao arĩa mooheirie andũ thuutha wa itũũra nĩmarĩtunyanĩte, na makĩona ndogo ĩgĩtoga kuuma itũũra, makĩgarũrũka na makĩũraga andũ acio a Ai.
english: When Joshua and all Israel saw that the ambush had taken the city, and that the smoke of the city ascended, then they turned back and killed the men of Ai.

kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, mũndũ ũmwe wa arĩa maarĩ na Jesũ agĩcomora rũhiũ rwake rwa njora, agĩtinia gũtũ kwa ndungata ya mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai ũrĩa mũnene.
english: Behold, one of those who were with Jesus stretched out his hand and drew his sword, and struck the servant of the high priest, and cut off his ear.

kikuyu: Kuuma Zera aarĩ: Jeueli. Andũ othe a kuuma Juda maarĩ 690.
english: Of the sons of Zerah: Jeuel and their brothers, six hundred ninety.

kikuyu: Moima Tera, magĩthiĩ makĩamba hema ciao Mithika.
english: They traveled from Terah, and encamped in Mithkah.

kikuyu: Nao athĩnjĩri-Ngai arĩa anene a Ayahudi makĩĩra Pilato atĩrĩ, “Tiga kwandĩka ‘Mũthamaki wa Ayahudi’, no andĩka atĩ mũndũ ũyũ nĩ kuuga oigire atĩ nĩwe mũthamaki wa Ayahudi.”
english: The chief priests of the Jews therefore said to Pilate, “Don’t write, ‘The King of the Jews,’ but, ‘he said, “I am King of the Jews.” ’ ”

kikuyu: Mũrũaru ũrĩ rĩera-inĩ nĩũmenyaga mahinda maguo ma gũcooka, o nacio ndirahũgĩ, na thũngũrũrũ, na nyamĩndigi nĩcimenyaga mahinda ma cio ma gũcooka. No andũ akwa matiũĩ maũndũ marĩa Jehova endaga mekwo.
english: Yes, the stork in the sky knows her appointed times. The turtledove, the swallow, and the crane observe the time of their coming; but my people don’t know Yahweh’s

kikuyu: Githĩ ndakaaga gũthikĩrĩria mũno makĩria rĩrĩa ũkuuga atĩ ndũramuona, na atĩ ciira waku ũrĩ mbere yake, na no nginya ũmweterere,
english: How much less when you say you don’t see him. The cause is before him, and you wait for him!

kikuyu: Andũ a Juda othe magĩkena nĩ ũndũ wa mwĩhĩtwa tondũ mawĩhĩtire na ngoro ciao ciothe. Makĩrongooria Ngai na kĩyo, na makĩmuona. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Jehova akĩmahe ũhurũko mĩena yothe.
english: All Judah rejoiced at the oath, for they had sworn with all their heart and sought him with their whole desire; and he was

kikuyu: atĩrĩ, nĩngũiga rũũa rwa ngʼondu kĩhuhĩro-inĩ kĩa ngano. Ime rĩngĩkorwo rũũa-inĩ rwiki na kũu kũngĩ thĩ gũkorwo kũrĩ kũmũ-rĩ, hĩndĩ ĩyo nĩngamenya atĩ nĩũkũhonokia Isiraeli na ũndũ wa guoko gwakwa, o ta ũrĩa uugĩte.”
english: behold, I will put a fleece of wool on the threshing floor. If there is dew on the fleece only, and it is dry on all the ground, then I’ll know that you will save Israel by my hand, as you have spoken.”

kikuyu: “ ‘Mũthenya wa ikũmi na ĩna wa mweri wa mbere nĩrĩo mũrĩkoragwo na Bathaka ya Jehova.
english: “ ‘In the first month, on the fourteenth day of the month, is Yahweh’s Passover.

kikuyu: Muonirio maũndũ macio nĩgeetha mũmenye atĩ Jehova nĩwe Ngai; tiga o we wiki gũtirĩ ũngĩ.
english: It was shown to you so that you might know that Yahweh is God. There is no one else besides him.

kikuyu: No Jehova akĩũmia ngoro ya Firaũni, nake akĩrega kũrekereria andũ a Isiraeli mathiĩ. Mũthiro wa Nduma
english: But Yahweh hardened Pharaoh’s heart, and he didn’t let the children of Israel go.

kikuyu: Nake Ayubu agĩcookeria Jehova, akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ:
english: Then Job answered Yahweh,

kikuyu: Ũtukũ ũmwe Mwathani akĩĩra Paũlũ na kĩoneki atĩrĩ, “Ndũkae gwĩtigĩra; thiĩ na mbere na kũheana ũhoro, na ndũgaakire.
english: The Lord said to Paul in the night by a vision, “Don’t be afraid, but speak and don’t be silent;

kikuyu: Kahĩĩ kau gataanamenya gwĩtana koige ‘Baba’ kana ‘Maitũ’, ũtonga wa Dameski na indo iria itahĩtwo nĩ Samaria nĩigakuuo nĩ mũthamaki wa Ashuri.”
english: For before the child knows how to say, ‘My father’ and ‘My mother,’ the riches of Damascus and the plunder of Samaria will be carried away by the king of Assyria.”

kikuyu: ũthenya o ro ũcio, Mũthamaki Ahasuerusu akĩhe mũtumia wake Esiteri indo cia Hamani, ũcio thũ ya Ayahudi.* Nake Moridekai agĩũka mbere ya mũthamaki, nĩ ũndũ Esiteri nĩoigĩte ũrĩa maatarainie.
english: n that day, King Ahasuerus gave the house of Haman, the Jews’ enemy, to Esther the queen. Mordecai came before the king; for Esther had told what he was to her.

kikuyu: Ningĩ ngĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Honge ici igĩrĩ cia mĩtamaiyũ irũgamanĩte na mĩberethi ĩno ya thahabu iraita maguta mahaana ta thahabu nĩ ndũũ?”
english: I asked him the second time, “What are these two olive branches, which are beside the two golden spouts that pour the golden oil out of themselves?”

kikuyu: Gamalieli mũrũ wa Pedazuru nĩwe warĩ mũtongoria wa gĩkundi kĩa mũhĩrĩga wa Manase,
english: Gamaliel the son of Pedahzur was over the army of the tribe of the children of Manasseh.

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo Johana aabatithanagia na maaĩ, no inyuĩ mũrĩbatithio na Roho Mũtheru mĩthenya mĩingĩ ĩtaanathira.”
english: For John indeed baptized in water, but you will be baptized in the Holy Spirit not many days from now.”

kikuyu: Joshua akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Rĩu-rĩ, kĩeheriei ngai cia kũngĩ iria irĩ thĩinĩ wanyu, na mũheane ngoro cianyu biũ harĩ Jehova Ngai wa Isiraeli.”
english: “Now therefore put away the foreign gods which are among you, and incline your heart to Yahweh, the God of Israel.”

kikuyu: Nĩtũhooyaga ũtukũ na mũthenya na kĩyo kĩingĩ nĩgeetha tũkoonana na inyuĩ rĩngĩ na tũmũrehere kĩrĩa mũtarĩ nakĩo ũhoro-inĩ wa gwĩtĩkia kwanyu.
english: night and day praying exceedingly that we may see your face and may perfect that which is lacking in your faith?

kikuyu: “Ĩka Harũni na ariũ ake maũndũ mothe marĩa ngwathĩte, na ũniine mĩthenya mũgwanja ũkĩmaamũra.
english: “You shall do so to Aaron and to his sons, according to all that I have commanded you. You shall consecrate them seven days.

kikuyu: ĩ niĩ Simoni Petero, ndungata na mũtũmwo wa Jesũ Kristũ, Ngũmwandĩkĩra marũa maya inyuĩ arĩa mũrĩkĩtie kwamũkĩra wĩtĩkio wa goro o ta ũrĩa tũrĩ naguo nĩ ũndũ wa ũthingu wa Jesũ Kristũ, ũrĩa arĩ we Ngai witũ na Mũtũhonokia:
english: imon Peter, a servant and apostle of Jesus Christ,† to those who have obtained a like precious faith with us in the righteousness of our God and Savior, Jesus Christ:

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo ndũmĩrĩri ya Jehova ĩkĩnginyĩra, ngĩĩrwo atĩrĩ:
english: Yahweh’s word came to me, saying,

kikuyu: (Hĩndĩ ĩno nĩ thuutha wa Mũthamaki Jekonia, na nyina wa mũthamaki, na anene a kwa mũthamaki, na atongoria a Juda na a Jerusalemu, na mabundi na atũũri gũkorwo matahĩtwo kuuma Jerusalemu.)
english: (after Jeconiah the king, the queen mother, the eunuchs, the princes of Judah and Jerusalem, the craftsmen, and the smiths had departed from Jerusalem),

kikuyu: Niĩ Jehova nĩ niĩ ngaatuĩka Ngai wacio, nake Daudi ndungata yakwa nĩagatuĩka mũthamaki gatagatĩ-inĩ ga cio. Niĩ Jehova nĩ niĩ njarĩtie ũhoro ũcio.
english: I, Yahweh, will be their God, and my servant David prince among them. I, Yahweh, have spoken it.

kikuyu: Anene a Firaũni makĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Nĩ nginya rĩ mũndũ ũyũ egũtũũra arĩ mũtego harĩ ithuĩ? Rekereria andũ aya mathiĩ, nĩgeetha makahooe Jehova Ngai wao. Ndũramenya atĩ bũrũri wa Misiri nĩwanangĩtwo?”
english: Pharaoh’s servants said to him, “How long will this man be a snare to us? Let the men go, that they may serve Yahweh, their God. Don’t you yet know that Egypt is destroyed?”

kikuyu: Gwake mũciĩ gũkoragwo na ũtonga na indo nyingĩ, naguo ũthingu wake ũtũũraga nginya tene.
english: Wealth and riches are in his house. His righteousness endures forever.

kikuyu: Nake Jehova akĩĩra Musa atĩrĩ:
english: Yahweh spoke to Moses, saying,

kikuyu: Wee nĩwe wekĩrire mũhaka ũrĩa matangĩkĩra; gũtirĩ hĩndĩ magaacooka kũhumbĩra thĩ.
english: You have set a boundary that they may not pass over, that they don’t turn again to cover the earth.

kikuyu: Ihinda-inĩ o rĩu Hazaeli mũthamaki wa Suriata akĩambata agĩthiĩ agĩtharĩkĩra Gathu na akĩrĩtaha. Ningĩ akĩhũndũka, agĩthiĩ gũtharĩkĩra Jerusalemu.
english: Then Hazael king of Syria went up and fought against Gath, and took it; and Hazael set his face to go up to Jerusalem.

kikuyu: Nĩngahũũnia athĩnjĩri-Ngai na bũthi, nao andũ akwa nĩmakaiganwo nĩ ũtugi wakwa,” ũguo nĩguo Jehova ekuuga.
english: I will satiate the soul of the priests with fatness, and my people will be satisfied with my goodness,” says Yahweh.

kikuyu: Ningĩ mũcooke mwambate nake, oke aikarĩre gĩtĩ gĩakwa kĩa ũnene, na athamake handũ hakwa. Nĩndĩmũthuurĩte aathanage Isiraeli na Juda.”
english: Then come up after him, and he shall come and sit on my throne; for he shall be king in my place. I have appointed him to be prince over Israel and over Judah.”

kikuyu: Andũ acio nĩmacaririe maandĩko ma nyũmba ciao, no matiigana kũmona, nĩ ũndũ ũcio makĩeherio thiritũ-inĩ ya athĩnjĩri-Ngai, magĩtuuo ta andũ maarĩ na thaahu.
english: These searched for their genealogical records, but couldn’t find them. Therefore they were deemed disqualified and removed from the priesthood.

kikuyu: Mũnene wa mbũtũ cia ita wa gatatũ, wa mweri wa ĩtatũ aarĩ Benaia mũrũ wa Jehoiada ũrĩa mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai. Nĩwe warĩ mũnene, na gĩkundi gĩake kĩarĩ na andũ 24,000.
english: The third captain of the army for the third month was Benaiah, the son of Jehoiada the chief priest. In his division were twenty-four thousand.

kikuyu: Wee Jehova, honokia na guoko gwaku kuuma kũrĩ andũ ta acio, ũũhonokie kuuma kũrĩ andũ a thĩ ĩno arĩa irĩhi rĩao rĩrĩ o muoyo-inĩ ũyũ tũrĩ. Ũniinagĩra andũ arĩa wendete ngʼaragu ũkamahũũnia; ciana ciao irĩ na cia kũigana, na nĩmaigagĩra ciana cia ciana ciao mũthiithũ.
english: from men by your hand, Yahweh, from men of the world, whose portion is in this life. You fill the belly of your cherished ones. Your sons have plenty, and they store up wealth for their children.

kikuyu: na Magidieli, na Iramu. Acio nĩo maarĩ anene a Edomu.
english: chief Magdiel, and chief Iram. These are the chiefs of Edom.

kikuyu: mũrũ wa Joanani, mũrũ wa Resa, mũrũ wa Zerubabeli, mũrũ wa Shelathieli, mũrũ wa Neri,
english: the son of Joanan, the son of Rhesa, the son of Zerubbabel, the son of Shealtiel, the son of Neri,

kikuyu: Nao Afilisti makĩũngana nĩguo marũe na andũ a Isiraeli, marĩ na ngaari cia ita ngiri ithatũ, na atwarithia a ngaari icio ngiri ithatũ, nacio thigari ciao ciarĩ nyingĩ ta mũthanga ũrĩa ũrĩ hũgũrũrũ-inĩ cia iria. Nao makĩambata magĩthiĩ makĩamba hema ciao kũu Mikimashi, mwena wa irathĩro wa Bethi- Aveni.
english: The Philistines assembled themselves together to fight with Israel: thirty thousand chariots, six thousand horsemen, and people as the sand which is on the seashore in multitude. They came up and encamped in Michmash, eastward of Beth Aven.

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio, mĩthiro yake nĩĩkamũkinyĩrĩra mũthenya o ro ũmwe: nayo nĩ gĩkuũ, na macakaya, o na ngʼaragu. Agaacinwo na mwaki,
english: Therefore in one day her plagues will come: death, mourning, and famine; and she will be utterly burned with fire, for the Lord God who has judged her is strong.

kikuyu: Nao makĩĩra andũ acio maatũmĩtwo atĩrĩ, “Ĩrai andũ acio a Jabeshi-Gileadi atĩrĩ, ‘rũciũ ihinda rĩrĩa riũa rĩgaakorwo rĩhiũhĩte, nĩguo mũkaahonokio.’ ” Rĩrĩa andũ acio maatũmĩtwo mathiire makĩheana ũhoro ũcio kũrĩ andũ a Jabeshi-rĩ, andũ acio makĩiyũrwo nĩ gĩkeno kĩnene.
english: They said to the messengers who came, “Tell the men of Jabesh Gilead, ‘Tomorrow, by the time the sun is hot, you will be rescued.’ ” The messengers came and told the men of Jabesh; and they were glad.

kikuyu: Musa na Harũni magĩthiĩ magĩtoonya Hema-inĩ-ya-Gũtũnganwo. Rĩrĩa moimire nja, makĩrathima andũ; naguo riiri wa Jehova ũkiumĩrĩra andũ othe.
english: Moses and Aaron went into the Tent of Meeting, and came out, and blessed the people; and Yahweh’s glory appeared to all the people.

kikuyu: Atĩrĩrĩ, Wee Ngai thikĩrĩria, na ũtũigue, ũhingũre maitho maku wone ihooru rĩa itũũra inene rĩrĩa rĩĩtanĩtio na Rĩĩtwa rĩaku. Tũtiragũthaitha nĩ ũndũ tũrĩ athingu, no nĩ ũndũ wa tha ciaku nyingĩ.
english: My God, turn your ear and hear. Open your eyes and see our desolations, and the city which is called by your name;

kikuyu: itugĩ iria igĩrĩ, na ciongo igĩrĩ iria ciahaanaga ta mbakũri irĩ igũrũ wa itugĩ icio; na irengeeri iria igĩrĩ ciagathĩkanĩtio ta mĩnyororo iria ciagemetie ciongo icio ciarĩ igũrũ wa itugĩ;
english: the two pillars, the bowls, the two capitals which were on the top of the pillars, the two networks to cover the two bowls of the capitals that were on the top of the pillars,

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo nĩguo maathondekire mũhianano watariĩ ta njaũ. Makĩũrutĩra magongona, na makĩgĩa na hĩndĩ ya gũkũngũĩra kĩrĩa maathondekete na moko mao.
english: They made a calf in those days, and brought a sacrifice to the idol, and rejoiced in the works of their hands.

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Pilato akiuma akĩmakora kũu nja akĩmooria atĩrĩ, “Mũndũ ũyũ mũramũthitangĩra kĩ?”
english: Pilate therefore went out to them and said, “What accusation do you bring against this man?”

kikuyu: Gũtirĩ mũndũ wendaga ciira wa kĩhooto, o na kana ageciirĩrĩra na njĩra ya ma. Mehokaga ngarari cia tũhũ na makaaria ndeto cia maheeni; magĩaga nda cia mathĩĩna na magaciara waganu.
english: No one sues in righteousness, and no one pleads in truth. They trust in vanity and speak lies. They conceive mischief and give birth to iniquity.

kikuyu: Rĩrĩa rĩothe mwatoonya nyũmba, ikarai kuo nginya rĩrĩa mũkoima itũũra rĩu.
english: He said to them, “Wherever you enter into a house, stay there until you depart from there.

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio, akĩĩra mũruti wa wĩra wa mũgũnda ũcio wa mĩthabibũ atĩrĩ, ‘Rĩu nĩ mĩaka ĩtatũ njũkaga gũcaria maciaro mũkũyũ-inĩ, ũyũ na ndirĩ ndoona ngũyũ o na ĩmwe. Ũteme! Ũũthũkagĩria mũgũnda nĩkĩ?’
english: He said to the vine dresser, ‘Behold, these three years I have come looking for fruit on this fig tree, and found none. Cut it down! Why does it waste the soil?’

kikuyu: nĩgũkorwo Ũcio ũrĩ Hinya nĩanjĩkĩire maũndũ manene, narĩo rĩĩtwa rĩake nĩ rĩtheru.
english: For he who is mighty has done great things for me. Holy is his name.

kikuyu: “Arĩria Alawii, ũmeere atĩrĩ: ‘Rĩrĩa mwamũkĩra icunjĩ cia ikũmi kuuma kũrĩ andũ a Isiraeli iria ndĩmũheete arĩ cio igai rĩanyu-rĩ, no nginya mũrehage gĩcunjĩ gĩa ikũmi gĩa gĩcunjĩ kĩu, kĩrĩ iruta kũrĩ Jehova.
english: “Moreover you shall speak to the Levites, and tell them, ‘When you take of the children of Israel the tithe which I have given you from them for your inheritance, then you shall offer up a wave offering of it for Yahweh, a tithe of the tithe.

kikuyu: Rĩĩtwa rĩa Jehova rĩrogoocwo, kuuma rĩu nginya tene na tene.
english: Blessed be Yahweh’s name, from this time forward and forever more.

kikuyu: Nao arutwo acio angĩ makĩmũrũmĩrĩra na marikabu maguucĩtie wabu ũiyũrĩte thamaki tondũ matiarĩ haraaya, maarĩ ta itĩĩna rĩa makinya igana rĩmwe kuuma hũgũrũrũ-inĩ.
english: But the other disciples came in the little boat (for they were not far from the land, but about two hundred cubits‡ away), dragging the net full of fish.

kikuyu: Ĩĩ, o na ningĩ nĩndabatithirie andũ a nyũmba ya Stefana; no tiga acio, ndingĩririkana kana nĩ kũrĩ mũndũ ũngĩ ndaabatithirie.
english: (I also baptized the household of Stephanas; besides them, I don’t know whether I baptized any other.)

kikuyu: Irio ici ciitũ cia rĩĩgu ciarĩ hiũ mũthenya ũrĩa tuoimire gwitũ tũke kũrĩ inyuĩ. No rĩu ta kĩonei ũrĩa ciũmĩte na ikagĩa mbuu.
english: This our bread we took hot for our supplies out of our houses on the day we went out to go to you; but now, behold, it is dry, and has become moldy.

kikuyu: Rĩrĩa ũgaakaya ũkĩenda ũteithio-rĩ, mĩhianano ĩyo ya kũhooywo ũnganĩtie-rĩ, nĩĩgagĩkũhonokia! Rũhuho nĩrũkamĩkuua yothe, yũmbũrwo, yeherio nĩ mĩhũmũ mĩtheri. No rĩrĩ, mũndũ ũrĩa ũnduuaga rĩũrĩro rĩake-rĩ, nĩwe ũkaagaya bũrũri, o na egwatĩre kĩrĩma gĩakwa kĩrĩa kĩamũre.” Kũhoorerio Andũ arĩa Maherete
english: When you cry, let those whom you have gathered deliver you, but the wind will take them. A breath will carry them all away, but he who takes refuge in me will possess the land, and will inherit my holy mountain.”

kikuyu: “ ‘Ngai ekuuga atĩrĩ, matukũ-inĩ ma kũrigĩrĩria nĩngaitĩrĩria andũ othe Roho wakwa. Ariũ na aarĩ anyu nĩmakarathaga mohoro, Aanake anyu nĩmakonaga cioneki, nao athuuri anyu marootage irooto.
english: ‘It will be in the last days, says God, that I will pour out my Spirit on all flesh. Your sons and your daughters will prophesy. Your young men will see visions. Your old men will dream dreams.

kikuyu: Ikaragai mũkenete hĩndĩ ciothe mũrĩ thĩinĩ wa Mwathani. O na nĩ nguuga o rĩngĩ: Ikaragai mũkenete!
english: Rejoice in the Lord always! Again I will say, “Rejoice!”

kikuyu: Mũthamaki wa Hamathu na kana mũthamaki wa Aripadi, kana mũthamaki wa itũũra inene rĩa Sefarivaimu kana wa Hena, o na kana wa Iva-rĩ, arĩ ha?” Ihooya rĩa Hezekia
english: Where is the king of Hamath, the king of Arpad, and the king of the city of Sepharvaim, of Hena, and Ivvah?’ ”

kikuyu: O ta ũrĩa kwandĩkĩtwo atĩrĩ, “Rĩĩtwa rĩa Ngai nĩrĩcambagio thĩinĩ wa andũ- a-Ndũrĩrĩ nĩ ũndũ wanyu.”
english: For “the name of God is blasphemed among the Gentiles because of you,”* just as it is written.

kikuyu: na cia Adini ciarĩ 454,
english: The children of Adin, four hundred fifty-four.

kikuyu: “No rĩrĩ, wee Bethilehemu Efiratha, o na harĩa arĩ we mũnini mũno mĩhĩrĩga-inĩ ya Juda, thĩinĩ waku nĩkuo gũkoima ũrĩa ũgaatuĩka mwathi wa Isiraeli, ũrĩa kĩhumo gĩake nĩ gĩa kuuma tene, kuuma mĩndĩ na mĩndĩ.”
english: But you, Bethlehem Ephrathah, being small among the clans of Judah, out of you one will come out to me who is to be ruler in Israel; whose goings out are from of old, from ancient times.

kikuyu: Niĩ ndungata yaku-rĩ, ngweterera nginya rĩ? Arĩa maanyariiraga ũkaamaherithia rĩ?
english: How many are the days of your servant? When will you execute judgment on those who persecute me?

kikuyu: Igongona rĩu ndĩrahaarĩria nĩ ũndũ wanyu, mũkaarĩa maguta nginya mwĩhũũnĩrĩrie, na mũnyue thakame nginya mũrĩĩo.
english: You shall eat fat until you are full, and drink blood until you are drunk, of my sacrifice which I have sacrificed for you.

kikuyu: Gĩtĩ kĩa ũnene gĩkaahaandwo na wendo; na mũndũ mwĩhokeku nĩwe ũgaagĩikarĩra, o mũndũ wa kuuma nyũmba ya Daudi, mũndũ ũrĩa ũgaatuanagĩra ciira arũmĩrĩire o kĩhooto, namo maũndũ ma ũthingu nĩmo akaahiũhaga gwĩka.
english: A throne will be established in loving kindness. One will sit on it in truth, in the tent of David, judging, seeking justice, and swift to do righteousness.

kikuyu: namo maruta ma Juda na Jerusalemu nĩmagetĩkĩrĩka nĩ Jehova, o ta matukũ marĩa mathirĩte, o na ta mĩaka ĩrĩa ya mbere.
english: Then the offering of Judah and Jerusalem will be pleasant to Yahweh as in the days of old and as in ancient years.

kikuyu: Nowe Saũlũ akiuga atĩrĩ, “Gũtirĩ mũndũ o na ũũ ũkũũragwo ũmũthĩ, nĩ ũndũ mũthenya ũyũ Jehova nĩahonoketie Isiraeli.”
english: Saul said, “No man shall be put to death today; for today Yahweh has rescued Israel.”

kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, mũthenya o ro ũcio arutwo eerĩ a Jesũ maarĩ rũgendo-inĩ magĩthiĩ itũũra rĩetagwo Emau, rĩarĩ ta kilomita ikũmi kuuma Jerusalemu.
