english: The caravans of Tema looked. The companies of Sheba waited for them.

kikuyu: Jesũ akĩmataarĩria ũhoro wothe ũrĩa waarĩtio Maandĩko-inĩ ũmũkoniĩ, ambĩrĩirie na maandĩko ma Musa na ma Anabii othe.
english: Beginning from Moses and from all the prophets, he explained to them in all the Scriptures the things concerning himself.

kikuyu: O tene nĩndarathire ũhoro wa maũndũ marĩa magooka, ngĩmaaria na kanua gakwa na ngĩtũma mamenyeke; na o ro rĩmwe ngĩmahingia, namo magĩĩkĩka.
english: I have declared the former things from of old. Yes, they went out of my mouth, and I revealed them. I did them suddenly, and they happened.

kikuyu: Ningĩ Mũfilisti ũcio akiuga atĩrĩ, “Ũmũthĩ nĩndoogita itungati cia Isiraeli! Heei mũndũ tũrũe nake.”
english: The Philistine said, “I defy the armies of Israel today! Give me a man, that we may fight together!”

kikuyu: waka-inĩ wa ĩna kuuma Jehoiakimu mũrũ wa Josia atuĩka mũthamaki wa Juda-rĩ, ndũmĩrĩri ĩno nĩyakinyĩire Jeremia yumĩte harĩ Jehova, akĩĩrwo ũũ:
english: n the fourth year of Jehoiakim the son of Josiah, king of Judah, this word came to Jeremiah from Yahweh, saying,

kikuyu: Ariũ na aarĩ a Ithe witũ, tũhooyagĩrei.
english: Brothers, pray for us.

kikuyu: Mwĩrĩgĩrĩro wa atĩ no ũmĩtoorie nĩ kwĩheenia; o kũmĩona tu nĩkũiganĩte gũtũma mũndũ amake, orwo nĩ hinya.
english: Behold, the hope of him is in vain. Won’t one be cast down even at the sight of him?

kikuyu: Kana mũngĩnengera mũndũ ũtangĩhota gũthoma ibuku rĩu rĩa gĩkũnjo, mũmwĩre atĩrĩ, “Twagũthaitha, tũthomere ibuku rĩĩrĩ,” nake akaamũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Niĩ ndiũĩ gũthoma.”
english: and the book is delivered to one who is not educated, saying, “Read this, please;” and he says, “I can’t read.”

kikuyu: a rĩrĩ, Jehoshafatu aarĩ na indo nyingĩ na agatĩĩka mũno, nake agĩtuma ũthoni na Ahabu.*
english: ow Jehoshaphat had riches and honor in abundance; and he allied himself with Ahab.

kikuyu: O na mangĩkehitha igũrũ rĩa kĩrĩma gĩa Karimeli, nĩngamaguĩma kuo ndĩmanyiite. O na mangĩkehitha mathiĩ rungu rwa iria-rĩ, o nakuo nĩngatha nyoka ĩmarũme.
english: Though they hide themselves in the top of Carmel, I will search and take them out from there; and though they be hidden from my sight in the bottom of the sea, there I will command the serpent, and it will bite them.

kikuyu: “Ũyũ nowe Musa ũrĩa werire andũ a Isiraeli atĩrĩ, ‘Ngai nĩakamũtũmĩra mũnabii ta niĩ kuuma kũrĩ andũ anyu kĩũmbe.’
english: This is that Moses who said to the children of Israel, ‘The Lord our God will raise up a prophet for you from among your brothers, like me.’‡*

kikuyu: Angĩkorwo, ti-itherũ, Iburahĩmu aatuirwo mũthingu na ũndũ wa ciĩko iria ekaga-rĩ, nĩarĩ na ũndũ wa kwĩrahĩra, no ti mbere ya Ngai.
english: For if Abraham was justified by works, he has something to boast about, but not toward God.

kikuyu: Nake Amazia mũthĩnjĩri-ngai wa Betheli agĩtũmana kũrĩ Jeroboamu, mũthamaki wa Isiraeli, akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Amosi nĩagũciirĩire agũũkĩrĩre arĩ o gũkũ thĩinĩ wa Isiraeli. Bũrũri ndũngĩhota gũkirĩrĩria ndeto ciake ciothe.
english: Then Amaziah the priest of Bethel sent to Jeroboam king of Israel, saying, “Amos has conspired against you in the middle of the house of Israel. The land is not able to bear all his words.

kikuyu: Nake Jesũ akĩmacookeria atĩrĩ, “Cookai mũkeere Johana maũndũ marĩa mwaigua na marĩa muona:
english: Jesus answered them, “Go and tell John the things which you hear and see:

kikuyu: Nake agĩcookia atĩrĩ, “Ingĩtaũkĩrwo atĩa, tiga hagĩire mũndũ wa kũndaarĩria?” Nĩ ũndũ ũcio akĩĩra Filipu ahaice ngaari ĩyo ya mbarathi, aikaranie nake.
english: He said, “How can I, unless someone explains it to me?” He begged Philip to come up and sit with him.

kikuyu: Nĩmegwatĩire bũrũri wake na makĩĩgwatĩra bũrũri wa Ogu mũthamaki wa Bashani, athamaki acio eerĩ a Aamori a kũu irathĩro rĩa Rũũĩ rwa Jorodani.
english: They took possession of his land and the land of Og king of Bashan, the two kings of the Amorites, who were beyond the Jordan toward the sunrise;

kikuyu: Musa akĩĩra Jehova atĩrĩ, “Andũ matingĩhaica Kĩrĩma gĩa Sinai tondũ we mwene nĩwatũkaanirie, ũgĩtwĩra atĩrĩ, ‘Ĩkĩra mũhaka ũthiũrũrũkĩrie kĩrĩma, na ũkĩamũre gĩtuĩke gĩtheru.’ ”
english: Moses said to Yahweh, “The people can’t come up to Mount Sinai, for you warned us, saying, ‘Set bounds around the mountain, and sanctify it.’ ”

kikuyu: “Nĩwoinangire icooki rĩaku rĩa ngingo o tene, na ũgĩtuuanga mĩhĩndo yaku; woigire atĩrĩ, ‘Ndigũcooka gũgũtungata!’ Tondũ-rĩ, tũrĩma-inĩ tũrĩa tuothe tũraihu na igũrũ, na rungu rwa mũtĩ o wothe mũruru nĩkuo wakomaga ũkĩhũũra ũmaraya.
english: “For long ago I broke off your yoke, and burst your bonds. You said, ‘I will not serve;’ for on every high hill and under every green tree you bowed yourself, playing the prostitute.

kikuyu: “Kũngĩkaagĩa ngʼaragu kana mũthiro bũrũri-inĩ, kana mĩgũnda ĩhĩe nĩ mbaa kana mbuu, kana gũũke ngigĩ kana ngũnga, kana thũ imarigiicĩrie itũũra-inĩ rĩmwe rĩao, na mwanangĩko o na ũrĩkũ kana mũrimũ ũngĩgooka-rĩ,
english: “If there is famine in the land, if there is pestilence, if there is blight, mildew, locust or caterpillar; if their enemy besieges them in the land of their cities, whatever plague, whatever sickness there is,

kikuyu: No Samũeli akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Ndigũcooka hamwe nawe. Nĩũregete kiugo kĩa Jehova, nake Jehova nĩakũregete ũtuĩke mũthamaki wa gũthamakĩra Isiraeli!”
english: Samuel said to Saul, “I will not return with you; for you have rejected Yahweh’s word, and Yahweh has rejected you from being king over Israel.”

kikuyu: nao Elihorefu na Ahija, ariũ a Shisha, nĩo maarĩ aandĩki-marũa; na Jehoshafatu mũrũ wa Ahiludu, ũrĩa waandĩkaga maũndũ ma ihinda rĩu;
english: Elihoreph and Ahijah, the sons of Shisha, scribes; Jehoshaphat the son of Ahilud, the recorder;

kikuyu: agĩtũma mũndũ athiĩ mbere yao: nĩwe Jusufu, ũrĩa wendirio arĩ ngombo.
english: He sent a man before them. Joseph was sold for a slave.

kikuyu: No ithuĩ-rĩ, twaragia ũhoro wa ũũgĩ wa Ngai ũrĩa wa hitho, ũũgĩ ũrĩa watũire ũrĩ mũhithe, o ũrĩa Ngai aathĩrĩire o mbere ya kũũmba thĩ nĩ ũndũ wa riiri witũ.
english: But we speak God’s wisdom in a mystery, the wisdom that has been hidden, which God foreordained before the worlds for our glory,

kikuyu: Nake mũthamaki agĩtua ciira atĩrĩ, “Nengerai mũndũ-wa-nja ũyũ wa mbere mwana ũyũ ũrĩ muoyo. Mũtikamũũrage; nĩwe nyina.”
english: Then the king answered, “Give the first woman the living child, and definitely do not kill him. She is his mother.”

kikuyu: “Juda, ariũ a thoguo nĩmagagũkumia; nakuo guoko gwaku nĩgũgatooria thũ ciaku; ariũ a thoguo nĩmagakũinamĩrĩra.
english: “Judah, your brothers will praise you. Your hand will be on the neck of your enemies. Your father’s sons will bow down before you.

kikuyu: Rĩrĩa maakinyire, makĩambata nyũmba ya igũrũ ĩrĩa maikaraga. Nao andũ arĩa maarĩ ho maarĩ Petero, na Johana, na Jakubu na Anderea; Filipu na Toma, na Baritholomayo na Mathayo; Jakubu mũrũ wa Alufayo, na Simoni Mũzelote, na Judasi mũrũ wa Jakubu.
english: When they had come in, they went up into the upper room where they were staying, that is Peter, John, James, Andrew, Philip, Thomas, Bartholomew, Matthew, James the son of Alphaeus, Simon the Zealot, and Judas the son of James.

kikuyu: na Baala, na Iimu, na Ezemu,
english: Baalah, Iim, Ezem,

kikuyu: marĩa maandĩkĩtwo ũũ: “Ũhoro nĩwarĩtio ndũrĩrĩ-inĩ, na Geshemu aroiga ũhoro ũcio nĩ wa ma, atĩ wee na Ayahudi nĩmũrathugunda kũremera mũthamaki mũmũũkĩrĩre, na nĩ ũndũ ũcio nĩmũraaka rũthingo. Ningĩ kũringana na ũhoro ũcio nĩũrenda gũtuĩka mũthamaki wao
english: in which was written, “It is reported among the nations, and Gashmu says it, that you and the Jews intend to rebel. Because of that, you are building the wall. You would be their king, according to these words.

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ niĩ na andũ akwa nĩtwendetio tũniinwo, na tũũragwo na tũhukio. Atĩrĩrĩ, korwo no kwendio twendetio tũgatuĩke ngombo cia arũme, na cia andũ-a-nja, niĩ nĩingĩrakirire tondũ thĩĩna ta ũcio ti mũiganu wa gũthĩĩnia mũthamaki.”
english: For we are sold, I and my people, to be destroyed, to be slain, and to perish. But if we had been sold for male and female slaves, I would have held my peace, although the adversary could not have compensated for the king’s loss.”

kikuyu: Mwathoma ũhoro ũyũ, nĩmũkũhota kũmenya ũrĩa ndaakuũkĩirwo nĩ ũhoro ũcio wa hitho wa Kristũ,
english: by which, when you read, you can perceive my understanding in the mystery of Christ,

kikuyu: na Bethi-Peoru, na iharũrũka cia Pisiga, na Bethi-Jeshimothu,
english: Beth Peor, the slopes of Pisgah, Beth Jeshimoth,

kikuyu: ũguo nĩguo niĩ ngaĩirwo mĩeri ya tũhũ, na ngatuĩrwo atĩ ũtukũ ndaarage ndĩ na kĩeha.
english: so I am made to possess months of misery, wearisome nights are appointed to me.

kikuyu: Ũcio nĩwe mũndũ ũrĩa ũgaatũũra kũndũ kũrĩa gũtũũgĩru, ũrĩa kĩĩhitho gĩake gĩgaakorwo kĩrĩ kĩirigo kĩrũmu kũu kĩrĩma-inĩ. Nĩakaheagwo irio ciake, na maaĩ make gũtirĩ hĩndĩ makaaga.
english: he will dwell on high. His place of defense will be the fortress of rocks. His bread will be supplied. His waters will be sure.

kikuyu: Narĩo riiko rĩu rĩa kĩgongona rĩiganaine mĩena yothe ĩna, rĩrĩ na ũraihu wa mĩkono ikũmi na ĩĩrĩ,† na wariĩ wa mĩkono ikũmi na ĩĩrĩ.
english: The altar hearth shall be twelve cubits long by twelve wide, square in its four sides.

kikuyu: Mũndũ o wothe ũngĩrĩa nyama cia kĩimba kĩu no nginya athambie nguo ciake, nake agaatinda arĩ na thaahu o nginya hwaĩ-inĩ. Mũndũ o wothe ũngĩoya kĩimba kĩu no nginya athambie nguo ciake, nake atiinde arĩ na thaahu o nginya hwaĩ-inĩ.
english: He who eats of its carcass shall wash his clothes, and be unclean until the evening. He also who carries its carcass shall wash his clothes, and be unclean until the evening.

kikuyu: Na makĩria ma ũguo-rĩ, tondũ mationire bata wa gũtũũra mamenyete Ngai, nake akĩmarekereria magĩe na meciiria ma ũmaramari, mekage maũndũ marĩa mataagĩrĩirwo nĩ gwĩkwo.
english: Even as they refused to have God in their knowledge, God gave them up to a reprobate mind, to do those things which are not fitting;

kikuyu: Nĩagianire na mũraika, na akĩmũtooria; akĩrĩra na akĩmũthaitha amũrathime. Aamũkorire Betheli na makĩaranĩria nake kũu:
english: Indeed, he struggled with the angel, and prevailed; he wept, and made supplication to him. He found him at Bethel, and there he spoke with us—

kikuyu: Tondũ nĩmathũũrire ũmenyo, na matiigana gũthuura gwĩtigĩra Jehova,
english: because they hated knowledge, and didn’t choose the fear of Yahweh.

kikuyu: Oyaga athĩĩni na igũrũ kuuma rũkũngũ-inĩ, na agokĩria arĩa abatari, akamaruta kĩhumbu-inĩ kĩa mũhu;
english: He raises up the poor out of the dust, and lifts up the needy from the ash heap,

kikuyu: Mwĩmenyererei mũndũ o na ũrĩkũ ndakanamũtahe meciiria na maũũgĩ ma maheeni ma tũhũ, marĩa moimanaga na mĩtugo ya andũ na irĩra iria cia ndũire cia thĩ ĩno, no ti kuuma kũrĩ Kristũ.
english: Be careful that you don’t let anyone rob you through his philosophy and vain deceit, after the tradition of men, after the elemental spirits of the world, and not after Christ.

kikuyu: Ariũ a Hebironi maarĩ: Kora, na Tapua, na Rekemu, na Shema.
english: The sons of Hebron: Korah, Tappuah, Rekem, and Shema.

kikuyu: na gategwa kamwe, na ndũrũme ĩmwe, na gatũrũme kamwe ka mwaka ũmwe, Nĩ ũndũ wa iruta rĩa njino;
english: one young bull, one ram, one male lamb a year old, for a burnt offering;

kikuyu: Ta mwĩciirie ũrĩa aarĩ mũnene: Atĩ o na ithe witũ Iburahĩmu nĩamũgaĩire gĩcunjĩ gĩa ikũmi kĩa indo iria aatahĩte!
english: Now consider how great this man was, to whom even Abraham the patriarch gave a tenth out of the best plunder.

kikuyu: Musa akĩiga metha thĩinĩ wa Hema-ya-Gũtũnganwo mwena wa gathigathini wa hema ĩyo nyamũre, nja ya gĩtambaya gĩa gũcuurio
english: He put the table in the Tent of Meeting, on the north side of the tabernacle, outside of the veil.

kikuyu: a rĩrĩ, Samaria kwarĩ na ariũ a nyũmba ya Ahabu mĩrongo mũgwanja. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Jehu akĩandĩka marũa akĩmatũma Samaria kũrĩ anene a Jezireeli, na athuuri, na areri a ciana cia Ahabu, akĩmeera atĩrĩ,
english: ow Ahab had seventy sons in Samaria. Jehu wrote letters and sent them to Samaria, to the rulers of Jezreel, even the elders, and to those who brought up Ahab’s sons, saying,

kikuyu: Nao rĩrĩa magaakorwo magĩtuĩkanĩria bũrũri ũcio, ũmwe wao angĩkoona ihĩndĩ rĩa mũndũ, nĩagekĩra rũũri hakuhĩ narĩo o nginya rĩrĩa athikani makaarĩthika kũu Gĩtuamba-inĩ kĩa Hamoni-Gogu.
english: Those who search through the land will pass through; and when anyone sees a man’s bone, then he will set up a sign by it, until the undertakers have buried it in the valley of Hamon Gog.

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio marakara ma Jehova makĩrĩrĩmbũkĩra bũrũri ũyũ, akĩũkinyĩria irumi iria ciothe ciandĩkĩtwo ibuku-inĩ rĩĩrĩ.
english: Therefore Yahweh’s anger burned against this land, to bring on it all the curses that are written in this book.

kikuyu: Ndikũmwarĩria ũhoro ũyũ ihinda iraaya, nĩgũkorwo mũnene wa gũkũ thĩ nĩarooka. We ndarĩ na hinya wa kũnjatha,
english: I will no more speak much with you, for the prince of the world comes, and he has nothing in me.

kikuyu: No Labani akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Angĩkorwo nĩnjĩtĩkĩrĩkĩte maitho-inĩ maku-rĩ, ndagũthaitha ikaranga. Nĩmenyete na ũndũ wa ũragũri atĩ Jehova nĩandathimĩte nĩ ũndũ waku.”
english: Laban said to him, “If now I have found favor in your eyes, stay here, for I have divined that Yahweh has blessed me for your sake.”

kikuyu: Mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai ũrĩa mũitĩrĩrie maguta na akaamũrwo gũcooka ithenya rĩa ithe ta mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai ũrĩa mũnene nĩwe ũkaahoroheria andũ. Nĩakehumbaga nguo iria nyamũre cia gatani
english: The priest, who is anointed and who is consecrated to be priest in his father’s place, shall make the atonement, and shall put on the linen garments, even the holy garments.

kikuyu: Jehova akĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Niĩ nĩngũkorwo ndĩ hamwe nawe, na nĩũkũhũũra Amidiani ũmaniine othe me hamwe.”
english: Yahweh said to him, “Surely I will be with you, and you shall strike the Midianites as one man.”

kikuyu: maanahinyĩrĩria mũno kuuma ũnini-inĩ wakwa, no rĩrĩ, matirĩ maahota kũndooria.
english: many times they have afflicted me from my youth up, yet they have not prevailed against me.

kikuyu: Ũragai tũtegwa twakuo tuothe, tũikũrũkiei tũgathĩnjwo! Kaĩ marĩ na haaro-ĩ! Nĩgũkorwo mũthenya watuo nĩmũkinyu, ihinda rĩatuo rĩa kũherithio.
english: Kill all her bulls. Let them go down to the slaughter. Woe to them! For their day has come, the time of their visitation.

kikuyu: meeohete mĩcibi njohero na iremba mĩtwe icunjuurĩte; othe monekaga mahaana ta atwarithia a ngaari cia mbarathi cia ita a Babuloni, andũ a gũciarĩrwo Kalidei.
english: dressed with belts on their waists, with flowing turbans on their heads, all of them looking like princes, after the likeness of the Babylonians in Chaldea, the land of their birth.

kikuyu: Wee Efiraimu, ndĩ na ũhoro ũngĩ ũrĩkũ na mĩhianano ya kũhooywo? Nĩngamũcookeria ũhoro na ndĩmũmenyerere. Haana ta mũkarakaba mũruru; gũciara gwaku kuumaga harĩ niĩ.”
english: Ephraim, what have I to do any more with idols? I answer, and will take care of him. I am like a green cypress tree; from me your fruit is found.”

kikuyu: Ha ũhoro wa maũndũ marĩa mangĩ makoniĩ wathani wa Jehoashu, na ũrĩa wothe eekire, na maũndũ marĩa aahingirie, o na mbaara yake na Amazia mũthamaki wa Juda-rĩ, githĩ matiandĩkĩtwo ibuku-inĩ rĩa mahinda ma Athamaki a Isiraeli?
english: Now the rest of the acts of Joash, and all that he did, and his might with which he fought against Amaziah king of Judah, aren’t they written in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Israel?

kikuyu: “Rehe Harũni na ariũ ake, na nguo ciao, na maguta ma gũitanĩrĩrio, na ndegwa ya iruta rĩa kũhoroherio mehia, na ndũrũme igĩrĩ, na gĩkabũ kĩa mĩgate ĩrugĩtwo ĩtarĩ na ndawa ya kũimbia,
english: “Take Aaron and his sons with him, and the garments, and the anointing oil, and the bull of the sin offering, and the two rams, and the basket of unleavened bread;

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo ngĩkumbũrĩra mehia makwa, na ndiahithire waganu wakwa, ndoigire atĩrĩ, “Nĩngumbũra mahĩtia makwa kũrĩ Jehova,” nawe ũkĩndekera ũũru wa mehia makwa.
english: I acknowledged my sin to you. I didn’t hide my iniquity. I said, I will confess my transgressions to Yahweh, and you forgave the iniquity of my sin. Selah.

kikuyu: Enoku aakinyia mĩaka mĩrongo ĩtandatũ na ĩtano-rĩ, akĩgĩa na mũriũ wetagwo Methusela.
english: Enoch lived sixty-five years, then became the father of Methuselah.

kikuyu: na andũ arĩa angĩ maacooketio nĩ mũgaathe ũrĩa mũnene Osinapari nĩgeetha matũũre itũũra inene rĩa Samaria, na kũrĩa kũngĩ guothe Mũrĩmo-wa-Farati.
english: and the rest of the nations whom the great and noble Osnappar brought over and settled in the city of Samaria, and in the rest of the country beyond the River, and so forth, wrote.

kikuyu: Ti-itherũ, mũndũ o wothe ũrĩa ũkwenda gũtũũra mũtũũrĩre wa gũkenia Ngai arĩ thĩinĩ wa Kristũ Jesũ nĩarĩnyariiragwo,
english: Yes, and all who desire to live godly in Christ Jesus will suffer persecution.

kikuyu: O na ningĩ, Jehova Ngai wanyu nĩagatũma magi ma mboora gatagatĩ ga cio, nginya o na matigari ma cio marĩa makaamwĩhitha makorwo mathirĩte.
english: Moreover Yahweh your God will send the hornet among them, until those who are left, and hide themselves, perish from before you.

kikuyu: Rĩrĩa ndaarĩ mwana nyũmba-inĩ ya baba, ndĩ o mũndũ mwĩthĩ, na ndĩ mwana wa mũmwe wa maitũ-rĩ,
english: For I was a son to my father, tender and an only child in the sight of my mother.

kikuyu: ndĩgaatithia kana ĩkue ngoro nginya hĩndĩ ĩrĩa ĩgaatũma gũkũ thĩ kũgĩe ciira wa kĩhooto. Andũ arĩa matũũraga icigĩrĩra-inĩ makehoka watho wayo.”
english: He will not fail nor be discouraged, until he has set justice in the earth, and the islands wait for his law.”

kikuyu: Wee Juda-rĩ, ũrĩ na ngai nyingĩ o ta ũrĩa matũũra maku maigana, na igongona iria ũhaandĩte cia gũcinĩra ngai ĩyo ya thoni ĩtagwo Baali ũbumba nĩ nyingĩ o ta barabara cia Jerusalemu.’
english: For according to the number of your cities are your gods, Judah; and according to the number of the streets of Jerusalem you have set up altars to the shameful thing, even altars to burn incense to Baal.’

kikuyu: O ta ũrĩa maaĩ mahũũaga kũu iria-inĩ, na ta ũrĩa mũtaro wa rũũĩ ũngʼaraga ũkooma-rĩ,
english: As the waters fail from the sea, and the river wastes and dries up,

kikuyu: Rĩrĩa moimire harĩ Firaũni-rĩ, magĩkora Musa na Harũni arĩa maametereire,
english: They met Moses and Aaron, who stood along the way, as they came out from Pharaoh.

kikuyu: “ ‘Aarĩkia gũtiga kuura, nĩageterera mĩthenya mũgwanja, na thuutha ũcio nĩagathirwo nĩ thaahu.
english: “ ‘But if she is cleansed of her discharge, then she shall count to herself seven days, and after that she shall be clean.

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa tũgũtuĩrwo ciira nĩ Mwathani-rĩ, nĩkũherithio tũherithagio nĩgeetha tũtikae gũtuĩrwo ciira hamwe na thĩ.
english: But when we are judged, we are disciplined by the Lord, that we may not be condemned with the world.

kikuyu: Mũthamaki Ahazu akĩeheria icunjĩ cia mwena-inĩ, na akĩeheria iraĩ kuuma makaari-inĩ macio. Akĩeheria itangi rĩrĩa rĩetagwo Karia igũrũ rĩa mĩhianano ya ndegwa cia gĩcango iria ciakanyiitĩrĩire, na agĩkaigĩrĩra itina-inĩ cia mahiga.
english: King Ahaz cut off the panels of the bases, and removed the basin from off them, and took down the sea from off the bronze oxen that were under it, and put it on a pavement of stone.

kikuyu: Nake Ahabu akĩĩra Nabothu atĩrĩ, “He mũgũnda waku wa mĩthabibũ haandage mboga, tondũ ũrĩ hakuhĩ na nyũmba yakwa ya ũthamaki. Nĩngũgũkũũranĩria na mũgũnda ũngĩ wa mĩthabibũ mwega gũkĩra ũyũ, kana ũngĩona arĩ wega, no ngũhe mbeeca ciiganĩte thogora waguo.”
english: Ahab spoke to Naboth, saying, “Give me your vineyard, that I may have it for a garden of herbs, because it is near my house; and I will give you for it a better vineyard than it. Or, if it seems good to you, I will give you its worth in money.”

kikuyu: Rĩrĩa Isiraeli aarĩ kaana-rĩ, nĩndamwendete, ngĩgĩĩta mũrũ ũcio wakwa oime bũrũri wa Misiri.
english: When Israel was a child, then I loved him, and called my son out of Egypt.

kikuyu: Na nĩngatũũrania na andũ a Isiraeli, na ndigatiganĩria andũ akwa a Isiraeli.”
english: I will dwell among the children of Israel, and will not forsake my people Israel.”

kikuyu: Wamũrũithirie na mbaara na gũtahwo: amũingataga na rũhuho rwake rwa hinya, ta mũthenya ũrĩa rũhuho rwa mwena wa irathĩro rũhurutanaga.
english: In measure, when you send them away, you contend with them. He has removed them with his rough blast in the day of the east wind.

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio, Jesũ akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Ihinda rĩakwa rĩrĩa rĩagĩrĩire rĩtirĩ rĩrakinya; no harĩ inyuĩ ihinda o rĩothe nĩrĩagĩrĩire.
english: Jesus therefore said to them, “My time has not yet come, but your time is always ready.

kikuyu: Nake Shaitani angĩĩyũkĩrĩra we mwene na aamũkane-rĩ, ndangĩtũũra; nĩ gũthira egũthira.
english: If Satan has risen up against himself, and is divided, he can’t stand, but has an end.

kikuyu: Jehova ekuuga atĩrĩ, “Andũ arĩa aaganu matikagĩa na thayũ o na rĩ.”
english: “There is no peace”, says Yahweh, “for the wicked.”

kikuyu: Hakĩrĩ ũguo-rĩ, nĩtũkuhĩrĩriei gĩtĩ kĩa Ũnene kĩrĩa kĩa Wega wake tũrĩ na ũũmĩrĩru, nĩgeetha tũkaiguĩrwo tha, na tuone Wega wake wa gũtũteithagia hĩndĩ ĩrĩa tũbatarĩte.
english: Let’s therefore draw near with boldness to the throne of grace, that we may receive mercy and may find grace for help in time of need.

kikuyu: lihu agĩthiĩ na mbere kwaria, akiuga atĩrĩ:
english: lihu also continued, and said,

kikuyu: na Alameleki, na Amadi, na Mishali. Mwena wa ithũĩro mũhaka wahutĩtie Karimeli na Shihoru-Libunathu.
english: Allammelech, Amad, Mishal. It reached to Carmel westward, and to Shihorlibnath.

kikuyu: Akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “No ũthiĩ.” Akĩmwĩtĩkĩria athiĩ mĩeri ĩĩrĩ. We marĩ na airĩtu acio angĩ magĩthiĩ irĩma-inĩ, makĩrĩranĩra tondũ ndarĩ hĩndĩ angĩkaahika.
english: He said, “Go.” He sent her away for two months; and she departed, she and her companions, and mourned her virginity on the mountains.

kikuyu: Kuuma kũrĩ andũ a Hebironi: maarĩ Hashabia na andũ a nyũmba yao, andũ ehoti 1,700; nĩo maarũgamĩrĩire wĩra wa Jehova wothe kũu Isiraeli mwena wa ithũĩro wa Jorodani, o na wĩra wa gũtungatĩra mũthamaki.
english: Of the Hebronites, Hashabiah and his brothers, one thousand seven hundred men of valor, had the oversight of Israel beyond the Jordan westward, for all the business of Yahweh and for the service of the king.

kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa ũngĩhutia kĩndũ o na kĩrĩkũ ta kĩu nĩakanyiitwo nĩ thaahu o nginya hwaĩ-inĩ. Ndakanarĩe kĩndũ o na kĩrĩkũ kĩa maruta marĩa maamũre, no akorirwo ethambĩte mwĩrĩ na maaĩ.
english: the person that touches any such shall be unclean until the evening, and shall not eat of the holy things unless he bathes his body in water.

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Musa na Harũni magĩthiĩ kũrĩ Firaũni makĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Jehova Ngai wa Ahibirania ekuuga ũũ: ‘Ũgũikara nginya rĩ ũregete kũnjĩnyiihĩria? Rekereria andũ akwa mathiĩ, nĩgeetha makandungatĩre.
english: Moses and Aaron went in to Pharaoh, and said to him, “This is what Yahweh, the God of the Hebrews, says: ‘How long will you refuse to humble yourself before me? Let my people go, that they may serve me.

kikuyu: na nĩho mũrĩtwaraga maruta manyu ma njino na magongona manyu, na icunjĩ cianyu cia ikũmi na maruta ma mwanya, na kĩrĩa gĩothe mũrĩrutaga kĩa mĩĩhĩtwa yanyu, o na maruta manyu ma kwĩyendera, na marigithathi ma ndũũru cianyu cia ngʼombe na cia ngʼondu.
english: You shall bring your burnt offerings, your sacrifices, your tithes, the wave offering of your hand, your vows, your free will offerings, and the firstborn of your herd and of your flock there.

kikuyu: Nĩũtũmĩte tũtuĩke a kuunagwo thimo nĩ ndũrĩrĩ;
english: You make us a byword among the nations, a shaking of the head among the peoples.

kikuyu: Rĩu maturuuraga njĩra-inĩ ta andũ atumumu. Mathaahĩtio nĩ thakame ũũ atĩ gũtirĩ mũndũ o na ũmwe ũngĩgeria kũhutia nguo ciao.
english: They wander as blind men in the streets. They are polluted with blood, So that men can’t touch their garments.

kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, gwatua gũtuka nĩethambe, na riũa rĩgĩthũa no acooke kambĩ-inĩ.
english: but it shall be, when evening comes, he shall bathe himself in water. When the sun is down, he shall come within the camp.

kikuyu: Akĩnjĩĩra atĩrĩ, “Nĩngũkwĩra ũrĩa gũkaahaana thuutha-inĩ hĩndĩ ya mangʼũrĩ, tondũ rĩrĩ, kĩoneki gĩkĩ gĩkoniĩ mahinda marĩa maathĩrĩirio ma ithirĩro.
english: He said, “Behold, I will make you know what will be in the latter time of the indignation, for it belongs to the appointed time of the end.

kikuyu: Nĩnyonete mũndũ mwaganu na mũndũ ũtarĩ tha agaacĩire ta mũtĩ mũruru ũrĩ o imerero-inĩ rĩaguo,
english: Wait for Yahweh, and keep his way, and he will exalt you to inherit the land. When the wicked are cut off, you shall see it. spreading himself like a green tree in its native soil.

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Rakeli agĩkua na agĩthikwo mũkĩra-inĩ wa njĩra ya gũthiĩ Efiratha (na nokuo Bethilehemu.)
english: Rachel died, and was buried on the way to Ephrath (also called Bethlehem).

kikuyu: No ũrĩa wothe ũngĩhĩtithia kamwe ga twana tũtũ tũnjĩtĩkĩtie keehie, nĩ kaba mũndũ ũcio aharũkio ihiga rĩa gĩthĩi ngingo aikio iria-inĩ kũrĩa kũriku.
english: but whoever causes one of these little ones who believe in me to stumble, it would be better for him if a huge millstone were hung around his neck and that he were sunk in the depths of the sea.

kikuyu: na akĩũraga athamaki maarĩ ngumo: Wendo wake nĩ wa tene na tene.
english: and killed mighty kings, for his loving kindness endures forever;

kikuyu: Nĩndarorire, ngĩona magũrũ mana ma ngaari hau mĩena-inĩ ya akerubi acio, o kũmwe gũkagĩa mwena-inĩ wa o ũmwe wa akerubi acio; magũrũ macio maahenagia ta rangi wa thumarati.
english: I looked, and behold, there were four wheels beside the cherubim, one wheel beside one cherub, and another wheel beside another cherub. The appearance of the wheels was like a beryl stone.

kikuyu: Akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Ũũ nĩguo Jehova Ngai wa Isiraeli o ũcio mwandekirie kũrĩ we ndĩmũtwarĩre mathaithana manyu, ekuuga:
english: and said to them, “Yahweh, the God of Israel, to whom you sent me to present your supplication before him, says:

kikuyu: Solomoni agĩcooka agĩthiĩ Hamathu-Zoba na akĩrĩtaha.
english: Solomon went to Hamath Zobah, and prevailed against it.

kikuyu: Ngũmwĩra atĩrĩ na ma, ũrĩa wothe wamũkagĩra mũndũ ũrĩa ndũmĩte nĩ niĩ mwene aamũkagĩra; na ũrĩa wothe ũnyamũkagĩra, aamũkagĩra ũrĩa wandũmire.”
english: Most certainly I tell you, he who receives whomever I send, receives me; and he who receives me, receives him who sent me.”

kikuyu: “Wee Jehova, ririkana ũrĩa ngoretwo ngĩthiĩ na mĩthiĩre ya kwĩhokeka mbere yaku, na ngeheana na ngoro yakwa yothe, na ngeeka ũrĩa kwagĩrĩire maitho-inĩ maku.” Nake Hezekia akĩrĩra mũno.
english: and said, “Remember now, Yahweh, I beg you, how I have walked before you in truth and with a perfect heart, and have done that which is good in your sight.” Then Hezekiah wept bitterly.

kikuyu: No tuonaga Jesũ, ũrĩa gwa kahinda kanini watuĩtwo mũnini gũkĩra araika, no rĩu ekĩrĩtwo thũmbĩ ya riiri na ya gĩtĩĩo nĩ tondũ nĩanyariirĩkire na agĩkua, nĩgeetha, nĩ ũndũ wa Wega wa Ngai atuĩke wa gũcama gĩkuũ handũ ha andũ othe.
english: But we see him who has been made a little lower than the angels, Jesus, because of the suffering of death crowned with glory and honor, that by the grace of God he should taste of death for everyone.

kikuyu: akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Thiĩi itũũra rĩĩrĩa rĩrĩ mbere yanyu, na mwatoonya o ũguo, nĩmũkuona njaũ ya ndigiri yohetwo hau, nayo ndĩrĩ yakuua mũndũ. Mĩohorei mũmĩrehe.
english: and said to them, “Go your way into the village that is opposite you. Immediately as you enter into it, you will find a young donkey tied, on which no one has sat. Untie him and bring him.

kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa ũngĩonania kũnyarara mũtuithania ciira kana mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai, ũrĩa ũrũgamaga agĩtungatĩra Jehova Ngai wanyu, no nginya ooragwo. No nginya mũniine ũũru ũcio wehere Isiraeli.
english: The man who does presumptuously in not listening to the priest who stands to minister there before Yahweh your God, or to the judge, even that man shall die. You shall put away the evil from Israel.

kikuyu: Aini maarĩ watho-inĩ wa mũthamaki, ũrĩa wamatuagĩra wĩra wao wa o mũthenya.
english: For there was a commandment from the king concerning them, and a settled provision for the singers, as every day required.

kikuyu: No nginya ũmũthĩ Jehova ndamũheete meciiria marĩ na ũmenyo, kana maitho me kuona, kana matũ me kũigua.
english: But Yahweh has not given you a heart to know, eyes to see, and ears to hear, to this day.

kikuyu: ake Abija akĩhurũka hamwe na maithe make na agĩthikwo kũu Itũũra-inĩ Inene rĩa Daudi. Nake mũriũ Asa agĩtuĩka mũthamaki ithenya rĩake, na matukũ-inĩ make bũrũri ũgĩikara na thayũ mĩaka ikũmi.
english: o Abijah slept with his fathers, and they buried him in David’s city; and Asa his son reigned in his place. In his days, the land was quiet ten years.

kikuyu: Makanitha ma bũrũri wa Asia nĩmamũgeithia. Akula na Pirisila nĩmamũgeithia mũno thĩinĩ wa Mwathani, o na kanitha ũrĩa ũcemanagia kwao nyũmba nĩwamũgeithia.
english: The assemblies of Asia greet you. Aquila and Priscilla greet you warmly in the Lord, together with the assembly that is in their house.

kikuyu: Mũkĩhaanda mwaka-inĩ wa kanana, mũkaarĩĩaga irio iria mwaigĩte mũthiithũ, na mũgaacirĩa nginya mũgethe maciaro ma mwaka wa kenda.
english: You shall sow the eighth year, and eat of the fruits from the old store until the ninth year. Until its fruits come in, you shall eat the old store.

kikuyu: ĩrĩa ihinda rĩa Daudi rĩakuhĩrĩirie rĩa gũkua-rĩ, agĩatha mũriũ Solomoni, akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ,
english: ow the days of David came near that he should die; and he commanded Solomon his son, saying,

kikuyu: Andũ arĩa maarĩ njamba maarĩ: Asaheli mũrũ wa nyina na Joabu, na Elihanani mũrũ wa Dodo wa kuuma Bethilehemu,
english: The mighty men of the armies also include Asahel the brother of Joab, Elhanan the son of Dodo of Bethlehem,

kikuyu: gĩthaka kĩrĩa Iburahĩmu aagũrĩte kuuma kũrĩ Ahiti. Hau nĩho Iburahĩmu aathikirwo hamwe na Sara mũtumia wake.
english: the field which Abraham purchased from the children of Heth. Abraham was buried there with Sarah, his wife.

kikuyu: Wee Ngai amũheaga Roho wake na akaringaga ciama gatagatĩ kanyu nĩ ũndũ wanyu gwathĩkĩra watho, kana nĩ ũndũ wa gwĩtĩkia ũhoro ũrĩa mwaiguire?
english: He therefore who supplies the Spirit to you and does miracles among you, does he do it by the works of the law, or by hearing of faith?

kikuyu: Rekei tũmwenderie Aishumaeli aya, no tũtikamwĩke ũũru na moko maitũ, tondũ ũyũ nĩ mũrũ wa ithe witũ, tũrĩ a mũthiimo ũmwe na thakame o ĩmwe.” Nao ariũ a ithe magĩtĩkĩra.
english: Come, and let’s sell him to the Ishmaelites, and not let our hand be on him; for he is our brother, our flesh.” His brothers listened to him.

kikuyu: Rĩrĩa Joshua aiguire inegene rĩa andũ makiugĩrĩria akĩĩra Musa atĩrĩ, “Kũrĩ mbugĩrĩrio ya mbaara kambĩ-inĩ.”
english: When Joshua heard the noise of the people as they shouted, he said to Moses, “There is the noise of war in the camp.”

kikuyu: Nĩaatemeire marakara make njĩra; ndamahonokirie kũrĩ gĩkuũ, no nĩamarekereirie kũrĩ mũthiro.
english: He made a path for his anger. He didn’t spare their soul from death, but gave their life over to the pestilence,

kikuyu: Nao makaarũgama haraaya nĩ ũndũ wa gwĩtigĩra kĩnyariirĩko gĩake, nao marĩre, makiugaga atĩrĩ: “ ‘Ĩiya-ĩ! Ĩiya-ĩ, wee itũũra inene, wee Babuloni, itũũra rĩa hinya! Ituĩro rĩaku rĩrĩ gũkinya ithaa-inĩ o rĩmwe!’
english: standing far away for the fear of her torment, saying, ‘Woe, woe, the great city, Babylon, the strong city! For your judgment has come in one hour.’

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Saũlũ agĩĩta andũ, akĩmacookanĩrĩria kũu Telaimu; nao maarĩ thigari cia magũrũ ngiri magana meerĩ na andũ ngiri ikũmi kuuma Juda.
english: Saul summoned the people, and counted them in Telaim, two hundred thousand footmen and ten thousand men of Judah.

kikuyu: nake Solomoni aarĩ ithe wa Rehoboamu, nake Rehoboamu aarĩ ithe wa Abija, nake Abija aarĩ ithe wa Asa,
english: Solomon became the father of Rehoboam. Rehoboam became the father of Abijah. Abijah became the father of Asa.

kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, rĩrĩa mũrathiĩ na thũ yaku kũrĩ mũciirithania-rĩ, geria mũno wĩiguithanie nayo mũrĩ o njĩra-inĩ, ndĩgagũkururie ĩgũtware kũrĩ mũciirithania, nake mũciirithania akũneane kũrĩ mũnene, nake mũnene ũcio agũikie njeera.
english: For when you are going with your adversary before the magistrate, try diligently on the way to be released from him, lest perhaps he drag you to the judge, and the judge deliver you to the officer, and the officer throw you into prison.  Micah 7:6

kikuyu: Rĩu nĩnjiguĩte atĩ wee no ũhote gũtaũra na kũhĩngĩcanũra maũndũ marĩa maritũ. Ũngĩhota gũthoma maandĩko maya na ũnjĩĩre ũrĩa moigĩte- rĩ, nĩũkũhumbwo nguo ya rangi wa ndathi, na wĩkĩrwo kĩrengeeri gĩa thahabu ngingo, na ũtuuo mwathani wa gatatũ ũthamaki-inĩ ũyũ.”
english: But I have heard of you, that you can give interpretations and dissolve doubts. Now if you can read the writing and make known to me its interpretation, you shall be clothed with purple, and have a chain of gold around your neck, and shall be the third ruler in the kingdom.”

kikuyu: “Ningĩ wĩre nyũmba ya ũthamaki wa Juda atĩrĩ, ‘Iguai ndũmĩrĩri ya Jehova;
english: “Concerning the house of the king of Judah, hear Yahweh’s word:

kikuyu: mũiyũro wa maaĩ nĩũngĩatũhubanĩirie, tũhumbwo nĩ nditi ya maaĩ macio;
english: then the waters would have overwhelmed us, the stream would have gone over our soul.

kikuyu: No mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai angĩgũra ngombo na mbeeca, kana ngombo ĩciarĩrwo gwake mũciĩ, ngombo ĩyo no ĩrĩe irio ciake.
english: But if a priest buys a slave, purchased by his money, he shall eat of it; and those who are born in his house shall eat of his bread.

kikuyu: Aikaria a ihingo maarĩ: Akubu, na Talimoni, na andũ a thiritũ yao, arĩa maarangagĩra ihingo, maarĩ andũ 172.
english: Moreover the gatekeepers, Akkub, Talmon, and their brothers, who kept watch at the gates, were one hundred seventy-two.

kikuyu: Rĩrĩa mũnene wa mbũtũ cia ita aaiguire atĩ mũthamaki wa Ashuri nĩoimĩte Lakishi, akĩehera Jerusalemu agĩthiĩ agĩkora mũthamaki akĩrũa mbaara na Libina.
english: So Rabshakeh returned, and found the king of Assyria warring against Libnah, for he heard that he had departed from Lachish.

kikuyu: Nake agĩĩka maũndũ marĩa maagĩrĩire maitho-inĩ ma Jehova, o ta ũrĩa ithe Daudi eekĩte.
english: He did that which was right in Yahweh’s eyes, according to all that David his father had done.

kikuyu: Ũmũthĩ-rĩ, andũ-a-nja arĩa marĩ igweta a Perisia na Media * 1:1 Wathani wagayanĩtio ngʼongo 127, na o rũgongo rũkagayanio na mabũrũri. † 1:2 Shushani rĩarĩ itũũra rĩmwe inene rĩa marĩa mana ma ũthamaki ũcio; macio mangĩ matatũ maarĩ Babuloni, Perisiapoli, na Ekibatani. ‡ 1:6 Andũ a Perisia maarĩĩaga metambũrũkĩtie itĩ-inĩ.
english: Today, the princesses of Persia and Media who have heard of the queen’s deed will tell all the king’s princes. This will cause much contempt and wrath.

kikuyu: magakweheragia harĩ mũndũ-wa-nja ũcio mwaganu, na magakũhonokia kuuma kũrĩ rũrĩmĩ rũnyoroku rwa mũtumia ũcio ngʼenyũ.
english: to keep you from the immoral woman, from the flattery of the wayward wife’s tongue.

kikuyu: Gũtirĩ mũndũ ũrĩ ũcamba mũiganu wa kũmĩarahũra.
english: None is so fierce that he dare stir him up. Who then is he who can stand before me?

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo Petero akĩrũgama hamwe na arĩa ikũmi na ũmwe, akĩanĩrĩra na akĩarĩria gĩkundi kĩu kĩa andũ akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Inyuĩ andũ aitũ Ayahudi o na inyuĩ inyuothe mũtũũraga Jerusalemu, rekei ndĩmũtaarĩrie ũhoro ũyũ; thikĩrĩriai wega ũrĩa nguuga.
english: But Peter, standing up with the eleven, lifted up his voice and spoke out to them, “You men of Judea and all you who dwell at Jerusalem, let this be known to you, and listen to my words.

kikuyu: Nĩ mũndũ ũrĩa mĩthiĩre yake ĩtarĩ ũcuuke, na ũrĩa wĩkaga maũndũ ma ũthingu, na kwaria aragia ũhoro wa ma kuuma ngoro-inĩ yake,
english: He who walks blamelessly and does what is right, and speaks truth in his heart;

kikuyu: Nake Jesũ akĩmacookeria, akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Ĩtĩkiai Ngai.
english: Jesus answered them, “Have faith in God.

kikuyu: Ngai aroraga ciana cia andũ arĩ o igũrũ, one kana nĩ harĩ ũrĩ na ũmenyo, o na kana nĩ harĩ ũrongoragia Ngai.
english: God looks down from heaven on the children of men, to see if there are any who understood, who seek after God.

kikuyu: “No rĩrĩ, korwo nĩ niĩ ingĩthaitha Mũrungu; ingĩneana ciira wakwa kũrĩ we.
english: “But as for me, I would seek God. I would commit my cause to God,

kikuyu: Nake Jehova akĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Nĩ kĩĩ kĩu ũrĩ nakĩo guoko?” Nake agĩcookia atĩrĩ, “Nĩ rũthanju.”
english: Yahweh said to him, “What is that in your hand?” He said, “A rod.”

kikuyu: Rũũĩ rwa mbere rwĩtagwo Pishoni; na nĩruo rũthiũrũrũkaga bũrũri wothe wa Havila, kũrĩa kũrĩ thahabu.
english: The name of the first is Pishon: it flows through the whole land of Havilah, where there is gold;

kikuyu: atukũ manini maathira, Jesũ agĩcooka Kaperinaumu rĩngĩ, nao andũ makĩigua atĩ nĩoka mũciĩ.
english: hen he entered again into Capernaum after some days, it was heard that he was at home.

kikuyu: Gĩcunjĩ gĩa ikũmi gĩothe kĩa rũũru rwa ngʼombe kana rwa mbũri, nyamũ ya ikũmi yothe ĩrĩa ĩhĩtũkagĩra rungu rwa rũthanju rwa mũrĩithi nĩĩkamũrĩrwo Jehova.
english: All the tithe of the herds or the flocks, whatever passes under the rod, the tenth shall be holy to Yahweh.

kikuyu: Maanjĩhotoreire mũthenya ũrĩa ndaarĩ na mũtino, no Jehova nĩwe warĩ mũtiirĩrĩri wakwa.
english: They came on me in the day of my calamity, but Yahweh was my support.

kikuyu: Nĩaarutire magongona na agĩcinĩra ũbumba kũndũ kũrĩa gũtũũgĩru, na tũcũmbĩrĩ-inĩ twa irĩma, na rungu rwa mĩtĩ ĩrĩa mĩruru.
english: He sacrificed and burned incense in the high places, and on the hills, and under every green tree.

kikuyu: Ningĩ mwena ũcio ũngĩ, mwena wa gathigathini wa hema ĩyo nyamũre, thondeka mbaũ mĩrongo ĩĩrĩ
english: For the second side of the tabernacle, on the north side, twenty boards,

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo niĩ mwene ndĩ mũndũ wathagwo, na ndĩ na thigari njathaga. Ndeera ũmwe ‘Thiĩ,’ nĩathiiaga, na ndeera ũngĩ atĩrĩ, ‘Ũka,’ nĩokaga. Na ningĩ ndeera ndungata yakwa atĩrĩ, ‘Ĩka ũna,’ nĩĩkaga.”
english: For I am also a man under authority, having under myself soldiers. I tell this one, ‘Go,’ and he goes; and tell another, ‘Come,’ and he comes; and tell my servant, ‘Do this,’ and he does it.”

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio, ariũ na aarĩ a Ithe witũ-rĩ, ĩkagĩrai kĩyo makĩria nĩguo mũtũme gwĩtwo kwanyu na gũthuurwo kwanyu kũrũme biũ. Nĩgũkorwo mũngĩĩka maũndũ macio-rĩ, gũtirĩ hĩndĩ mũkaagũa,
english: Therefore, brothers,‡ be more diligent to make your calling and election sure. For if you do these things, you will never stumble.

kikuyu: Marĩ njĩra-inĩ, makĩamba hema ciao gũkuhĩ na Kiriathu- Jearimu kũu Juda. Nĩkĩo gĩtũmaga handũ hau he mwena wa ithũĩro rĩa Kiriathu-Jearimu heetwo
english: They went up and encamped in Kiriath Jearim in Judah. Therefore they call that place Mahaneh Dan to this day. Behold, it is behind Kiriath Jearim.

kikuyu: Mũtikahaane ta Kaini, ũrĩa warĩ wa ũrĩa mũũru, na akĩũraga mũrũ wa nyina. Nĩ kĩĩ gĩatũmire amũũrage? Aamũũragire tondũ ciĩko ciake mwene ciarĩ njũru, nacio cia mũrũ wa nyina ciarĩ cia ũthingu.
english: unlike Cain, who was of the evil one and killed his brother. Why did he kill him? Because his deeds were evil, and his brother’s righteous.

kikuyu: na wa Ahiti, na wa Aperizi, na wa Arefai,
english: the Hittites, the Perizzites, the Rephaim,

kikuyu: Matukũ-inĩ make andũ arĩa athingu nĩmagatheerema; naguo ũgaacĩru ũingĩhe o nginya rĩrĩa mweri ũgaakorwo ũtarĩ ho.
english: In his days, the righteous shall flourish, and abundance of peace, until the moon is no more.

kikuyu: nyũngũ, na icakũri, na hũũma cia nyama, na indo ciothe iria ciakonainie nacio. Indo icio ciothe Huramu-Abi aathondekeire Mũthamaki Solomoni cia hekarũ ya Jehova, ciarĩ cia gĩcangogĩkumuthegĩkahenia.
english: Huram-abi† also made the pots, the shovels, the forks, and all its vessels for King Solomon, for Yahweh’s house, of bright bronze.

kikuyu: No amwe ao makĩũmia ngoro; makĩaga gwĩtĩkia na magĩcambia Njĩra ĩyo mbere ya kĩrĩndĩ. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Paũlũ akĩmeherera. Akĩoya arutwo agĩthiĩ nao nyũmba ĩrĩa yathomithagĩrio andũ ya Turano, akaaranagĩria na andũ o mũthenya arĩ kuo.
english: But when some were hardened and disobedient, speaking evil of the Way before the multitude, he departed from them and separated the disciples, reasoning daily in the school of Tyrannus.

kikuyu: No-o makĩanĩrĩra, makiuga atĩrĩ, “Mweherie! Mweherie! Mwambithie mũtĩ-igũrũ!” Nake Pilato akĩmooria atĩrĩ, “Nyambithie Mũthamaki wanyu?” Nao athĩnjĩri-Ngai arĩa anene magĩcookia atĩrĩ, “Ithuĩ tũtirĩ mũthamaki ũngĩ tiga Kaisari.”
english: They cried out, “Away with him! Away with him! Crucify him!” Pilate said to them, “Shall I crucify your King?” The chief priests answered, “We have no king but Caesar!”

kikuyu: No Jehova akĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Ũrĩ na kĩhooto o na kĩrĩkũ gĩa gũtũma ũrakare?”
english: Yahweh said, “Is it right for you to be angry?”

kikuyu: “Hĩndĩ ĩyo akĩĩra ndungata ciake atĩrĩ, ‘Iruga rĩa ũhiki nĩrĩhaarĩrie no rĩrĩ, arĩa ndĩretire-rĩ, matiraagĩrĩirwo nĩ gũũka.
english: “Then he said to his servants, ‘The wedding is ready, but those who were invited weren’t worthy.

kikuyu: na muoye ndegwa na ndũrũme cia iruta rĩa ũiguano irutwo igongona mbere ya Jehova, hamwe na iruta rĩa mũtu ũtukanĩtio na maguta. Nĩgũkorwo ũmũthĩ Jehova nĩekũmuumĩrĩra.’ ”
english: and a bull and a ram for peace offerings, to sacrifice before Yahweh; and a meal offering mixed with oil: for today Yahweh appears to you.’ ”

kikuyu: maingĩhe o ta ndũũru cia kũrutwo magongona kũu Jerusalemu hĩndĩ ya ciathĩ iria nyamũre. Ũguo nĩguo matũũra macio manene manangĩtwo makaiyũrio ndũũru cia andũ. Hĩndĩ ĩyo nĩmakamenya atĩ niĩ nĩ niĩ Jehova.”
english: As the flock for sacrifice, as the flock of Jerusalem in her appointed feasts, so the waste cities will be filled with flocks of men. Then they will know that I am Yahweh.’ ”

kikuyu: “Kũgwatwo nĩ kĩrumi-rĩ, nĩ mũndũ ũrĩa ũkomaga na nyina wa mũtumia wake.” Hĩndĩ ĩyo nao andũ othe moige, “Ameni!”
english: ‘Cursed is he who lies with his mother-in-law.’ All the people shall say, ‘Amen.’

kikuyu: na Elikana, na Ishia, na Azareli, na Joezeru, na Jashobeamu arĩa maarĩ a mbarĩ ya Kora;
english: Elkanah, Isshiah, Azarel, Joezer, and Jashobeam, the Korahites;

kikuyu: ao andũ a Jerusalemu magĩtua Ahazia mũriũ wa Jehoramu ũrĩa warĩ mũnini mũno mũthamaki ithenya rĩake, nĩgũkorwo atharĩkĩri ao arĩa mookire kambĩ-inĩ hamwe na Arabu, nĩmooragĩte ariũ arĩa akũrũ othe. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Ahazia mũriũ wa Jehoramu mũthamaki wa Juda akĩambĩrĩria gũthamaka.
english: he inhabitants of Jerusalem made Ahaziah his youngest son king in his place, because the band of men who came with the Arabians to the camp had slain all the oldest. So Ahaziah the son of Jehoram king of Judah reigned.

kikuyu: Ningĩ ngĩona Gatũrũme arũgamĩte gatagatĩ ga gĩtĩ kĩa ũnene, nako kahaanaga ta gathĩnjĩtwo, gathiũrũrũkĩirio nĩ ciũmbe iria inya irĩ muoyo, na athuuri acio. Kaarĩ na hĩa mũgwanja na maitho mũgwanja, na nĩmo maroho mũgwanja ma Ngai marĩa matũmĩtwo mathiiage thĩ yothe.
english: I saw in the middle of the throne and of the four living creatures, and in the middle of the elders, a Lamb standing, as though it had been slain, having seven horns and seven eyes, which are the seven Spirits of God, sent out into all the earth.

kikuyu: Cia mwena ũcio ũngĩ, mwena wa gathigathini wa hema ĩyo nyamũre, magĩthondeka buremu mĩrongo ĩĩrĩ
english: For the second side of the tabernacle, on the north side, he made twenty boards

kikuyu: Ningĩ nĩ kwarĩ andũ aingĩ maarĩ na mangũ thĩinĩ wa Isiraeli ihinda rĩa Elisha ũrĩa mũnabii, no gũtirĩ mũndũ o na ũmwe wao wahonirio tiga Naamani ũrĩa Mũsuriata.”
english: There were many lepers in Israel in the time of Elisha the prophet, yet not one of them was cleansed, except Naaman, the Syrian.”

kikuyu: No ũngĩhũũrwo nĩ ũndũ wa gwĩka ũũru o na ũkirĩrĩrie-rĩ, ũngĩkĩgaathĩrwo kĩ? No ũngĩnyariirwo nĩ ũndũ wa gwĩka wega na
english: For what glory is it if, when you sin, you patiently endure beating? But if when you do well, you patiently endure suffering, this is commendable with God.

kikuyu: nacio ndigiri ciarĩ 30,500, na kuuma harĩ icio Jehova akĩrutĩrwo ndigiri 61;
english: The donkeys were thirty thousand five hundred, of which Yahweh’s tribute was sixty-one.

kikuyu: Rũmena rũrehaga nyamũkano, no wendani ũhumbagĩra ũũru wothe.
english: Hatred stirs up strife, but love covers all wrongs.

kikuyu: ake Jehova akĩĩra Musa Kĩrĩma-inĩ gĩa Sinai atĩrĩ,
english: ahweh said to Moses on Mount Sinai,

kikuyu: Atĩrĩrĩ, Jehova areera andũ a Juda, o na andũ a Jerusalemu ũũ: “Cimbai mĩgũnda yanyu ĩrĩa ĩrĩ ngamba, na mũtikahaande irio mĩigua-inĩ.
english: For Yahweh says to the men of Judah and to Jerusalem, “Break up your fallow ground, and don’t sow among thorns.

kikuyu: Gĩthaku gĩakĩo kĩangʼetheire nja ĩyo ya nyumĩrĩra; nacio itingĩ ciakĩo nĩciagemetio na mĩkĩndũ mĩena yeerĩ, na ngathĩ ya makinya manana yaambatĩte ĩgakinya ho.
english: Its arches were toward the outer court. Palm trees were on its posts on this side and on that side. The ascent to it had eight steps.

kikuyu: Nake Samũeli agĩcookia atĩrĩ, “Niĩ nĩ niĩ muoni-maũndũ. Ambata, ũthiĩ mbere yakwa nginya handũ harĩa hatũũgĩru, nĩgũkorwo ũmũthĩ nĩũkũrĩanĩra na niĩ, na rũciinĩ nĩngakũrekereria ũthiĩ, na ngwĩre maũndũ mothe marĩa me ngoro-inĩ yaku.
english: Samuel answered Saul and said, “I am the seer. Go up before me to the high place, for you are to eat with me today. In the morning I will let you go and will tell you all that is in your heart.

kikuyu: Igongona cianyu nĩikamomorwo, na igongona cianyu cia gũcinĩrwo ũbumba ihehenjwo; na nĩngooragĩra andũ anyu hau mbere ya mĩhianano ĩyo yanyu.
english: Your altars will become desolate, and your incense altars will be broken. I will cast down your slain men before your idols.

kikuyu: Aya nĩo maarĩ anene a andũ arĩa njamba a Daudi, o ene hamwe na andũ a Isiraeli othe, nĩmatirĩrĩire ũthamaki wake na hinya nĩguo ũtheereme bũrũri wothe, o ta ũrĩa Jehova eeranĩire.
english: Now these are the chief of the mighty men whom David had, who showed themselves strong with him in his kingdom, together with all Israel, to make him king, according to Yahweh’s word concerning Israel.

kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa mũngĩrekera, o na niĩ nĩndamũrekera. Naguo ũndũ ũrĩa ndekanĩire-rĩ (hangĩkorwo nĩ haarĩ ũndũ o na ũrĩkũ wa kũrekanĩra), ũndũ ũcio ndaũrekanĩra nĩ ũndũ wanyu ndĩ mbere ya Kristũ,
english: Now I also forgive whomever you forgive anything. For if indeed I have forgiven anything, I have forgiven that one for your sakes in the presence of Christ,

kikuyu: Rĩrĩa Joshua eerirwo atĩ athamaki acio nĩmonetwo mehithĩte ngurunga-inĩ kũu Makeda,
english: Joshua was told, saying, “The five kings have been found, hidden in the cave at Makkedah.”

kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, nĩgũtuĩke o ta ũrĩa kwandĩkĩtwo, atĩrĩ: “Arĩa matarĩ meerwo ũhoro wake nĩo makaawona, na arĩa matarĩ maigua nĩo magaataũkĩrwo nĩguo.”
english: But, as it is written, “They will see, to whom no news of him came. They who haven’t heard will understand.”*

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio, arahũkai, tũtharĩkĩre kũrĩ ũtukũ, tũharaganie ciĩgitio ciake iria nũmu!”
english: Arise! Let’s go up by night, and let’s destroy her palaces.”

kikuyu: Nĩakiragia mĩromo ya aheani kĩrĩra arĩa meehoketwo, nao athuuri akamatunya ũhoro wa gũkũũrana maũndũ.
english: He removes the speech of those who are trusted, and takes away the understanding of the elders.

kikuyu: “Jehova ekuuga atĩrĩ: ‘Ũguo nĩguo ngaaniina rũtũrĩko rwa Juda o na rũtũrĩko rũnene rwa Jerusalemu.
english: “Yahweh says, ‘In this way I will ruin the pride of Judah, and the great pride of Jerusalem.

kikuyu: Nake Jesũ akĩmooria atĩrĩ, “O na inyuĩ mũtirĩ mũrataũkĩrwo o na rĩu?
english: So Jesus said, “Do you also still not understand?

kikuyu: no Ngai aatwĩrire atĩrĩ, ‘Mũtikanarĩe matunda ma mũtĩ ũrĩa ũrĩ gatagatĩ ka mũgũnda ũyũ, na mũtikanaũhutie, nĩguo mũtigakue.’ ”
english: but not the fruit of the tree which is in the middle of the garden. God has said, ‘You shall not eat of it. You shall not touch it, lest you die.’ ”

kikuyu: esũ agĩcooka akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Ngũmwĩra atĩrĩ na ma, amwe marũngiĩ haha matigacama gĩkuũ matonete ũthamaki wa Ngai ũgĩũka wĩ na hinya!” Jesũ Kũgarũrũka ũrĩa Aatariĩ
english: 1310 Mark 9:29 9 1 He said to them, “Most certainly I tell you, there are some standing here who will in no way taste death until they see God’s Kingdom come with power.”

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo Jehova Ngai wanyu nĩahũithirie maaĩ ma Jorodani mbere yanyu nginya rĩrĩa mwarikire kũringa. Jehova Ngai wanyu eekire Rũũĩ rwa Jorodani o ta ũrĩa eekire Iria Itune rĩrĩa aarĩhũithirie mbere iitũ nginya rĩrĩa twarĩkirie kũringa.
english: For Yahweh your God dried up the waters of the Jordan from before you until you had crossed over, as Yahweh your God did to the Red Sea, which he dried up from before us, until we had crossed over,

kikuyu: No kuona atĩ nĩ ũhoro wa ciũria ikoniĩ ciugo na marĩĩtwa na watho wanyu inyuĩ ene-rĩ, menyai ũrĩa mũgwĩka. Niĩ ndigũtuĩka mũtui ciira wa maũndũ ta macio.”
english: but if they are questions about words and names and your own law, look to it yourselves. For I don’t want to be a judge of these matters.”

kikuyu: Nĩekire maũndũ mooru maitho-inĩ ma Jehova, na ndaatiganire na mehia o na mamwe ma Jeroboamu mũrũ wa Nebati, marĩa aatũmĩte Isiraeli meehie; aathiire na mbere namo.
english: He did that which was evil in Yahweh’s sight. He didn’t depart from all the sins of Jeroboam the son of Nebat,

kikuyu: Moimbũraga mehia mao, nake akamabatithĩria Rũũĩ-inĩ rwa Jorodani.
english: They were baptized by him in the Jordan, confessing their sins.

kikuyu: Daudi we mwene, akĩaria arĩ na Roho Mũtheru, oigire atĩrĩ: “ ‘Mwathani eerire Mwathani wakwa atĩrĩ: “ikara guoko-inĩ gwakwa kwa ũrĩo nginya ngaiga thũ ciaku rungu rwa makinya maku.” ’
english: For David himself said in the Holy Spirit, ‘The Lord said to my Lord, “Sit at my right hand, until I make your enemies the footstool of your feet.” ’*

kikuyu: Gũgĩcooka gũkĩgĩa heni, na marurumĩ, na ngwa o na gĩthingithia kĩnene. Gũtirĩ kwagĩa gĩthingithia kĩngĩ kĩnene ta kĩu kuuma rĩrĩa thĩ kwagĩire andũ, nĩ ũrĩa gĩthingithia kĩu kĩarĩ kĩnene mũno.
english: There were lightnings, sounds, and thunders; and there was a great earthquake such as has not happened since there were men on the earth—so great an earthquake and so mighty.

kikuyu: Ndibei yaheanagwo na ikombe cia thahabu, na gũtirĩ kĩahaanaine na kĩrĩa kĩngĩ, na ndibei ya nyũmba ya ũthamaki yarĩ nyingĩ mũno o ta ũrĩa ũtaana wa mũthamaki watariĩ.
english: They gave them drinks in golden vessels of various kinds, including royal wine in abundance, according to the bounty of the king.

kikuyu: Bũrũri wanyu ũgaatambũrũka kuuma werũ-inĩ o nginya Lebanoni, na kuuma rũũĩ rũrĩa rũnene rwa Farati, nĩguo bũrũri wothe wa Ahiti, o nginya Iria-inĩ rĩrĩa Inene rĩa mwena wa ithũĩro.
english: From the wilderness and this Lebanon even to the great river, the river Euphrates, all the land of the Hittites, and to the great sea toward the going down of the sun, shall be your border.

kikuyu: Nĩmwĩrĩga kwĩrira, na mũgĩĩka maũndũ marĩa magĩrĩire makĩonwo nĩ niĩ: Tondũ o ũmwe wanyu nĩanĩrĩire ũhoro wa kuohorwo kwa mũndũ o mũndũ bũrũri-inĩ. O na ningĩ mũkĩgĩa na kĩrĩkanĩro kĩu mũrĩ mbere yakwa thĩinĩ wa nyũmba ĩrĩa ĩĩtanĩtio na rĩĩtwa rĩakwa.
english: You had now turned, and had done that which is right in my eyes, in every man proclaiming liberty to his neighbor. You had made a covenant before me in the house which is called by my name;

kikuyu: a iguai ũhoro ũyũ, inyuĩ ngʼombe cia Bashani mũrĩ kũu igũrũ rĩa Kĩrĩma gĩa Samaria, o inyuĩ andũ-a-nja arĩa mũhinyagĩrĩria athĩĩni na mũkahehenja arĩa abatari, na mũkeeraga athuuri anyu atĩrĩ: “Tũreherei ndibei tũnyue!”
english: isten to this word, you cows of Bashan, who are on the mountain of Samaria, who oppress the poor, who crush the needy, who tell their husbands, “Bring us drinks!”

kikuyu: Saũlũ nĩathuurire andũ ngiri ithatũ kuuma Isiraeli; ngiri igĩrĩ magĩikara nake kũu Mikimashi na bũrũri ũrĩa ũrĩ irĩma wa Betheli, nao andũ ngiri maarĩ na Jonathani kũu Gibea ya Benjamini. Andũ arĩa angĩ akĩmeera mainũke kwao mĩciĩ.
english: Saul chose for himself three thousand men of Israel, of which two thousand were with Saul in Michmash and in the Mount of Bethel, and one thousand were with Jonathan in Gibeah of Benjamin. He sent the rest of the people to their own tents.

kikuyu: Bathaka ĩyo yakũngũĩirwo mwaka wa ikũmi na ĩnana wa wathani wa Josia.* Gĩkuũ kĩa Josia
english: This Passover was kept in the eighteenth year of the reign of Josiah.

kikuyu: Mĩĩrĩ yao yothe, o hamwe na mĩgongo yao, na moko mao na mathagu mao, maiyũrĩte maitho kũndũ guothe, na magũrũ macio mana ma ngaari o namo.
english: Their whole body, including their backs, their hands, their wings, and the wheels, were full of eyes all around, even the wheels that the four of them had.

kikuyu: Nowe Jesũ akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Nũmĩrĩra, na ũreke arĩa akuũ mathike akuũ ao ene.” Jesũ Kũhooreria Kĩhuhũkanio
english: But Jesus said to him, “Follow me, and leave the dead to bury their own dead.”

kikuyu: Jehova ekuuga atĩrĩ: “O ta ũrĩa ngogoyo yonekaga kĩmanjĩka-inĩ gĩa thabibũ, nao andũ makoiga atĩrĩ, ‘Tiga gũkĩananga, hahota gũkorwo na kĩndũ kĩega thĩinĩ wakĩo,’ ũguo noguo ngeeka nĩ ũndũ wa ndungata ciakwa; ndigaciniina ciothe.
english: Yahweh says, “As the new wine is found in the cluster, and one says, ‘Don’t destroy it, for a blessing is in it:’ so I will do for my servants’ sake, that I may not destroy them all.

kikuyu: “Na niĩ ngĩcookia atĩrĩ, ‘Aca, Mwathani! Gũtirĩ kĩndũ gĩtarĩ kĩamũre kana kĩrĩ thaahu kĩrĩ gĩatoonya kanua gakwa.’
english: But I said, ‘Not so, Lord, for nothing unholy or unclean has ever entered into my mouth.’

kikuyu: Na ha ũhoro wa Juda akiuga atĩrĩ, “Wee Jehova, thikĩrĩria kĩrĩro kĩa Juda; mũrehe kũrĩ andũ ake. Nĩarũagĩrĩra maũndũ make na guoko gwake. Wee-rĩ, tuĩka wa kũmũteithia akĩrũa na thũ ciake.”
english: This is for Judah. He said, “Hear, Yahweh, the voice of Judah. Bring him in to his people. With his hands he contended for himself. You shall be a help against his adversaries.”

kikuyu: na mũriũ wake wa irigithathi eetagwo Abidoni, akarũmĩrĩrwo nĩ Zuru, na Kishu, na Baali, na Neri, na Nadabu,
english: His firstborn son was Abdon, then Zur, Kish, Baal, Ner, Nadab,

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo Mũthamaki Nebukadinezaru akĩgega, akĩrũgama na igũrũ o rĩmwe, akĩũria andũ arĩa maamũheaga kĩrĩra atĩrĩ, “Githĩ ti andũ atatũ tuohire na twamaikia mwaki-inĩ?” Nao makĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Ĩĩ, nĩ ma ũguo nĩguo, mũthamaki.”
english: Then Nebuchadnezzar the king was astonished and rose up in haste. He spoke and said to his counselors, “Didn’t we cast three men bound into the middle of the fire?” They answered the king, “True, O king.”

kikuyu: Nĩatũmĩte kũgĩe thayũ mĩhaka-inĩ yaku, na agakũhũũnia na ngano ĩrĩa njega mũno.
english: He makes peace in your borders. He fills you with the finest of the wheat.

kikuyu: Mĩanya yakĩo, na gĩthaku gĩakĩo na magemio * 40:3 Rũthanju rũrũ rwa gũthima rwarĩ na ũraihu wa ta mita 3 † 40:5 nĩ ta mita 3 ‡ 40:9 nĩ ta mita 4 § 40:9 nĩ ta mita 1 * 40:11 nĩ ta mita 5 † 40:11 nĩ ta mita ithathatũ na robo ithatũ (6.75) ‡ 40:12 nĩ ta nuthu mita (0.5) § 40:12 nĩ ta mita 3 * 40:13 nĩ ta mita 13 † 40:14 nĩ ta mita 31 ‡ 40:15 nĩ ta mita 26 § 40:19 nĩ ta mita 52
english: Its windows, its arches, and its palm trees were the same as the measure of the gate which faces toward the east. They went up to it by seven steps. Its arches were before them.

kikuyu: Ndibatarĩtio nĩ ndegwa yumĩte kiugũ gĩaku, kana mbũri ciumĩte ciugũ ciaku,
english: I have no need for a bull from your stall, nor male goats from your pens.

kikuyu: Mwĩohei indo ciothe cia Ngai cia mbaara nĩguo mũhote gwĩtiiria mawara ma mũcukani.
english: Put on the whole armor of God, that you may be able to stand against the wiles of the devil.

kikuyu: Handũ ha ũguo-rĩ: “Thũ yaku ĩngĩkorwo ĩhũtiĩ-rĩ, mĩhe irio; ĩngĩkorwo nĩĩnyootiĩ-rĩ, mĩhe gĩa kũnyua. Gwĩka ũguo-rĩ, nĩkũmĩigĩrĩra makara ma mwaki mũtwe.”
english: Therefore “If your enemy is hungry, feed him. If he is thirsty, give him a drink; for in doing so, you will heap coals of fire on his head.”*

kikuyu: Angĩgakorwo atarĩ na mũirĩtu-rĩ, heagai ariũ a nyina igai rĩake.
english: If he has no daughter, then you shall give his inheritance to his brothers.

kikuyu: Mũinĩrei, mũmũinĩre mũkĩmũgoocaga, mũheane ũhoro wa ciĩko ciake ciothe cia magegania.
english: Sing to him. Sing praises to him. Tell of all his marvelous works.

kikuyu: nĩ ũndũ wa kũrutana mĩtugo ĩrĩa ithuĩ andũ a Roma tũtagĩrĩirwo nĩ gwĩtĩkĩra o na kana kũmĩĩka.”
english: and advocate customs which it is not lawful for us to accept or to observe, being Romans.”

kikuyu: “Rĩrĩa mũndũ angĩhĩtĩria mũndũ wa itũũra rĩake na gũtuĩke no nginya ehĩte, nake oke ehĩtĩre hau mbere ya kĩgongona gĩaku kĩa hekarũ-inĩ ĩno-rĩ,
english: “If a man sins against his neighbor, and an oath is laid on him to cause him to swear, and he comes and swears before your altar in this house,

kikuyu: We no arĩe irio iria theru mũno cia Ngai wake, o na irio iria theru;
english: He shall eat the bread of his God, both of the most holy, and of the holy.

kikuyu: “Matukũ-inĩ macio andũ matigacooka kuuga atĩrĩ, “ ‘Maithe ma ciana nĩmarĩĩte thabibũ irĩ rwagatha, namo magego ma ciana nĩmo maiguĩte thiithi.’
english: “In those days they will say no more, “ ‘The fathers have eaten sour grapes, and the children’s teeth are set on edge.’

kikuyu: Marĩ o eerĩ magĩtengʼera, no mũrutwo ũcio ũngĩ agĩkĩria Petero ihenya, agĩkinya mbĩrĩra-inĩ mbere.
english: They both ran together. The other disciple outran Peter and came to the tomb first.

kikuyu: Aheani ũhoro eerĩ kana atatũ magĩrĩirwo maarie, nao andũ arĩa angĩ makũũrane wega ũrĩa kũreerwo.
english: Let two or three of the prophets speak, and let the others discern.  The word for “brothers” here and where context allows may also be correctly translated “brothers and sisters”

kikuyu: “Kaĩ gũkũ thĩ kũrĩ na haaro-ĩ, nĩ ũndũ wa maũndũ marĩa matũmaga andũ mehie! Maũndũ ta macio no nginya moke, no mũndũ ũrĩa ũtũmaga moke kaĩ arĩ na haaro-ĩ!
english: “Woe to the world because of occasions of stumbling! For it must be that the occasions come, but woe to that person through whom the occasion comes!

kikuyu: Tondũ wa mehia maitũ, magetha maguo maingĩ mathiiaga kũrĩ athamaki arĩa ũigĩte igũrũ riitũ. Maathaga mĩĩrĩ iitũ na ngʼombe ciitũ o ũrĩa mekwenda. Tũrĩ ahinyĩrĩrie mũno. Kĩrĩko kĩa Andũ
english: It yields much increase to the kings whom you have set over us because of our sins. Also they have power over our bodies and over our livestock, at their pleasure, and we are in great distress.

kikuyu: Ningĩ Jehova akĩĩra Musa atĩrĩ, “Oya mahuti manungi wega marĩa metagwo thitakiti, na onuka na garibane, hamwe na ũbani ũrĩa mwega mũno, mothe mathimĩtwo maiganaine,
english: Yahweh said to Moses, “Take to yourself sweet spices, gum resin, onycha, and galbanum: sweet spices with pure frankincense. There shall be an equal weight of each.

kikuyu: Arahũka; ũhoro ũyũ ũrĩ moko-inĩ maku. Ithuĩ tũrĩ hamwe nawe; nĩ ũndũ ũcio, wĩyũmĩrĩrie ũwĩke.”
english: Arise, for the matter belongs to you and we are with you. Be courageous, and do it.”

kikuyu: Bũrũri wao ũiyũrĩte betha na thahabu, igĩĩna ciao itingĩthira. Bũrũri wao ũiyũrĩte mbarathi; na ngaari ciao cia ita itingĩtarĩka.
english: Their land is full of silver and gold, neither is there any end of their treasures. Their land also is full of horses, neither is there any end of their chariots.

kikuyu: Maya nĩmo marĩĩtwa ma mbarĩ cia Agerishoni: Nĩ Libini na Shimei.
english: These are the names of the sons of Gershon by their families: Libni and Shimei.

kikuyu: Tũtigacooka mĩciĩ iitũ o nginya hĩndĩ ĩrĩa Mũisiraeli o wothe akaamũkĩra igai rĩake.
english: We will not return to our houses until the children of Israel have all received their inheritance.

kikuyu: Namo mahiga manene ma mbura ya mbembe, ma ũritũ wa ta kilo mĩrongo ĩtano o ihiga, makĩgwĩra andũ. Nao makĩruma Ngai nĩ ũndũ wa mũthiro ũcio wa mahiga ma mbura ya mbembe, tondũ mũthiro ũcio warĩ wa kũmakania mũno.
english: Great hailstones, about the weight of a talent,‡ came down out of the sky on people. People blasphemed God because of the plague of the hail, for this plague was exceedingly severe.

kikuyu: nĩ ũndũ ũcio ngĩkĩgũra mũgũnda ũcio warĩ Anathothu kuuma kũrĩ Hanameli mũrũ wa mama, na ngĩmũthimĩra cekeri ikũmi na mũgwanja† cia betha.
english: I bought the field that was in Anathoth of Hanamel my uncle’s son, and weighed

kikuyu: Na nĩ ũndũ ũcio mũgĩikara kũu Kadeshi matukũ maingĩ, ihinda rĩu rĩothe mwaikarire kuo.
english: So you stayed in Kadesh many days, according to the days that you remained.

kikuyu: Andũ magaatuĩka eyendi, na endi mbeeca, na egani, na egaathi, na arumani, na andũ mataiguaga aciari ao, na matarĩ ngaatho, na matarĩ atheru.
english: For men will be lovers of self, lovers of money, boastful, arrogant, blasphemers, disobedient to parents, unthankful, unholy,

kikuyu: O ta ũrĩa Jehova aathire Musa-rĩ, Harũni akĩiga mana mbere ya ithandũkũ rĩa Ũira, nĩguo matũũre ho.
english: As Yahweh commanded Moses, so Aaron laid it up before the Testimony, to be kept.

kikuyu: Na rĩrĩa kĩanagĩrĩra gĩgĩteaga nguo ciakĩo na gĩkĩũmbũraga rũkũngũ,
english: As they cried out, threw off their cloaks, and threw dust into the air,

kikuyu: Ningĩ Jehova akĩĩra Joshua atĩrĩ,
english: Yahweh spoke to Joshua, saying,

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ-rĩ, angĩkorwo mwatinirio kuuma mũtamaiyũ-inĩ wa kĩrĩti, na mũgĩcooka mũgĩciarithanio na mũtamaiyũ wa mũgũnda, na ũcio nĩ ũhoro ũtarĩ wa ndũire-rĩ, githĩ ti ũhũthũ makĩria honge ici, o ici ciarĩ ciaguo, gũciarithanio na mũtamaiyũ wa mũgũnda o ũcio warĩ wao kĩũmbe! Andũ a Isiraeli othe Nĩmakahonokio
english: For if you were cut out of that which is by nature a wild olive tree, and were grafted contrary to nature into a good olive tree, how much more will these, which are the natural branches, be grafted into their own olive tree?

kikuyu: agĩtaha matũũra manene marĩa maarĩ mairigĩre ma Juda na agĩthiĩ nginya Jerusalemu.
english: He took the fortified cities which belonged to Judah, and came to Jerusalem.

kikuyu: Kĩu nĩkĩo gĩtũmaga aini a marebeta moige atĩrĩ: “Ũkaai Heshiboni nĩgeetha rĩakwo rĩngĩ; itũũra inene rĩa Sihoni nĩrĩcokererio.
english: Therefore those who speak in proverbs say, “Come to Heshbon. Let the city of Sihon be built and established;

kikuyu: Zeba na Zalimuna, athamaki acio eerĩ a Midiani, makĩũra, nowe akĩmatengʼeria, akĩmanyiita, na akĩhoota mbũtũ ĩyo yao yothe.
english: Zebah and Zalmunna fled and he pursued them. He took the two kings of Midian, Zebah and Zalmunna, and confused all the army.

kikuyu: Nao makĩhahũka na makĩmaka, magĩĩciiria atĩ nĩ ngoma moonaga.
english: But they were terrified and filled with fear, and supposed that they had seen a spirit.

kikuyu: akiuga atĩrĩ, “Heei ũhoti ũyũ o na niĩ nĩgeetha ũrĩa wothe ndaigĩrĩra moko makwa akaamũkĩra Roho Mũtheru.”
english: saying, “Give me also this power, that whomever I lay my hands on may receive the Holy Spirit.”

kikuyu: Andũ aingĩ magĩũka gũtũnga Jesũ nĩ ũndũ nĩmaiguĩte atĩ nĩaringĩte kĩama kĩu.
english: For this cause also the multitude went and met him, because they heard that he had done this sign.

kikuyu: Ta kĩone ũrĩa nyendete mawatho maku; tũũria muoyo wakwa, Wee Jehova, kũringana na wendo waku.
english: Consider how I love your precepts. Revive me, Yahweh, according to your loving kindness.

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo mũgambo ũkĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Petero, ũkĩra. Thĩnja na ũrĩe.”
english: A voice came to him, “Rise, Peter, kill and eat!”

kikuyu: No arĩ o muoyo-rĩ, akĩhe ariũ a thuriya* ciake iheo, agĩcooka akĩmeheranĩria na mũriũ Isaaka magĩthiĩ na kũu bũrũri wa irathĩro.
english: but Abraham gave gifts to the sons of Abraham’s concubines. While he still lived, he sent them away from Isaac his son, eastward, to the east country.

kikuyu: “Na rĩrĩ, haarĩriai ngaari njerũ, na ngʼombe igĩrĩ njiairĩ itarĩ ciohwo icooki. Muoherere ngʼombe icio ngaari-inĩ ĩyo, no mweherie njaũ ciacio mũcihingĩre kiugũ.
english: “Now therefore take and prepare yourselves a new cart and two milk cows on which there has come no yoke; and tie the cows to the cart, and bring their calves home from them;

kikuyu: ningĩ mariũko ma njũũĩ nĩmanyiite, na kũrĩa kũrĩ marura gũgaacinwo na mwaki, nacio thigari ikanyiitwo nĩ guoya.”
english: So the passages are seized. They have burned the reeds with fire. The men of war are frightened.”

kikuyu: Saũlũ akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Nĩ kĩĩ gĩtũmĩte mũciirĩre kũnjũkĩrĩra, wee na mũrũ wa Jesii, nawe ũkamũhe mĩgate, o na rũhiũ rwa njora, na ũkamũtuĩrĩria ũhoro harĩ Jehova, nĩgeetha aanemere na aikare anjoheirie, ta ũrĩa ekĩte ũmũthĩ?”
english: Saul said to him, “Why have you conspired against me, you and the son of Jesse, in that you have given him bread, and a sword, and have inquired of God for him, that he should rise against me, to lie in wait, as it is today?”

kikuyu: Angĩkorwo he mũndũ o na ũ ũtendete Mwathani-rĩ, arogwatwo nĩ kĩrumi. Ũka, Mwathani!
english: If any man doesn’t love the Lord Jesus Christ, let him be cursed.† Come, Lord!‡

kikuyu: No ciũmbe ciothe iria irĩ iria-inĩ kana tũrũũĩ-inĩ, na itirĩ mathagu manyiitaine o hamwe na ngaracũ mwĩrĩ, irĩ imwe cia iria ithiiaga irĩ hamwe, kana irĩ ciothe cia iria irĩ muoyo kũu maaĩ-inĩ; no nginya mũcithũũre biũ.
english: All that don’t have fins and scales in the seas and rivers, all that move in the waters, and all the living creatures that are in the waters, they are an abomination to you,

kikuyu: O na gũtuĩka Jusufu nĩamenyire ariũ a ithe-rĩ, o matiigana kũmũmenya.
english: Joseph recognized his brothers, but they didn’t recognize him.

kikuyu: ũmenyo wake nĩguo wagayanirie kũrĩa kũriku, namo matu magatatagia ime.
english: By his knowledge, the depths were broken up, and the skies drop down the dew.

kikuyu: “Tuĩkanĩriai kambĩ-inĩ mũkĩĩraga andũ atĩrĩ, ‘Haarĩriai rĩĩgu mũige, nĩ ũndũ mĩthenya ĩtatũ kuuma ũmũthĩ yathira nĩguo mũkaaringa Rũũĩ rwa Jorodani nĩgeetha mũthiĩ mũtoonye na mwĩgwatĩre bũrũri ũrĩa Jehova Ngai wanyu ekũmũhe ũtuĩke wanyu.’ ”
english: “Pass through the middle of the camp, and command the people, saying, ‘Prepare food; for within three days you are to pass over this Jordan, to go in to possess the land which Yahweh your God gives you to possess.’ ”

kikuyu: nake Amazia aarĩ mũriũ wa Joashu, nake Azaria aarĩ mũriũ wa Amazia, nake Jothamu aarĩ mũriũ wa Azaria,
english: Amaziah his son, Azariah his son, Jotham his son,

kikuyu: Ikaragai mwĩiguĩte na mwĩhũgage. Tondũ-rĩ, thũ yanyu mũcukani ĩcangacangaga ta mũrũũthi ũkũrarama ũgĩcaria mũndũ wa gũtambuura.
english: Be sober and self-controlled. Be watchful. Your adversary, the devil, walks around like a roaring lion, seeking whom he may devour.

kikuyu: Gĩthima gĩaku kĩrorathimwo, nawe ũkenagĩre mũtumia wa ũnini waku.
english: Let your spring be blessed. Rejoice in the wife of your youth.

kikuyu: Thikĩrĩriai, ndĩmwĩre ũndũ wa hitho: Tũtigaakua ithuothe, no nĩkũgarũrwo tũkaagarũrwo ithuothe,
english: Behold,§ I tell you a mystery. We will not all sleep, but we will all be changed,

kikuyu: Kuuma Karũgamo ka Mweri ũmwe nginya Karũgamo ka Mweri ũrĩa ũngĩ, na kuuma Thabatũ ĩmwe nginya Thabatũ ĩrĩa ĩngĩ, andũ othe nĩmagookaga kũhooya mainamĩrĩire mbere yakwa,” ũguo nĩguo Jehova ekuuga.
english: It shall happen that from one new moon to another, and from one Sabbath to another, all flesh will come to worship before me,” says Yahweh.

kikuyu: Musa akĩhe andũ a Gadi na a Rubeni na nuthu ya mũhĩrĩga wa Manase mũrũ wa Jusufu, bũrũri ũrĩa wothe wathamakagwo nĩ Sihoni mũthamaki wa Aamori, na ũrĩa wothe wathamakagwo nĩ Ogu mũthamaki wa Bashani, akĩmahe bũrũri ũcio wothe, na matũũra manene makuo o na ngʼongo iria ciamarigiicĩirie.
english: Moses gave to them, even to the children of Gad, and to the children of Reuben, and to the half-tribe of Manasseh the son of Joseph, the kingdom of Sihon king of the Amorites, and the kingdom of Og king of Bashan; the land, according to its cities and borders, even the cities of the surrounding land.

kikuyu: Ũguo nĩ ta kuuga atĩrĩ, ti ciana iria iciarĩtwo na njĩra ya ndũire ingĩtuĩka ciana cia Ngai, no nĩ ciana cia kĩĩranĩro
english: That is, it is not the children of the flesh who are children of God, but the children of the promise are counted as heirs.

kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, niĩ njiyũrĩtwo nĩ mangʼũrĩ ma Jehova, ndingĩhota kũmahingĩrĩria. “Maitĩrĩrie ciana iria irĩ barabara-inĩ, na ũmaitĩrĩrie aanake arĩa monganĩte hamwe; mũthuuri na mũtumia wake, maitĩkĩrwo nĩmo, o ũndũ ũmwe na andũ arĩa akũrũ, arĩa maingĩhĩtie mĩaka.
english: Therefore I am full of Yahweh’s wrath. I am weary with holding it in. “Pour it out on the children in the street, and on the assembly of young men together; for even the husband with the wife will be taken, the aged with him who is full of days.

kikuyu: Magĩcooka magĩthiĩ kũrĩ Mũthamaki Hezekia, makĩmwĩra atĩrĩ: “Nĩtũtheretie hekarũ ya Jehova yothe, kĩgongona kĩa maruta ma njino, na indo ciakĩo ciothe, na metha ĩrĩa ĩigagĩrĩrwo mĩgate ĩrĩa mĩamũre, na indo ciayo ciothe.
english: Then they went in to Hezekiah the king within the palace and said, “We have cleansed all Yahweh’s house, including the altar of burnt offering with all its vessels, and the table of show bread with all its vessels.

kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, Herode nĩanyiitĩte Johana akamuoha, na akamũikia njeera nĩ ũndũ wa Herodia, ũrĩa warĩ mũtumia wa mũrũ wa nyina Filipu,
english: For Herod had arrested John, bound him, and put him in prison for the sake of Herodias, his brother Philip’s wife.

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo mũndũ ũrĩa wothe wendaga kũhonokia muoyo wake nĩakoorwo nĩguo; no ũrĩa wothe ũkoorwo nĩ muoyo wake nĩ ũndũ wakwa nĩakaũhonokia.
english: For whoever desires to save his life will lose it, and whoever will lose his life for my sake will find it.

kikuyu: Ndagũthaitha honokia kuuma moko-inĩ ma mũrũ wa maitũ Esaũ, nĩgũkorwo nĩndĩretigĩra ndagoke gũũtharĩkĩra, niĩ hamwe na ciana ici, na manyina maacio.
english: Please deliver me from the hand of my brother, from the hand of Esau; for I fear him, lest he come and strike me and the mothers with the children.

kikuyu: Ebedi-Meleku akiuma hau nja ya nyũmba ya ũthamaki, akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ,
english: Ebedmelech went out of the king’s house, and spoke to the king, saying,

kikuyu: na andũ a Azimavethu maarĩ 42,
english: The children of Azmaveth, forty-two.

kikuyu: No Afarisai makiuma nja, magĩcookania ndundu ya ũrĩa mangĩũragithia Jesũ. Ndungata ya Ngai ĩrĩa Thuure
english: But the Pharisees went out and conspired against him, how they might destroy him.

kikuyu: Na marĩ njĩra thigari ikĩnyiita mũndũ wahĩtũkaga akiuma mĩgũnda-inĩ, wetagwo Simoni wa kuuma Kurene, ithe wa Alekisandero na Rufusi, na ikĩmũkuuithia mũtharaba wa Jesũ na hinya.
english: They compelled one passing by, coming from the country, Simon of Cyrene, the father of Alexander and Rufus, to go with them that he might bear his cross.

kikuyu: Ihinda rĩrĩa rĩhĩtũkĩte, o na Saũlũ arĩ o mũthamaki, wee nĩwe ũratongoragia Isiraeli mbaara-inĩ ciao. Nake Jehova Ngai waku agĩkwĩra atĩrĩ, ‘Nĩwe ũkaarĩithia andũ akwa Isiraeli, na nĩũgatuĩka mwathi wao.’ ”
english: In times past, even when Saul was king, it was you who led out and brought in Israel. Yahweh your God said to you, ‘You shall be shepherd of my people Israel, and you shall be prince over my people Israel.’ ”

kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, Nebukadinezaru mũthamaki wa Babuloni nĩarutĩte watho ũkoniĩ Jeremia, akeera Nebuzaradani ũcio mũnene wa arangĩri a mũthamaki atĩrĩ:
english: Now Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon commanded Nebuzaradan the captain of the guard concerning Jeremiah, saying,

kikuyu: o rĩrĩ, Daudi agĩĩciiria atĩrĩ, “Mũthenya ũmwe nĩndĩrĩniinwo nĩ guoko gwa Saũlũ. Ũndũ ũrĩa mwega ingĩĩka nĩ kũũrĩra bũrũri wa Afilisti. Hĩndĩ ĩyo Saũlũ nĩagatiga kũnjaria kũndũ o na kũ thĩinĩ wa Isiraeli, na niĩ nĩngehonokia guoko-inĩ gwake.”
english: avid said in his heart, “I will now perish one day by the hand of Saul. There is nothing better for me than that I should escape into the land of the Philistines; and Saul will despair of me, to seek me any more in all the borders of Israel. So I will escape out of his hand.”

kikuyu: Rĩrĩa twaiguire ũhoro ũcio, ngoro ciitũ nĩciaringĩkire na ũmĩrĩru wa mũndũ o mũndũ akĩũrwo nĩ hinya nĩ ũndũ wanyu, nĩgũkorwo Jehova Ngai wanyu nĩwe Ngai wa kũu igũrũ o na wa gũkũ thĩ.
english: As soon as we had heard it, our hearts melted, and there wasn’t any more spirit in any man, because of you: for Yahweh your God, he is God in heaven above, and on earth beneath.

kikuyu: Inyuĩ ndũrĩrĩ, kenai hamwe na andũ ake, nĩgũkorwo nĩakarĩhanĩria thakame ya ndungata ciake; acirĩhĩrie harĩ thũ ciake,
english: Rejoice, you nations, with his people, for he will avenge the blood of his servants. He will take vengeance on his adversaries, and will make atonement for his land and for his people.‡

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio angĩkorwo nĩũtuaga tũrĩ rũmwe nawe-rĩ, mwamũkĩre o ta ũrĩa ũngĩnyamũkĩra niĩ mwene.
english: If then you count me a partner, receive him as you would receive me.

kikuyu: Hakĩrĩ ũguo-rĩ, nĩ kĩĩ kĩratũma mwende kũgeria Ngai na ũndũ wa gwĩkĩra arutwo icooki ngingo rĩrĩa ithuĩ o na kana maithe maitũ tũtarĩ twahota gũkuua?
english: Now therefore why do you tempt God, that you should put a yoke on the neck of the disciples which neither our fathers nor we were able to bear?

kikuyu: Tondũ tũngĩthiĩ na mbere na kwĩhia na ũtũrĩka thuutha wa kũmenya ũhoro-ũrĩa-wa-ma-rĩ, gũtirĩ na igongona rĩngĩ rĩa kweheria mehia rĩtigaire,
english: For if we sin willfully after we have received the knowledge of the truth, there remains no more a sacrifice for sins,

kikuyu: Rĩrĩa Afilisti maaiguire atĩ Isiraeli maagomanĩte kũu Mizipa, aathani a Afilisti makĩambata magĩthiĩ makamatharĩkĩre. Na rĩrĩa andũ a Isiraeli maaiguire ũhoro ũcio, makĩnyiitwo nĩ guoya nĩ ũndũ wa Afilisti.
english: When the Philistines heard that the children of Israel were gathered together at Mizpah, the lords of the Philistines went up against Israel. When the children of Israel heard it, they were afraid of the Philistines.

kikuyu: Nake akĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Maathani macio mothe ndũire ndĩmarũmĩtie kuuma ndĩ o mũnini.”
english: He said, “I have observed all these things from my youth up.”

kikuyu: Nacio icigĩrĩra ikĩũra ciothe, nacio irĩma ikĩaga gũcooka kuoneka.
english: Every island fled away, and the mountains were not found.

kikuyu: Ũhoro ũcio wa ũhonokio-rĩ, nĩguo anabii, arĩa maarathaga ũhoro wa wega wa Ngai ũrĩa warĩ ũũke kũrĩ inyuĩ, maacaragia na kĩyo, na makaũtuĩria na ũmenyereri mũnene,
english: Concerning this salvation, the prophets sought and searched diligently. They prophesied of the grace that would come to you,

kikuyu: Wee weĩraga atĩrĩ na ngoro yaku, “Ngũhaica nginye igũrũ; na gĩtĩ gĩakwa kĩa ũnene ndĩkĩambararie gĩkĩre njata cia Mũrungu; na njikarĩre kĩrĩma-inĩ gĩa gũtũnganwo, o gacũmbĩrĩ ga kĩrĩma kĩrĩa kĩamũre.
english: You said in your heart, “I will ascend into heaven! I will exalt my throne above the stars of God! I will sit on the mountain of assembly, in the far north!

kikuyu: Ciongo iria ciarĩ igũrũ wa itugĩ cia gĩthaku ciathondeketwo na mũhianĩre wa mahũa ma itoka, na ciarĩ na ũraihu wa mĩkono ĩna.§
english: The capitals that were on the top of the pillars in the porch were of lily work, four cubits.

kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, Saũlũ nĩ arĩ na thuriya yetagwo Rizipa mwarĩ wa Aia. Nake Ishi-Boshethu akĩũria Abineri atĩrĩ, “Wakomire na thuriya ya baba nĩkĩ?”
english: Now Saul had a concubine, whose name was Rizpah, the daughter of Aiah; and Ishbosheth said to Abner, “Why have you gone in to my father’s concubine?”

kikuyu: “Nĩ ũndũ wa ũguo nĩngũmũguucĩrĩria na waara; ngũmũtongoria nginya werũ-inĩ, na ndĩmwarĩrie na ciugo cia wendo.
english: “Therefore behold, I will allure her, and bring her into the wilderness, and speak tenderly to her.

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo Amunoni akĩĩra Tamaru atĩrĩ, “Ndehera irio haha nyũmba yakwa ya toro, nĩguo ndĩe ũcinyiitĩte na guoko gwaku.” Nake Tamaru akĩoya mũgate ũrĩa aathondekete, akĩũtwarĩra mũrũ wa ithe Amunoni nyũmba yake ya toro.
english: Amnon said to Tamar, “Bring the food into the room, that I may eat from your hand.” Tamar took the cakes which she had made, and brought them into the room to Amnon her brother.

kikuyu: “ ‘Ndũkanakome na tataguo kana mwarĩ wa nyina na thoguo, nĩ ũndũ gwĩka ũguo nĩ gũconorithia mũndũ ũrĩa mũrĩ rũrĩra; inyuĩ eerĩ nĩmũgacookererwo nĩ ũũru ũcio mwĩkĩte.
english: “ ‘You shall not uncover the nakedness of your mother’s sister, nor of your father’s sister, for he has made his close relative naked. They shall bear their iniquity.

kikuyu: Ririkanaga atĩ o na we warĩ ngombo kũu bũrũri wa Misiri, no Jehova Ngai waku agĩgũkũũra. Kĩu nĩkĩo gĩatũma ngũhe rĩathani rĩu ũmũthĩ.
english: You shall remember that you were a slave in the land of Egypt, and Yahweh your God redeemed you. Therefore I command you this thing today.

kikuyu: Mũthamaki agĩcooka agĩatha Doegu, akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Hũgũka ũũrage athĩnjĩri-Ngai.” Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Doegu ũcio Mũedomu akĩhũgũka akĩmooraga. Mũthenya ũcio akĩũraga andũ mĩrongo ĩnana na atano arĩa meehumbĩte ebodi ya gatani.
english: The king said to Doeg, “Turn and attack the priests!” Doeg the Edomite turned, and he attacked the priests, and he killed on that day eighty-five people who wore a linen ephod.

kikuyu: Nĩngamaingatithia na rũhiũ rwa njora, na ngʼaragu, na mũthiro, na ndũme matuĩke kĩndũ kĩmeneku harĩ mothamaki mothe ma gũkũ thĩ, na matuĩke ta kĩndũ kĩnyiite nĩ kĩrumi na kĩrĩ magigi, na kĩndũ gĩa kũnyararwo na kũmeneka, manyararwo ndũrĩrĩ-inĩ ciothe kũrĩa ngaamatwarithia.
english: Yahweh of Armies says: “Behold, I will send on them the sword, the famine, and the pestilence, and will make them like rotten figs that can’t be eaten, they are so bad. sword, with the famine, and with the pestilence, and will deliver them to be tossed back and forth among all the kingdoms of the earth, to be an object of horror, an astonishment, a hissing, and a reproach among all the nations where I have driven them,

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio athigaani acio makĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Mũrutani, nĩtũũĩ wee waragia na ũkarutana maũndũ marĩa magĩrĩire, na atĩ ndũtĩagĩra andũ maũthĩ, no wee ũrutanaga ũhoro wa Ngai na ma, ũrĩa kwagĩrĩire.
english: They asked him, “Teacher, we know that you say and teach what is right, and aren’t partial to anyone, but truly teach the way of God.

kikuyu: Nĩekire maũndũ mooru maitho-inĩ ma Jehova Ngai wake, na ndaigana kwĩnyiihia mbere ya Jeremia ũrĩa mũnabii, ũrĩa waaragia kiugo kĩa Jehova.
english: He did that which was evil in Yahweh his God’s sight. He didn’t humble himself before Jeremiah the prophet speaking from Yahweh’s mouth.

kikuyu: No mbere ya kahĩĩ kau kũmenya kũrega ũũru na gwĩthuurĩra maũndũ marĩa mega-rĩ, bũrũri ũcio wa athamaki acio eerĩ wĩtigĩrĩte nĩũkanangwo.
english: For before the child knows to refuse the evil and choose the good, the land whose two kings you abhor shall be forsaken.

kikuyu: Andũ arĩa akuũ matigoocaga Jehova, acio maikũrũkaga kũu ũkiri-inĩ;
english: The dead don’t praise Yah, nor any who go down into silence,

kikuyu: Kũu werũ-inĩ, mũingĩ ũcio wothe nĩwatetirie Musa na Harũni.
english: The whole congregation of the children of Israel murmured against Moses and against Aaron in the wilderness;

kikuyu: Kĩmera kĩrĩa kĩarũmĩrĩire gĩakinya-rĩ, Beni-Hadadi agĩcookanĩrĩria Asuriata, magĩthiĩ Afeki makarũe na andũ a Isiraeli.
