english: Then a wise woman cried out of the city, “Hear, hear! Please say to Joab, ‘Come near here, that I may speak with you.’ ”

kikuyu: Mwĩmenyererei mũtikanarutĩre magongona manyu ma njino handũ harĩa hothe mũngĩenda.
english: Be careful that you don’t offer your burnt offerings in every place that you see;

kikuyu: Kuuma harĩo, Benjamini na Hashubu magĩcookereria rũthingo gĩcunjĩ kĩrĩa kĩarĩ mbere ya nyũmba yao; na kũrigania nao, Azaria mũrũ wa Maaseia, mũrũ wa Anania, agĩcookereria rũthingo hakuhĩ na nyũmba yake.
english: After them, Benjamin and Hasshub made repairs across from their house. After them, Azariah the son of Maaseiah the son of Ananiah made repairs beside his own house.

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Jehova agĩtũruta bũrũri wa Misiri na guoko gwake kwa hinya, na gũtambũrũkĩtio, akĩhũthĩra maũndũ manene ma kũmakania, na akĩringa ciama, o na agĩĩka maũndũ ma magegania.
english: Yahweh brought us out of Egypt with a mighty hand, with an outstretched arm, with great terror, with signs, and with wonders;

kikuyu: Mathiaka ma mĩguĩ yao matariĩ ta mbĩrĩra iria ĩtathikĩtwo;* othe nĩ njamba irĩ hinya.
english: Their quiver is an open tomb. They are all mighty men.

kikuyu: Andũ arĩa matarĩ ehokeku nĩmakarĩhwo kũna nĩ ũndũ wa mĩthiĩre yao, nake mũndũ mwega aheo kĩheo kũringana na mĩthiĩre yake.
english: The unfaithful will be repaid for his own ways; likewise a good man will be rewarded for his ways.

kikuyu: Andũ a Isiraeli magĩgĩka o ũrĩa Jehova aathire Musa na Harũni.
english: The children of Israel went and did so; as Yahweh had commanded Moses and Aaron, so they did.

kikuyu: Athĩnjĩri-Ngai, na Alawii, na aini a nyĩmbo, na aikaria a ihingo, na ndungata cia hekarũ maatũũrire matũũra-inĩ mao, o hamwe na andũ amwe a acio angĩ, nao andũ arĩa angĩ a Isiraeli magĩtũũra matũũra-inĩ mao. § 2:65 Hamwe na Alawii arĩa mainaga, nĩ kwarĩ na aini angĩ mainaga hĩndĩ ya ciathĩ, na ya mohiki, na hĩndĩ ĩrĩa andũ maakua. * 2:69
english: So the priests and the Levites, with some of the people, the singers, the gatekeepers, and the temple servants, lived in their cities, and all Israel in their cities.

kikuyu: Rũmena rwake no rũhithwo na maheeni, no waganu wake ũkaaguũrio kĩũngano-inĩ.
english: His malice may be concealed by deception, but his wickedness will be exposed in

kikuyu: Ndũrĩrĩ ciothe iria ũmbĩte nĩigooka igũthathaiye irĩ mbere yaku, Wee Mwathani; nĩikarehera rĩĩtwa rĩaku riiri.
english: All nations you have made will come and worship before you, Lord. They shall glorify your name.

kikuyu: Kanua ka mũndũ mũthingu nĩ gĩthima kĩa muoyo, no kanua ka mũndũ mwaganu kaiyũraga ngũĩ.
english: The mouth of the righteous is a spring of life, but violence covers the mouth of the wicked.

kikuyu: Nĩamaiyũrĩtie na ũũgĩ wa kũruta mawĩra mothe, marĩ mabundi, na acori, na agemia na ndigi cia rangi wa bururu, na cia rangi wa ndathi, na cia rangi mũtune ta gakarakũ, na gatani ĩrĩa njega, o na atumi: othe marĩ mabundi moogĩ, na acori oogĩ.
english: He has filled them with wisdom of heart to work all kinds of workmanship, of the engraver, of the skillful workman, and of the embroiderer, in blue, in purple, in scarlet, and in fine linen, and of the weaver, even of those who do any workmanship, and of those who make skillful works.

kikuyu: Kũu iria-inĩ gũgaatuĩka kũndũ gwa gũtambũrũkĩria nga cia gũtega thamaki, nĩgũkorwo ũhoro ũcio nĩ niĩ njarĩtie, ũguo nĩguo Mwathani Jehova ekuuga. Narĩo itũũra rĩu rĩgaatuĩka rĩa gũtahwo nĩ ndũrĩrĩ,
english: She will be a place for the spreading of nets in the middle of the sea; for I have spoken it,’ says the Lord Yahweh. ‘She will become plunder for the nations.

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio rekereria ciana ciao ngʼaragu; cineane, iniinwo na rũhiũ rwa njora. Reke atumia ao matigwo matarĩ ciana, na marĩ a ndigwa; rekereria arũme ao mooragwo, o nao aanake ao mooragĩrwo mbaara-inĩ na rũhiũ rwa njora.
english: Therefore deliver up their children to the famine, and give them over to the power of the sword. Let their wives become childless and widows. Let their men be killed and their young men struck by the sword in battle.

kikuyu: “Gũtirĩ mũndũ ũũĩ ũhoro wa mũthenya ũcio kana ithaa rĩu, o na araika a igũrũ matiũĩ, o na Mũriũ ndooĩ, tiga no Ithe wiki.
english: “But no one knows of that day and hour, not even the angels of heaven,‡ but my Father only.

kikuyu: Thuutha ũcio andũ-a-nja eerĩ a maraya magĩũka kũrĩ mũthamaki, makĩrũgama mbere yake.
english: Then two women who were prostitutes came to the king, and stood before him.

kikuyu: “Mũndũ angĩhithia mũndũ wa itũũra rĩake ndigiri, kana ndegwa, kana ngʼondu kana nyamũ o yothe amũigĩre, nayo ĩkue, kana ĩtihio, kana ĩiywo, hatarĩ mũndũ ũroona-rĩ,
english: “If a man delivers to his neighbor a donkey, an ox, a sheep, or any animal to keep, and it dies or is injured, or driven away, no man seeing it;

kikuyu: Mũtwe wake ũhaana ta thahabu theru mũno; njuĩrĩ yake ĩrĩ na mĩkumba, na ĩkaira ta ihuru.
english: His head is like the purest gold. His hair is bushy, black as a raven.

kikuyu: nake Ahija akĩoya nguo ĩyo njerũ eehumbĩte, akĩmĩtarũra icunjĩ ikũmi na igĩrĩ.
english: Ahijah took the new garment that was on him, and tore it in twelve pieces.

kikuyu: marokorererwo nĩ mwanangĩko matehaarĩirie, wabu ũcio manyambĩire ũromanyiita o ene, marogwa irima rĩu manyenjeire.
english: Let destruction come on him unawares. Let his net that he has hidden catch himself. Let him fall into that destruction.

kikuyu: Nacio mbaũ cia mĩratho, na buremu cia mĩrango, na thingo, na mĩrango ya hekarũ, agĩcigemia na thahabu, na agĩkururithia mĩhiano ya makerubi thingo-inĩ.
english: He also overlaid the house, the beams, the thresholds, its walls, and its doors with gold, and engraved cherubim on the walls.

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Esaũ akĩhũndũka mũthenya o ro ũcio agĩthiĩ arorete Seiru.
english: So Esau returned that day on his way to Seir.

kikuyu: Baraba aikĩtio njeera nĩ ũndũ wa kũrutithia ngũĩ itũũra-inĩ rĩu inene, na nĩ ũndũ wa kũũragana.)
english: one who was thrown into prison for a certain revolt in the city, and for murder.

kikuyu: Ũguo nĩguo ũkaamũra Alawii, ũmeherie kuuma kũrĩ andũ arĩa angĩ a Isiraeli, nao Alawii acio matuĩke akwa.
english: Thus you shall separate the Levites from among the children of Israel, and the Levites shall be mine.

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio magĩcookeria Jesũ atĩrĩ, “Ithuĩ tũtiũĩ.” Nake Jesũ akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “O na niĩ ndikũmwĩra njĩkaga maũndũ maya na ũhoti ũrĩkũ.”
english: They answered Jesus, “We don’t know.” Jesus said to them, “Neither will I tell you by what authority I do these things.”

kikuyu: hĩndĩ ĩyo nĩũkamaigua ũrĩ o kũu igũrũ, gĩikaro-inĩ gĩaku, na mũndũ ũcio wa kũngĩ ũkamũhingĩria ũrĩa wothe agakũhooya, nĩgeetha andũ othe a thĩ mamenye rĩĩtwa rĩaku na magwĩtigĩre o ta ũrĩa andũ aku a Isiraeli magwĩtigĩrĩte, na mamenye atĩ nyũmba ĩno njakĩte ĩĩtanĩtio na Rĩĩtwa rĩaku.
english: hear in heaven, your dwelling place, and do according to all that the foreigner calls to you for; that all the peoples of the earth may know your name, to fear you, as do your people Israel, and that they may know that this house which I have built is called by your name.

kikuyu: Nowe akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Nĩ niĩ; tigai gwĩtigĩra.”
english: But he said to them, “It is I.§ Don’t be afraid.”

kikuyu: “Kũrĩ andũ eerĩ maarĩ na thiirĩ wa mũndũ wakombithanagia mbeeca. Ũmwe aarĩ na thiirĩ wa dinari magana matano, na ũrĩa ũngĩ dinari mĩrongo ĩtano.
english: “A certain lender had two debtors. The one owed five hundred denarii, and the other fifty.

kikuyu: Rũthanju rwa mũndũ ũrĩa niĩ ngaathuura nĩrũgũthundũka, na niĩ ndĩyehererie kũnuguna gũkũ ũnugunagĩrwo hĩndĩ ciothe nĩ andũ a Isiraeli.”
english: It shall happen that the rod of the man whom I shall choose shall bud. I will make the murmurings of the children of Israel, which they murmur against you, cease from me.”

kikuyu: Nao atongoria amwe a nyũmba nĩmaheanire indo nĩ ũndũ wa wĩra ũcio, durakima 20,000† cia thahabu, na ratiri 2,200‡ cia betha, nacio ikĩigwo kĩgĩĩna-inĩ.
english: Some of the heads of fathers’ households gave into the treasury of the work twenty thousand darics of gold, and two thousand two hundred minas§ of silver.

kikuyu: Wee mwene, na Alawii, o na andũ a kũngĩ arĩa marĩ thĩinĩ wanyu, nĩmũgakenera maũndũ mothe mega marĩa Jehova Ngai wanyu amũheete na akahe andũ a nyũmba cianyu.
english: You shall rejoice in all the good which Yahweh your God has given to you, and to your house, you, and the Levite, and the foreigner who is among you.

kikuyu: Rĩrĩa mũraika aatam- bũrũkirie guoko gwake nĩguo aanange Jerusalemu-rĩ, Jehova akĩigua kĩeha nĩ ũndũ wa kĩnyariirĩko kĩu akĩĩra mũraika ũcio wanangaga andũ atĩrĩ, “Tigĩra hau! Eheria guoko gwaku.” Hĩndĩ ĩyo mũraika ũcio wa Jehova aarũgamĩte kĩhuhĩro-inĩ kĩa ngano kĩa Arauna, ũrĩa Mũjebusi.
english: When the angel stretched out his hand toward Jerusalem to destroy it, Yahweh relented of the disaster, and said to the angel who destroyed the people, “It is enough. Now withdraw your hand.” Yahweh’s angel was by the threshing floor of Araunah the Jebusite.

kikuyu: O rĩngĩ Jesũ nĩoimagarire agĩthiĩ hũgũrũrũ-inĩ cia iria; nakĩo kĩrĩndĩ kĩnene gĩgĩũka kũrĩ we, nake akĩambĩrĩria gũkĩruta ũhoro.
english: He went out again by the seaside. All the multitude came to him, and he taught them.

kikuyu: Nĩ kaba mũndũ akorwo na kĩndũ kĩnini na akorwo na ũhooreri, gũkĩra gwĩtungumania agĩe na indo nyingĩ, na gũtengʼeria rũhuho.
english: Better is a handful, with quietness, than two handfuls with labor and chasing after wind.

kikuyu: Itugĩ ciayo atumithĩtie cia betha, naguo mwĩtiiro wayo nĩ wa thahabu, gĩtĩ kĩayo kĩgemetio na gĩtambaya kĩa rangi wa ndathi, mwena wa thĩinĩ wayo ũgemetio na wendo nĩ aarĩ a Jerusalemu.
english: He made its pillars of silver, its bottom of gold, its seat of purple, the middle of it being paved with love, from the daughters of Jerusalem.

kikuyu: Magĩtharĩkĩra Juda, na magĩkũhũũra, na magĩkuua indo ciothe iria ciarĩ nyũmba ya mũthamaki, o hamwe na ariũ ake na atumia ake. Ndaatigĩirio mũriũ o na ũmwe o tiga Ahazia ũrĩa warĩ mũnini mũno.
english: and they came up against Judah, broke into it, and carried away all the possessions that were found in the king’s house, including his sons and his wives, so that there was no son left to him except Jehoahaz, the youngest of his sons.

kikuyu: Mĩromo ya arĩa athingu ĩhũnagia andũ aingĩ, no andũ akĩĩgu makuaga nĩ kwaga gũkũũrana maũndũ.
english: The lips of the righteous feed many, but the foolish die for lack of understanding.

kikuyu: Ũgeeka o ũguo, ũngĩkoona ndigiri ya mũrũ wa thoguo, kana kabuti gake, kana o kĩndũ gĩothe atete, ndũkanetue ta ũtacionete.
english: So you shall do with his donkey. So you shall do with his garment. So you shall do with every lost thing of your brother’s, which he has lost and you have found. You may not hide yourself.

kikuyu: Mũthenya ũcio, Jehova akĩrĩĩkanĩra na Aburamu kĩrĩkanĩro, akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Nĩndahe njiaro ciaku bũrũri ũyũ, kuuma rũũĩ rwa Misiri nginya rũũĩ rũrĩa rũnene rwa Farati:
english: In that day Yahweh made a covenant with Abram, saying, “I have given this land to your offspring, from the river of Egypt to the great river, the river Euphrates:

kikuyu: Na maarĩkia kũringa mũrĩmo ũrĩa ũngĩ, magĩkinya bũrũri wa Genesareti.
english: When they had crossed over, they came to the land of Gennesaret.

kikuyu: na to ũndũ wa gũũka gwake gwiki, no ningĩ nĩ ũndũ wa ũhoorerania ũrĩa mwamũheete. Nĩatũheire ũhoro wa ũrĩa mwendaga kũnyona, na kĩeha kĩnene kĩrĩa mũrĩ nakĩo, o na ũrĩa mwĩtangaga mũno nĩ ũndũ wakwa; nĩ ũndũ ũcio gĩkeno gĩakwa gĩkĩongerereka gũkĩra mbere.
english: and not by his coming only, but also by the comfort with which he was comforted in you while he told us of your longing, your mourning, and your zeal for me, so that I rejoiced still more.

kikuyu: Nake akĩona mũkũyũ mũkĩra wa njĩra, agĩthiĩ harĩ guo, no ndaigana kuona kĩndũ thĩinĩ waguo tiga o mathangũ. Nake akĩwĩra atĩrĩ, “Ũroaga gũgaaciara maciaro rĩngĩ!” O hĩndĩ ĩyo mũtĩ ũcio ũkĩhoha.
english: Seeing a fig tree by the road, he came to it and found nothing on it but leaves. He said to it, “Let there be no fruit from you forever!” Immediately the fig tree withered away.

kikuyu: Na mũndũ mũrũme wothe thĩinĩ wa nyũmba ya Iburahĩmu, arĩa maciarĩirwo gwake, kana mũndũ wagũrĩtwo kuuma kũrĩ mũndũ wa kũngĩ, maaruanĩire hamwe nake.
english: All the men of his house, those born in the house, and those bought with money from a foreigner, were circumcised with him.

kikuyu: Ningĩ mahinda manyu ma gũkena, nĩmo ma ciathĩ cianyu iria njathane, na ciathĩ cia Karũgamo ka Mweri, nĩ mũrĩhuhaga tũrumbeta nĩ ũndũ wa maruta ma njino na maruta ma ũiguano, na nĩmo magaatuĩka kĩririkano kĩanyu mbere ya Ngai wanyu. Niĩ nĩ niĩ Jehova Ngai Wanyu.” Andũ a Isiraeli Kuuma Sinai
english: “Also in the day of your gladness, and in your set feasts, and in the beginnings of your months, you shall blow the trumpets over your burnt offerings, and over the sacrifices of your peace offerings; and they shall be to you for a memorial before your God. I am Yahweh your God.”

kikuyu: Na inyuĩ maithe-rĩ, mũtikarakaragie ciana cianyu; handũ ha ũguo-rĩ, cireragei mũgĩcirutaga na mũgĩcitaaraga thĩinĩ wa Mwathani. Ngombo na Aathani
english: You fathers, don’t provoke your children to wrath, but nurture them in the discipline and instruction of the Lord.

kikuyu: agĩtoonya mũciĩ gwa Zakaria, na akĩgeithia Elizabethi.
english: and entered into the house of Zacharias and greeted Elizabeth.

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa magaathiĩ na ndũũru ciao cia mbũri na cia ngʼombe kũrongooria Jehova-rĩ, matikamuona; we nĩamehereire.
english: They will go with their flocks and with their herds to seek Yahweh, but they won’t find him. He has withdrawn himself from them.

kikuyu: atĩ nĩagatũhonokia kuuma guoko-inĩ gwa thũ ciitũ, na atũhotithie kũmũtungatagĩra tũtarĩ na guoya,
english: to grant to us that we, being delivered out of the hand of our enemies, should serve him without fear,

kikuyu: Rongoriai Jehova, mũtũũre muoyo, ndakae gũtuthũkĩra nyũmba ya Jusufu ta mwaki; naguo mwaki ũcio nĩũkaniinana,
english: Seek Yahweh, and you will live, lest he break out like fire in the house of Joseph, and it devour, and there be no one to quench it in Bethel.

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio-rĩ, andũ a Isiraeli othe nĩmakahonokio, o ta ũrĩa kwandĩkĩtwo atĩrĩ:
english: and so all Israel will be saved. Even as it is written, “There will come out of Zion the Deliverer, and he will turn away ungodliness from Jacob.

kikuyu: Ihinda rĩa mĩthenya mũgwanja, mũkaarĩĩaga mĩgate ĩtarĩ mĩĩkĩre ndawa ya kũimbia. Mũthenya wa mbere mũkeeheria ndawa ya kũimbia kuuma nyũmba cianyu,† nĩgũkorwo mũndũ o wothe ũngĩrĩa kĩndũ kĩrĩ ndawa ya kũimbia kuuma mũthenya wa mbere nginya wa mũgwanja-rĩ, nĩakaingatwo eherio harĩ andũ a Isiraeli.
english: “ ‘Seven days you shall eat unleavened bread; even the first day you shall put away yeast out of your houses, for whoever eats leavened bread from the first day until the seventh day, that soul shall be cut off from Israel.

kikuyu: O kũrĩa guothe Roho aacierekagĩria, nokuo ciathiiaga, namo magũrũ macio magookĩra magatwarana nacio, tondũ roho wa ciũmbe icio irĩ muoyo warĩ thĩinĩ wa magũrũ macio.
english: Wherever the spirit was to go, they went. The spirit was to go there. The wheels were lifted up beside them; for the spirit of the living creature was in the wheels.

kikuyu: Nĩmetĩĩagĩra ũgemu wa mathaga mao, na makamahũthĩra gũthondeka mĩhianano yao ĩrĩ magigi, na mĩhiano ĩtarĩ kĩene. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio-rĩ, nĩngatũma indo icio ituĩke kĩndũ kĩrĩ thaahu harĩo.
english: As for the beauty of his ornament, he set it in majesty; but they made the images of their abominations and their detestable things therein. Therefore I have made it to them as an unclean thing.

kikuyu: No inyuĩ ingĩmũhe ngano ĩrĩa njega mũno mũrĩĩage; ingĩmũhũũnagia na ũũkĩ wa kuuma rwaro-inĩ rwa ihiga.”
english: But he would have also fed them with the finest of the wheat. I will satisfy you with honey out of the rock.”

kikuyu: na thaani ĩmwe nene ya thahabu ya ũritũ wa cekeri ikũmi, ĩiyũrĩtio ũbumba;
english: one golden ladle of ten shekels, full of incense;

kikuyu: Ũhithĩte ũthiũ waku nĩkĩ, na ũkariganĩrwo nĩ mathĩĩna maitũ na kũhinyĩrĩrio gwitũ?
english: Why do you hide your face, and forget our affliction and our oppression?

kikuyu: Ha Ũhoro-ũrĩa-Mwega-rĩ, andũ a Isiraeli nĩmatuirwo thũ nĩ ũndũ wanyu, no ha ũhoro wa gũthuurwo-rĩ, nĩmendetwo nĩ ũndũ wa maithe mao ma tene,
english: Concerning the Good News, they are enemies for your sake. But concerning the election, they are beloved for the fathers’ sake.

kikuyu: Rĩrĩa Asuriata a kuuma Dameski mookire gũteithia Hadadezeri mũthamaki wa Zoba, Daudi akĩũraga Asuriata ngiri mĩrongo ĩĩrĩ na igĩrĩ.
english: When the Syrians of Damascus came to help Hadadezer king of Zobah, David struck twenty two thousand men of the Syrians.

kikuyu: “Wee Jehova, guoko gwaku kwa ũrĩo kwarĩ gwa kũgegania, gũkagĩa hinya. Wee Jehova, guoko gwaku kwa ũrĩo nĩ gwathuthangire thũ.
english: Your right hand, Yahweh, is glorious in power. Your right hand, Yahweh, dashes the enemy in pieces.

kikuyu: No andũ arĩa othe aaganu na arĩa a haaro thĩinĩ wa arũmĩrĩri a Daudi makiuga atĩrĩ, “Tondũ matiathiire hamwe na ithuĩ-rĩ, tũtikũgayana nao indo cia gũtahwo iria tũcookithĩtie. No rĩrĩ, o mũndũ no oe mũtumia wake na ciana athiĩ.”
english: Then all the wicked men and worthless fellows of those who went with David answered and said, “Because they didn’t go with us, we will not give them anything of the plunder that we have recovered, except to every man his wife and his children, that he may lead them away and depart.”

kikuyu: Johana akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Mũrutani, nĩtũronire mũndũ akĩingata ndaimono thĩinĩ wa rĩĩtwa rĩaku tũramwĩra atige, tondũ ti ũmwe witũ.”
english: John said to him, “Teacher, we saw someone who doesn’t follow us casting out demons in your name; and we forbade him, because he doesn’t follow us.”

kikuyu: Atĩrĩrĩ ariũ na aarĩ a Ithe witũ, mũndũ ũmwe wanyu angĩagaga oime ũhoro-inĩ wa ma nake mũndũ ũngĩ amũgarũre-rĩ,
english: Brothers, if any among you wanders from the truth and someone turns him back,

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo mũndũ wothe wĩkaga ũrĩa Baba ũrĩa ũrĩ igũrũ endaga, ũcio nĩwe mũrũ na mwarĩ wa maitũ, na nĩ we maitũ.”
english: For whoever does the will of my Father who is in heaven, he is my brother, and sister, and mother.”

kikuyu: Shalumu aarĩ mũriũ wa Shauli, nake Mibisamu aarĩ mũriũ wa Shalumu, nake Mishima aarĩ mũriũ wa Mibisamu.
english: Shallum his son, Mibsam his son, and Mishma his son.

kikuyu: “Ĩra andũ a Isiraeli mandehere iruta. Nĩwe ũkwamũkĩra iruta rĩu handũ hakwa kuuma kũrĩ mũndũ o wothe ũrĩa ngoro yake ĩkwenda kũheana.
english: “Speak to the children of Israel, that they take an offering for me. From everyone whose heart makes him willing you shall take my offering.

kikuyu: Athĩnjĩri-Ngai matikanarĩe nyamũ o na ĩrĩkũ ĩngĩkorwo ĩkuĩte, ĩrĩ nyoni, kana matigari ma nyamũ yũragĩtwo nĩ nyamũ ĩngĩ.
english: The priests shall not eat of anything that dies of itself or is torn, whether it is bird or animal.

kikuyu: Ngĩcooka ngĩtiira maitho, na rĩrĩ, hau mbere yakwa ngĩona andũ-a-nja eerĩ, mareretio nĩ rũhuho; tondũ maarĩ na mathagu ta ma njũũ, makĩoya gĩkabũ kĩu magĩgĩtwara rĩera-inĩ.
english: Then I lifted up my eyes and saw, and behold, there were two women; and the wind was in their wings. Now they had wings like the wings of a stork, and they lifted up the ephah basket between earth and the sky.

kikuyu: mũhaandĩtwo na mũgaakwo thĩinĩ wake, na mũkĩongagĩrĩrwo hinya thĩinĩ wa wĩtĩkio o ta ũrĩa mwarutirwo, na mũkĩrĩrĩrie gũcookia ngaatho.
english: rooted and built up in him and established in the faith, even as you were taught, abounding in it in thanksgiving.

kikuyu: “Ũguo noguo gũkahaana mũthenya ũrĩa Mũrũ wa Mũndũ akaaguũranĩrio.
english: It will be the same way in the day that the Son of Man is revealed.

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio, inyuĩ arata akwa nyendete mũno, kuona atĩ nĩmwĩrĩgĩrĩire ũndũ ũyũ-rĩ, kĩĩrutanĩriei mũkoragwo mũtarĩ na kĩmeni, na mũtarĩ na ũcuuke, na mũrĩ na thayũ nake.
english: Therefore, beloved, seeing that you look for these things, be diligent to be found in peace, without defect and blameless in his sight.

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo Zedekia mũthamaki wa Juda nĩamuohithĩtie kũu, akoiga atĩrĩ, “Ũtũrathagĩra ũguo nĩkĩ? uugaga atĩrĩ, ‘Jehova ekuuga atĩrĩ: Ndĩ hakuhĩ kũneana itũũra rĩĩrĩ inene kũrĩ mũthamaki wa Babuloni, nake nĩakarĩĩnyitĩra.
english: For Zedekiah king of Judah had shut him up, saying, “Why do you prophesy, and say, ‘Yahweh says, “Behold, I will give this city into the hand of the king of Babylon, and he will take it;

kikuyu: Ti-itherũ nĩoĩ andũ arĩa maheenanagia; na rĩrĩa oona ũndũ mũũru-rĩ, githĩ ndaũmenyaga?
english: For he knows false men. He sees iniquity also, even though he doesn’t consider it.

kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, Daudi ndangĩathiire mbere yakĩo gũtuĩria ũhoro harĩ Ngai tondũ nĩetigagĩra rũhiũ rwa njora rwa mũraika ũcio wa Jehova.
english: But David couldn’t go before it to inquire of God, for he was afraid because of the sword of Yahweh’s angel.

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo nĩguo andũ arĩa maikaraga hũgũrũrũ-inĩ ici cia iria makoiga atĩrĩ, ‘Onei ũrĩa kũhanĩte kũrĩ arĩa tũtũire twĩhokete, acio tuorĩire kũrĩ o atĩ nĩguo matũteithie na matũhonokie kuuma kũrĩ mũthamaki wa Ashuri! Rĩu tũngĩkĩhonoka atĩa?’ ”
english: The inhabitants of this coast land will say in that day, ‘Behold, this is our expectation, where we fled for help to be delivered from the king of Assyria. And we, how will we escape?’ ”

kikuyu: Jehova akĩandĩka Maathani macio Ikũmi ihengere-inĩ icio, o ta ũrĩa maandĩkĩtwo hĩndĩ ya mbere, o marĩa aamwarĩirie kũu kĩrĩma-inĩ arĩ thĩinĩ wa mwaki, mũthenya ũrĩa mwagomanĩte. Jehova agĩcooka akĩĩnengera ihengere icio.
english: He wrote on the tablets, according to the first writing, the ten commandments, which Yahweh spoke to you on the mountain out of the middle of the fire in the day of the assembly; and Yahweh gave them to me.

kikuyu: Nake Gaali mũrũ wa Ebedi nĩoimagarĩte akarũgama itoonyero-inĩ rĩa kĩhingo gĩa itũũra inene, o hĩndĩ ĩyo Abimeleku na thigari ciake moimagĩra kũrĩa meehithĩte.
english: Gaal the son of Ebed went out, and stood in the entrance of the gate of the city. Abimelech rose up, and the people who were with him, from the ambush.

kikuyu: kũgeria kũmũgirĩrĩria nĩ ta kũgeria kũgirĩrĩria rũhuho, kana kũgeria gũkumbatĩria maguta na guoko.
english: restraining her is like restraining the wind, or like grasping oil in his right hand.

kikuyu: Rĩrĩa mũraika ũcio wamwarĩirie aathiire, Korinelio agĩĩta ndungata ciake igĩrĩ na mũthigari ũmwe weamũrĩire Ngai, na aarĩ ũmwe wa andũ arĩa maamũtungatagĩra.
english: When the angel who spoke to him had departed, Cornelius called two of his household servants and a devout soldier of those who waited on him continually.

kikuyu: Ariũ a Ezara maarĩ: Jetheru, na Meredi, na Eferi, na Jaloni. Mũtumia ũmwe wa Meredi nĩwe waciarire Miriamu, na Shamai, na Ishiba ũrĩa warĩ ithe wa Eshitemoa.
english: The sons of Ezrah: Jether, Mered, Epher, and Jalon; and Mered’s wife bore Miriam, Shammai, and Ishbah the father of Eshtemoa.

kikuyu: Angĩkaaruta gatũrũme-rĩ, nĩagakaneana mbere ya Jehova.
english: If he offers a lamb for his offering, then he shall offer it before Yahweh;

kikuyu: a rĩrĩ, Elija ũrĩa Mũtishibi kuuma Tishibi, thĩinĩ wa Gileadi, akĩĩra Ahabu atĩrĩ, “O ta ũrĩa Jehova, Ngai wa Isiraeli, atũũraga muoyo, ũrĩa niĩ ndungatagĩra-rĩ, mĩaka ĩrĩa ĩgũũka gũtigũcooka kũgĩa ime kana mbura, tiga na ũndũ wa kiugo gĩakwa.”
english: lijah the Tishbite, who was one of the settlers of Gilead, said to Ahab, “As Yahweh, the God of Israel, lives, before whom I stand, there shall not be dew nor rain these years, but according to my word.”

kikuyu: Mũtikanagerie Jehova Ngai wanyu ta ũrĩa mwekire kũu Masa.
english: You shall not tempt Yahweh your God, as you tempted him in Massah.

kikuyu: “ ‘Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Mwathani Jehova ekuuga atĩrĩ: Nĩngarekereria rũhuho rũrĩ na hinya ndĩ na mangʼũrĩ, na kũgĩe mbura ya mbembe na mbura ya kĩboboto ndĩ na mathũgũta ma kwananga.
english: “ ‘Therefore the Lord Yahweh says: “I will even tear it with a stormy wind in my wrath. There will be an overflowing shower in my anger, and great hailstones in wrath to consume it.

kikuyu: Mũndũ o wothe ũrĩa ũthũire mũrũ kana mwarĩ wa ithe-rĩ, ũcio nĩ mũũragani, na inyuĩ nĩmũũĩ atĩ gũtirĩ mũũragani ũrĩ na muoyo wa tene na tene.
english: Whoever hates his brother is a murderer, and you know that no murderer has eternal life remaining in him.

kikuyu: Mũtikanareke ndeto njũru ciume tũnua-inĩ twanyu, tiga o iria cia gwaka andũ arĩa angĩ kũringana na mabata mao, nĩguo igunage arĩa marĩciiguaga.
english: Let no corrupt speech proceed out of your mouth, but only what is good for building others up as the need may be, that it may give grace to those who hear.

kikuyu: Ningĩ hau igũrũ rĩa mwena wa nja ya itoonyero rĩa gũtoonya thĩinĩ wa handũ-harĩa-haamũre, na igũrũ rĩa thingo matĩĩna maiganaine kũrigiicĩria mwena wa thĩinĩ na wa nja ya handũ-harĩa-haamũre
english: to the space above the door, even to the inner house, and outside, and by all the wall all around inside and outside, by measure.

kikuyu: No mũrutwo ũmwe wa Jesũ wetagwo Judasi Isikariota, ũrĩa wacookire kũmũkunyanĩra, akĩregana na ũndũ ũcio, akiuga atĩrĩ,
english: Then Judas Iscariot, Simon’s son, one of his disciples, who would betray him, said,

kikuyu: akĩmataarĩria na akĩmaiguithia atĩ kwarĩ o nginya Kristũ anyariirĩke na ariũke kuuma kũrĩ arĩa akuũ. Akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Jesũ ũyũ ndĩramũhe ũhoro wake- rĩ, nĩwe Kristũ.”
english: explaining and demonstrating that the Christ had to suffer and rise again from the dead, and saying, “This Jesus, whom I proclaim to you, is the Christ.”

kikuyu: “ ‘Andũ aya maaheaga gĩtĩĩo na mĩromo, no ngoro ciao irĩ kũraya na niĩ.
english: ‘These people draw near to me with their mouth, and honor me with their lips; but their heart is far from me.

kikuyu: No kũringana na ũrĩa eeranĩire-rĩ, nĩtwĩrĩgĩrĩire igũrũ rĩerũ na thĩ njerũ, o kuo kũrĩa gũtũũraga ũthingu.
english: But, according to his promise, we look for new heavens and a new earth, in which righteousness dwells.

kikuyu: Andũ acio magĩthiĩ mbere magĩtweterera kũu Teroa.
english: But these had gone ahead, and were waiting for us at Troas.

kikuyu: na wa gatatũ makĩmũhikia, na makĩrũmanĩrĩra ũguo nginya wa mũgwanja; othe magĩkua, makĩmũtiga atarĩ na ciana.
english: The third took her, and likewise the seven all left no children, and died.

kikuyu: Gũtheria gĩthiithi nĩkũrehagĩra ngoro gĩkeno, naguo ũhoro ũrĩa mwega nĩwĩkĩraga mahĩndĩ hinya.
english: The light of the eyes rejoices the heart. Good news gives health to the bones.

kikuyu: Kaĩ nĩũgaakorwo ũrĩ ũndũ mwega harĩ ndungata ĩyo, rĩrĩa mwathi wayo agooka akore ĩgĩĩka o ũguo-ĩ!
english: Blessed is that servant whom his lord finds doing so when he comes.

kikuyu: “ ‘Ningĩ andũ a Dedani nĩo mwonjorithanagia nao matandĩko ma mbarathi.
english: “ ‘ “Dedan was your merchant in precious saddle blankets for riding.

kikuyu: Ningĩ Elisha akĩũria Gehazi atĩrĩ, “Mũtumia ũyũ angĩĩkĩrwo atĩa?” Gehazi akiuga atĩrĩ, “Ndarĩ mwana wa kahĩĩ, na mũthuuriwe nĩ mũkũrũ.”
english: He said, “What then is to be done for her?” Gehazi answered, “Most certainly she has no son, and her husband is old.”

kikuyu: Kuona atĩ nĩatũũgĩrĩtio guoko-inĩ kwa ũrĩo kwa Ngai, na nĩamũkĩrire kĩĩranĩro kĩa Roho Mũtheru kuuma kũrĩ Ithe, nake nĩatũitĩrĩirie Roho ta ũrĩa mũrona rĩu na mũkaigua.
english: Being therefore exalted by the right hand of God, and having received from the Father the promise of the Holy Spirit, he has poured out this which you now see and hear.

kikuyu: “Niĩ-rĩ, kaĩ mateta makwa meerekeirio mũndũ? Ngũkĩaga kũremwo nĩ gũkirĩrĩria nĩkĩ?
english: As for me, is my complaint to man? Why shouldn’t I be impatient?

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo Jethero, mũthoni-we wa Musa, akĩrehe maruta ma njino na magongona mangĩ ma kũrutĩra Ngai, nake Harũni agĩũka marĩ na athuuri othe a Isiraeli nĩguo marĩĩanĩre irio na mũthoni-we wa Musa, marĩ mbere ya Ngai.
english: Jethro, Moses’ father-in-law, took a burnt offering and sacrifices for God. Aaron came with all the elders of Israel, to eat bread with Moses’ father-in-law before God.

kikuyu: Andũ acio maaikarĩtio mbere yake kũringana na ũkũrũ wao, kuuma irigithathi nginya ũrĩa warĩ mũnini biũ; nao makĩrorana o mũndũ na mũndũ nĩ kũgega.
english: They sat before him, the firstborn according to his birthright, and the youngest according to his youth, and the men marveled with one another.

kikuyu: nake Obedi aarĩ ithe wa Jesii, nake Jesii aarĩ ithe wa Daudi.
english: and Obed became the father of Jesse, and Jesse became the father of David.

kikuyu: Mũthenya ũrĩa ũkoima kuo ũringe Mũkuru wa Kidironi, ũmenye wega no ũgaakua; nayo thakame yaku nĩĩgagũcookerera wee mwene.”
english: For on the day you go out and pass over the brook Kidron, know for certain that you will surely die. Your blood will be on your own head.”

kikuyu: Nowe Loti akĩmaringĩrĩria na hinya mũno mathiĩ gwake mũciĩ, nginya magĩtĩkĩra. Magĩthiĩ nake, magĩtoonya nyũmba gwake. Nake akĩmathondekera irio, akĩruga mĩgate ĩtaarĩ ndawa ya kũimbia, nao makĩrĩa.
english: He urged them greatly, and they came in with him, and entered into his house. He made them a feast, and baked unleavened bread, and they ate.

kikuyu: Kundĩka ciana mĩrongo ĩtano mũthia-inĩ wa itambaya cia gũcuurio cia mwena ũmwe, ningĩ wĩke o ũguo mũthia-inĩ wa itambaya icio cia mwena ũrĩa ũngĩ.
english: You shall make fifty loops on the edge of the one curtain that is outermost in the coupling, and fifty loops on the edge of the curtain which is outermost in the second coupling.

kikuyu: Nĩmahutatĩire makaaga kũhũgũra mothiũ mao kũrĩ niĩ; o na gũtuĩka nĩndamarutire kaingĩ kaingĩ, matiathikĩrĩirie kana magĩtĩkĩra gũtaarwo.
english: They have turned their backs to me, and not their faces. Although I taught them, rising up early and teaching them, yet they have not listened to receive instruction.

kikuyu: Iburahĩmu agĩcookia atĩrĩ, “Mũrũ wakwa, Ngai we mwene nĩekũheana gatũrũme ka igongona rĩa njino.” Nao eerĩ magĩthiĩ na mbere marĩ hamwe.
english: Abraham said, “God will provide himself the lamb for a burnt offering, my son.” So they both went together.

kikuyu: tondũ andũ arĩa othe matongoragio nĩ Roho wa Ngai acio nĩo ciana cia Ngai.
english: For as many as are led by the Spirit of God, these are children of God.

kikuyu: Nao maarĩkia gwakia mwaki o nja gatagatĩ na gũikara thĩ hamwe, Petero nake agĩikara thĩ hamwe nao.
english: When they had kindled a fire in the middle of the courtyard and had sat down together, Peter sat among them.

kikuyu: ĩrĩa Isiraeli moimire bũrũri wa Misiri, nyũmba ya Jakubu ĩkiuma kũrĩ andũ a rũthiomi rũngĩ-rĩ,
english: hen Israel went out of Egypt, the house of Jacob from a people of foreign language,

kikuyu: Tondũ kwenda mbeeca nĩkĩo kĩhumo kĩa mĩthemba yothe ya ũũru. Andũ amwe nĩ ũndũ wa kwenda mbeeca mũno-rĩ, nĩmatigĩte wĩtĩkio, na magetheeca ngoro na kĩeha kĩingĩ. Paũlũ Gũtaara Timotheo
english: For the love of money is a root of all kinds of evil. Some have been led astray from the faith in their greed, and have pierced themselves through with many sorrows.

kikuyu: Jehova o we wiki nĩwe wamũtongoririe; gũtiarĩ ngai ngʼeni yarĩ hamwe nake.
english: Yahweh alone led him. There was no foreign god with him.

kikuyu: Nĩngeheria ngaari cia ita kuuma Efiraimu, na njeherie mbarathi cia mbaara kuuma Jerusalemu, naguo ũta wa mbaara-rĩ, nĩũkoinangwo. Nake aanĩrĩre thayũ ndũrĩrĩ-inĩ. Wathani wake-rĩ, ũgaatambũrũka kuuma iria rĩmwe nginya rĩrĩa rĩngĩ, na kuuma Rũũĩ rwa Farati o nginya ituri cia thĩ.
english: I will cut off the chariot from Ephraim and the horse from Jerusalem. The battle bow will be cut off; and he will speak peace to the nations. His dominion will be from sea to sea, and from the River to the ends of the earth.

kikuyu: Na nĩmaikĩtie ngai ciao mwaki-inĩ na magacithũkangia, tondũ itiarĩ ngai no ciarĩ mĩtĩ na mahiga, nacio ciathondeketwo na moko ma andũ.
english: and have cast their gods into the fire; for they were no gods, but the work of men’s hands, wood and stone; therefore they have destroyed them.

kikuyu: Kuuma kũrĩ njiaro cia Harimu nĩ: Maaseia, na Elija, na Shemaia, na Jehieli, na Uzia.
english: Of the sons of Harim: Maaseiah, Elijah, Shemaiah, Jehiel, and Uzziah.

kikuyu: Kuuma ũkengi ũrĩa warĩ harĩ we, mbura ya mbembe na heni igũtũrĩkanĩria matu-inĩ make.
english: At the brightness before him his thick clouds passed, hailstones and coals of fire.

kikuyu: mũngĩaga kũhinyĩrĩria mũndũ wa kũngĩ, kana mwana wa ngoriai, kana mũtumia wa ndigwa-rĩ, na mwage gũitithia thakame ya mũndũ ũteehĩtie kũndũ gũkũ-rĩ, o na ningĩ mwage kwĩgerithia ngero nĩ ũndũ wa kũrũmĩrĩra ngai ingĩ-rĩ,
english: if you don’t oppress the foreigner, the fatherless, and the widow, and don’t shed innocent blood in this place, and don’t walk after other gods to your own hurt,

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo no ngĩe na ũndũ wa kũũhooreria, ũndũ wa gĩkeno ruo-inĩ rũrũ rũtarathira, atĩ niĩ ndikaanĩte ciugo cia Ũrĩa Mũtheru.
english: Let it still be my consolation, yes, let me exult in pain that doesn’t spare, that I have not denied the words of the Holy One.

kikuyu: Kwarooka gũkĩa-rĩ, Balaki akĩoya Balamu makĩambata nginya Bamothu-Baali,* na marĩ kũu Balamu akĩona gĩcunjĩ kĩmwe kĩa andũ acio.
english: In the morning, Balak took Balaam, and brought him up into the high places of Baal; and he saw from there part of the people.

kikuyu: Andũ a kuuma Benjamini, ta ũrĩa maandĩkĩtwo maandĩko-inĩ ma njiarwa ciao, maarĩ 956. Arũme acio othe maarĩ atongoria a nyũmba ciao.
english: and their brothers, according to their generations, nine hundred fifty-six. All these men were heads of fathers’ households by their fathers’ houses.

kikuyu: Nĩndarutire watho na ũhoro ũcio ũgĩtuĩrio, na gũkĩoneka atĩ itũũra rĩĩrĩ inene nĩrĩũĩkaine kuuma tene atĩ nĩ rĩa kũregana na watho wa athamaki, na rĩtũũraga rĩrĩ iremi, na nĩ rĩa kũringĩrĩria andũ mookĩrĩre thirikari.†
english: I decreed, and search has been made, and it was found that this city has made insurrection against kings in the past, and that rebellion and revolts have been made in it.

kikuyu: Nĩngũtũma ũciare mũno; na ndũme ũtuĩke ithe wa ndũrĩrĩ nyingĩ, nao athamaki nĩmakoima thĩinĩ waku.
english: I will make you exceedingly fruitful, and I will make nations of you. Kings will come out of you.

kikuyu: Kaĩ arĩa maguucagia mehia na mĩhĩndo ya maheeni marĩ na haaro-ĩ! o acio maguucagia waganu na mĩkanda ta ĩrĩa ĩguucagia ngaari cia ngʼombe,
english: Woe to those who draw iniquity with cords of falsehood, and wickedness as with cart rope,

kikuyu: Ningĩ ngĩigua kĩndũ kĩarurumire ta mũgambo ũkuuma gatagatĩ-inĩ ga ciũmbe icio inya irĩ muoyo, ũkiuga atĩrĩ, “Kĩbaba kĩa ngano kiumage mũcaara wa mũthenya ũmwe, na ibaba ithatũ cia ngano ya cairi ciumage mũcara wa mũthenya ũmwe, no ndũgathũkie maguta kana ũthũkie ndibei!”
english: I heard a voice in the middle of the four living creatures saying, “A choenix† of wheat for a denarius, and three choenix of barley for a denarius! Don’t damage the oil and the wine!”

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo makĩĩra Jeremia atĩrĩ, “Jehova arotuĩka mũira wa ma na mwĩhokeku wa gũtũũkĩrĩra, tũngĩkaaga gwĩka kũringana na ũrĩa wothe Jehova Ngai waku agaagũtũma ũtwĩre.
english: Then they said to Jeremiah, “May Yahweh be a true and faithful witness among us, if we don’t do according to all the word with which Yahweh your God sends you to tell us.

kikuyu: “ ‘Mũthenya ũcio-rĩ, nĩngaahe Gogu handũ ha gũthikanwo kũu bũrũri-inĩ wa Isiraeli, gĩtuamba- inĩ kĩa agendi arĩa mathiiaga mwena wa irathĩro werekeire Iria rĩa Cumbĩ. Nao agendi magirĩrĩrio gũcooka kũgera njĩra ĩyo, tondũ Gogu na kĩrĩndĩ gĩake nĩkuo magaathikwo. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio hageetagwo Gĩtuamba kĩa Hamoni-Gogu.
english: “ ‘ “It will happen in that day, that I will give to Gog a place for burial in Israel, the valley of those who pass through on the east of the sea; and it will stop those who pass through. They will bury Gog and all his multitude there, and they will call it ‘The valley of Hamon Gog’.

kikuyu: O na hĩndĩ ya gũtheka ngoro no ĩkorwo ĩgĩtuura, na gĩkeno no kĩrigĩrĩrie na kĩeha.
english: Even in laughter the heart may be sorrowful, and mirth may end in heaviness.

kikuyu: wa kana aarĩ Adonija ũrĩa waciarĩtwo nĩ Hagithu; wa gatano aarĩ Shefatia ũrĩa waciarĩtwo nĩ Abitali;
english: and the fourth, Adonijah the son of Haggith; and the fifth, Shephatiah the son of Abital;

kikuyu: “Tara Amerari kũringana na mbarĩ na nyũmba ciao.
english: “As for the sons of Merari, you shall count them by their families, by their fathers’ houses;

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo hatĩka ndĩkunũkaga kuuma thĩ, kana thĩĩna ũkamera tĩĩri-inĩ.
english: For affliction doesn’t come out of the dust, neither does trouble spring out of the ground;

kikuyu: Rĩu nĩnjigĩirwo thũmbĩ ĩrĩa ya ũthingu, ĩrĩa Mwathani, ũrĩa ũtuaga ciira na kĩhooto, akaahe mũthenya ũcio, na to niĩ nyiki akaahe, no ningĩ nĩ andũ othe arĩa makoretwo merirĩirie kũguũranĩrio gwake. Maũndũ ma Paũlũ Mwene
english: From now on, the crown of righteousness is stored up for me, which the Lord, the righteous judge, will give to me on that day; and not to me only, but also to all those who have loved his appearing.

kikuyu: Mwĩmenyererei, nĩgeetha ũhoro ũrĩa anabii maaririe ndũkanamũkore, rĩrĩa moigire atĩrĩ:
english: Beware therefore, lest that come on you which is spoken in the prophets:

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo rĩrĩa rĩothe mũkũrĩa mũgate ũyũ na mũkanyuĩra gĩkombe gĩkĩ-rĩ, nĩkuumbũra mũkuumbũra gũkua kwa Mwathani o nginya hĩndĩ ĩrĩa agooka.
english: For as often as you eat this bread and drink this cup, you proclaim the Lord’s death until he comes.

kikuyu: Jehova nĩeerĩte Musa atĩrĩ, “Firaũni nĩakarega gũkũigua, nĩgeetha morirũ makwa gũkũ bũrũri wa Misiri mongerereke.”
english: Yahweh said to Moses, “Pharaoh won’t listen to you, that my wonders may be multiplied in the land of Egypt.”

kikuyu: “Ũcio nĩguo bũrũri ũrĩa mũkaagayania ũtuĩke igai rĩa mĩhĩrĩga ya Isiraeli, nacio icio nĩcio igaatuĩka icigo ciao,” ũguo nĩguo Mwathani Jehova ekuuga. Ihingo cia Itũũra Inene
english: “This is the land which you shall divide by lot to the tribes of Israel for inheritance, and these are their several portions, says the Lord Yahweh.

kikuyu: Nĩwe waakire Elathu rĩngĩ, na akĩrĩcookeria Juda thuutha wa Amazia kũhurũka na maithe make. Jeroboamu wa Keerĩ Mũthamaki wa Isiraeli
english: He built Elath and restored it to Judah. After that the king slept with his fathers.

kikuyu: Mahambaataga nduma-inĩ kũrĩa gũtarĩ ũtheri; ningĩ atũmaga matũgũũge ta mũndũ mũrĩĩu.
english: They grope in the dark without light. He makes them stagger like a drunken man.

kikuyu: Kaĩ mũrĩ na haaro inyuĩ hĩndĩ ĩrĩa andũ othe mekũmũgaathĩrĩria-ĩ, nĩgũkorwo ũguo noguo maithe mao meekaga anabii a maheeni. Kwenda Thũ
english: Woe,† when‡ men speak well of you, for their fathers did the same thing to the false prophets.

kikuyu: Epafara, ũrĩa arĩ ũmwe wanyu na ndungata ya Kristũ Jesũ, nĩamũgeithia. We nĩamũhooyagĩra mũno hĩndĩ ciothe, nĩguo mũhote kwĩhaanda wega ũhoro-inĩ wothe wa kũhingia wendi wa Ngai, mũrĩ agima na mũrĩ na kĩĩrĩgĩrĩro gĩkinyanĩru.
english: Epaphras, who is one of you, a servant of Christ, salutes you, always striving for you in his prayers, that you may stand perfect and complete in all the will of God.

kikuyu: Nake akiuga na mũgambo mũnene atĩrĩ, “Mwĩtigĩrei Ngai na mũmũgooce we, tondũ ihinda rĩake rĩa gũtua ciira nĩikinyu. Mũhooei ũrĩa wombire igũrũ, na thĩ, na iria rĩrĩa inene, na ithima cia maaĩ.”
english: He said with a loud voice, “Fear the Lord, and give him glory, for the hour of his judgment has come. Worship him who made the heaven, the earth, the sea, and the springs of waters!”

kikuyu: Maingatithie ũrĩ na marakara, na ũmaniine mathire gũkũ mũhuro wa igũrũ rĩa Jehova.
english: You will pursue them in anger, and destroy them from under the heavens of Yahweh.

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio-rĩ, nĩtwĩkĩragei kĩyo gĩa gwĩkaga maũndũ marĩa marehaga ũhoro wa thayũ na magatũma tuonganĩrĩre hinya.
english: So then, let’s follow after things which make for peace, and things by which we may build one another up.

kikuyu: Rĩrĩa aamuonire-rĩ, agĩtembũranga nguo ciake agĩkaya, akiuga atĩrĩ, “Ũũi! Mwarĩ wakwa! Nĩwandua mũndũ mũthĩĩnĩku na mũnyamarĩku nĩ ũndũ nĩndĩhĩtĩte mwĩhĩtwa harĩ Jehova ũrĩa itangĩaga kũhingia.”
english: When he saw her, he tore his clothes, and said, “Alas, my daughter! You have brought me very low, and you are one of those who trouble me; for I have opened my mouth to Yahweh, and I can’t go back.”

kikuyu: Agĩaka magongona thĩinĩ wa hekarũ ya Jehova, o ĩrĩa Jehova oigĩte ũhoro wayo atĩrĩ, “Thĩinĩ wa Jerusalemu nĩkuo ngaatũũria rĩĩtwa rĩakwa.”
english: He built altars in Yahweh’s house, of which Yahweh said, “I will put my name in Jerusalem.”

kikuyu: Thuutha wa matukũ meerĩ, nĩagatwarahũra; mũthenya wa ĩtatũ nĩagatũcookereria nĩguo tũtũũre muoyo mbere yake.
english: After two days he will revive us. On the third day he will raise us up, and we will live before him.

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo, Labani aathiĩte kwenja ngʼondu ciake guoya-rĩ, Rakeli akĩiya ngai cia ithe cia mĩhianano.
english: Now Laban had gone to shear his sheep; and Rachel stole the teraphim† that were her father’s.

kikuyu: Na guoko gwaku kwa ũrĩo kũngĩtũma wĩhie-rĩ, gũtinie ũgũte. Nĩ kaba kĩĩga kĩmwe kĩa mwĩrĩ waku kĩũre gũkĩra mwĩrĩ waku wothe ũikio Jehanamu. Gũtharia Ũhiki
english: If your right hand causes you to stumble, cut it off, and throw it away from you. For it is more profitable for you that one of your members should perish, than for your whole body to be cast into Gehenna.†

kikuyu: Naarĩ njĩra akĩinũka, ndungata ciake ikĩmũtũnga na ndũmĩrĩri atĩ mũrũwe arĩ o muoyo.
english: As he was going down, his servants met him and reported, saying “Your child lives!”

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo mũndũ ũrĩa wothe ũrĩĩaga na akanyua ategũkũũranamwĩrĩwaMwathani, nĩituĩrorĩakewemweneetuagĩra.
english: For he who eats and drinks in an unworthy way eats and drinks judgment to himself if he doesn’t discern the Lord’s body.

kikuyu: ee Jehova-rĩ, ririkana maũndũ marĩa matũkorete; ta rora wone gũconoka gwitũ.
english: emember, Yahweh, what has come on us. Look, and see our reproach.

kikuyu: Niĩ ndingĩconokera Ũhoro-ũrĩa-Mwega, tondũ nĩguo hinya wa Ngai wa gũtũma mũndũ o wothe ũrĩa ũwĩtĩkĩtie ahonoke: O mbere Ayahudi, na thuutha wao nĩ andũ-a-Ndũrĩrĩ.
english: For I am not ashamed of the Good News of Christ, because it is the power of God for salvation for everyone who believes, for the Jew first, and also for the Greek.

kikuyu: “Tharĩkĩrai bũrũri wa Merathaimu,* mũũkĩrĩre arĩa matũũraga Pekodi. Matengʼeriei, na mũmoorage, mũmaniine biũ,” ũguo nĩguo Jehova ekuuga. “Ĩkai ũrĩa wothe ndĩmwathĩte.
english: “Go up against the land of Merathaim, even against it, and against the inhabitants of Pekod. Kill and utterly destroy after them,” says Yahweh, “and do according to all that I have commanded you.

kikuyu: “ ‘Nĩtũngĩahonirie Babuloni, no rĩrĩ, gũtingĩhonio. Nĩtũgũtigei, na o mũndũ acooke bũrũri wake kĩũmbe, nĩgũkorwo ituĩro rĩakuo rĩkinyĩte o matu-inĩ, rĩambatĩte o nginya matu-inĩ marĩa mairũ.’
english: “We would have healed Babylon, but she is not healed. Forsake her, and let’s each go into his own country; for her judgment reaches to heaven, and is lifted up even to the skies.

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio mwanangĩko nĩũgagũkora, na ndũkaamenya ũrĩa ũngĩũũrĩra. Nĩũgakorererwo nĩ mũtino ũrĩa ũtangĩhota gwĩkũũra kuuma kũrĩ guo; ningĩ nĩũgakorererwo o ro rĩmwe nĩ ihooru rĩa maũndũ ũterĩgĩrĩire.
english: Therefore disaster will come on you. You won’t know when it dawns. Mischief will fall on you. You won’t be able to put it away. Desolation will come on you suddenly, which you don’t understand.

kikuyu: Arata akwa nyenda mũno, mũtikagegio nĩ magerio marĩa mũranyariirwo namo, taarĩ ũndũ mũgeni ũrekĩka thĩinĩ wanyu.
english: Beloved, don’t be astonished at the fiery trial which has come upon you to test you, as though a strange thing happened to you.

kikuyu: ũguo nĩ ta kuuga nyama icio ingĩ ciothe cia ndegwa ĩyo, no nginya agaaciruta nja ya kambĩ, acitware handũ hatarĩ thaahu, harĩa mũhu ũitagwo, na acicinĩre na mwaki wa ngũ hau mũhu-inĩ ũcio.
english: —all the rest of the bull—outside of the camp to a clean place where the ashes are poured out, and burn it on wood with fire. It shall be burned where the ashes are poured out.

kikuyu: Nake akĩnjĩĩra atĩrĩ, “Maya nĩmo mariiko marĩa andũ arĩa matungataga thĩinĩ wa hekarũ marĩrugagĩra indo cia magongona iria irutĩtwo nĩ andũ.”
english: Then he said to me, “These are the boiling houses, where the ministers of the house shall boil the sacrifice of the people.”

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa Zimuri onire atĩ itũũra inene nĩrĩanyiitwo-rĩ, agĩtoonya gĩikaro-inĩ gĩa kwĩgitĩra kĩa nyũmba ya ũthamaki, agĩgĩcina na mwaki arĩ kuo thĩinĩ. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio agĩkua,
english: When Zimri saw that the city was taken, he went into the fortified part of the king’s house and burned the king’s house over him with fire, and died,

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Tamaru agĩthiĩ nyũmba ya mũrũ wa ithe Amunoni, akĩmũkora akomete. Akĩoya mũtu, akĩũkanda, agĩthondeka mũgate Amunoni akĩmuonaga, na akĩũruga.
english: So Tamar went to her brother Amnon’s house; and he was lying down. She took dough, kneaded it, made cakes in his sight, and baked the cakes.

kikuyu: Magĩcina matũũra mothe kũrĩa andũ a Midiani maatũũrĩte, o ũndũ ũmwe na kambĩ ciao.
english: All their cities in the places in which they lived, and all their encampments, they burned with fire.

kikuyu: Na marĩ njĩra-inĩ magĩthiĩ-rĩ, magĩkinya handũ haarĩ na maaĩ, nake mũndũ ũcio warĩ mũhakũre akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Ta rora, maaĩ nĩmo maya. Nĩ kĩĩ kĩngĩgiria mbatithio?”
english: As they went on the way, they came to some water; and the eunuch said, “Behold, here is water. What is keeping me from being baptized?”

kikuyu: Nĩngwanĩrĩra na ngoro yakwa yothe, njũũrie atĩrĩ, “Nũũ ũhaana tawe, Wee Jehova? O Wee ũhatũraga andũ arĩa athĩĩni harĩ arĩa marĩ hinya kũmakĩra, ũkahatũra athĩĩni na abatari harĩ arĩa mamatunyaga indo.”
english: All my bones shall say, “Yahweh, who is like you, who delivers the poor from him who is too strong for him; yes, the poor and the needy from him who robs him?”

kikuyu: ĩ niĩ Paũlũ, o niĩ ndeetirwo nduĩke mũtũmwo wa Kristũ Jesũ nĩ ũndũ wa kwenda kwa Ngai, ndĩratũma ngeithi ici tũrĩ hamwe na mũrũ wa Ithe witũ Sosithene,*
english: aul, called to be an apostle of Jesus Christ† through the will of God, and our brother Sosthenes,

kikuyu: Nake Shaitani agĩcookeria Jehova, akiuga atĩrĩ, “Gĩkonde kĩrĩhagwo na gĩkonde! Mũndũ no arute indo ciake ciothe nĩguo ahonokie muoyo wake.
english: Satan answered Yahweh, and said, “Skin for skin. Yes, all that a man has he will give for his life.

kikuyu: Nake Jesũ akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Mũtikamũgirie! Gũtirĩ mũndũ ũngĩringa kĩama thĩinĩ wa rĩĩtwa rĩakwa na thuutha ũcio ahote gũcooka kwaria ũũru wakwa,
english: But Jesus said, “Don’t forbid him, for there is no one who will do a mighty work in my name and be able quickly to speak evil of me.

kikuyu: Alekisandero ũrĩa mũturi wa indo cia rũthuku nĩanjĩkire ũũru mũno. Mwathani nĩwe ũkaamũrĩha kũringana na ciĩko ciake.
english: Alexander the coppersmith did much evil to me. The Lord will repay him according to his deeds.

kikuyu: Ũngĩũraga andũ aya othe o ro rĩmwe-rĩ, ndũrĩrĩ iria ciiguĩte ũhoro ũyũ waku nĩikoiga atĩrĩ,
english: Now if you killed this people as one man, then the nations which have heard the fame of you will speak, saying,

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo ũrĩa wothe wendaga kũhonokia muoyo wake nĩakoorwo nĩguo, no ũrĩa wothe ũkoorwo nĩ muoyo wake nĩ ũndũ wakwa nĩakaũhonokia.
english: For whoever desires to save his life will lose it, but whoever will lose his life for my sake will save it.

kikuyu: no mwĩhonokerie mũirĩtu o wothe ũrĩa ũtarĩ wakoma na mũndũ mũrũme.
english: But all the girls, who have not known man by lying with him, keep alive for yourselves.

kikuyu: Ndakanethaahie nĩ ũndũ wa athoni ake, na nĩ ũndũ ũcio anyiitwo nĩ thaahu.
english: He shall not defile himself, being a chief man among his people, to profane himself.

kikuyu: Iruta rĩothe rĩa mũtu rĩrugĩirwo riiko-inĩ kana rĩgakarangwo na rũgĩo kana na kĩbaati nĩ rĩa mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai ũrĩa ũrĩrĩrutagĩra Jehova,
english: Every meal offering that is baked in the oven, and all that is prepared in the pan and on the griddle, shall be the priest’s who offers it.

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio akĩmooria atĩrĩ, “Nĩ kĩĩ gĩtũmaga mwĩke maũndũ ta maya? Nĩndĩraigua kuuma kũrĩ andũ othe ũhoro wa ciĩko ici cianyu cia waganu.
english: He said to them, “Why do you do such things? For I hear of your evil dealings from all these people.

kikuyu: Rĩrĩa twahubanĩirio nĩ mehia, Wee nĩwatũrekeire mahĩtia maitũ.
english: Sins overwhelmed me, but you atoned for our transgressions.

kikuyu: Jehova Mwene-Hinya-Wothe ekuuga atĩrĩ, “Ndũrĩrĩ ciothe nĩikamwĩtaga andũ arathime, nĩgũkorwo bũrũri wanyu ũgaakorwo ũrĩ wa gĩkeno.”
english: “All nations shall call you blessed, for you will be a delightful land,” says Yahweh of Armies.

kikuyu: Nĩngamomora rũthingo rũu mũhakĩte coka na ndĩrũharaganie tĩĩri-inĩ nginya mũthingi wa ruo ũguũranio. Rĩrĩa rũkaagũa, mũkaanangĩrwo kuo; na nĩmũkamenya atĩ niĩ nĩ niĩ Jehova.
english: So I will break down the wall that you have plastered with whitewash, and bring it down to the ground, so that its foundation will be uncovered. It will fall, and you will be consumed in the middle of it. Then you will know that I am Yahweh.

kikuyu: nĩguo mbura yure bũrũri ũtatũũraga mũndũ, na yure werũ kũrĩa gũtarĩ mũndũ,
english: to cause it to rain on a land where there is no man, on the wilderness, in which there is no man,

kikuyu: “Nĩ niĩ Jehova Ngai waku, ũrĩa wakũrutire bũrũri wa Misiri, kuuma bũrũri wa ũkombo.
english: “I am Yahweh your God, who brought you out of the land of Egypt, out of the house of bondage.

kikuyu: Mũrutwo ti mũnene kũrĩ mũmũruti, no ũrĩa wothe mũrute wega, no atuĩke ta mũmũruti.
english: A disciple is not above his teacher, but everyone when he is fully trained will be like his teacher.

kikuyu: Ĩra Turo, itũũra rĩu rĩrĩ itoonyero-inĩ rĩa iria, rĩu rĩonjoranagia na andũ a ndwere-inĩ nyingĩ cia iria atĩrĩ, ‘Mwathani Jehova ekuuga ũũ: “ ‘Uugaga atĩrĩ, wee Turo, “Niĩ thakarĩte ngarĩkia.”
english: and tell Tyre, ‘You who dwell at the entry of the sea, who are the merchant of the peoples to many islands, the Lord Yahweh says: “You, Tyre, have said, ‘I am perfect in beauty.’

kikuyu: Makiuga atĩrĩ, “Abenjamini arĩa maahonokire no nginya magĩe na a kũmagaya, nĩguo gũtikagĩe mũhĩrĩga o na ũmwe wa Isiraeli ũkũniinũkio.
english: They said, “There must be an inheritance for those who are escaped of Benjamin, that a tribe not be blotted out from Israel.

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo andũ makiuga maheo mũthamaki, nake Ngai akĩmahe Saũlũ mũrũ wa Kishu, wa mũhĩrĩga wa Benjamini, nake agĩathana mĩaka mĩrongo ĩna.
english: Afterward they asked for a king, and God gave to them Saul the son of Kish, a man of the tribe of Benjamin, for forty years.

kikuyu: Nake Ahazu akĩhurũka hamwe na maithe make na agĩthikwo itũũra-inĩ rĩa Jerusalemu, no ndaigirwo mbĩrĩra-inĩ cia athamaki a Isiraeli. Nake mũriũ Hezekia agĩtuĩka mũthamaki ithenya rĩake.
english: Ahaz slept with his fathers, and they buried him in the city, even in Jerusalem, because they didn’t bring him into the tombs of the kings of Israel; and Hezekiah his son reigned in his place.

kikuyu: Wee Ngai, unanga magego marĩa marĩ tũnua twao; Wee Jehova, munya mĩthangiri ya mĩrũũthi ĩyo!
english: Break their teeth, God, in their mouth. Break out the great teeth of the young lions, Yahweh.

kikuyu: Ohagĩrĩra ciohe cia ndigi mĩthia-inĩ ĩrĩa ĩna ya kabuti karĩa wĩhumbaga. Gũthũkia Kĩhiko
english: You shall make yourselves fringes† on the four corners of your cloak with which you cover yourself.

kikuyu: Angĩkorwo tũtũire muoyo nĩ ũndũ wa Roho-rĩ, kĩrekei tũtongoragio nĩ Roho.
english: If we live by the Spirit, let’s also walk by the Spirit.

kikuyu: Cooka woe rũgĩo rwa kĩgera, ũrũige rũtuĩke taarĩ rũgiri rwa kĩgera gatagatĩ gaku na itũũra rĩu inene, na ũhũgũre ũthiũ waku na kũu rĩrĩ. Narĩo rĩrigiicĩrio, na nĩwe ũkaarĩrigiicĩria. Ũndũ ũcio nĩũgaatuĩka kĩmenyithia harĩ andũ a nyũmba ya Isiraeli.
english: Take for yourself an iron pan and set it for a wall of iron between you and the city. Then set your face toward it. It will be besieged, and you shall lay siege against it. This shall be a sign to the house of Israel.

kikuyu: Na thuutha wa gũtuĩka ithe wa Eberi, Shela agĩtũũra muoyo mĩaka ĩngĩ magana mana na ĩtatũ, na akĩgĩa na ciana ingĩ cia aanake na cia airĩtu.
english: Shelah lived four hundred three years after he became the father of Eber, and became the father of more sons and daughters.

kikuyu: “Mbere ya gũkũũmba nda-inĩ ya maitũguo-rĩ, no ndaakũũĩ, o na ũtanaciarwo-rĩ, nĩndakwamũrĩte; nĩndagũtuire mũnabii wa kũrathagĩra ndũrĩrĩ.”
english: “Before I formed you in the womb, I knew you. Before you were born, I sanctified you. I have appointed you a prophet to the nations.”

kikuyu: Iraatũ ciothe cia njamba cia ita iria ihũthagĩrwo mbaara-inĩ, na nguo ciothe ĩtoboketio thakame-inĩ, igaatuĩka cia gũcinwo ta irĩ ngũ cia mwaki.
english: For all the armor of the armed man in the noisy battle, and the garments rolled in blood, will be for burning, fuel for the fire.

kikuyu: Thondekera kĩgongona kĩu gĩtara, na ningĩ ũthondeke mũkwa wa gĩcango, na ũthondeke mĩrĩnga ya gĩcango, o ũmwe wĩkĩrwo koine-inĩ iria inya cia mũkwa ũcio.
english: You shall make a grating for it of network of bronze. On the net you shall make four bronze rings in its four corners.

kikuyu: Nũũ ũtangĩgwĩtigĩra, na agoocithie rĩĩtwa rĩaku, wee Mwathani? Nĩgũkorwo Wee wiki nĩwe Mũtheru. Ndũrĩrĩ ciothe nĩigooka ihooe irĩ mbere yaku, nĩgũkorwo ciĩko ciaku cia ũthingu nĩiguũrĩtio.”
english: Who wouldn’t fear you, Lord, and glorify your name? For you only are holy. For all the nations will come and worship before you. For your righteous acts have been revealed.”

kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, Toma (ũrĩa wetagwo Wa-mahatha), ũmwe wa arĩa ikũmi na eerĩ, ndaarĩ hamwe na arutwo rĩrĩa Jesũ okire.
english: But Thomas, one of the twelve, called Didymus,§ wasn’t with them when Jesus came.

kikuyu: Akĩnjĩĩra atĩrĩ, “Mũrũ wa mũndũ, haha nĩho handũ ha gĩtĩ gĩakwa gĩa ũthamaki, o na nĩho handũ ha gũkinyithia makinya makwa. Haha nĩho ngũtũũra ndĩ gatagatĩ-inĩ ka andũ a Isiraeli nginya tene. Nyũmba ya Isiraeli ndĩrĩ hĩndĩ ĩngĩ ĩgaacooka gũthaahia rĩĩtwa rĩakwa itheru, o ene o na kana athamaki ao marĩthaahie na ũndũ wa ũmaraya wao na kũhooya mĩhianano ĩtarĩ muoyo ya athamaki ao kũndũ kwao kũrĩa gũtũũgĩru.
english: He said to me, “Son of man, this is the place of my throne and the place of the soles of my feet, where I will dwell among the children of Israel forever. The house of Israel will no more defile my holy name, neither they nor their kings, by their prostitution and by the dead bodies of their kings in their high places;

kikuyu: “Ngai aamarutire bũrũri wa Misiri; marĩ na hinya ta wa ndegwa ya gĩthaka. Matambuuraga ndũrĩrĩ iria imathũire, na makoinanga mahĩndĩ ma cio tũcunjĩ; macitheecaga na mĩguĩ yao.
english: God brings him out of Egypt. He has as it were the strength of the wild ox. He shall consume the nations his adversaries, shall break their bones in pieces, and pierce them with his arrows.

kikuyu: Nake Jesũ, tondũ nĩamenyaga meciiria mao, akĩoya kaana kanini agĩkarũgamia hakuhĩ nake.
english: Jesus, perceiving the reasoning of their hearts, took a little child, and set him by his side,

kikuyu: Rĩrĩa aikarire metha-inĩ hamwe nao, akĩoya mũgate, agĩcookia ngaatho, akĩwenyũranga, akĩmahe.
english: When he had sat down at the table with them, he took the bread and gave thanks. Breaking it, he gave it to them.

kikuyu: Ũndũ ũcio nĩguo ũtũmaga Ayahudi arĩa maatũũraga tũtũũra-inĩ marũmie mũthenya wa ikũmi na ĩna wa mweri wa Adari, ũrĩ mũthenya wa gĩkeno na wa ndĩa, na mũthenya wa kũheana iheo harĩ mũndũ na ũrĩa ũngĩ.
english: Therefore the Jews of the villages, who live in the unwalled towns, make the fourteenth day of the month Adar a day of gladness and feasting, a holiday, and a day of sending presents of food to one another.

kikuyu: Haragĩriai gatũrũme ga keerĩ hwaĩ-inĩ kũrĩ mairia hamwe na iruta rĩa mũtu o na iruta rĩa kĩndũ gĩa kũnyuuo o ta ũrĩa mũharagĩria rĩa rũciinĩ. Rĩu nĩrĩo igongona rĩa gũcinwo na mwaki rĩrĩ na mũtararĩko mwega wa gũkenia Jehova. Maruta ma Thabatũ
english: The other lamb you shall offer at evening. As the meal offering of the morning, and as its drink offering, you shall offer it, an offering made by fire, for a pleasant aroma to Yahweh.

kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa ũtarĩ mũruu mwĩrĩ na nĩahingagia ũrĩa watho uugĩte-rĩ, nĩakamũtuĩra ciira, inyuĩ mwagararaga watho ũcio o na mwakorwo nĩmũruĩte na mũrĩ na watho ũrĩa mwandĩke.
english: Won’t those who are physically uncircumcised, but fulfill the law, judge you, who with the letter and circumcision are a transgressor of the law?

kikuyu: Na nĩnjũũĩ atĩ mũndũ ũcio, kana aarĩ mwĩrĩ-inĩ kana ndaarĩ mwĩrĩ-inĩ-rĩ, niĩ ndiũĩ, no Ngai nĩwe ũũĩ,
english: I know such a man (whether in the body, or outside of the body, I don’t know; God knows),

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio andũ a Isiraeli makĩhe Alawii matũũra macio na ũrĩithio wamo.
english: The children of Israel gave to the Levites the cities with their pasture lands.

kikuyu: Mĩthenya mũgwanja mũkaarutagĩra Jehova maruta ma gũcinwo na mwaki, naguo mũthenya wa ĩnana-rĩ, mũgĩe na kĩũngano gĩtheru, na mũrutĩre Jehova iruta rĩa gũcinwo na mwaki. Mũthenya ũcio nĩguo wa kũhinga kĩũngano; mũtikanarute wĩra ũrĩa mũrutaga.
english: Seven days you shall offer an offering made by fire to Yahweh. On the eighth day shall be a holy convocation to you. You shall offer an offering made by fire to Yahweh. It is a solemn assembly; you shall do no regular work.

kikuyu: Nake athũngũthie kĩohe kĩu mbere ya Jehova nĩguo gĩĩtĩkĩrĩke handũ- inĩ hanyu; mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai agagĩthũngũthia mũthenya ũrĩa ũrũmĩrĩire Thabatũ.
english: He shall wave the sheaf before Yahweh, to be accepted for you. On the next day after the Sabbath the priest shall wave it.

kikuyu: Rĩrĩa Jesũ oonire ũguo, akĩrakara, akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Rekei twana tũu tũũke kũrĩ niĩ, tigai gũtũgiria, nĩgũkorwo ũthamaki wa Ngai nĩ wa arĩa matariĩ ta tuo.
english: But when Jesus saw it, he was moved with indignation and said to them, “Allow the little children to come to me! Don’t forbid them, for God’s Kingdom belongs to such as these.

kikuyu: Mwaki ũkiuma harĩ Jehova ũgĩcina iruta rĩu rĩa njino na maguta marĩa maarĩ kĩgongona-inĩ. Na rĩrĩa andũ othe maawoonire makĩanĩrĩra nĩ gũkena, makĩinamĩrĩra maturumithĩtie mothiũ mao thĩ.
english: Fire came out from before Yahweh, and consumed the burnt offering and the fat upon the altar. When all the people saw it, they shouted, and fell on their faces.

kikuyu: Nake Moridekai agĩtũmĩra Ayahudi othe arĩa maarĩ mabũrũri-inĩ 127 ma ũthamaki wa Ahasuerusu marũa, marĩ ciugo cia kũmeciiria wega na kũmoomĩrĩria,
english: He sent letters to all the Jews in the hundred twenty-seven provinces of the kingdom of Ahasuerus with words of peace and truth,

kikuyu: huutha wa Daudi kũrĩĩkia kwaria na Saũlũ, Jonathani akiumĩrania ngoro na Daudi, akĩmwenda o ta ũrĩa eyendete we mwene.
english: hen he had finished speaking to Saul, the soul of Jonathan was knit with the soul of David, and Jonathan loved him as his own soul.

kikuyu: “Oya maguta mamwe ma ndũrũme ĩyo, maguta ma mũtingʼoe ũrĩa mũnoru, na maguta marĩa mahumbĩire nyama cia nda, na maguta marĩa mahumbĩire ini, na higo cierĩ na maguta ma cio, na kĩero kĩa mwena wa ũrĩo. (Ĩyo nĩyo ndũrũme ya igongona rĩa kwamũrana.)
english: Also you shall take some of the ram’s fat, the fat tail, the fat that covers the innards, the cover of the liver, the two kidneys, the fat that is on them, and the right thigh (for it is a ram of consecration),

kikuyu: Andũ arĩa mathugundaga maũndũ ma wĩki naaĩ nĩmanguhĩrĩirie, no-o nĩmaraihanĩrĩirie na watho waku.
english: They draw near who follow after wickedness. They are far from your law.

kikuyu: Nake Balamu akĩaria ndũmĩrĩri yake, akiuga atĩrĩ: “Balaki andehithĩtie kuuma bũrũri wa Aramu, mũthamaki wa Moabi akanduta kuuma irĩma-inĩ cia mwena wa irathĩro. Akoiga atĩrĩ, ‘Ũka ũnumĩre Jakubu;
english: He took up his parable, and said, “From Aram has Balak brought me, the king of Moab from the mountains of the East. Come, curse Jacob for me. Come, defy Israel.

kikuyu: Jesũ aamenya ũrĩa meeciiragia, akĩmooria atĩrĩ, “Mũreciiria ũũru ngoro-inĩ cianyu nĩkĩ?
english: Jesus, knowing their thoughts, said, “Why do you think evil in your hearts?

kikuyu: Ningĩ nĩmo mataaraga ndungata yaku; kũmamenyerera nĩ ũndũ ũrĩ irĩhi inene.
english: Moreover your servant is warned by them. In keeping them there is great reward.

kikuyu: Ingĩruta nyama cia kũhe andũ aya othe kũ? Marandĩrĩra makiugaga atĩrĩ, ‘Tũhe nyama tũrĩe!’
english: Where could I get meat to give all these people? For they weep before me, saying, ‘Give us meat, that we may eat.’

kikuyu: na nĩarĩ na indo nyingĩ matũũra-inĩ macio ma Juda. Agĩcooka akĩiga thigari iria ciooĩ wega ũhoro wa mbaara kũu Jerusalemu.
english: He had many works in the cities of Judah; and men of war, mighty men of valor, in Jerusalem.

kikuyu: “Mwĩmenyererei andũ; tondũ nĩmakamũneana ciama-inĩ, na mamũhũũre na iboko thunagogi- inĩ ciao.
english: But beware of men, for they will deliver you up to councils, and in their synagogues they will scourge you.

kikuyu: Na rĩrĩa andũ maathiiaga na mwena wa irathĩro, magĩkinya werũ-inĩ ũrĩa mwaraganu wa Shinaru, na magĩtũũra kuo.
english: As they traveled east,† they found a plain in the land of Shinar, and they lived there.

kikuyu: Arĩ o hakuhĩ gũtoonya Misiri-rĩ, akĩĩra mũtumia wake Sarai atĩrĩ, “Nĩnjũũĩ atĩ ũrĩ mũndũ-wa-nja kĩĩrorerwa.
english: When he had come near to enter Egypt, he said to Sarai his wife, “See now, I know that you are a beautiful woman to look at.

kikuyu: We nĩũũĩ ũrĩa nyũrũrĩtio, na ngaconorithio na ngahumbwo thoni; thũ ciakwa ciothe irĩ mbere yaku.
english: You know my reproach, my shame, and my dishonor. My adversaries are all before you.

kikuyu: “Mũthenya ũcio-rĩ, ndũrĩrĩ nyingĩ nĩikanyiitithanio na Jehova, na nĩmagatuĩka andũ akwa. Ngaatũũra gatagatĩ-inĩ kanyu na nĩmũkamenya atĩ Jehova Mwene-Hinya- Wothe nĩwe ũndũmĩte kũrĩ inyuĩ.”
english: Many nations shall join themselves to Yahweh in that day, and shall be my people; and I will dwell among you, and you shall know that Yahweh of Armies has sent me to you.

kikuyu: Ndũkahiũhage kwehera harĩ mũthamaki. Ndũgekage ũndũ mũũru, nĩgũkorwo we ekaga ũndũ ũrĩa ũngĩmũkenia.
english: Don’t be hasty to go out of his presence. Don’t persist in an evil thing, for he does whatever pleases him,

kikuyu: Nake Saũlũ akĩĩra Samũeli atĩrĩ, “Nĩnjĩhĩtie. Nĩnjagararĩte watho wa Jehova na uuge waku. Nĩ andũ ndeetigĩrire ngĩkĩmaathĩkĩra.
english: Saul said to Samuel, “I have sinned; for I have transgressed the commandment of Yahweh and your words, because I feared the people and obeyed their voice.

kikuyu: Naguo ũhoro wa Mwathani nĩwahunjire uumĩte kũrĩ inyuĩ, na to Makedonia na Akaia gwiki, no rĩrĩ, o na wĩtĩkio wanyu thĩinĩ wa Ngai nĩũmenyekete kũndũ guothe. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio tũtibataire kuuga ũndũ ũhoro-inĩ ũcio,
english: For from you the word of the Lord has been declared, not only in Macedonia and Achaia, but also in every place your faith toward God has gone out, so that we need not say anything.

kikuyu: Na ndathiĩ na ndamũthondekera handũ-rĩ, nĩngacooka ndĩmũgĩĩre nĩgeetha o na inyuĩ mũkoragwo harĩa ndĩ.
english: If I go and prepare a place for you, I will come again and will receive you to myself; that where I am, you may be there also.

kikuyu: Koine-inĩ ici cierĩ, buremu ciarĩ igĩrĩ igĩrĩ, kuuma thĩ nginya igũrũ, ikoohanio na mũrĩnga ũmwe; nacio cierĩ igakorwo ihaanaine.
english: They were double beneath, and in the same way they were all the way to its top to one ring. He did this to both of them in the two corners.

kikuyu: Niĩ njĩkĩte maũndũ ma ũthingu na ma kĩhooto; ndũkandiganĩrie harĩ arĩa maahinyagĩrĩria.
english: I have done what is just and righteous. Don’t leave me to my oppressors.

kikuyu: Nao makĩhikia andũ-a-nja a Moabi, ũmwe eetagwo Oripa, na ũrĩa ũngĩ Ruthu. Thuutha wa gũtũũra kũu ta ihinda rĩa mĩaka ikũmi,
english: They took for themselves wives of the women of Moab. The name of the one was Orpah, and the name of the other was Ruth. They lived there about ten years.

kikuyu: gũtiarĩ kahinga o na kamwe ga gĩthaka koonekete thĩ kana mũmera wa gĩthaka ũkamera. Nĩ tondũ Jehova Ngai ndoirĩtie mbura thĩ na gũtiarĩ mũndũ wa kũrĩma mũgũnda,
english: No plant of the field was yet in the earth, and no herb of the field had yet sprung up; for Yahweh God had not caused it to rain on the earth. There was not a man to till the ground,

kikuyu: Mũndũ ũcio arĩkoragwo arĩ na thaahu hĩndĩ ĩrĩa angĩkorwo akiura hĩndĩ ciothe, o na kana angĩtiga kuura. Kuura kũu arĩ nakuo kũrĩĩtũmaga athaahe na njĩra ici:
english: This shall be his uncleanness in his discharge: whether his body runs with his discharge, or his body has stopped from his discharge, it is his uncleanness.

kikuyu: O akĩaragia-rĩ, itu ikengu rĩgĩũka rĩkĩmahumbĩra, na mũgambo ũkiuma itu-inĩ rĩu ũkiuga atĩrĩ, “Ũyũ nĩwe Mũrũ wakwa ũrĩa nyendete; na nĩngenagio mũno nĩwe. Mũiguagei!”
english: While he was still speaking, behold, a bright cloud overshadowed them. Behold, a voice came out of the cloud, saying, “This is my beloved Son, in whom I am well pleased. Listen to him.”

kikuyu: Mũtikanarĩe kĩũmbe gĩothe kĩrĩa gĩtambaga thĩ, kana gĩthiiaga na nda, kana na magũrũ mothe mana, kana magũrũ maingĩ; icio mũcithũũre mũno.
english: Whatever goes on its belly, and whatever goes on all fours, or whatever has many feet, even all creeping things that creep on the earth, them you shall not eat; for they are an abomination.

kikuyu: Andũ amwe arĩa oogĩ nĩmakaagũa, nĩguo makumuthwo, na mathambio mage imeni nginya mahinda ma ithirĩro, amu ihinda rĩu rĩtuĩtwo no rĩgaakinya. Mũthamaki Ũrĩa Wĩtũgagĩria
english: Some of those who are wise will fall—to refine them, and to purify, and to make them white, even to the time of the end, because it is yet for the time appointed.

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ wa acio angĩ nguuga ũũ, no nĩ niĩ ndĩroiga, ti Mwathani: Angĩkorwo mũrũ wa Ithe witũ arĩ na mũtumia ũtetĩkĩtie, na mũtumia ũcio etĩkĩre matũũranie-rĩ, nĩatige kũmũingata.
english: But to the rest I—not the Lord—say, if any brother has an unbelieving wife, and she is content to live with him, let him not leave her.

kikuyu: Jehova arotũtuithania ciira niĩ nawe. Na Jehova arondĩhĩria maũru marĩa wee ũnjĩkĩte, no guoko gwakwa gũtigakũhutia.
english: May Yahweh judge between me and you, and may Yahweh avenge me of you; but my hand will not be on you.

kikuyu: “Nĩ mũciari ũrĩkũ thĩinĩ wanyu ũngĩĩtio thamaki nĩ mwana wake, nake amũhe nyoka?
english: “Which of you fathers, if your son asks for bread, will give him a stone? Or if he asks for a fish, he won’t give him a snake instead of a fish, will he?

kikuyu: No rĩrĩa andũ othe maakomete-rĩ, thũ yake ĩgĩũka, ĩkĩhura itindiĩ thĩinĩ wa ngano, na ĩgĩĩthiĩra.
english: but while people slept, his enemy came and sowed darnel weeds† also among the wheat, and went away.

kikuyu: Nowe akĩmacookeria akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Maheei kĩndũ gĩa kũrĩa arĩ inyuĩ.” Nao makĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Ũrenda tũthiĩ tũkagũre mĩgate ya dinari magana meerĩ tũmahe marĩe?”
english: But he answered them, “You give them something to eat.” They asked him, “Shall we go and buy two hundred denarii‡ worth of bread and give them something to eat?”

kikuyu: na Kenani, na Mahalaleli, na Jaredi,
english: Kenan, Mahalalel, Jared,

kikuyu: Wee nĩũkaiyũrwo nĩ ũrĩĩu na kĩeha, gĩkombe kĩu kĩa mwanangĩko na ihooru, gĩkombe kĩu kĩa mwarĩ wa nyũkwa Samaria.
english: You will be filled with drunkenness and sorrow, with the cup of astonishment and desolation, with the cup of your sister Samaria.

kikuyu: Nĩahingĩire njĩra na niĩ ndingĩhota kũhĩtũka; tũcĩra twakwa nĩatwĩkĩrĩte nduma.
english: He has walled up my way so that I can’t pass, and has set darkness in my paths.

kikuyu: Na rĩrĩa Musa na Harũni mehereire Firaũni, Musa agĩkaĩra Jehova ũhoro wĩgiĩ ciũra iria aarehithĩirie Firaũni.
english: Moses and Aaron went out from Pharaoh, and Moses cried to Yahweh concerning the frogs which he had brought on Pharaoh.

kikuyu: nake mũthuuriwe ũcio wa keerĩ age kũmwenda, na amwandĩkĩre marũa ma ndigano, amũnengere na amũingate oime gwake mũciĩ, kana mũthuuri ũcio wa keerĩ akue,
english: If the latter husband hates her, and writes her a certificate of divorce, puts it in her hand, and sends her out of his house; or if the latter husband dies, who took her to be his wife;

kikuyu: Rĩu- rĩ, mũngĩĩhaarĩria nĩgeetha rĩrĩa mũrĩigua mũgambo wa coro, na mĩtũrirũ, na thethe, na kĩnanda gĩa kĩnũbi, na kĩnanda kĩa mũgeeto, na mĩtũrirũ ĩrĩa mĩariĩ, na ũini wa mĩthemba yothe, mwĩgũithie thĩ, mũhooe mũhianano ũcio niĩ thondekithĩtie, nĩ wega mũno. No rĩrĩ, mũngĩrega kũhooya mũhianano
english: Now if you are ready whenever you hear the sound of the horn, flute, zither, lyre, harp, pipe, and all kinds of music to fall down and worship the image which I have made, good; but if you don’t worship, you shall be cast the same hour into the middle of a burning fiery furnace.

kikuyu: Jehova ekuuga atĩrĩ: “Nĩ ũndũ wa mehia matatũ ma Amoni, o na mana-rĩ, niĩ ndikahũndũra mangʼũrĩ makwa. Tondũ nĩatarũrire nda cia andũ-a-nja a Gileadi arĩa maarĩ na nda, nĩgeetha aaramie mĩhaka ya gwake.
english: Yahweh says: “For three transgressions of the children of Ammon, yes, for four, I will not turn away its punishment, because they have ripped open the pregnant women of Gilead, that they may enlarge their border.

kikuyu: Atongoria amwe a nyũmba nĩmarutire indo ciao nĩ ũndũ wa wĩra ũcio. Barũthi wa kũu nĩaheanire durakima 1,000* cia thahabu, na mbakũri 50, na nguo cia athĩnjĩri-Ngai 530, nacio ikĩigwo kĩgĩĩna-inĩ.
english: Some from among the heads of fathers’ households gave to the work. The governor gave to the treasury one thousand darics of gold,‡ fifty basins, and five hundred thirty priests’ garments.

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio itu rĩa Jehova rĩaikaraga igũrũ wa hema ĩyo nyamũre mũthenya, naguo mwaki wakoragwo itu-inĩ rĩu ũtukũ, rĩgakĩonagwo nĩ nyũmba yothe ya Isiraeli mahinda mothe ngʼendo-inĩ ciao ciothe.
english: For the cloud of Yahweh was on the tabernacle by day, and there was fire in the cloud by night, in the sight of all the house of Israel, throughout all their journeys.

kikuyu: 1420 1 Timotheo 3:16 na nĩũkenagia Ngai Mũhonokia witũ, 4o we wendete andũ othe mahonokio na mamenye ũhoro-ũrĩa- wa-ma.
english: who desires all people to be saved and come to full knowledge of the truth.

kikuyu: Rĩrĩa aaki maarĩkirie gwaka mũthingi wa Hekarũ ya Jehova, athĩnjĩri-Ngai mehumbĩte nguo ciao na marĩ na tũrumbeta, na Alawii (ariũ a Asafu) marĩ na thaani iria ihũũrithanagio ikagamba, makĩĩhaarĩria kũgooca Jehova, o ta ũrĩa gwathanĩtwo nĩ Daudi mũthamaki wa Isiraeli.
english: When the builders laid the foundation of Yahweh’s temple, they set the priests in their vestments with trumpets, with the Levites the sons of Asaph with cymbals, to praise Yahweh, according to the directions of David king of Israel.

kikuyu: O rĩrĩa wambĩrĩirie kũhooya-rĩ, noguo icookio rĩheanirwo, na niĩ ndatũmwo njũke ngwĩre, tondũ ũrĩ mũndũ mwende mũno. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio, cũrania ũhoro wa ndũmĩrĩri ĩno ngũkwĩra, na ũtaũkĩrwo nĩ kĩoneki kĩu:
english: At the beginning of your petitions the commandment went out, and I have come to tell you, for you are greatly beloved. Therefore consider the matter and understand the vision.

kikuyu: Nguo ĩyo, kana ndigi icio njogothe, kana taama mũtume, kana kĩndũ kĩa rũũa kĩrĩa gĩthambĩtio gĩkaniinwo ũgumu ũcio no nginya gĩthambio rĩngĩ, na nĩ gĩgaathirwo nĩ thaahu.”
english: The garment, either the warp, or the woof, or whatever thing of skin it is, which you shall wash, if the plague has departed from them, then it shall be washed the second time, and it will be clean.”

kikuyu: Akĩanĩrĩra, akĩĩra mũndũ ũcio wa Ngai ũrĩa woimĩte Juda atĩrĩ, “Jehova ekuuga ũũ: ‘Nĩũreganĩte na kiugo kĩa Jehova, na ũkaaga kũrũmia watho ũrĩa waathirwo nĩ JehovaNgaiwaku.
english: and he cried out to the man of God who came from Judah, saying, “Yahweh says, ‘Because you have been disobedient to Yahweh’s word, and have not kept the commandment which Yahweh your God commanded you,

kikuyu: Menyererai mũrutage gĩcunjĩ gĩa ikũmi kĩa maciaro mothe ma mĩgũnda yanyu mwaka o mwaka.
english: You shall surely tithe all the increase of your seed, that which comes out of the field year by year.

kikuyu: “Manase mũthamaki wa Juda nĩekĩte mehia marĩ magigi. Ageeka maũndũ mooru gũkĩra Aamori arĩa maarĩ mbere yake,* na agatongoria andũ a Juda kwĩhia nĩ ũndũ wa kũhooya mĩhianano yake.
english: “Because Manasseh king of Judah has done these abominations, and has done wickedly above all that the Amorites did, who were before him, and has also made Judah to sin with his idols;

kikuyu: wee mũthamaki, nĩwe mũtĩ ũcio! Nĩũnenehete na ũkagĩa na hinya; ũnene waku ũgakũra nginya ũkahutia matu mairũ, naguo wathani waku ũgatambũrũka ũgakinya kũndũ kũraya gũkũ thĩ.
english: it is you, O king, that have grown and become strong; for your greatness has grown, and reaches to the sky, and your dominion to the end of the earth.

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo Balamu na Balaki magĩthiĩ Kiriathu-Huzothu.
english: Balaam went with Balak, and they came to Kiriath Huzoth.

kikuyu: Namũtikeĩĩrenangorocianyuatĩrĩ, ‘IthewitũnĩIburahĩmu.’ Ngũmwĩra atĩrĩ, Ngai no atũme mahiga maya matuĩke ciana cia Iburahĩmu.
english: Don’t think to yourselves, ‘We have Abraham for our father,’ for I tell you that God is able to raise up children to Abraham from these stones.

kikuyu: Nao atũũri othe a Shekemu na a Bethi-Milo makĩũngana mũtĩ-inĩ ũrĩa mũnene o hau gĩtugĩ-inĩ kĩrĩa kĩrĩ Shekemu, magĩtua Abimeleku mũthamaki.
english: All the men of Shechem assembled themselves together with all the house of Millo, and went and made Abimelech king by the oak of the pillar that was in Shechem.

kikuyu: nĩrai Jehova mũkenete, inyuĩ andũ arĩa athingu; andũ arĩa arũngĩrĩru ngoro nĩo maagĩrĩirwo nĩkũmũgooca.
english: ejoice in Yahweh, you righteous! Praise is fitting for the upright.

kikuyu: Na gũkĩgĩa hwaĩ-inĩ na gũkĩgĩa rũciinĩ. Ũcio ũgĩtuĩka mũthenya wa gatano.
english: There was evening and there was morning, a fifth day.

kikuyu: Nĩwe wombire mwena wa gathigathini na wa gũthini; Taboru na Herimoni ciinaga ikenete nĩ ũndũ wa rĩĩtwa rĩaku.
english: You have created the north and the south. Tabor and Hermon rejoice in your name.

kikuyu: Aroikarĩra gĩtĩ kĩa ũnene arĩ mbere ya Ngai nginya tene; tũma wendo na wĩhokeku waku imũgitagĩre.
english: He shall be enthroned in God’s presence forever. Appoint your loving kindness and truth, that they may preserve him.

kikuyu: tondũ aarĩ hakuhĩ gũkua nĩ ũndũ wa wĩra wa Kristũ, ategũcaaĩra muoyo wake, nĩgeetha andeithĩrĩrie na ũteithio ũrĩa inyuĩ mũtangĩahotire kũũhe.
english: because for the work of Christ he came near to death, risking his life to

kikuyu: “ ‘Igongona rĩa ndũrũme-rĩ, haarĩria igongona rĩa mũtu icunjĩ igĩrĩ cia ikũmi cia eba ĩmwe‡ ya mũtu mũhinyu mũno, ũtukanĩtio na gĩcunjĩ gĩa ithatũ kĩa hini ĩmwe§ ya maguta,
english: “ ‘For a ram, you shall prepare for a meal offering two tenths of an ephah§ of fine flour mixed with the third part of a hin of oil;

kikuyu: Nake Abimeleku akĩũria Iburahĩmu atĩrĩ, “Mĩatĩ ĩno mũgwanja-rĩ, wamĩamũra kuuma rũũru-inĩ nĩkĩ?”
english: Abimelech said to Abraham, “What do these seven ewe lambs, which you have set by themselves, mean?”

kikuyu: na ngoragwo ndĩ thĩinĩ wake, itarĩ na ũthingu wakwa mwene ũrĩa uumanaga na watho, no ũkorwo arĩ ũthingu ũrĩa uumanaga na gwĩtĩkia Kristũ; ũguo nĩ kuuga, ũthingu ũrĩa uumaga kũrĩ Ngai, na wonekaga na ũndũ wa gwĩtĩkia.
english: and be found in him, not having a righteousness of my own, that which is of the law, but that which is through faith in Christ, the righteousness which is from God by faith,

kikuyu: Nĩonire mũndũ ũmwe wao akĩnyariirwo nĩ mũndũ wa Misiri. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio agĩthiĩ kũmũũrĩria na akĩmũrĩhĩria na ũndũ wa kũũraga mũndũ ũcio Mũmisiri.
english: Seeing one of them suffer wrong, he defended him and avenged him who was oppressed, striking the Egyptian.

kikuyu: Arĩria andũ aya a nyũmba ĩno nemi na ngerekano, ũmeere atĩrĩ, ‘Mwathani Jehova ekuuga atĩrĩ: “ ‘Hagĩra nyũngũ ya kũruga; mĩhagĩre na ũmĩitĩrĩre maaĩ.
english: Utter a parable to the rebellious house, and tell them, ‘The Lord Yahweh says, “Put the cauldron on the fire. Put it on, and also pour water into it.

kikuyu: “ ‘Rũmagiai irĩra ciakwa cia watho ciothe na mawatho makwa mothe, na mũmarũmagĩrĩre. Niĩ nĩ niĩ Jehova.’ ” 20 Kũherithio nĩ Ũndũ wa Mehia
english: “ ‘You shall observe all my statutes and all my ordinances, and do them. I am Yahweh.’ ”

kikuyu: Nake Jusufu agĩtũma ariũ a Isiraeli mehĩte na mwĩhĩtwa, akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Ti-itherũ Ngai nĩagooka kũmũteithia, na hĩndĩ ĩyo yakinya no nginya mũgaakuua mahĩndĩ makwa kuuma kũndũ gũkũ.”
english: Joseph took an oath from the children of Israel, saying, “God will surely visit you, and you shall carry up my bones from here.”

kikuyu: Jehova akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Nũũ waheire mũndũ kanua? Nũũ ũtũmaga mũndũ aage kũigua kana aremwo nĩ kwaria? Nũũ ũtũmaga mũndũ one kana atuĩke mũtumumu? Githĩ ti niĩ, Jehova?
english: Yahweh said to him, “Who made man’s mouth? Or who makes one mute, or deaf, or seeing, or blind? Isn’t it I, Yahweh?

kikuyu: “Iburahĩmu akĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, ‘Marĩ na Musa na Anabii; nĩmamathikĩrĩrie.’
english: “But Abraham said to him, ‘They have Moses and the prophets. Let them listen to them.’

kikuyu: Jehova Mwene-Hinya-Wothe ekuuga ũũ: “Andũ aingĩ na atũũri a matũũra maingĩ manene nĩ magooka,
english: Yahweh of Armies says: “Many peoples and the inhabitants of many cities will yet come.

kikuyu: ũrĩa wamũrũmĩrĩire nĩ Jehozabadu, na aarĩ na andũ 180,000 arĩa maarĩ na indo cia mbaara.
english: and next to him Jehozabad, and with him one hundred eighty thousand ready and prepared for war.

kikuyu: ĩthenya ĩtandatũ mbere ya Gĩathĩ kĩa Bathaka gũkinya, Jesũ agĩthiĩ Bethania, kũrĩa Lazaro ũrĩa aariũkĩtie aatũũraga.
english: hen, six days before the Passover, Jesus came to Bethany, where Lazarus was, who had been dead, whom he raised from the dead.

kikuyu: O rũciinĩ reke njiguage kiugo kĩa wendo waku ũrĩa ũtathiraga, nĩgũkorwo nĩwe ndĩĩhokete. Nyonia njĩra ĩrĩa njagĩrĩirwo nĩkũgera, nĩgũkorwo muoyo wakwa ndĩwerekeirie igũrũ kũrĩ we.
english: Cause me to hear your loving kindness in the morning, for I trust in you. Cause me to know the way in which I should walk, for I lift up my soul to you.

kikuyu: Ndaacũthĩrĩirie thĩ, ngĩkĩona atĩ ndĩarĩ ũrĩa yatariĩ na ndĩarĩ na kĩndũ; ningĩ ngĩcũthĩrĩria matu-inĩ, ngĩona atĩ gũtiarĩ na ũtheri.
english: I saw the earth and, behold, it was waste and void, and the heavens, and they had no light.

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio andũ a Isiraeli magĩtũũra bũrũri ũcio wa Aamori.
english: Thus Israel lived in the land of the Amorites.

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio no ngene kũhũthĩra kĩrĩa gĩothe ndĩ nakĩo nĩ ũndũ wanyu, o na niĩ mwene ndĩĩhũthĩre. Ingĩmwenda makĩria- rĩ, inyuĩ no mũkĩnyende o hanini?
english: I will most gladly spend and be spent for your souls. If I love you more abundantly, am I loved the less?

kikuyu: Ndetereire ngirĩrĩirie nginya gũgĩkĩa, na akiunanga mahĩndĩ makwa mothe o ta mũrũũthi. Mũthenya na ũtukũ-rĩ, wee ũtuĩkĩte o wa kũũniina.
english: I waited patiently until morning. He breaks all my bones like a lion. From day even to night you will make an end of me.

kikuyu: “No rĩrĩ, ũngĩkahutatĩra na ũtiganĩrie kĩrĩra kĩa watho wakwa wa kũrũmĩrĩrwo, na maathani marĩa ngũheete, na ũthiĩ gũtungatĩra ngai ingĩ na gũcihooya-rĩ,
