english: But if you turn away and forsake my statutes and my commandments which I have set before you, and go and serve other gods and worship them,

kikuyu: Jehoshafatu mũrũ wa Asa aatuĩkire mũthamaki wa Juda mwaka-inĩ wa ĩna wa Ahabu mũthamaki wa Isiraeli.
english: Jehoshaphat the son of Asa began to reign over Judah in the fourth year of Ahab king of Israel.

kikuyu: Nĩagĩĩkĩrire hinya harĩ Jakubu kĩrĩ kĩrĩra gĩake kĩa watho wa kũrũmagĩrĩrwo, agĩgĩĩkĩra hinya kũrĩ Isiraeli kĩrĩ kĩrĩkanĩro gĩa gũtũũra tene na tene, akiuga atĩrĩ:
english: He confirmed it to Jacob for a statute, and to Israel for an everlasting covenant,

kikuyu: hĩndĩ ĩyo ũkaamaigua ũrĩ o kũu igũrũ, na ũrekere ndungata ciaku, andũ aku a Isiraeli, mehia mao. Marute mũtũũrĩre ũrĩa mwagĩrĩru, na ũmoirĩrie mbura bũrũri-inĩ ũcio waheire andũ aku ũtuĩke igai rĩao.
english: then hear in heaven, and forgive the sin of your servants, and of your people Israel, when you teach them the good way in which they should walk; and send rain on your land which you have given to your people for an inheritance.

kikuyu: Ũtonga na gĩtĩĩo cionekaga harĩ niĩ, o hamwe na ũtonga wa gũtũũra, na ũgaacĩru.
english: With me are riches, honor, enduring wealth, and prosperity.

kikuyu: Nao andũ a Isiraeli makĩambata makĩrĩrĩra mbere ya Jehova nginya hwaĩ-inĩ, nao magĩtuĩria ũhoro harĩ Jehova atĩrĩ, “Hihi twambate rĩngĩ tũkahũũrane na andũ a Benjamini, aya ariũ a ithe witũ?” Nake Jehova akĩmacookeria atĩrĩ, “Ambatai mũkahũũrane nao.”
english: The children of Israel went up and wept before Yahweh until evening; and they asked of Yahweh, saying, “Shall I again draw near to battle against the children of Benjamin my brother?” Yahweh said, “Go up against him.”

kikuyu: Nĩ niĩ ngũgũcookeria ngaatho kĩũngano-inĩ kĩrĩa kĩnene; ndĩkũgoocaga ndĩ gatagatĩ ka andũ aingĩ.
english: I will give you thanks in the great assembly. I will praise you among many people.

kikuyu: Jehova agĩĩtana rĩngĩ, “Samũeli!” Nake Samũeli agĩũkĩra, agĩthiĩ kũrĩ Eli, akiuga atĩrĩ, “Nĩ wanjĩta; niĩ ũyũ haha.” Eli akiuga atĩrĩ, “Mũrũ wakwa, niĩ ndiĩtanĩte; cooka ũgakome.”
english: Yahweh called yet again, “Samuel!” Samuel arose and went to Eli and said, “Here I am; for you called me.” He answered, “I didn’t call, my son. Lie down again.”  “Behold”, from “הִ ͏נֵּה”, means look at, take notice, observe, see, or gaze at. It is often used as an interjection.  or, blind your eyes with tears

kikuyu: Aũcomoraga kuuma ngʼongʼo wake, mũtheece ũcio mũkengu akaũcomora kuuma ini-inĩ rĩake. Imakania nĩikamũkorerera;
english: He draws it out, and it comes out of his body. Yes, the glittering point comes out of his liver. Terrors are on him.

kikuyu: Kayũ ka mũkayo wao nĩkaiguĩtwo mĩhaka-inĩ ya Moabi; nakuo kũgirĩka kwao gũgakinya Egilaimu, o namo macakaya mao magakinya o Biri-Elimu.
english: For the cry has gone around the borders of Moab, its wailing to Eglaim, and its wailing to Beer Elim.

kikuyu: “Ndegwa ĩngĩtheeca mũndũ mũrũme kana mũndũ-wa-nja na rũhĩa akue, ndegwa ĩyo no nginya ĩhũũrwo na mahiga nginya ĩkue, na nyama ciayo itikarĩĩo. No mwene ndegwa ndangĩũrio.
english: “If a bull gores a man or a woman to death, the bull shall surely be stoned, and its meat shall not be eaten; but the owner of the bull shall not be held responsible.

kikuyu: “Ndawa nini tu ya kũimbia mĩgate nĩĩtũmaga mũtu ũrĩa mũkande nayo wothe ũimbe.”
english: A little yeast grows through the whole lump.  Isaiah 54:1  Genesis 21:10

kikuyu: Nake akĩmacookeria atĩrĩ, “Kaĩ mũtathomete ũrĩa Daudi eekire rĩrĩa we na arĩa maarĩ nake maarĩ ahũtu?
english: But he said to them, “Haven’t you read what David did when he was hungry, and those who were with him:

kikuyu: yũ nĩguo ũhoro wa Amosi, ũmwe wa arĩithi arĩa maarĩithagia Tekoa,* wa ũndũ ũrĩa onire ũkoniĩ Isiraeli mĩaka ĩĩrĩ mbere ya gĩthingithia, hĩndĩ ĩrĩa Uzia aarĩ mũthamaki wa Juda nake Jeroboamu mũrũ wa Joashu aarĩ mũthamaki wa Isiraeli.
english: he words of Amos, who was among the herdsmen of Tekoa, which he saw concerning Israel in the days of Uzziah king of Judah and in the days of Jeroboam the son of Joash, king of Israel, two years before the earthquake.

kikuyu: Nĩngaiyũria irĩma ciaku na arĩa moragĩtwo; arĩa moragĩtwo na rũhiũ rwa njora makaagũa tũrĩma-inĩ twaku, na ituamba-inĩ ciaku, o na mĩkuru-inĩ yaku yothe.
english: I will fill its mountains with its slain. The slain with the sword will fall in your hills and in your valleys and in all your watercourses.

kikuyu: Wee no kwĩrorera ũkerorera na maitho maku wĩonere iherithia rĩa andũ arĩa aaganu.
english: You will only look with your eyes, and see the recompense of the wicked.

kikuyu: Nake akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Mũrutani, macio mothe ndũire ndĩmarũmĩtie kuuma ndĩ mũnini.”
english: He said to him, “Teacher, I have observed all these things from my youth.”

kikuyu: No nginya acine nguo ĩyo, kana ndigi icio njogothe, kana taama ũcio mũtume na guoya wa ngʼondu kana gatani, kana kĩndũ kĩngĩ gĩothe kĩa rũũa rũrĩa rũgwatie ũgumu ũcio, nĩ ũndũ ũgumu ũcio nĩ wa kwananga; kĩndũ kĩu no nginya gĩcinwo.
english: He shall burn the garment, whether the warp or the woof, in wool or in linen, or anything of leather, in which the plague is, for it is a destructive mildew. It shall be burned in the fire.

kikuyu: No mũngĩkorwo mũtirekanagĩra mehia, nake Ithe wanyu ũrĩa ũrĩ igũrũ ndarĩmũrekagĩra mehia manyu.) Kĩũria Gĩkoniĩ Ũhoti wa Jesũ
english: But if you do not forgive, neither will your Father in heaven forgive your transgressions.”§

kikuyu: Nao makĩmũgooca na magĩcooka Jerusalemu makenete mũno.
english: They worshiped him and returned to Jerusalem with great joy,

kikuyu: Thuutha ũcio agĩciara Habili, mũrũ wa nyina. Habili aarĩ mũrĩithi wa mbũri nake Kaini aarĩ mũrĩmi.
english: Again she gave birth, to Cain’s brother Abel. Abel was a keeper of sheep, but Cain was a tiller of the ground.

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo athĩnjĩri-Ngai na anabii makĩĩra anene acio na andũ othe atĩrĩ, “Mũndũ ũyũ agĩrĩire atuĩrwo kũũragwo, tondũ nĩarathĩte maũndũ mooru makoniĩ itũũra rĩĩrĩ inene. Nĩmũmeiguĩrĩire na matũ manyu!”
english: Then the priests and the prophets spoke to the princes and to all the people, saying, “This man is worthy of death, for he has prophesied against this city, as you have heard with your ears.”

kikuyu: Akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Githĩ Jehova Ngai wanyu ndarĩ hamwe na inyuĩ? Na githĩ ndamũheete ũhurũko mĩena yothe? Nĩgũkorwo nĩaneanĩte atũũri a bũrũri ũyũ moko-inĩ makwa, na rĩu bũrũri ũrĩ watho-inĩ wa Jehova na andũ ake.
english: “Isn’t Yahweh your God with you? Hasn’t he given you rest on every side? For he has delivered the inhabitants of the land into my hand; and the land is subdued before Yahweh and before his people.

kikuyu: nĩguo mamakinyĩrie ituĩro rĩrĩa mandĩkĩirwo. Ũcio nĩguo riiri wa andũ ake othe arĩa aamũre. Goocai Jehova.
english: to execute on them the written judgment. All his saints have this honor. Praise Yah!

kikuyu: Gũtigacooka gũkorwo na mũndũ ũkũruta ũrĩa ũngĩ, kana mũndũ arute mũrũ wa ithe amwĩre atĩrĩ, ‘Menyana na Mwathani,’ tondũ andũ othe nĩmagakorwo maamenyete, kuuma ũrĩa mũnini mũno wao nginya ũrĩa mũnene.
english: They will not teach every man his fellow citizen‡ and every man his brother, saying, ‘Know the Lord,’ for all will know me, from their least to their greatest.

kikuyu: Ningĩ kuuma mwaka wa mĩrongo ĩĩrĩ wa Mũthamaki Aritashashita, rĩrĩa ndathuurirwo nduĩke barũthi wao bũrũri wa Juda, nginyagia mwaka wa mĩrongo ĩtatũ na ĩĩrĩ, mĩaka ikũmi na ĩĩrĩ-rĩ, niĩ o na kana ariũ a baba, tũtiarĩire irio iria ciagayagĩrwo barũthi.
english: Moreover from the time that I was appointed to be their governor in the land of Judah, from the twentieth year even to the thirty-second year of Artaxerxes the king, that is, twelve years, I and my brothers have not eaten the bread of the governor.

kikuyu: Ũhoro ũcio ũkĩhunja bũrũri ũcio wothe. Jesũ Kũhonia Atumumu na Mũndũ Ũtaaragia
english: The report of this went out into all that land.

kikuyu: huutha wa ihinda iraaya, mwaka-inĩ wa gatatũ, kiugo kĩa Jehova gĩgĩkinyĩra Elija, akĩĩrwo atĩrĩ, “Ũkĩra ũthiĩ ũkeyonanie harĩ Ahabu, na thuutha wa ũguo nĩnguuria mbura bũrũri-inĩ ũyũ.”
english: fter many days, Yahweh’s word came to Elijah, in the third year, saying, “Go, show yourself to Ahab; and I will send rain on the earth.”

kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, gũtirĩ o na ũmwe wao warĩ na mbeeca cia kũmũrĩha, nĩ ũndũ ũcio marĩ o eerĩ akĩmarekera thiirĩ ũcio. Rĩu-rĩ, acio eerĩ nũũ ũngĩmwenda makĩria?”
english: When they couldn’t pay, he forgave them both. Which of them therefore will love him most?”

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo o ũrĩa ũũgĩ waingĩha, noguo ihooru rĩingĩhĩte; na ũrĩa ũmenyo waingĩha, noguo kĩeha kĩingĩhĩte.
english: For in much wisdom is much grief; and he who increases knowledge increases sorrow.

kikuyu: Thahabu kana icicio-rĩ, itingĩhota kũgerekanio naguo; o na kana ũkũũranio na mathaga ma thahabu.
english: Gold and glass can’t equal it, neither will it be exchanged for jewels of fine gold.

kikuyu: Mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai nĩakarora ũgumu ũcio, na aige kĩndũ kĩu kĩrĩ na ũgumu handũ mwanya mĩthenya mũgwanja.
english: The priest shall examine the plague, and isolate the plague seven days.

kikuyu: Nao magĩkena na magĩtĩkĩra kũmũhe mbeeca.
english: They were glad, and agreed to give him money.

kikuyu: Ti-itherũ harĩ o-rĩ, wee ũtuĩkĩte o ta mũndũ ũrĩa ũinaga nyĩmbo cia wendo arĩ na mũgambo mwega, na akahũũra kĩnanda wega, nĩgũkorwo nĩmaiguaga ciugo ciaku no matiĩkaga ũrĩa ciugĩte.
english: Behold, you are to them as a very lovely song of one who has a pleasant voice, and can play well on an instrument; for they hear your words, but they don’t do them.

kikuyu: “Kũngũyagĩra Gĩathĩ kĩa Mĩgate ĩtarĩ mĩĩkĩre ndawa ya kũimbia. Handũ-inĩ ha mĩthenya mũgwanja, rĩĩagai mĩgate ĩtarĩ mĩĩkĩre ndawa ya kũimbia, o ta ũrĩa ndaamwathire. Ĩkagai ũndũ ũyũ ihinda rĩaguo rĩakinya mweri-inĩ wa Abibu, nĩgũkorwo nĩguo mweri ũrĩa mwoimire bũrũri wa Misiri.
english: “You shall keep the feast of unleavened bread. Seven days you shall eat unleavened bread, as I commanded you, at the time appointed in the month Abib; for in the month Abib you came out of Egypt.

kikuyu: ee Ngai, ndũrĩrĩ nĩitharĩkĩire igai rĩaku; nĩithaahĩtie hekarũ yaku theru, imomorete itũũra rĩa Jerusalemu rĩgatuĩka irũndo cia mahiga.
english: od, the nations have come into your inheritance. They have defiled your holy temple. They have laid Jerusalem in heaps.

kikuyu: hamwe na andũ a thiritũ yake arĩa maarĩ atongoria a nyũmba ciao, maarĩ andũ 242; Amashusai mũrũ wa Azareli, mũrũ wa Ahazai, mũrũ wa Meshilemothu, mũrũ wa Imeri,
english: and his brothers, chiefs of fathers’ households, two hundred forty-two; and Amashsai the son of Azarel, the son of Ahzai, the son of Meshillemoth, the son of Immer,

kikuyu: Igwatanĩtio hamwe irĩ nũmu; inyiitanĩte hamwe ũndũ itangĩtigithũkana.
english: They are joined to one another. They stick together, so that they can’t be pulled apart.

kikuyu: “Mwathani Jehova ekwĩra Turo atĩrĩ: Mabũrũri marĩa marĩ ndwere-inĩ cia iria githĩ matikainaina nĩ mũrurumo wa kũgũa gwaku, hĩndĩ ĩrĩa arĩa magurarĩtio magaacaaya, nayo njũragano yũraganwo thĩinĩ waku?
english: “The Lord Yahweh says to Tyre: ‘Won’t the islands shake at the sound of your fall, when the wounded groan, when the slaughter is made within you?

kikuyu: tondũ andũ nĩmakaigua ngumo ya Rĩĩtwa rĩaku inene, na ũhoro wa ciĩko cia guoko gwaku kũrĩ hinya gũtambũrũkĩtio, rĩrĩa agooka ahooe erekeire hekarũ ĩno,
english: (for they shall hear of your great name and of your mighty hand and of your outstretched arm), when he comes and prays toward this house,

kikuyu: Mbĩrĩra ciao irĩ irima kũrĩa kũriku, nacio mbũtũ ciake cia ita igũĩte irigiicĩirie mbĩrĩra yake. Arĩa othe maiyũrĩtie guoya thĩinĩ wa bũrũri wa arĩa marĩ muoyo nĩmoragĩtwo, makooragwo na rũhiũ rwa njora.
english: whose graves are set in the uttermost parts of the pit, and her company is around her grave, all of them slain, fallen by the sword, who caused terror in the land of the living.

kikuyu: No ingiuga atĩrĩ, “Ndigũcooka kũmũgweta kana kwaria ngĩgwetaga rĩĩtwa rĩake,” ndũmĩrĩri yake ĩrĩa ĩrĩ ngoro-inĩ yakwa ĩhaana ta mwaki, o ta mwaki ũhingĩrĩirio mahĩndĩ-inĩ makwa. Nĩnogetio nĩkũmĩiga thĩinĩ wakwa, na ti-itherũ ndingĩhota.
english: If I say that I will not make mention of him, or speak any more in his name, then there is in my heart as it were a burning fire shut up in my bones. I am weary with holding it in. I can’t.

kikuyu: na Atarothu-Shofani, na Jazeri, na Jogibeha,
english: Atroth-shophan, Jazer, Jogbehah,

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo nĩũgakũũrana maũndũ marĩa magĩrĩire, na ma kĩhooto, na marĩa mega: ũgĩkũũrane njĩra o yothe njega.
english: Then you will understand righteousness and justice, equity and every good path.

kikuyu: Matukũ ma mũndũ nĩ matare; nĩũtuĩte mũigana wa mĩeri yake, na nĩũmũigĩire mĩhaka ĩrĩa ataangĩkĩra.
english: Seeing his days are determined, the number of his months is with you, and you have appointed his bounds that he can’t pass.

kikuyu: Wamũtheecire mũtwe na itimũ rĩake mwene, * 3:7 Andũ a Kushani na Midiani maarĩ Aarabu arĩa matũũraga gũkuhĩ na Edomu. † 3:9 Mĩguĩ haha nĩ heni na marurumĩ iria Mwathani aahũthĩrire.
english: You pierced the heads of his warriors with their own spears. They came as a whirlwind to scatter me, gloating as if to devour the wretched in secret.

kikuyu: “Nake mũrori wa indo icio akĩĩyũria na ngoro atĩrĩ, ‘Ngwĩka atĩa rĩu? Mwathi wakwa nĩekũndig- ithia wĩra. Ndirĩ na hinya mũiganu wa kũrĩma, na nĩngũconoka kũhooya.
english: “The manager said within himself, ‘What will I do, seeing that my lord is taking away the management position from me? I don’t have strength to dig. I am ashamed to beg.

kikuyu: ingĩaga gũũka narua, ũmenye ũrĩa andũ magĩrĩirwo nĩgũikaraga marĩ thĩinĩ wa nyũmba ya Ngai, na nĩyo kanitha wa Ngai ũrĩa ũrĩ muoyo, naguo nĩguo gĩtugĩ na mũthingi mũrũmu wa kũnyiitĩrĩra ũhoro-ũrĩa-wa-ma.
english: but if I wait long, that you may know how men ought to behave themselves in God’s house, which is the assembly of the living God, the pillar and ground of the truth.

kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, mũiguithania ndakoragwo arũgamĩrĩire o mwena ũmwe; no Ngai nĩ ũmwe.
english: Now a mediator is not between one, but God is one.

kikuyu: Nake Jesũ akĩmacookeria atĩrĩ, “Mwĩmenyagĩrĩrei mũtikanaheenio nĩ mũndũ o na ũrĩkũ.
english: Jesus answered them, “Be careful that no one leads you astray.

kikuyu: Airĩtu a Zelofehadi, nĩo Mahala, na Tiriza, na Hogila, na Milika, na Noa, maahikirio nĩ ariũ a mũrũ wa nyina na ithe wao.
english: for Mahlah, Tirzah, Hoglah, Milcah, and Noah, the daughters of Zelophehad, were married to their father’s brothers’ sons.

kikuyu: Nake Abisalomu akĩĩra Joabu atĩrĩ, “Ndagũtũmanĩire, ngĩkwĩra atĩrĩ, ‘Ũka, nĩguo ngũtũme kwa mũthamaki, ũkamũũrie atĩrĩ, “Nĩ kĩĩ gĩatũmire nyume Geshuru? Ũngĩrĩ ũndũ mwega korwo nĩkuo ndũũraga o na rĩu!” ’ Rĩu-rĩ, nĩngwenda kuona ũthiũ wa mũthamaki, na hangĩkorwo nĩ harĩ na ũndũ njĩkĩte mũũru, nĩakĩĩnjũrage.”
english: Absalom answered Joab, “Behold, I sent to you, saying, ‘Come here, that I may send you to the king, to say, “Why have I come from Geshur? It would be better for me to be there still. Now therefore, let me see the king’s face; and if there is iniquity in me, let him kill me.” ’ ”

kikuyu: Andũ acio maarahũkaga ta mũrũũthi wa nga; mookagĩra ta mũrũũthi wa njamba, ũrĩa ũtangĩhurũka ũtarĩĩte kĩrĩa ũnyiitĩte, na ũkanyua thakame ya kĩrĩa ũragĩte.”
english: Behold, a people rises up as a lioness. As a lion he lifts himself up. He shall not lie down until he eats of the prey, and drinks the blood of the slain.”

kikuyu: Na hĩndĩ ĩrĩa maahaicire gatarũ-rĩ, rũhuho rũgĩtiga kũhurutana.
english: When they got up into the boat, the wind ceased.

kikuyu: Rũthanju rwa Lawi ũrwandĩke rĩĩtwa rĩa Harũni, nĩ ũndũ no nginya kũgĩe na rũthanju rũmwe rwa mũtongoria wa o mũhĩrĩga wa maithe mao ma tene.
english: You shall write Aaron’s name on Levi’s rod. There shall be one rod for each head of their fathers’ houses.

kikuyu: Thuutha wa maũndũ macio, niĩ Danieli ngĩrora, ngĩona hau mbere yakwa harũngiĩ andũ angĩ eerĩ, ũmwe wao arũngiĩ hũgũrũrũ-inĩ cia mũrĩmo ũyũ wa rũũĩ nake ũcio ũngĩ akarũgama hũgũrũrũ-inĩ cia mũrĩmo ũrĩa ũngĩ.
english: Then I, Daniel, looked, and behold, two others stood, one on the river bank on this side, and the other on the river bank on that side.

kikuyu: Ahabu mũrũ wa Omuri nĩekire maũndũ maingĩ mooru maitho-inĩ ma Jehova, makĩria ya mũndũ o wothe wa arĩa maarĩ mbere yake.
english: Ahab the son of Omri did that which was evil in Yahweh’s sight above all that were before him.

kikuyu: Musa akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Cirĩei ũmũthĩ, tondũ ũmũthĩ nĩ mũthenya wa Thabatũ ya Jehova. Ũmũthĩ mũtigũciona igwĩte thĩ.
english: Moses said, “Eat that today, for today is a Sabbath to Yahweh. Today you shall not find it in the field.

kikuyu: o icio njigĩte nĩ ũndũ wa mahinda ma thĩĩna, ngaciiga nĩ ũndũ wa matukũ ma mbaara na ma kũrũa.
english: which I have reserved against the time of trouble, against the day of battle and war?

kikuyu: Mũrĩithi wa mũcaara tiwe mũrĩithi ũrĩa mwene ngʼondu. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio rĩrĩa ona njũũi ĩgĩũka, atiganagĩria ngʼondu, akoora. Hĩndĩ ĩyo nayo njũũi ĩgatharĩkĩra rũũru, ĩkarũhurunja.
english: He who is a hired hand, and not a shepherd, who doesn’t own the sheep, sees the wolf coming, leaves the sheep, and flees. The wolf snatches the sheep and scatters them.

kikuyu: o na atumia amwe a arĩa maarutĩtwo ngoma thũku na arĩa maahonetio mĩrimũ: nao nĩ Mariamu (ũrĩa wetagwo Mũmagidali), nĩwe warutĩtwo ndaimono mũgwanja;
english: and certain women who had been healed of evil spirits and infirmities: Mary who was called Magdalene, from whom seven demons had gone out;

kikuyu: nao aya nĩo maarĩ a njiaro cia Beria: kuuma kũrĩ Heberi, nĩ mbarĩ ya Aheberi; na kuuma kũrĩ Malikieli, nĩ mbarĩ ya Amalikieli.
english: Of the sons of Beriah: of Heber, the family of the Heberites; of Malchiel, the family of the Malchielites.

kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa ũngĩhutia ũrĩrĩ ũcio no nginya athambie nguo ciake na ethambe na maaĩ, na atiinde arĩ na thaahu o nginya hwaĩ-inĩ.
english: Whoever touches his bed shall wash his clothes, and bathe himself in water, and be unclean until the evening.

kikuyu: Na niĩ ngĩũria atĩrĩ, “Mwathi wakwa, ici nacio nĩ cia kĩ?” Mũraika ũcio waaragia na niĩ akĩnjookeria atĩrĩ, “Nĩngũkwĩra nĩ cia kĩ.”
english: Then I asked, ‘My lord, what are these?’ ” The angel who talked with me said to me, “I will show you what these are.”

kikuyu: Maarĩrĩrie, na ũmatuĩre ciira na kĩhooto; rũĩrĩra kĩhooto kĩa arĩa athĩĩni na arĩa abatari.” Ciugo cia Kũrigĩrĩria: Mũtumia ũrĩa Ngatha
english: Open your mouth, judge righteously, and serve justice to the poor and needy.”

kikuyu: Solomoni ndaathimire indo icio tondũ ciarĩ nyingĩ mũno; ũritũ wa gĩcango ndwamenyekanire ũrĩa waiganaga.
english: Solomon left all the vessels unweighed, because there were so many of them. The weight of the bronze could not be determined.

kikuyu: Matukũ maingĩ maathira Jehova akĩnjĩĩra atĩrĩ, “Rĩu ũkĩra ũthiĩ ũkinye Rũũĩ-inĩ rwa Farati, woe mũcibi ũrĩa ndakwĩrire ũkaũhithe kuo.”
english: After many days, Yahweh said to me, “Arise, go to the Euphrates, and take the belt from there, which I commanded you to hide there.”

kikuyu: Tũngiuga atĩrĩ, ‘Nĩtũgũthiĩ thĩinĩ wa itũũra,’ o nakuo kũrĩ na ngʼaragu, na no tũgũkua. Na tũngĩikara haha, no tũgũkua. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio rekei tũthiĩ kũu kambĩ-inĩ ya Asuriata, twĩneane kũrĩĩo. Mangĩtũhonokia, tũgĩtũũre muoyo; mangĩtũũraga, tũgĩkue.”
english: If we say, ‘We will enter into the city,’ then the famine is in the city, and we will die there. If we sit still here, we also die.  A kab was about 2 liters, so a fourth of a kab would be about 500 milliliters or about a pint  1 seah is about 7 liters or 1.9 gallons or 0.8 pecks  A shekel is about 10 grams or about 0.35 ounces. In this context, it was probably a silver coin weighing that much.

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Jehu akĩniina biũ ũhooi wa Baali thĩinĩ wa Isiraeli.
english: Thus Jehu destroyed Baal out of Israel.

kikuyu: Nake Jesũ akĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Rũciaro rũrũ rũtetĩkĩtie na rũremi, nĩ nginya rĩ ngũikara na inyuĩ? Ngũmũkirĩrĩria nginya rĩ? Ndeherai kamwana kau.”
english: Jesus answered, “Faithless and perverse generation! How long will I be with you? How long will I bear with you? Bring him here to me.”

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo andũ othe magĩthiĩ o mũndũ gwake mũciĩ, nake Daudi agĩcooka mũciĩ akarathime andũ a nyũmba yake.
english: All the people departed, each man to his house; and David returned to bless his house.

kikuyu: Jehova agaagũtua wa gũtongoria, no ti wa kũrigia. Ũngĩrũmbũiya maathani ma Jehova Ngai waku marĩa ndĩrakũhe ũmũthĩ, na ũmenyerere ũmarũmĩrĩre, ũrĩkoragwo hĩndĩ ciothe ũrĩ wa mbere na ndũgatuĩka wa kũrigagia.
english: Yahweh will make you the head, and not the tail. You will be above only, and you will not be beneath, if you listen to the commandments of Yahweh your God which I command you today, to observe and to do,

kikuyu: Thĩ nĩnjatũku, yatũkĩte ĩkarekania, ningĩ nĩĩrathingitha mũno mũno.
english: The earth is utterly broken. The earth is torn apart. The earth is shaken violently.

kikuyu: Andũ a Gaza makeenjwo njuĩrĩ macakae; Ashikeloni nakuo gũgaakira ki. Atĩrĩrĩ, inyuĩ matigari maya mũrĩ kũu werũ-inĩ, mũgũtũũra mwĩtemangaga nginya-rĩ?
english: Baldness has come on Gaza; Ashkelon is brought to nothing. You remnant of their valley, how long will you cut yourself?

kikuyu: Anene ake makĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Ta rora, nĩtũiguĩte atĩ athamaki a nyũmba ya Isiraeli nĩ marĩ tha. Twĩtĩkĩrie tũthiĩ kũrĩ mũthamaki wa Isiraeli twĩhotorete nguo cia makũnia njohero na twĩrigiicĩirie mĩhĩndo mĩtwe iitũ. No gũkorwo nĩagetĩkĩra kũhonokia muoyo waku.”
english: His servants said to him, “See now, we have heard that the kings of the house of Israel are merciful kings. Please let us put sackcloth on our bodies and ropes on our heads, and go out to the king of Israel. Maybe he will save your life.”

kikuyu: Gũtikageranwo ngero kana kũniinanwo, kũu kĩrĩma-inĩ gĩakwa gĩtheru, nĩgũkorwo thĩ nĩĩkaiyũrwo nĩ ũmenyo wa Jehova, o ta ũrĩa maaĩ maiyũrĩte iria-inĩ.
english: They will not hurt nor destroy in all my holy mountain; for the earth will be full of the knowledge of Yahweh, as the waters cover the sea.

kikuyu: Nao andũ arĩa maarĩ agwete na marĩĩtwa makĩoya mĩgwate ĩyo, na arĩa maarĩ njaga makĩmahumba nguo kuuma indo-inĩ iria ciatahĩtwo. Makĩmahe nguo na iraatũ, na irio na kĩndũ gĩa kũnyua, na maguta ma kũhonania. Nao arĩa othe mataarĩ na hinya makĩhaicio ndigiri igũrũ. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio makĩmacookia kũrĩ andũ a bũrũri wao o kũu Jeriko, Itũũra Inene rĩa Mĩkĩndũ, nao magĩcooka Samaria.
english: The men who have been mentioned by name rose up and took the captives, and with the plunder clothed all who were naked among them, dressed them, gave them sandals, gave them something to eat and to drink, anointed them, carried all the feeble of them on donkeys, and brought them to Jericho, the city of palm trees, to their brothers. Then they returned to Samaria.

kikuyu: Nĩmĩ ciao itariĩ ta mĩguĩ ya kũũragana; no ndeto cia maheeni ciaragia.
english: Their tongue is a deadly arrow. It speaks deceit. One speaks peaceably to his neighbor with his mouth, but in his heart, he waits to ambush him.

kikuyu: Ningĩ Jehova akĩĩra Musa atĩrĩ, “Nĩ nginya hĩndĩ ĩrĩkũ mũgũtũũra mũregete kũmenyerera maathani makwa na kũigua ũrutani wakwa?
english: Yahweh said to Moses, “How long do you refuse to keep my commandments and my laws?

kikuyu: (Aciari ake matiamenyaga atĩ ũndũ ũcio woimĩte kũrĩ Jehova, ũrĩa wacaragia handũ ha kũrutia Afilisti amokĩrĩre, nĩ ũndũ hĩndĩ ĩyo Afilisti nĩo maathaga Isiraeli.)
english: But his father and his mother didn’t know that it was of Yahweh; for he sought an occasion against the Philistines. Now at that time the Philistines ruled over Israel.

kikuyu: Nake agĩcookia atĩrĩ, “Nĩ kĩĩ gĩgũtũma ũnjũũrie rĩĩtwa rĩakwa? Ndũngĩhota kũrĩmenya, nĩ rĩa magegania.”
english: Yahweh’s angel said to him, “Why do you ask about my name, since it is incomprehensible†?”

kikuyu: Nake agĩcookia atĩrĩ, “Niĩ ndiũĩ kana nĩ mwĩhia kana ti mwĩhia. No njũũĩ ũndũ ũyũ ũmwe; ndĩraarĩ mũtumumu na rĩu nĩndĩrona!”
english: He therefore answered, “I don’t know if he is a sinner. One thing I do know: that though I was blind, now I see.”

kikuyu: nake mũthuuriwe aigue ũhoro ũcio na aage kũmwĩra ũndũ, hĩndĩ ĩyo mĩĩhĩtwa yake kana ciĩranĩro icio ehingĩte nacio we mwene nĩigatũũra.
english: and her husband hears it, and says nothing to her in the day that he hears it; then her vows shall stand, and her pledges with which she has bound her soul shall stand.

kikuyu: “Rĩu mũrũ wa mũndũ-rĩ, hũgũra ũthiũ waku kũrĩ airĩtu a andũ anyu arĩa marathaga mohoro moimĩte meciiria-inĩ mao ene, ũmarathĩre ũhoro wa kũmookĩrĩra,
english: You, son of man, set your face against the daughters of your people, who prophesy out of their own heart; and prophesy against them,

kikuyu: Ririkanaga warĩ ngombo bũrũri wa Misiri, na Jehova Ngai waku nĩagũkũũrire ũkiuma kuo. Nĩkĩo ndĩragwatha wĩkage ũguo.
english: but you shall remember that you were a slave in Egypt, and Yahweh your God redeemed you there. Therefore I command you to do this thing.

kikuyu: Jehova arĩ igũrũ aroraga thĩ akoona andũ othe;
english: Yahweh looks from heaven. He sees all the sons of men.

kikuyu: nake one rũhiũ rwa njora rũgĩũka gũũkĩrĩra bũrũri ũcio, nake ahuhe karumbeta nĩgeetha amenyithie andũ mehũũge-rĩ,
english: if, when he sees the sword come on the land, he blows the trumpet and warns the people,

kikuyu: Ũcooke ũrĩrigiicĩrie: Rĩakĩrĩre indo cia mbaara cia gũteithia gũtharĩkĩra itũũra, na wake kĩhumbu gĩkinyĩte itũũra, na ũrĩakĩrĩre kambĩ na nja, na ũigithie mĩgogo mĩtungu ya kũrĩmomora ĩrĩrigiicĩirie.
english: Lay siege against it, build forts against it, and cast up a mound against it. Also set camps against it and plant battering rams against it all around.

kikuyu: Nake ũrĩa wĩhĩtaga na igũrũ, ehĩtaga na gĩtĩ kĩa ũnene kĩa Ngai, na akehĩta na ũrĩa ũgĩikaragĩra.
english: He who swears by heaven, swears by the throne of God and by him who sits on it.

kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, mũnabii wa Jehova wetagwo Odedi aarĩ kuo, nake akiumagara agatũnge mbũtũ cia ita igĩcooka Samaria. Nake agĩciĩra atĩrĩ, “Tondũ Jehova, o we Ngai wa maithe manyu, nĩarakarĩire andũ a Juda-rĩ, nĩamaneanire moko-inĩ manyu. No inyuĩ nĩmũmooragĩte na marakara, namo nĩ makinyĩte o igũrũ.
english: But a prophet of Yahweh was there, whose name was Oded; and he went out to meet the army that came to Samaria, and said to them, “Behold, because Yahweh, the God of your fathers, was angry with Judah, he has delivered them into your hand, and you have slain them in a rage which has reached up to heaven.

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo mũcukani akĩmũtiga, nao araika magĩũka makĩmũtungatĩra. Jesũ Kwambĩrĩria Kũhunjia
english: Then the devil left him, and behold, angels came and served him.

kikuyu: Nĩngamatheria mathirwo nĩ mehia mothe marĩa mananjĩhĩria na ndĩmarekere mehia mao mothe ma kũrega kũnjathĩkĩra.
english: I will cleanse them from all their iniquity by which they have sinned against me. I will pardon all their iniquities by which they have sinned against me and by which they have transgressed against me.

kikuyu: No Jesũ ndaigana gũcookia ũndũ, nake Pilato akĩgega.
english: But Jesus made no further answer, so that Pilate marveled.

kikuyu: Nao makĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Kira ki! Ndũkoige ũndũ. Ũka ũtwarane na ithuĩ, ũtuĩke ithe witũ na mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai witũ. Githĩ ti wega ũgĩtungatĩra rũrĩrĩ na mũhĩrĩga thĩinĩ wa Isiraeli ũrĩ mũthĩnjĩri- Ngai, handũ ha gũtungatĩra nyũmba ya mũndũ ũmwe?”
english: They said to him, “Hold your peace, put your hand on your mouth, and go with us. Be a father and a priest to us. Is it better for you to be priest to the house of one man, or to be priest to a tribe and a family in Israel?”

kikuyu: Daudi akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Hĩndĩ ĩyo nĩguo ũrĩĩonera ũrĩa ndungata yaku ĩngĩhota gwĩka.” Akishi akĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Ũguo nĩ wega, na niĩ nĩngũgũtua mũnangĩri wa hĩndĩ ciothe.”
english: David said to Achish, “Therefore you will know what your servant can do.” Achish said to David, “Therefore I will make you my bodyguard forever.”

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo Nathanieli akiuga atĩrĩ, “Rabii, wee nĩwe Mũrũ wa Ngai; wee nĩwe Mũthamaki wa Isiraeli.”
english: Nathanael answered him, “Rabbi, you are the Son of God! You are King of Israel!”

kikuyu: na gategwa kamwe, na ndũrũme ĩmwe, na gatũrũme kamwe ka mwaka ũmwe, nĩ ũndũ wa iruta rĩa njino;
english: one young bull, one ram, one male lamb a year old, for a burnt offering;

kikuyu: We angũũraga kuuma mbaara-inĩ ĩrĩa ndũithagio, ngoima itagurarĩtio, o na gwakorwo nĩ andũ aingĩ mandũithagia.
english: He has redeemed my soul in peace from the battle that was against me, although there are many who oppose me.

kikuyu: “ ‘Nyama iria ingĩhutania na kĩndũ o ro gĩothe kĩrĩ na thaahu itikanarĩĩo; no nginya icinwo na mwaki. Nonyamaicioingĩmũndũorowotheũtethaahĩtienoacirĩe.
english: “ ‘The meat that touches any unclean thing shall not be eaten. It shall be burned with fire. As for the meat, everyone who is clean may eat it;

kikuyu: Ũhoro wa ariũ ake na morathi maingĩ maamũkoniĩ, na maandĩko makoniĩ gũcookererio kwa hekarũ ya Ngai maandĩkĩtwo thĩinĩ wa ibuku rĩa ũtaũranĩri rĩa athamaki. Nake mũriũ Amazia agĩtuĩka mũthamaki ithenya rĩake.
english: Now concerning his sons, the greatness of the burdens laid on him, and the rebuilding of God’s house, behold, they are written in the commentary of the book of the kings. Amaziah his son reigned in his place.

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Ngai akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Kuona ũguo nĩguo wahooya, na ndũnahooya ũingĩhĩrio matukũ ma gũtũũra muoyo, kana ũtonga waku wee mwene, o na kana thũ ciaku iniinwo, no wahooya ũmenyo wa kũrũgamĩrĩra kĩhooto-rĩ,
english: God said to him, “Because you have asked this thing, and have not asked for yourself long life, nor have you asked for riches for yourself, nor have you asked for the life of your enemies, but have asked for yourself understanding to discern justice,

kikuyu: “Wee gĩkuũ-rĩ, gũtoorania gwaku gũkĩrĩ ha? Wee gĩkuũ-rĩ, rũboora rwaku rũkĩrĩ ha?”
english: “Death, where is your sting? Hades,† where is your victory?”*

kikuyu: Bela mũrũ wa Beori nĩwe watuĩkire mũthamaki wa Edomu. Itũũra rĩake inene rĩetagwo Dinihaba.
english: Bela, the son of Beor, reigned in Edom. The name of his city was Dinhabah.

kikuyu: Maũndũ macio mothe meekĩkire nĩ ũndũ andũ a Isiraeli nĩmehĩirie Jehova Ngai wao, ũrĩa wamarutĩte bũrũri wa Misiri moime watho-inĩ wa Firaũni mũthamaki wa Misiri. Nao makĩinamĩrĩra ngai ingĩ, magĩcihooya,
english: It was so because the children of Israel had sinned against Yahweh their God, who brought them up out of the land of Egypt from under the hand of Pharaoh king of Egypt, and had feared other gods,

kikuyu: akĩrutũrũra aikari akuo, akĩmahe wĩra wa gwatũra mbaũ na mĩthumeno, na aruti wĩra na harũ cia igera na wa mathanwa, na wĩra wa gũthondeka maturubarĩ. Nake eekire ũguo matũũra-inĩ mothe ma Aamoni. Thuutha ũcio Daudi na mbũtũ ciake ciothe cia ita magĩcooka Jerusalemu.
english: He brought out the people who were in it, and put them to work under saws, under iron picks, under axes of iron, and made them go to the brick kiln; and he did so to all the cities of the children of Ammon. Then David and all the people returned to Jerusalem.

kikuyu: Gatagatĩ-inĩ ka andũ acio nĩ haarĩ na mũtumia ũmwe watũire oiraga thakame mĩaka ikũmi na ĩĩrĩ, na gũtirĩ mũndũ ũngĩamũhonirie.
english: A woman who had a flow of blood for twelve years, who had spent all her living on physicians and could not be healed by any,

kikuyu: Ngĩgwa thĩ, na ngĩigua mũgambo mũnene ũkĩnjarĩria, ũkĩnjĩĩra atĩrĩ, ‘Saũlũ! Saũlũ! Ũũnyariiraga nĩkĩ?’
english: I fell to the ground and heard a voice saying to me, ‘Saul, Saul, why are you persecuting me?’

kikuyu: Agĩcooka akĩoya gĩkombe, agĩcookia ngaatho akĩmanengera, nao othe magĩkĩnyuĩra.
english: He took the cup, and when he had given thanks, he gave to them. They all drank of it.

kikuyu: “Arĩria andũ a Isiraeli, ũmeere atĩrĩ: ‘Rĩrĩa mũgaatoonya bũrũri ũcio ngũmũhe na mũgethe magetha maguo, nĩmũkarehere mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai kĩohe kĩa mbere kĩa ngano ĩrĩa mũkaagetha.
english: “Speak to the children of Israel, and tell them, ‘When you have come into the land which I give to you, and shall reap its harvest, then you shall bring the sheaf of the first fruits of your harvest to the priest.

kikuyu: Jesũ Kristũ atũũraga atekũgarũrũka, ira, na ũmũthĩ, o na nginya tene.
english: Jesus Christ is the same yesterday, today, and forever.

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ wa ũguo magĩcookanĩrĩria aathani othe a Afilisti makĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Cookiai ithandũkũ rĩĩrĩ rĩa ngai ya Isiraeli rĩehere gũkũ; nĩrĩcookio kũrĩa riumĩte kana rĩtũũrage hamwe na andũ aitũ.” Nĩgũkorwo gĩkuũ nĩkĩaiyũrĩtie itũũra rĩu inene kĩmako; guoko kwa Ngai nĩ kwarĩũmĩire mũno.
english: They sent therefore and gathered together all the lords of the Philistines, and they said, “Send the ark of the God of Israel away, and let it go again to its own place, that it not kill us and our people.” For there was a deadly panic throughout all the city. The hand of God was very heavy there.

kikuyu: “Ti-itherũ, Jehova, athamaki a Ashuri nĩmanangĩte andũ a ndũrĩrĩ na mabũrũri mao biũ.
english: Truly, Yahweh, the kings of Assyria have destroyed all the countries and their land,

kikuyu: goro yakwa yetagĩrĩra Ngai o we wiki; ũhonokio wakwa uumaga harĩ we.
english: y soul rests in God alone. My salvation is from him.

kikuyu: Nake Jesũ agĩthiĩ kũrĩ o akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Nĩheetwo ũhoti wothe igũrũ na gũkũ thĩ.
english: Jesus came to them and spoke to them, saying, “All authority has been given to me in heaven and on earth.

kikuyu: Roranagiai maũndũ mothe. Rũmagiai maũndũ marĩa mega.
english: Test all things, and hold firmly that which is good.

kikuyu: Na indo ici nĩcio aatũmĩire ithe: Ndigiri ikũmi ikuuĩte indo iria njega mũno cia Misiri, na ndigiri ingĩ ikũmi cia mĩgoma ikuuĩte ngano na mĩgate na rĩĩgu wake wa rũgendo.
english: He sent the following to his father: ten donkeys loaded with the good things of Egypt, and ten female donkeys loaded with grain and bread and provision for his father by the way.

kikuyu: No ũrĩa ũkangaana mbere ya andũ ũcio nĩagakaanwo mbere ya araika a Ngai.
english: but he who denies me in the presence of men will be denied in the presence of God’s angels.

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo ũteithio wa Misiri nĩ wa tũhũ na ndũrĩ kĩguni. Nĩ ũndũ bũrũri ũcio ndĩwĩtĩte Rahabu ũrĩa Ũtarĩ-ũndũ-Ekaga.
english: For Egypt helps in vain, and to no purpose; therefore I have called her Rahab who sits still.

kikuyu: Anene a kũu matiamenyire kũrĩa ndathiĩte kana ũrĩa ndekaga tondũ ndiamenyithĩtie Ayahudi, kana athĩnjĩri-Ngai, kana arĩa maarĩ igweta, kana anene, o na kana arĩa angĩ mangĩarutire wĩra.
english: The rulers didn’t know where I went, or what I did. I had not as yet told it to the Jews, nor to the priests, nor to the nobles, nor to the rulers, nor to the rest who did the work.

kikuyu: tĩrĩrĩ, nĩgũkagĩa mũthamaki wa gũthamaka na ũthingu, na kũgĩe na anene a gwathana na kĩhooto.
english: ehold, a king shall reign in righteousness, and princes shall rule in justice.

kikuyu: Ningĩ Johana akĩheana ũira ũyũ: “Nĩndonire Roho agĩikũrũka kuuma igũrũ atariĩ ta ndutura, agĩikara igũrũ rĩake.
english: John testified, saying, “I have seen the Spirit descending like a dove out of heaven, and it remained on him.

kikuyu: O hĩndĩ ĩyo kĩũngano gĩothe gĩa Isiraeli kĩrũgamĩte o hau, mũthamaki agĩkĩhũgũkĩra, agĩkĩrathima.
english: The king turned his face around and blessed all the assembly of Israel; and all the assembly of Israel stood.

kikuyu: kana rĩrĩa mũndũ mũrũme angĩnyiitwo nĩ ũiru nĩ ũndũ nĩegwĩkũũa mũtumia ũcio wake.
english: or when the spirit of jealousy comes on a man, and he is jealous of his wife; then he shall set the woman before Yahweh, and the priest shall execute on her all this law.

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio, ngũmwĩra ũmũthĩ atĩ gũtirĩ thakame ya mũndũ o na ũmwe ngoorio.
english: Therefore I testify to you today that I am clean from the blood of all men,

kikuyu: matarĩtwo na mbarĩ ciao, maarĩ andũ 3,200.
english: even those who were counted of them by their families, were three thousand two hundred.

kikuyu: O hĩndĩ ĩyo Simoni Petero arũgamĩte agĩota mwaki, akĩũrio atĩrĩ, “Kaĩ wee ũrĩ ũmwe wa arutwo a mũndũ ũyũ?” Nake agĩkaana, akiuga atĩrĩ, “Aca, ndirĩ ũmwe wao!”
english: Now Simon Peter was standing and warming himself. They said therefore to him, “You aren’t also one of his disciples, are you?” He denied it and said, “I am not.”

kikuyu: “Niĩ Jehova nĩ niĩ ngwĩtĩte na njĩra ya ũthingu; nĩngakũnyiita guoko. Nĩngakũmenyagĩrĩra na ngũheane ũtuĩke wa kũrĩkanĩra kĩrĩkanĩro na andũ, o na ũtuĩke ũtheri wa gũtherera andũ a Ndũrĩrĩ,
english: “I, Yahweh, have called you in righteousness. I will hold your hand. I will keep you, and make you a covenant for the people, as a light for the nations,

kikuyu: Ningĩ gũkĩra maũndũ macio mothe mega- rĩ, gĩai na wendani, tondũ nĩguo wohanagia maũndũ mothe hamwe nĩguo magĩrĩre kũna.
english: Above all these things, walk in love, which is the bond of perfection.

kikuyu: Afarisai amwe maarĩ gĩkundi-inĩ kĩu makĩĩra Jesũ atĩrĩ, “Mũrutani, kaania arutwo aku!”
english: Some of the Pharisees from the multitude said to him, “Teacher, rebuke your disciples!”

kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, ta ũtukũ gatagatĩ, Paũlũ na Sila nĩkũhooya maahooyaga makĩinagĩra Ngai nyĩmbo, nao andũ arĩa angĩ mohetwo makamathikĩrĩria.
english: But about midnight Paul and Silas were praying and singing hymns to God, and the prisoners were listening to them.

kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, rĩrĩa ihinda rĩao rĩa gũtherio rĩakinyire kũringana na Watho wa Musa-rĩ,† Jusufu na Mariamu magĩgatwara Jerusalemu nĩguo magakaneane harĩ Mwathani
english: When the days of their purification according to the law of Moses were fulfilled, they brought him up to Jerusalem to present him to the Lord

kikuyu: Mũrũũthi* nĩumĩte kĩmamo-inĩ kĩaguo; mũniinani wa kũniina ndũrĩrĩ nĩoimagarĩte. Oimĩte gwake oke aanange bũrũri waku. Matũũra maku makaanangĩka, mage mũndũ wa gũtũũra kuo.
english: A lion has gone up from his thicket, and a destroyer of nations. He is on his way. He has gone out from his place, to make your land desolate, that your cities be laid waste, without inhabitant.

kikuyu: Wee nĩũkanĩte kĩrĩkanĩro kĩrĩa ũrĩkanĩire na ndungata yaku, na ũkarangĩrĩria thũmbĩ yake rũkũngũ-inĩ.
english: You have renounced the covenant of your servant. You have defiled his crown in the dust.

kikuyu: Nake mũrutwo ũngĩ wake wetagwo Anderea, mũrũ wa nyina na Simoni Petero, akiuga atĩrĩ,
english: One of his disciples, Andrew, Simon Peter’s brother, said to him,

kikuyu: Jehova nĩarĩĩtũmaga thũ iria ingĩarahũka igũũkĩrĩre ihootagĩrwo mbere yaku. Irĩkũhithũkagĩra na njĩra ĩmwe, no irĩkũũragĩra na njĩra mũgwanja.
english: Yahweh will cause your enemies who rise up against you to be struck before you. They will come out against you one way, and will flee before you seven ways.

kikuyu: Nake mũnabii akĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Tiga gwĩtigĩra, nĩgũkorwo arĩa marĩ mwena witũ nĩ aingĩ gũkĩra arĩa marĩ mwena wao.”
english: He answered, “Don’t be afraid, for those who are with us are more than those who are with them.”

kikuyu: Ngaamuoherera handũ harũmu aikare ho ta higĩ hũũrĩrĩre; atuĩke gĩtĩ gĩa gĩtĩĩo thĩinĩ wa nyũmba ya ithe.
english: I will fasten him like a nail in a sure place. He will be for a throne of glory to his father’s house.

kikuyu: Nao makĩguthũkĩra indo iria maatahĩte, makĩoya ngʼondu, na ngʼombe, na tũcaũ, magĩcithĩnjĩra hau thĩ, na magĩcirĩanĩria na thakame.
english: and the people pounced on the plunder, and took sheep, cattle, and calves, and killed them on the ground; and the people ate them with the blood.

kikuyu: “Jehova we mwene nĩakarũgamia mũthamaki ũngĩ wa gũthamakĩra Isiraeli ũrĩa ũkaaniina nyũmba ya Jeroboamu. Ũmũthĩ nĩguo mũthenya! Atĩ atĩa? Ĩĩ, amĩniine o ro rĩu.
english: Moreover Yahweh will raise up a king for himself over Israel who will cut off the house of Jeroboam. This is the day! What? Even now.

kikuyu: Na rĩrĩa ariũ a ithe moonire atĩ ithe nĩamwendete kũmakĩra-rĩ, makĩmũmena na matingĩamwarĩirie kiugo o na kĩmwe kĩega.
english: His brothers saw that their father loved him more than all his brothers, and they hated him, and couldn’t speak peaceably to him.

kikuyu: Menyai ũũ, atĩ gũtirĩ kiugo o na kĩmwe kĩarĩtio nĩ Jehova igũrũ rĩa nyũmba ya Ahabu gĩtakahingio. Jehova ekĩte o ũrĩa oigĩte na kanua ka ndungata yake Elija.”
english: Know now that nothing will fall to the earth of Yahweh’s word, which Yahweh spoke concerning Ahab’s house. For Yahweh has done that which he spoke by his servant Elijah.”

kikuyu: Mbere oigire atĩrĩ, “Magongona na maruta, na maruta ma njino, o na maruta ma kũhoroherio mehia, ti mo weriragĩria, o na kana ũgakenio nĩmo” (o na gũtuĩka watho nĩwaathanĩte marutagwo).
english: Previously saying, “Sacrifices and offerings and whole burnt offerings and sacrifices for sin you didn’t desire, neither had pleasure in them” (those which are offered according to the law),

kikuyu: “No ithe akĩĩra ndungata ciake atĩrĩ, ‘Thiĩi narua mũrehe nguo ĩrĩa njega mũno mũmũhumbe, na mũmwĩkĩre gĩcũhĩ kĩara, na iraatũ magũrũ.
english: “But the father said to his servants, ‘Bring out the best robe and put it on him. Put a ring on his hand and sandals on his feet.

kikuyu: Jakubu agĩĩta handũ hau Ngai aamwarĩirie Betheli. Gĩkuũ kĩa Rakeli na kĩa Isaaka
english: Jacob called the name of the place where God spoke with him “Bethel”.

kikuyu: “Njookererai, inyuĩ andũ aya mũũhutatĩire; nĩngũmũhonia nĩguo mũtigacooke gũcooka na thuutha.” Magĩcookia atĩrĩ, “Ĩĩ nĩguo, nĩtũgũũka harĩwe, nĩgũkorwo wee nĩwe Jehova Ngai witũ.
english: Return, you backsliding children, and I will heal your backsliding. “Behold, we have come to you; for you are Yahweh our God.

kikuyu: Ningĩ ariũ a Harũni, acio athĩnjĩri-Ngai, matandaiye tũcunjĩ tũu twa gategwa, o ro hamwe na mũtwe na maguta, igũrũ rĩa ngũ icio irakanĩra hau kĩgongona-inĩ.
english: and Aaron’s sons, the priests, shall lay the pieces, the head, and the fat in order on the wood that is on the fire which is on the altar;

kikuyu: Mwarĩkia kũringa Rũũĩ rwa Jorodani mũtoonye bũrũri ũrĩa Jehova Ngai wanyu ekũmũhe-rĩ, mũkaahaanda mahiga manene na mũmathinge na rĩũmba.
english: It shall be on the day when you shall pass over the Jordan to the land which Yahweh your God gives you, that you shall set yourself up great stones, and coat them with plaster.

kikuyu: nĩgũkorwo magũrũ mao mahanyũkaga mathiĩ mehia-inĩ, nao mahiũhaga magaite thakame.
english: for their feet run to evil. They hurry to shed blood.

kikuyu: Rĩrĩa Mwene-Hinya-Wothe aahurunjire athamaki bũrũri-inĩ-rĩ, kwahaanaga o ta rĩrĩa tharunji ĩgwĩte kĩrĩma-inĩ gĩa Zalimoni.
english: When the Almighty scattered kings in her, it snowed on Zalmon.

kikuyu: ingĩ Jesũ akĩĩra ikundi cia andũ o hamwe na arutwo ake atĩrĩ:
english: hen Jesus spoke to the multitudes and to his disciples,

kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, inyuĩ mũtiathagwo nĩ mwĩrĩ ũyũ wĩhagia, no mwathagwo nĩ Roho, angĩkorwo Roho wa Ngai atũũragathĩinĩ wanyu. No rĩrĩ, mũndũ o wothe ũtarĩ na Roho wa Kristũ-rĩ, ũcio ti wa Kristũ.
english: But you are not in the flesh but in the Spirit, if it is so that the Spirit of God dwells in you. But if any man doesn’t have the Spirit of Christ, he is not his.

kikuyu: Andũ acio nĩo tũgwatha na tũkamaringĩrĩria thĩinĩ wa Mwathani Jesũ Kristũ atĩ makindĩrie, na marĩĩage irio iria marutĩire wĩra.
english: Now those who are that way, we command and exhort in the Lord Jesus Christ, that they work with quietness and eat their own bread.

kikuyu: tĩrĩrĩ, mũthenya wa Jehova nĩũrooka, rĩrĩa indo cianyu igaatahwo na igayanĩrwo gatagatĩ-inĩ kanyu.
english: ehold, a day of Yahweh comes, when your plunder will be divided within you.

kikuyu: Akĩmũnyiita guoko gwake kwa ũrĩo, akĩmũrũgamia na igũrũ, na o hĩndĩ ĩyo magũrũ na thũngʼwa cia mũndũ ũcio ikĩgĩa na hinya.
english: He took him by the right hand and raised him up. Immediately his feet and his ankle bones received strength.  Psalms 16:8-11  “Christ” means “Anointed One”.  or, Hell  Psalms 110:1  3:00 p.m.

kikuyu: Niĩ ndaarĩ o kuo hĩndĩ ĩrĩa aathondekire igũrũ na akĩrĩiga handũ ha rĩo, hĩndĩ ĩrĩa eekĩrire rũkiriri rwa kũrĩa kũriku,
english: When he established the heavens, I was there. When he set a circle on the surface of the deep,

kikuyu: “Ũhoro ũcio ũgĩthirĩra hau. Niĩ Danieli-rĩ, nĩndatangĩkire mũno meciiria-inĩ makwa, na ngĩtukia gĩthiithi, no ngĩikara ngĩĩcũũranagia ũhoro ũcio ngoro-inĩ yakwa.”
english: “Here is the end of the matter. As for me, Daniel, my thoughts troubled me greatly, and my face was changed in me; but I kept the matter in my heart.”

kikuyu: Mwagĩrĩirwo nĩgũkirĩrĩria, nĩguo mwarĩkia gwĩka ũrĩa Ngai endaga, mũkaamũkĩra kĩrĩa eeranĩire.
english: For you need endurance so that, having done the will of God, you may receive the promise.

kikuyu: Ithuothe tũtuĩkĩte ta mũndũ ũrĩ na thaahu, na ciĩko ciothe cia ũthingu witũ ihaana ta ndangari irĩ magigi; ithuothe tũhohaga tũkahaana ta ithangũ, namo mehia maitũ magatũhuruta o ta ũrĩa rũhuho rũhuruutaga maragara.
english: For we have all become like one who is unclean, and all our righteousness is like a polluted garment. We all fade like a leaf; and our iniquities, like the wind, take us away.

kikuyu: Nake Jehova akĩũria Shaitani atĩrĩ, “Uuma kũ?” Nake Shaitani agĩcookeria Jehova atĩrĩ, “Nyuma kũriũnga thĩ o na gũcangacanga kuo kũndũ na kũndũ.”
english: Yahweh said to Satan, “Where have you come from?” Satan answered Yahweh, and said, “From going back and forth in the earth, and from walking up and down in it.”

kikuyu: Thuutha wa ũguo, andũ a Juda magĩikũrũka makahũũrane na andũ a Kaanani arĩa maatũũraga bũrũri ũrĩa ũrĩ irĩma wa Negevu na magũrũ-inĩ ma irĩma cia mwena wa ithũĩro.
english: After that, the children of Judah went down to fight against the Canaanites who lived in the hill country, and in the South, and in the lowland.

kikuyu: Ti-itherũ, inyuĩ nĩ inyuĩ riiri witũ na gĩkeno giitũ.
english: For you are our glory and our joy.

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ wa gwĩtĩkia-rĩ, andũ nĩmatuĩkanĩirie Iria Itune taarĩ thĩ nyũmũ maagereire; no rĩrĩa andũ a Misiri maageririe gwĩka ũguo, makĩrindanio nĩ maaĩ.
english: By faith they passed through the Red Sea as on dry land. When the Egyptians tried to do so, they were swallowed up.

kikuyu: Aburamu nĩatongete mũno na ũtonga wa mahiũ, na wa betha, na wa thahabu.
english: Abram was very rich in livestock, in silver, and in gold.

kikuyu: na andũ a Harimu maarĩ 320,
english: The children of Harim, three hundred twenty.

kikuyu: Ũguo no taguo, mũndũ ũrĩa ũhanyũkaga mathako-inĩ ma mahenya ndangĩheo thũmbĩ ya ũhootani atathaakĩte kũringana na ũrĩa gwathanĩtwo.
english: Also, if anyone competes in athletics, he isn’t crowned unless he has competed by the rules.

kikuyu: “Meere atĩrĩ, ‘Ũũ nĩguo Mwathani Jehova ekuuga: Hihi nĩũgatheerema? Githĩ ndũkamunywo, na maciaro maguomahũrũrwonĩguoũhoohe? Mathangũmaguomothe ma mũringũranonĩmakahooha. Ndũkabatara guoko kũrĩ na hinya, kana andũ aingĩ a kũũmunya na mĩri.
english: “Say, ‘The Lord Yahweh says: “Will it prosper? Won’t he pull up its roots and cut off its fruit, that it may wither, that all its fresh springing leaves may wither? It can’t be raised from its roots by a strong arm or many people.

kikuyu: Mahinda maingĩ nĩmageririe Mũrungu; maathirĩkirie Ũrĩa-Mũtheru-wa-Isiraeli.
english: They turned again and tempted God, and provoked the Holy One of Israel.

kikuyu: na Ladani mũriũ wa Tahani, na Amihudu mũriũ wa Ladani, na Elishama mũriũ wa Amihudu,
english: Ladan his son, Ammihud his son, Elishama his son,

kikuyu: o na Ashidodi na mĩciĩ ĩrĩa yarĩthiũrũrũkĩirie, na tũtũũra twarĩo; na Gaza na mĩciĩ ĩrĩa yarĩthiũrũrũkĩirie, na tũtũũra twa rĩo, nginya karũũĩ ka bũrũri wa Misiri, na hũgũrũrũ cia Iria rĩrĩa Inene.
english: Ashdod, its towns and its villages; Gaza, its towns and its villages; to the brook of Egypt, and the great sea with its coastline.

kikuyu: Kuuma kũrĩ thigari iria ciarũire mbaara, rutai gĩcunjĩ kĩa Jehova, kĩndũ kĩmwe thĩinĩ wa indo magana matano, marĩ andũ, kana ngʼombe, kana ndigiri, kana ngʼondu, o na kana mbũri.
english: Levy a tribute to Yahweh of the men of war who went out to battle: one soul of five hundred; of the persons, of the cattle, of the donkeys, and of the flocks.

kikuyu: Rĩrĩa mũthamaki wa Isiraeli aamoonire-rĩ, akĩũria Elisha atĩrĩ, “Baba ndĩmoorage? Nĩ wega ndĩmoorage?”
english: The king of Israel said to Elisha, when he saw them, “My father, shall I strike them? Shall I strike them?”

kikuyu: nĩgũkorwo andũ o ene nĩo maheanaga ũhoro wa ũrĩa mwatũnyiitire ũgeni. Nĩmaheanaga ũhoro wa ũrĩa mwagarũrũkĩire Ngai, mũgĩtigana na mĩhianano nĩguo mũtungatagĩre Ngai ũrĩa ũrĩ muoyo na ũrĩa wa ma,
english: For they themselves report concerning us what kind of a reception we had from you, and how you turned to God from idols to serve a living and true God,

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo tũgĩkaĩra Jehova, o we Ngai wa maithe maitũ, nake Jehova akĩigua mũgambo witũ, na akĩona mĩtangĩko iitũ, na wĩra mũritũ na ũrĩa twahiinyagĩrĩrio.
english: Then we cried to Yahweh, the God of our fathers. Yahweh heard our voice, and saw our affliction, our toil, and our oppression.

kikuyu: Nĩngatũma andũ arĩa makũhinyagĩrĩria marĩe nyama cia mĩĩrĩ yao; makaarĩĩo nĩ thakame yao ene o ta marĩĩtwo nĩ njoohi. Nao andũ othe nĩmakamenya,
english: I will feed those who oppress you with their own flesh; and they will be drunk on their own Then all flesh shall know that I, Yahweh, am your Savior and your Redeemer, the Mighty One of Jacob.”

kikuyu: no nĩndetigĩrire kĩnyararo gĩa thũ, nĩguo mũmuku ndakaage gũtaũkĩrwo, oige atĩrĩ, ‘Guoko gwitũ nĩgũtooranĩtie; Jehova tiwe wĩkĩte ũũ wothe.’ ”
english: were it not that I feared the provocation of the enemy, lest their adversaries should judge wrongly, lest they should say, ‘Our hand is exalted; Yahweh has not done all this.’ ”

kikuyu: Mũkaũgayana ũtuĩke igai rĩanyu inyuĩ ene, na rĩa andũ a kũngĩ arĩa matũũraga gatagatĩ-inĩ kanyu na nĩ marĩ na ciana. Nĩmũkamatua ta marĩ andũ a Isiraeli maciarĩirwo bũrũri ũcio; acio makaagaĩrwo igai hamwe na inyuĩ gatagatĩ-inĩ ka mĩhĩrĩga ya Isiraeli.
english: You shall divide it by lot for an inheritance to you and to the aliens who live among you, who will father children among you. Then they shall be to you as the native-born among the children of Israel. They shall have inheritance with you among the tribes of Israel.

kikuyu: O rĩngĩ Petero agĩkaana, na o hĩndĩ ĩyo ngũkũ ĩgĩkũga. Jesũ Mbere ya Pilato
english: Peter therefore denied it again, and immediately the rooster crowed.

kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, Mwathani Jehova Mwene-Hinya-Wothe ekuuga atĩrĩ: “Thiĩ kũrĩ Shebina, ũrĩa mũramati na mũrũgamĩrĩri wa nyũmba ya ũthamaki, ũmwĩre atĩrĩ:
english: The Lord, Yahweh of Armies says, “Go, get yourself to this treasurer, even to Shebna, who is over the house, and say,

kikuyu: Ningĩ kiugo kĩa Jehova nĩkĩanginyĩrĩire, ngĩĩrwo atĩrĩ:
english: Moreover Yahweh’s word came to me, saying,

kikuyu: Moimagarire nĩ ũndũ wa Rĩĩtwa rĩake na makĩrega ũteithio kuuma kũrĩ andũ arĩa matetĩkĩtie Ngai.
english: because for the sake of the Name they went out, taking nothing from the Gentiles.

kikuyu: No Ngai akĩmahutatĩra, na akĩmarekereria mahooyage ciũmbe cia igũrũ. Ũhoro ũyũ ũtwaranĩte na ũrĩa kwandĩkĩtwo ibuku-inĩ rĩa anabii, atĩrĩ: “ ‘Nĩmwandutĩire magongona na maruta mĩaka mĩrongo ĩna kũu werũ-inĩ, inyuĩ nyũmba ya Isiraeli?
english: But God turned away and gave them up to serve the army of the sky,§ as it is written in the book of the prophets, ‘Did you offer to me slain animals and sacrifices forty years in the wilderness, O house of Israel?

kikuyu: No gĩtũũgĩre Wee Jehova, nĩ ũndũ wa hinya waku;
english: Be exalted, Yahweh, in your strength, so we will sing and praise your power.

kikuyu: O akĩaragia-rĩ, mũkinyia-ũhoro ũngĩ agĩũka, akiuga atĩrĩ, “Mwaki wa Ngai uumire na igũrũ na wacina ngʼondu o na ndungata waniina biũ; no niĩ nyiki ndahonoka, ndagĩũka gũkũrehere ũhoro!”
english: While he was still speaking, another also came and said, “The fire of God has fallen from the sky, and has burned up the sheep and the servants, and consumed them, and I alone have escaped to tell you.”

kikuyu: Nake ahaana ta mũndũ-rĩ, akĩĩnyiihia, na agĩathĩka o nginya akĩũragwo, o kũũragwo kũrĩa kwa mũtharaba!
english: And being found in human form, he humbled himself, becoming obedient to the point of death, yes, the death of the cross.

kikuyu: ‘Mũthenya wa mbere wa mweri wa mũgwanja, gĩagai na kĩũngano kĩamũre na mũtikarute wĩra wa ndũire. Nĩ mũthenya wanyu wa kũhuhaga tũrumbeta.
english: ‘In the seventh month, on the first day of the month, you shall have a holy convocation; you shall do no regular work. It is a day of blowing of trumpets to you.

kikuyu: Ngĩcooka ngĩona nyamũ ĩngĩ, ĩkiuma na thĩ. Nyamũ ĩyo yarĩ na hĩa igĩrĩ ta cia gatũrũme, no yaaragia ta ndamathia.
english: I saw another beast coming up out of the earth. He had two horns like a lamb and it spoke like a dragon.

kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, andũ arĩa abatari matigũtũũra mariganĩire, na kana mwĩhoko wa arĩa ahinyĩrĩrie ũthire nginya tene.
english: For the needy shall not always be forgotten, nor the hope of the poor perish forever.

kikuyu: Kĩrĩa ehokete no kĩndũ kĩhũthũ; kĩrĩa egiritanagia nakĩo no ta rũrenda rwa mbũmbũĩ.
english: whose confidence will break apart, whose trust is a spider’s web.

kikuyu: Mũndũ angĩenja irima, nĩwe ũkaagũa rĩo; mũndũ angĩgaragaria ihiga, rĩkaagaragara rĩmũcookerere.
english: Whoever digs a pit shall fall into it. Whoever rolls a stone, it will come back on him.

kikuyu: Thuutha wa kwambata, magĩkinya kĩanda kĩa Eshikoli na makĩĩrorera bũrũri ũcio-rĩ, nĩmatũmire andũ a Isiraeli makue ngoro makĩaga gũtoonya bũrũri ũrĩa Jehova amaheete.
english: For when they went up to the valley of Eshcol, and saw the land, they discouraged the heart of the children of Israel, that they should not go into the land which Yahweh had given them.

kikuyu: Rĩrĩa Jesũ oonire gĩkundi kĩa andũ arĩa maamũthiũrũrũkĩirie, agĩathana maringe mũrĩmo ũrĩa ũngĩ wa iria.
english: Now when Jesus saw great multitudes around him, he gave the order to depart to the other side.

kikuyu: No handũ ha ũguo-rĩ, mũrũ kana mwarĩ wa Ithe witũ atwaraga ũrĩa ũngĩ igooti- inĩ, na nĩ igooti-inĩ rĩa arĩa matetĩkĩtie!
english: But brother goes to law with brother, and that before unbelievers!

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Joshua na mbũtũ yake yothe ya ita, makĩmakorerera o rĩmwe kũu maaĩ ma Meromu, makĩmatharĩkĩra,
english: So Joshua came suddenly, with all the warriors, against them by the waters of Merom, and attacked them.

kikuyu: Indo iria aarutire ciarĩ thaani ĩmwe ya betha ya ũritũ wa cekeri igana rĩa mĩrongo ĩtatũ, na mbakũri ĩmwe ya betha ya kũminjaminjĩria ya ũritũ wa cekeri mĩrongo mũgwanja, cierĩ kũringana na gĩthimo gĩa cekeri ya harĩa haamũre, na o ĩmwe ĩiyũrĩtio mũtu ũrĩa mũhinyu mũno ũtukanĩtio na maguta, ũrĩ iruta rĩa mũtu;
english: gave his offering: one silver platter, the weight of which was one hundred thirty shekels, one silver bowl of seventy shekels, according to the shekel of the sanctuary, both of them full of fine flour mixed with oil for a meal offering;

kikuyu: Maathe mekage wega, na maingĩhĩrwo nĩ gwĩkaga ciĩko njega, na matuĩke andũ a gũtugana na kũgayagĩra andũ indo ciao na ũtaana.
english: that they do good, that they be rich in good works, that they be ready to distribute, willing to share;

kikuyu: Nake akĩoya ndũũru ciothe cia mbũri na ngʼombe, nao andũ ake magĩcitwara mbere ya mahiũ marĩa mangĩ, makiugaga atĩrĩ, “Ici nĩ indo iria Daudi aatahĩte.”
english: David took all the flocks and the herds, which they drove before those other livestock, and said, “This is David’s plunder.”

kikuyu: Ningĩ ũreke moe gategwa hamwe na iruta rĩako rĩa mũtu ũrĩa mũhinyu mũno ũtukanĩtio na maguta; nawe ũcooke woe gategwa kangĩ ga keerĩ ga kũrutwo igongona rĩa kũhoroherio mehia.
english: Then let them take a young bull and its meal offering, fine flour mixed with oil; and another young bull you shall take for a sin offering.

kikuyu: na Ashani, na Juta, na Bethi-Shemeshu, hamwe na ũrĩithio wamo.
english: Ashan with its pasture lands, and Beth Shemesh with its pasture lands;

kikuyu: Nake Jehova akĩĩra Musa atĩrĩ,
english: Yahweh spoke to Moses, saying,

kikuyu: Jehova ekuuga atĩrĩ, “Mũthenya ũcio-rĩ, “nĩngananga mbarathi ciaku ikweherere biũ, nacio ngaari ciaku cia ita ndĩcitharie.
english: “It will happen in that day”, says Yahweh, “that I will cut off your horses from among you and will destroy your chariots.

kikuyu: Nĩakaigĩrĩra guoko gwake igũrũ rĩa mũtwe wa harika ĩyo ya iruta rĩa kũhoroherio mehia, na amĩthĩnjĩre harĩa iruta rĩa njino rĩthĩnjagĩrwo.
english: He shall lay his hand on the head of the sin offering, and kill the sin offering in the place of burnt offering.

kikuyu: Nao makĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Rĩu ta ikara thĩ, ũtũthomere.” Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Baruku akĩmathomera.
english: They said to him, “Sit down now, and read it in our hearing.” So Baruch read it in their hearing.

kikuyu: “Mũrũ wa mũndũ, ariũ a thoguo, ariũ a thoguo arĩa marĩ a rũrĩra rwanyu na nyũmba yothe ya Isiraeli, acio nĩo andũ a Jerusalemu maarĩtie ũhoro wao makoiga atĩrĩ, ‘Marĩ haraaya mũno na Jehova; bũrũri ũyũ nĩ ithuĩ twaheirwo ũtuĩke witũ.’ Kĩĩranĩro gĩa Gũcooka Gwa Isiraeli
english: “Son of man, your brothers, even your brothers, the men of your relatives, and all the house of Israel, all of them, are the ones to whom the inhabitants of Jerusalem have said, ‘Go far away from Yahweh. This land has been given to us for a possession.’

kikuyu: Roho Mũtheru we mwene nĩwe mũira hamwe na maroho maitũ atĩ ithuĩ tũrĩ ciana cia Ngai.
english: The Spirit himself testifies with our spirit that we are children of God;

kikuyu: Mwanake ũcio akiuga atĩrĩ, “Maũndũ macio mothe ndũire ndĩmarũmĩtie, nĩ kĩĩ kĩngĩ ndigairie?”
english: The young man said to him, “All these things I have observed from my youth. What do I still lack?”

kikuyu: Ũrĩa wothe magaaciirĩra gwĩka Jehova-rĩ, we nĩakaũniina; o na thĩĩna ndũgooka hĩndĩ ya keerĩ.
english: What do you plot against Yahweh? He will make a full end. Affliction won’t rise up the second time.

kikuyu: We nĩwe Rwaro rwa Ihiga, mawĩra make nĩ makinyanĩru, nacio njĩra ciake ciothe nĩ cia kĩhooto. Nĩ Ngai mwĩhokeku na ndahĩtagia, nĩ mũrũngĩrĩru, na nĩ wa kĩhooto.
english: The Rock: his work is perfect, for all his ways are just. A God of faithfulness who does no wrong, just and right is he.

kikuyu: uuge atĩrĩ, ‘Ũũ nĩguo Mwathani Jehova areera Jerusalemu: Kĩhumo gĩaku na gũciarwo gwaku cioimanire na bũrũri wa andũ a Kaanani; thoguo aarĩ Mũamori, nake maitũguo aarĩ Mũhiti.
english: and say, ‘The Lord Yahweh says to Jerusalem: “Your origin and your birth is of the land of the Canaanite. An Amorite was your father, and your mother was a Hittite.

kikuyu: Nao aanake magĩũka, makĩoha mwĩrĩ wake na cuka, makĩuumia nja na makĩũthika.
english: The young men arose and wrapped him up, and they carried him out and buried him.

kikuyu: Kĩũngano kĩu gĩothe gĩkĩinamĩrĩra, gĩkĩhooya, nao aini makĩinaga na tũrumbeta tũkĩhuhagwo. Maũndũ macio mothe magĩthiĩ na mbere nginya igongona rĩu rĩa njino rĩkĩrĩka kũrutwo.
english: All the assembly worshiped, the singers sang, and the trumpeters sounded. All this continued until the burnt offering was finished.

kikuyu: Agĩĩta athĩnjĩri-Ngai na Alawii hamwe, akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Thiĩi matũũra-inĩ ma Juda mũnganie mbeeca iria ciagĩrĩirwo nĩ kũrĩhwo o mwaka kuuma Isiraeli cia gũcookereria hekarũ ya Ngai wanyu. Ĩkai ũndũ ũcio o rĩu.” No Alawii matiigana gwĩka ũndũ ũcio narua.
english: He gathered together the priests and the Levites, and said to them, “Go out to the cities of Judah, and gather money to repair the house of your God from all Israel from year to year. See that you

kikuyu: Mũthuuriwe nĩagatuuo ndarĩ na ihĩtia o na rĩrĩkũ, no mũtumia ũcio nĩwe ũgaacookererwo nĩ mehia make.’ ”
english: The man shall be free from iniquity, and that woman shall bear her iniquity.’ ”

kikuyu: Warutire andũ aku a Isiraeli kuuma bũrũri wa Misiri na ũndũ wa kũringa ciama na kuonania morirũ, na ũndũ wa guoko gwaku kũrĩ hinya, ũgũtambũrũkĩtie, o na ũgĩĩka maũndũ manene ma kũguoyohia.
english: and brought your people Israel out of the land of Egypt with signs, with wonders, with a strong hand, with an outstretched arm, and with great terror;

kikuyu: Wohaga magũrũ makwa na mĩnyororo; ũrangagĩra njĩra ciakwa ciothe na ũndũ wa gũcora njoro makinya ma magũrũ makwa.
english: You also put my feet in the stocks, and mark all my paths. You set a bound to the soles of my feet,

kikuyu: “Mũrũ wa mũndũ, ngʼethera irĩma cia Isiraeli; cirathĩre ũhoro wa gũciũkĩrĩra,
english: “Son of man, set your face toward the mountains of Israel, and prophesy to them,

kikuyu: Ti-itherũ muoyo waguo ũmaga ũgathira, naho hau tĩĩri-inĩ ũcio hagakũra mĩtĩ ĩngĩ.
english: Behold, this is the joy of his way. Out of the earth, others will spring.

kikuyu: Moabi gũtigacooka kũgaathĩrĩrio rĩngĩ; kũu Heshiboni andũ nĩmagathugunda ũrĩa megũgũũkĩrĩra, makoiga atĩrĩ: ‘Ũkai, nĩtũniinei rũrĩrĩ rũu.’ O nawe itũũra rĩa Madimeni-rĩ, nĩũgakirio ki; rũhiũ rwa njora nĩrũgagũtengʼeria.
english: The praise of Moab is no more. In Heshbon they have devised evil against her: ‘Come! Let’s cut her off from being a nation.’ You also, Madmen, will be brought to silence. The sword will pursue you.

kikuyu: Mbarĩ imwe cia Akohathu ciaheirwo matũũra nĩ mũhĩrĩga wa Efiraimu matuĩke bũrũri wao.
english: Some of the families of the sons of Kohath had cities of their borders out of the tribe of Ephraim.

kikuyu: Ningĩ akĩndwara nja ĩrĩa ya nyumĩrĩra. Ngĩona hau nĩ haarĩ na tũnyũmba, na nĩ haarĩtwo mahiga makarigiicĩria nja ĩyo yothe; nĩ haarĩ na tũnyũmba mĩrongo ĩtatũ twakĩtwo tũtwaranĩte na hau haarĩtwo mahiga.
english: Then he brought me into the outer court. Behold, there were rooms and a pavement made for the court all around. Thirty rooms were on the pavement.

kikuyu: Ĩgaatuĩka ta gĩthũnũ gĩa kũrehe kĩĩruru gĩa kwĩgitia kuuma kũrĩ ũrugarĩ wa mũthenya, na handũ ha kũũrĩra na ha kwĩgitia kuuma kũrĩ kĩhuhũkanio kĩa rũhuho na kĩa mbura.
english: There will be a pavilion for a shade in the daytime from the heat, and for a refuge and for a shelter from storm and from rain.

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo ũũgĩ nĩũgatoonya ngoro yaku, naguo ũmenyo ũkenagie muoyo waku.
english: For wisdom will enter into your heart. Knowledge will be pleasant to your soul.

kikuyu: Inyuĩ mũtũũraga Maresha nĩngamũrehithĩria mũndũ wa kũmũũkĩrĩra, amũtoorie. Ũrĩa we ũrĩ Riiri wa Isiraeli nĩagooka Adulamu.
english: I will yet bring a conqueror to you, inhabitants of Mareshah. The glory of Israel will come to Adullam.

kikuyu: mũthamaki agĩtũmanĩra Shimei, akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Githĩ ndiakwĩhĩtithirie na rĩĩtwa rĩa Jehova na ngĩgũkaania, ngĩkwĩra atĩrĩ, ‘Mũthenya ũrĩa ũkoimagara ũthiĩ handũ hangĩ, ũmenye kũna no ũgaakua’? Nawe hĩndĩ ĩyo ũkĩnjookeria atĩrĩ, ‘Ũguo woiga nĩ wega. Niĩ nĩngwathĩka.’
english: The king sent and called for Shimei, and said to him, “Didn’t I adjure you by Yahweh and warn you, saying, ‘Know for certain that on the day you go out and walk anywhere else, you shall surely die’? You said to me, ‘The saying that I have heard is good.’

kikuyu: Namo maaĩ maya marehaga kĩrumi marotoonya mwĩrĩ waku, nayo nda yaku ĩimbe na kĩero gĩaku kĩhinyare.” “ ‘Nake mũtumia ũcio acooke oige atĩrĩ, “Ameni, nĩgũtuĩke ũguo.”
english: and this water that brings a curse will go into your bowels, and make your body swell, and your thigh fall away.” The woman shall say, “Amen, Amen.”

kikuyu: Ririkana ũrĩa mũtũũrĩre wakwa ũrĩ wa gũthira o narua. Kaĩ akĩrĩ ũndũ wa tũhũ watũmire ũmbe andũ othe!
english: Remember how short my time is, for what vanity you have created all the children of men!

kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, Ehudu nĩathondekete rũhiũ rwa njora rũũgĩ mĩena yeerĩ, ta rwa buti ĩmwe na nuthu† kũraiha rũrĩa oohereire njohero-inĩ yake mwena wa ũrĩo nguo-inĩ thĩinĩ.
english: Ehud made himself a sword which had two edges, a cubit† in length; and he wore it under his clothing on his right thigh.

kikuyu: Joabu akĩmenyithia Daudi mũigana wa andũ arĩa maangĩarũire mbaara ta ũũ: Isiraeli guothe kwarĩ na andũ 1,100,000 arĩa maangĩahotire kũhũthĩra rũhiũ rwa njora, thĩinĩ wao haarĩ na andũ 470,000 kuuma Juda.
english: Joab gave the sum of the census of the people to David. All those of Israel were one million one hundred thousand men who drew a sword; and in Judah were four hundred seventy thousand men who drew a sword.

kikuyu: na Benaia, na Bedeia, na Keluhi,
english: Benaiah, Bedeiah, Cheluhi,

kikuyu: na Ifita, na Ashina, na Nezibu,
english: Iphtah, Ashnah, Nezib,

kikuyu: Mwathani aranjĩĩra atĩrĩ: “Mwaka ũmwe ũtanathira, ũtarĩtwo ta ũrĩa mĩaka ya mũndũ wa kwandĩkwo wĩra ĩrĩkanagĩrwo-rĩ, riiri wothe wa Kedari nĩũgaathira.
english: For the Lord said to me, “Within a year, as a worker bound by contract would count it, all the glory of Kedar will fail,

kikuyu: Mũtikanarutĩre Jehova nyamũ ndumumu kana ndihie, kana ĩrathua, kana kĩndũ o gĩothe kĩrĩ na mbatata, kana kĩrĩ na ironda iratogota kana iroira. Mũtikanaigĩrĩre o na ĩmwe ya icio kĩgongona-inĩ ĩtuĩke ya iruta rĩa gũcinwo na mwaki harĩ Jehova.
english: You shall not offer what is blind, is injured, is maimed, has a wart, is festering, or has a running sore to Yahweh, nor make an offering by fire of them on the altar to Yahweh.

kikuyu: Ngĩcooka ngĩigua mũgambo warĩ ta wa kĩrĩndĩ kĩnene mũno, wahaanaga ta mũrurumo wa maaĩ megũtherera na ta mũrurumo mũnene wa ngwa, naguo ũkĩanĩrĩra, ũkiuga atĩrĩ: “Haleluya! Nĩgũkorwo Mwathani Ngai witũ, o we Mwene-Hinya-Wothe, nĩarathamaka.
english: I heard something like the voice of a great multitude, and like the voice of many waters, and like the voice of mighty thunders, saying, “Hallelujah! For the Lord our God, the Almighty, reigns!

kikuyu: Angĩgooka arĩ wiki, akaarekererio athiĩ arĩ o wiki; no angĩgooka arĩ na mũtumia, makaarekererio mathiĩ marĩ hamwe.
english: If he comes in by himself, he shall go out by himself. If he is married, then his wife shall go out with him.

kikuyu: Kuuma hĩndĩ ĩyo, Pilato akĩgeria kuohora Jesũ, no Ayahudi magĩthiĩ na mbere kwanĩrĩra atĩrĩ, “Ũngĩrekereria mũndũ ũyũ, wee ndũrĩ mũrata wa Kaisari. Mũndũ o wothe wĩtuaga mũthamaki nĩamenete Kaisari.”
english: At this, Pilate was seeking to release him, but the Jews cried out, saying, “If you release this man, you aren’t Caesar’s friend! Everyone who makes himself a king speaks against Caesar!”

kikuyu: No Jesũ agĩũka, akĩmahutia na akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Arahũkai, tigai gwĩtigĩra.”
english: Jesus came and touched them and said, “Get up, and don’t be afraid.”

kikuyu: Nake akĩmooria atĩrĩ, “Inyuĩ na inyuĩ-rĩ, mugaga atĩa? Mugaga niĩ nĩ niĩ ũ?” Petero akĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Wee nĩwe Kristũ ũrĩa wa Ngai.”
english: He said to them, “But who do you say that I am?” Peter answered, “The Christ of God.”

kikuyu: Ahabu akĩĩra Obadia atĩrĩ, “Thiũrũrũka bũrũri wothe ũthiĩ ithima-inĩ ciothe, na ituamba-inĩ. No gũkorwo tuona handũ harĩ nyeki ĩngĩtũũria mbarathi na nyũmbũ muoyo, nĩguo tũtikoorage nyamũ o na ĩmwe iitũ.”
english: Ahab said to Obadiah, “Go through the land, to all the springs of water, and to all the brooks. Perhaps we may find grass and save the horses and mules alive, that we not lose all the animals.”

kikuyu: makamwĩra atĩrĩ, “Nĩ wega, kiuge, ‘Shibolethu’.” Nake angĩoigire, “Sibolethu,” tondũ ndangĩahotire kũgweta kiugo kĩu wega, makamũnyiita na makamũũragĩra kũu mariũko-inĩ ma Rũũĩ rwa Jorodani. Aefiraimu 42,000 makĩũragwo ihinda-inĩ rĩu.
english: then they said to him, “Now say ‘Shibboleth;’ ” and he said “Sibboleth”; for he couldn’t manage to pronounce it correctly, then they seized him and killed him at the fords of the Jordan. At that time, forty-two thousand of Ephraim fell.

kikuyu: Na rĩrĩa Boazu aarĩkirie kũrĩa na kũnyua, na agĩkorwo arĩ mũkenu, agĩthiĩ agĩkoma mũthia-inĩ biũ wa hĩba ya cairi. Nake Ruthu agĩthiĩ acemete, akĩmũhumbũria magũrũ, agĩkoma ho.
english: When Boaz had eaten and drunk, and his heart was merry, he went to lie down at the end of the heap of grain. She came softly, uncovered his feet, and lay down.

kikuyu: Na angĩmĩona-rĩ, ngũmwĩra atĩrĩ na ma, akenagĩra ngʼondu ĩyo ĩmwe ĩrorĩte kũrĩ ũrĩa akenagĩra icio mĩrongo kenda na kenda itaroorĩte.
english: If he finds it, most certainly I tell you, he rejoices over it more than over the ninety-nine which have not gone astray.

kikuyu: Ũyũ nĩguo ũhoro wa Nuhu. Nuhu aarĩ mũndũ mũthingu, ũtaarĩ na ũcuuke kũrĩ andũ a hĩndĩ yake, na nĩatwaranaga na Ngai.
english: This is the history of the generations of Noah: Noah was a righteous man, blameless among the people of his time. Noah walked with God.

kikuyu: Ũũgĩ wake nĩ mũingĩ mũno, na ũhoti wake nĩ mũnene. Nũũ wanaregana nake akĩgaacĩra?
english: God is wise in heart, and mighty in strength. Who has hardened himself against him and prospered?

kikuyu: No rĩrĩa ndahĩngirwo ngĩgwa, o moonganire hamwe mangenerere; atharĩkĩri makĩũngana maatharĩkĩre itekũmenya. Manjaambirie mategũtigithĩria.
english: But in my adversity, they rejoiced, and gathered themselves together. The attackers gathered themselves together against me, and I didn’t know it. They tore at me, and didn’t cease.

kikuyu: waka-inĩ wa kenda wa wathani wa Zedekia mũthamaki wa Juda, mweri-inĩ wa ikũmi, Nebukadinezaru mũthamaki wa Babuloni nĩathiire gũtharĩkĩra itũũra rĩa Jerusalemu arĩ na mbũtũ ciake ciothe cia ita, akĩrĩrigiicĩria arĩtunyane.
english: n the ninth year of Zedekiah king of Judah, in the tenth month, Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon and all his army came against Jerusalem, and besieged it.

kikuyu: Nĩngũririkana ciĩko cia Jehova; ĩĩ nĩguo, nĩngũririkana ciama ciaku cia tene.
english: I will remember Yah’s deeds; for I will remember your wonders of old.

kikuyu: Ehudu aarĩkia gũthiĩ, ndungata cia mũthamaki igĩũka igĩkora mĩrango ya nyũmba ya igũrũ ĩrĩ mĩhinge, ikiuga atĩrĩ, “No nginya akorwo nĩ gwĩteithia areteithia kanyũmba-inĩ ka na thĩinĩ.”
english: After he had gone, his servants came and saw that the doors of the upper room were locked. They said, “Surely he is covering his feet‡ in the upper room.”

kikuyu: Moigaga atĩrĩ, ‘Githĩ ihinda rĩtigũkinya o narua rĩa gwaka nyũmba? Itũũra rĩĩrĩ inene nĩ nyũngũ ya kũruga, na ithuĩ nĩ ithuĩ nyama.’
english: who say, ‘The time is not near to build houses. This is the cauldron, and we are the meat.’

kikuyu: Na rĩrĩa ndungata ĩyo ya mũirĩtu yamuonire ho, o rĩngĩ ĩkĩĩra arĩa maarũngiĩ ho atĩrĩ, “Mũndũ ũyũ nĩ ũmwe wao.”
english: The maid saw him and began again to tell those who stood by, “This is one of them.”

kikuyu: Ndagũthaitha ĩtĩkĩra kĩheo kĩu ũreheirwo, tondũ Ngai nĩanjĩkĩte wega, na ndĩ na indo cia kũnjigana.” Na tondũ Jakubu nĩamũringĩrĩirie-rĩ, Esaũ agĩciĩtĩkĩra.
english: Please take the gift that I brought to you, because God has dealt graciously with me, and because I have enough.” He urged him, and he took it.

kikuyu: Wee ũrehanyũrũra nĩ ũndũ wa ũrĩa ũrakarĩte-rĩ, thĩ no ĩthaamwo nĩ ũndũ waku? Kana no nginya ndwaro cia mahiga cieherio harĩa itũire?
english: You who tear yourself in your anger, will the earth be forsaken for you? Or will the rock be removed out of its place?

kikuyu: Ngoro yakwa yahĩĩahĩaga thĩinĩ wakwa, na o rĩrĩa ndecũũranagia, mwaki ũgĩakana; hĩndĩ ĩyo ngĩaria na rũrĩmĩ rwakwa, ngiuga atĩrĩ:
english: My heart was hot within me. While I meditated, the fire burned. I spoke with my tongue:

kikuyu: Thuutha ũcio andũ a Isiraeli nĩmagacooka na marongorie Jehova Ngai wao na Daudi mũthamaki wao. Nĩmagooka makĩinainaga kũrĩ Jehova na kũrĩ irathimo ciake matukũ-inĩ ma mũthia.
english: Afterward the children of Israel shall return and seek Yahweh their God, and David their king, and shall come with trembling to Yahweh and to his blessings in the last days.

kikuyu: Ningĩ agĩthima ũraihu wa nyũmba ĩyo yangʼetheire nja ya mwena wa na thuutha wa hekarũ, o hamwe na ithaku ciayo cia o mwena; naguo warĩ wa mĩkono igana. Mwena wa nja ya handũ-harĩa-haamũre, na mwena wa na thĩinĩ wa handũ-harĩa-haamũre, na gĩthaku kĩrĩa kĩangʼetheire nja ĩyo,
english: He measured the length of the building before the separate place which was at its back, and its galleries on the one side and on the other side, one hundred cubits from the inner temple, and the porches of the court,

kikuyu: na Kedeshi, na Hazoru, na Ithinani,
english: Kedesh, Hazor, Ithnan,

kikuyu: “Andũ othe arĩa matũũraga bũrũri ũrĩa ũrĩ irĩma kuuma Lebanoni nginya Misirefothu-Maimu, na nĩo Asidoni othe, nĩ niĩ ngaamaingata mehere mbere ya andũ a Isiraeli. Nawe ũtigĩrĩre nĩwagaĩra Isiraeli bũrũri ũcio ũtuĩke igai rĩao o ta ũrĩa ngwathĩte,
english: all the inhabitants of the hill country from Lebanon to Misrephoth Maim, even all the Sidonians. I will drive them out from before the children of Israel. Just allocate it to Israel for an inheritance, as I have commanded you.

kikuyu: Nĩngarĩrehithĩria mũthiro, na ndũme thakame ĩtherere njĩra-inĩ ciarĩo. Arĩa oorage nĩmakaagũa thĩinĩ warĩo, naruo rũhiũ rwa njora rũrĩũkĩrĩre mĩena yothe. Hĩndĩ ĩyo nĩmakamenya atĩ niĩ nĩ niĩ Jehova.
english: For I will send pestilence into her, and blood into her streets. The wounded will fall within her, with the sword on her on every side. Then they will know that I am Yahweh.

kikuyu: Tondũ ũcio tũikaraga hĩndĩ ciothe tũrĩ na ũũmĩrĩru, tũkĩmenyaga atĩ rĩrĩa rĩothe tũikaraga thĩinĩ wa mwĩrĩ taarĩ guo mũciĩ witũ, tũikaraga tũraihanĩrĩirie na Mwathani.
english: Therefore we are always confident and know that while we are at home in the body, we are absent from the Lord;

kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, nĩ kĩĩ ũrĩ nakĩo gĩa kũrĩa? He mĩgate ĩtano, kana o kĩrĩa gĩothe ũngĩhota kuona.”
english: Now therefore what is under your hand? Please give me five loaves of bread in my hand, or whatever is available.”

kikuyu: Tiga kũũhitha ũthiũ waku rĩrĩa ndĩ mĩnyamaro-inĩ. Inamĩrĩria gũtũ gwaku ũnjigue; ndagũkaĩra, njĩtĩka o narua.
english: Don’t hide your face from me in the day of my distress. Turn your ear to me. Answer me quickly in the day when I call.

kikuyu: Jehova arĩ igũrũ rĩa mũiyũro wa maaĩ aikarĩire gĩtĩ kĩa ũnene; Jehova egũtũũra aikarĩire gĩtĩ kĩa ũnene arĩ Mũthamaki nginya tene.
english: Yahweh sat enthroned at the Flood. Yes, Yahweh sits as King forever.

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo rũrĩrĩ kana ũthamaki ũrĩa ũtagagũtungatĩra nĩũkaniinwo; ũkaanangwo biũ.
english: For that nation and kingdom that will not serve you shall perish; yes, those nations shall be utterly wasted.

kikuyu: Shelomithu na andũ a nyũmba yao nĩo maarũgamagĩrĩra igĩĩna ciothe cia indo iria ciarĩ nyamũre nĩ Mũthamaki Daudi, na indo iria ciarĩ nyamũre nĩ atongoria a nyũmba arĩa maarĩ anene a ikundi cia ngiri ngiri na ikundi cia igana igana, na ikaamũrwo nĩ anene arĩa angĩ a mbũtũ cia ita.
english: This Shelomoth and his brothers were over all the treasuries of the dedicated things, which David the king, and the heads of the fathers’ households, the captains over thousands and hundreds, and the captains of the army, had dedicated.

kikuyu: Taragai ciumia mũgwanja kuuma mwambĩrĩria kũgetha ngano ĩrĩa ĩtarĩ ngethe.
english: You shall count for yourselves seven weeks. From the time you begin to put the sickle to the standing grain you shall begin to count seven weeks.

kikuyu: Nĩ niĩ njĩĩraga maaĩ ma kũrĩa kũriku atĩrĩ, ‘Hũai, na nĩngahũithia tũrũũĩ twanyu.’
english: who says to the deep, ‘Be dry,’ and ‘I will dry up your rivers,’

kikuyu: hamwe na iheo iria njega mũno cia thĩ, na indo iria ciyũrĩte kuo, na ũtugi ũrĩa waikarĩte kĩhinga-inĩ kĩrĩa kĩahĩaga. Maũndũ maya mothe maroikara mũtwe-inĩ wa Jusufu, maroikara ũthiũ-inĩ wa mũriũ wa mũthamaki arĩ harĩ ariũ a ithe.
english: for the precious things of the earth and its fullness, the good will of him who lived in the bush.‡ Let this come on the head of Joseph, on the crown of the head of him who was separated from his brothers.

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo ngĩkĩona atĩ ũũgĩ nĩ mwega gũkĩra ũkĩĩgu, o ta ũrĩa ũtheri ũrĩ mwega gũkĩra nduma.
english: Then I saw that wisdom excels folly, as far as light excels darkness.

kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, wendo wakwa ndũrĩ hĩndĩ ũkeeherio kũrĩ we, ta ũrĩa
english: but my loving kindness will not depart from him, as I took it from Saul, whom I put away before you.

kikuyu: Nake mũraika ũcio akĩmũcookeria, akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Roho Mũtheru nĩagooka igũrũ rĩaku naguo hinya wa Ũrĩa-ũrĩ-Igũrũ-Mũno nĩũgakũhumbĩra, nĩ ũndũ ũcio mwana ũrĩa ũgaaciarwo nĩ mũtheru na ageetagwo Mũrũ wa Ngai.
english: The angel answered her, “The Holy Spirit will come on you, and the power of the Most High will overshadow you. Therefore also the holy one who is born from you will be called the Son of God.

kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, kũngĩgaatuĩka atĩ thitango ĩyo nĩ ya ma, na kwage kĩmenyithia gĩa kuonania atĩ mũirĩtu ũcio aarĩ gathirange,
english: But if this thing is true, that the tokens of virginity were not found in the young lady,

kikuyu: Jehova Mwene-Hinya-Wothe, Ngai wa Isiraeli, ekuuga atĩrĩ, “Ti-itherũ o ta ũrĩa niĩ ndũũraga muoyo-rĩ, Moabi gũgaatuĩka o ta Sodomu, naguo bũrũri wa Aamoni ũtuĩke ta Gomora, kũndũ kwa riya, na marima ma cumbĩ, kũndũ gũtakũraga kĩndũ kuuma tene. Matigari ma andũ akwa nĩmakaamataha; nao andũ a rũrĩrĩ rwakwa arĩa makaahonoka nĩmakagaya bũrũri wao.”
english: Therefore, as I live, says Yahweh of Armies, the God of Israel, surely Moab will be as Sodom, and the children of Ammon as Gomorrah, a possession of nettles and salt pits, and a perpetual desolation. The remnant of my people will plunder them, and the survivors of my nation will inherit them.

kikuyu: Riũa nĩrĩkagarũrwo rĩtuĩke nduma, naguo mweri ũtuĩke ta thakame, mbere ya mũthenya ũcio mũnene wa Mwathani ũrĩa ũrĩ riiri gũkinya.
english: The sun will be turned into darkness, and the moon into blood, before the great and glorious day of the Lord comes.

kikuyu: magũrũ maguo maarĩ ma kĩgera, namo makinya maarĩ ma kĩgera gĩtukanĩte na rĩũmba rĩcine.
english: its legs of iron, its feet part of iron and part of clay.

kikuyu: Ningĩ Jesũ akĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Simoni mũrũ wa Jona, ti-itherũ nĩũnyendete?” Nake akĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Ĩĩ Mwathani, wee nĩũũĩ atĩ nĩngwendete.” Nake Jesũ akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Rĩithagia ngʼondu ciakwa.”
english: He said to him again a second time, “Simon, son of Jonah, do you love me?” He said to him, “Yes, Lord; you know that I have affection for you.” He said to him, “Tend my sheep.”

kikuyu: Agĩcooka akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Mũrũ wakwa ndehere nyama imwe cia ũguĩmi waku ndĩe nĩgeetha ngũrathime na kĩrathimo gĩakwa.” Jakubu akĩmũrehera nake akĩrĩa, na akĩmũrehera ndibei na akĩnyua.
english: He said, “Bring it near to me, and I will eat of my son’s venison, that my soul may bless you.” He brought it near to him, and he ate. He brought him wine, and he drank.

kikuyu: Ningĩ atongoria a Isiraeli, atongoria a nyũmba ciao arĩa maarĩ atongoria a mĩhĩrĩga arĩa maarũgamĩrĩire andũ arĩa maatarĩtwo, makĩrehe maruta.
english: the princes of Israel, the heads of their fathers’ houses, gave offerings. These were the princes of the tribes. These are they who were over those who were counted;

kikuyu: “Kaĩ mũrĩ na haaro-ĩ, inyuĩ arutani a watho na Afarisai, hinga ici! Mũrutaga gĩcunjĩ gĩa ikũmi kĩa nyeni; na nĩcio terere, na bitharĩ, na thuu. No nĩmũtiganĩirie maũndũ marĩa ma bata makĩria marĩa makoniĩ watho na nĩmo kĩhooto, na kũiguanĩra tha, na wĩhokeku. Maya-rĩ, nĩmo mwagĩrĩirwo nĩ gwĩka mũtegũtiganĩria macio mangĩ.
english: “Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! For you tithe mint, dill, and cumin,† and have left undone the weightier matters of the law: justice, mercy, and faith. But you ought to have done these, and not to have left the other undone.

kikuyu: Rũthanju rwa ũthamaki rũtikoima harĩ Juda, o na kana rũthanju rwa ũnene rũthenge gatagatĩ-inĩ ka magũrũ make, o nginya agooka ũrĩa mwene ruo, o we ũrĩa ũgaathĩkagĩrwo nĩ thĩ.
