english: The scepter will not depart from Judah, nor the ruler’s staff from between his feet, until he comes to whom it belongs. The obedience of the peoples will be to him.

kikuyu: Tega matũ maku Wee Jehova, ũigue; hingũra maitho maku Wee Jehova, wone; ũigue ciugo cia Senakeribu iria atũmĩte cia kũruma Ngai ũrĩa ũrĩ muoyo.
english: Incline your ear, Yahweh, and hear. Open your eyes, Yahweh, and see. Hear the words of Sennacherib, which he has sent to defy the living God.

kikuyu: Taranda† ya thahabu therie ĩhũthĩrwo gũthondeka mũtĩ wa kũigĩrĩrwo matawa o na indo icio ciothe.
english: It shall be made of a talent§ of pure gold, with all these accessories.

kikuyu: na Azamavethu ũrĩa Mũbahurimu, na Eliahaba ũrĩa Mũshaaliboni,
english: Azmaveth the Baharumite, Eliahba the Shaalbonite,

kikuyu: Wee Ngai-rĩ, tũma tũgaacĩre rĩngĩ; tũma ũthiũ waku ũtwarĩre, nĩguo tũhonoke.
english: Turn us again, God. Cause your face to shine, and we will be saved.

kikuyu: Na angĩkorwo no mwende kwamũkĩra ũhoro ũcio-rĩ, ũcio nĩwe Elija ũrĩa werĩtwo nĩagooka.
english: If you are willing to receive it, this is Elijah, who is to come.

kikuyu: Nyũmba ĩngĩamũkana na andũ ayo mokanĩrĩre, nyũmba ĩyo ndĩngĩtũũra.
english: If a house is divided against itself, that house cannot stand.

kikuyu: Ngũtũũra ngũgoocaga nginya tene nĩ ũndũ wa ũrĩa wĩkĩte; ndĩrĩĩhokaga rĩĩtwa rĩaku, nĩgũkorwo rĩĩtwa rĩaku nĩ rĩega. Ndĩrĩkũgoocaga ndĩ mbere ya andũ aku arĩa aamũre.
english: I will give you thanks forever, because you have done it. I will hope in your name, for it is good,

kikuyu: Mũtikaneke maũndũ marĩa andũ a Misiri, kũrĩa mwatũũraga, meekaga, na mũtikaneke maũndũ marĩa mekagwo bũrũri wa Kaanani, kũrĩa ndĩramũtwara. Mũtikarũmĩrĩre mĩthiĩre yao.
english: You shall not do as they do in the land of Egypt, where you lived. You shall not do as they do in the land of Canaan, where I am bringing you. You shall not follow their statutes.

kikuyu: Niĩ ndĩ na ũmenyo mũingĩ gũkĩra arutani akwa othe, nĩgũkorwo ndaranagia ũhoro wa mawatho maku.
english: I have more understanding than all my teachers, for your testimonies are my meditation.

kikuyu: Nao magĩtiganĩria hekarũ ya Jehova, Ngai wa maithe mao, na makĩhooya itugĩ cia Ashera na mĩhianano. Tondũ wa wĩhia wao-rĩ, marakara ma Ngai magĩkinyĩra Juda na Jerusalemu.
english: They abandoned the house of Yahweh, the God of their fathers, and served the Asherah poles and the idols, so wrath came on Judah and Jerusalem for this their guiltiness.

kikuyu: na agĩtũhonokia kuuma kũrĩ thũ ciitũ, Wendo wake nĩ wa tene na tene.
english: and has delivered us from our adversaries, for his loving kindness endures forever;

kikuyu: Nake akĩĩra Kĩama atĩrĩ, “Andũ aya a Isiraeli, cũraniai wega ũrĩa mũratanya gwĩka andũ aya.
english: He said to them, “You men of Israel, be careful concerning these men, what you are about to do.

kikuyu: a rĩrĩ, Samusoni agĩikũrũka agĩthiĩ Timina,* na kũu akĩona mũirĩtu Mũfilisti.
english: amson went down to Timnah, and saw a woman in Timnah of the daughters of the Philistines.

kikuyu: Maahũthagĩra kĩũria gĩkĩ kĩrĩ ta mũtego, nĩgeetha magĩe na ũndũ wa kũmũthitangĩra. No Jesũ akĩinamĩrĩra, na akĩambĩrĩria kwandĩka thĩ na kĩara gĩake.
english: When he had said this, he spat on the ground, made mud with the saliva, anointed the blind man’s eyes with the mud,

kikuyu: Geithiai ariũ na aarĩ a Ithe witũ kũu Laodikia, na mũgeithie Numifa na andũ a kanitha ũrĩa ũrĩ gwake mũciĩ.
english: Greet the brothers who are in Laodicea, with Nymphas and the assembly that is in his house.

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo maitho ma Jehova morũũraga thĩ yothe gwĩkĩra hinya arĩa maheanĩte ngoro ciao biũ kũrĩ we. Nĩwĩkĩte ũndũ wa ũrimũ, na kuuma rĩu nĩũgũcooka kũgĩa na mbaara.”
english: For Yahweh’s eyes run back and forth throughout the whole earth, to show himself strong in the behalf of them whose heart is perfect toward him. You have done foolishly in this; for from now on you will have wars.”

kikuyu: na Shalumu nĩwe warĩ ithe wa Hilikia, na Hilikia nĩwe warĩ ithe wa Azaria,
english: Shallum became the father of Hilkiah. Hilkiah became the father of Azariah.

kikuyu: O na gũtuĩka Jehova nĩatũmire anabii kũrĩ andũ nĩguo mamacookie kũrĩ we-rĩ, na o na aakorwo nĩmarutire ũira wa kũmookĩrĩra-rĩ, matiigana kũmaigua.
english: Yet he sent prophets to them to bring them again to Yahweh, and they testified against them; but they would not listen.

kikuyu: Ithuĩ tũtikaamũkĩra igai hamwe nao mũrĩmo ũrĩa ũngĩ wa Jorodani, nĩ ũndũ igai riitũ tũrĩ narĩo mwena wa irathĩro wa Rũũĩ rwa Jorodani.”
english: For we will not inherit with them on the other side of the Jordan and beyond, because our inheritance has come to us on this side of the Jordan eastward.”

kikuyu: gũkĩra ũthiĩ Jehanamu kũrĩa kũrĩ mwaki ũtahoraga ũrĩ na moko meerĩ.
english: ‘where their worm doesn’t die, and the fire is not quenched.’ *‡

kikuyu: na ndegwa igĩrĩ, na ndũrũme ithano, na thenge ithano na tũtũrũme tũtano twa mwaka ũmwe, irutwo irĩ igongona rĩa ũiguano. Ici nĩcio indo iria ciarutirwo nĩ Elizuru mũrũ wa Shedeuru.
english: and for the sacrifice of peace offerings, two head of cattle, five rams, five male goats, and five male lambs a year old. This was the offering of Elizur the son of Shedeur.

kikuyu: Mwaki ũrĩa ũrĩ kĩgongona-inĩ no nginya ũikare o ũgĩakanaga; ndũkanarekwo ũhore. Hĩndĩ ciothe o rũciinĩ, mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai nĩarĩongagĩrĩra ngũ, na nĩarĩtandayagia iruta rĩu rĩa njino igũrũ rĩa mwaki ũcio, na acinĩre maguta ma maruta ma ũiguano ho.
english: The fire on the altar shall be kept burning on it, it shall not go out; and the priest shall burn wood on it every morning. He shall lay the burnt offering in order upon it, and shall burn on it the fat of the peace offerings.

kikuyu: Ndingĩĩtigĩra andũ makũmi ma ngiri, acio mehaarĩirie kũnjũkĩrĩra na mĩena yothe.
english: I will not be afraid of tens of thousands of people who have set themselves against me on every side.

kikuyu: Ha ũhoro wa maũndũ marĩa mangĩ ma wathani wa Amazia, githĩ matiandĩkĩtwo ibuku-inĩ rĩa mahinda ma athamaki a Juda?
english: Now the rest of the acts of Amaziah, aren’t they written in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Judah?

kikuyu: Mĩaka ĩngĩkorwo ĩtigarĩte mĩingĩ, mũkongerera thogora, na mĩaka ĩngĩkorwo ĩtigarĩte mĩnini, mũkanyiihia thogora, nĩ ũndũ kĩrĩa arakwenderia nĩ mũigana wa magetha.
english: According to the length of the years you shall increase its price, and according to the shortness of the years you shall diminish its price; for he is selling the number of the crops to you.

kikuyu: nĩo Sihoni mũthamaki wa Aamori, Wendo wake nĩ wa tene na tene.
english: Sihon king of the Amorites, for his loving kindness endures forever;

kikuyu: Mĩaka ĩyo yathira Jakubu akĩĩra Labani atĩrĩ, “Rĩu kĩĩhe mũtumia wakwa ndĩmũhikie, tondũ nĩndĩrarĩkirie ihinda rĩakwa rĩa gũtungata.”
english: Jacob said to Laban, “Give me my wife, for my days are fulfilled, that I may go in to her.”

kikuyu: Akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Mwĩciiragiei wega ũrĩa mũgwĩka, tondũ rĩrĩ, mũtirĩtuithanagia ciira nĩ ũndũ wa mũndũ, no makĩria nĩ ũndũ wa Jehova, ũrĩa ũrĩkoragwo na inyuĩ rĩrĩa rĩothe mũrĩtuaga ciira.
english: and said to the judges, “Consider what you do, for you don’t judge for man, but for Yahweh; and he is with you in the judgment.

kikuyu: Nĩ kũrĩ ngai yanageria kwĩyoera rũrĩrĩ ĩrũrutĩte thĩinĩ wa rũrĩa rũngĩ, na ũndũ wa magerio, na kũringa ciama, na morirũ, na kũhũũrana mbaara, na gwĩka maũndũ na njara ĩrĩ na hinya na guoko gũtambũrũkĩtio; o na kana ciĩko ingĩ nene na cia gwĩtigĩrwo ta maũndũ marĩa mothe Jehova Ngai wanyu aamwĩkĩire kũu bũrũri wa Misiri mũkĩĩonagĩra na maitho?
english: Or has God tried to go and take a nation for himself from among another nation, by trials, by signs, by wonders, by war, by a mighty hand, by an outstretched arm, and by great terrors, according to all that Yahweh your God did for you in

kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, andũ-a-nja nĩ makaahonoka nĩ ũndũ wa gũciara ciana, mangĩtũũra metĩkĩtie marĩ na wendani na andũ atheru, o na marĩ na ũũgĩ wa ngoro.
english: but she will be saved through her childbearing, if they continue in faith, love, and holiness with sobriety.

kikuyu: Riitho rĩaku rĩa ũrĩo rĩngĩtũma wĩhie-rĩ, rĩkũũre, ũrĩte. Nĩ kaba kĩĩga kĩmwe kĩa mwĩrĩ waku kĩũre gũkĩra mwĩrĩ waku wothe ũikio Jehanamu.
english: If your right eye causes you to stumble, pluck it out and throw it away from you. For it is more profitable for you that one of your members should perish than for your whole body to be cast into Gehenna.§

kikuyu: Nawe ndũkahonoka atĩ wĩeherie moko-inĩ make, tondũ ti-itherũ nĩũkanyiitwo na ũneanwo kũrĩ we. Nĩũkeyonera mũthamaki wa Babuloni na maitho maku, na nĩmũkaranĩria nake ũthiũ kwa ũthiũ. Nawe nĩũgatwarwo Babuloni.
english: You won’t escape out of his hand, but will surely be taken and delivered into his hand. Your eyes will see the eyes of the

kikuyu: “Ningĩ ũthiĩ na mbere na kwanĩrĩra uuge atĩrĩ: Jehova Mwene-Hinya-Wothe ekuuga ũũ, ‘Matũũra makwa nĩmakaiyũrĩrĩra ũgaacĩru rĩngĩ, nake Jehova nĩakahooreria Zayuni rĩngĩ na ethuurĩre Jerusalemu.’ ” Hĩa Inya na Mabundi Mana
english: “Proclaim further, saying, ‘Yahweh of Armies says: “My cities will again overflow with prosperity, and Yahweh will again comfort Zion, and will again choose Jerusalem.” ’ ”

kikuyu: No arĩa othe maamũũĩ, o hamwe na atumia arĩa maamũrũmĩrĩire kuuma Galili, makĩrũgama o haraaya, meroreire maũndũ macio. Gũthikwo kwa Jesũ
english: All his acquaintances and the women who followed with him from Galilee stood at a distance, watching these things.

kikuyu: Jehova nĩ mũigua tha na nĩ mũtugi, ndahiũhaga kũrakara, na nĩaiyũirwo nĩ wendo mũingĩ.
english: Yahweh is merciful and gracious, slow to anger, and abundant in loving kindness.

kikuyu: Andũ arĩa maikaraga megangarĩte manyũrũragia mũtino, makona ta ũndũ ũcio ũkoraga o andũ arĩa magũrũ mao matenderũkaga.
english: In the thought of him who is at ease there is contempt for misfortune. It is ready for them whose foot slips.

kikuyu: na Ateri, na Hezekia, na Azuri,
english: Ater, Hezekiah, Azzur,

kikuyu: Tuĩka rwaro rwakwa rwa ihiga rwa kũũrĩra, harĩa ingĩthiĩ hĩndĩ ciothe; athana honokio, nĩgũkorwo nĩwe rwaro rwakwa rwa ihiga na kĩirigo gĩakwa kĩa hinya.
english: Be to me a rock of refuge to which I may always go. Give the command to save me, for you are my rock and my fortress.

kikuyu: Samũeli akĩĩra ũrĩa warugaga atĩrĩ, “Rehe gĩcunjĩ kĩa nyama kĩrĩa ngũnengerire, kĩrĩa ngwĩrire ũige handũ mwanya.”
english: Samuel said to the cook, “Bring the portion which I gave you, of which I said to you, ‘Set it aside.’ ”

kikuyu: Andũ nĩmethondekagĩra ngai ciao ene? Ĩĩ, no rĩrĩ, icio ti ngai!”
english: Should a man make to himself gods which yet are no gods?”

kikuyu: Rĩrĩa Jusufu ookĩrire, agĩĩka o ta ũrĩa mũraika wa Mwathani aamwathĩte, nake akĩinũkia Mariamu gwake mũciĩ, agĩtuĩka mũtumia wake.
english: Joseph arose from his sleep, and did as the angel of the Lord commanded him, and took his wife to himself;

kikuyu: dũmĩrĩri nĩyakinyĩrĩire Jeremia ĩkoniĩ andũ othe a Juda, mwaka-inĩ wa kana wa gũthamaka kwa Jehoiakimu mũrũ wa Josia mũthamaki wa Juda, naguo nĩguo warĩ mwaka wa mbere wa gũthamaka kwa Nebukadinezaru mũthamaki wa Babuloni.
english: he word that came to Jeremiah concerning all the people of Judah, in the fourth year of Jehoiakim the son of Josiah, king of Judah (this was the first year of Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon),

kikuyu: Inyuĩ mũhĩahĩaga nĩ thuti cianyu mũrĩ kũu mĩgandi-inĩ, na mũrĩ rungu rwa mũtĩ o wothe mũruru; mũrutaga ciana cianyu magongona kũu ituamba-inĩ, na mĩatũka-inĩ ya ndwaro cia mahiga.
english: you who inflame yourselves among the oaks, under every green tree; who kill the children in the valleys, under the clefts of the rocks?

kikuyu: Ndũkoorie atĩrĩ, “Nĩ kĩĩ gĩatũmaga matukũ ma tene makorwo marĩ mega gũkĩra maya?” Nĩgũkorwo ti ũũgĩ kũũria ũguo.
english: Don’t say, “Why were the former days better than these?” For you do not ask wisely about this.

kikuyu: Niĩ ndiokire gwĩta andũ arĩa athingu, no nĩ arĩa ehia, nĩguo merire.” Jesũ Kũũrio Kĩũria Igũrũ rĩa Kwĩhinga Kũrĩa
english: I have not come to call the righteous, but sinners, to repentance.”

kikuyu: Ndeto cia mũndũ wa mũhuhu nĩ ta twenyũ twega twa irio; tũmerũkaga tũgathiĩ mwĩrĩ wa mũndũ o thĩinĩ biũ.
english: The words of a whisperer are as dainty morsels, they go down into the innermost parts.

kikuyu: a rĩrĩ, Nahashu ũrĩa Mũamoni nĩambatire agĩthiĩ akĩrigiicĩria Jabeshi-Gileadi. Nao andũ othe a Jabeshi makĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Gĩa na kĩrĩĩko kĩa ũiguano, na ithuĩ nĩ tũrĩgũtungatagĩra.”
english: hen Nahash the Ammonite came up and encamped against Jabesh Gilead; and all the men of Jabesh said to Nahash, “Make a covenant with us, and we will serve you.”

kikuyu: na Ira ũrĩa Mũithiri, na Garebi ũrĩa Mũithiri,
english: Ira the Ithrite, Gareb the Ithrite,

kikuyu: “Kuuma rũciaro-inĩ rwa mũndũ ũyũ, Ngai nĩareheire andũ a Isiraeli Mũhonokia, na nĩwe Jesũ, o ta ũrĩa eeranĩire.
english: From this man’s offspring, God has brought salvation‡ to Israel according to his promise,

kikuyu: Ũtukũ-rĩ, Aburamu akĩgayania andũ ake mamatharĩkĩre, nake akĩmahoota na akĩmaingatithia nginya Hoba, itũũra rĩrĩ gathigathini wa Dameski.
english: He divided himself against them by night, he and his servants, and struck them, and pursued them to Hobah, which is on the left hand of Damascus.

kikuyu: Andũatanoanyumakaahũũraandũigana, nao andũ anyu igana makaahũũra andũ ngiri ikũmi, nacio thũ nĩ mũgaacihoota mũciũrage na rũhiũ rwa njora.
english: Five of you shall chase a hundred, and a hundred of you shall chase ten thousand; and your enemies shall fall before you by the sword.

kikuyu: Rekagĩrĩria Jehova njĩra yaku; mwĩhokage o we, nake nĩarĩĩkaga maũndũ maya:
english: Commit your way to Yahweh. Trust also in him, and he will do this:

kikuyu: Tondũ korwo Joshua nĩamaheete ũhurũko-rĩ, Ngai ndangĩacookire kwaria ũhoro wa mũthenya ũngĩ thuutha ũcio.
english: For if Joshua had given them rest, he would not have spoken afterward of another day.

kikuyu: Mũnene wa ikũmi na ũmwe, wa mweri wa ikũmi na ũmwe, aarĩ Benaia ũrĩa Mũpirathoni wa rũciaro rwa Aefiraimu. Gĩkundi gĩake kĩarĩ na andũ 24,000.
english: The eleventh captain for the eleventh month was Benaiah the Pirathonite, of the children of Ephraim. In his division were twenty-four thousand.

kikuyu: makiumĩrĩra Jesũ marĩ na riiri mũnene makĩaria nake. Maaragia ũhoro wa gũkua gwake, kũrĩa aakirie kũhingia akinya Jerusalemu.
english: who appeared in glory and spoke of his departure,† which he was about to accomplish at Jerusalem.

kikuyu: hĩndĩ ĩyo ũcio Mwene-Hinya-Wothe agaatuĩka thahabu yaku, na atuĩke betha ĩrĩa njega mũno harĩ we.
english: The Almighty will be your treasure, and precious silver to you.

kikuyu: Matukũ macio maathira ũcooke ũtũroranie na aanake acio angĩ marĩĩaga irio icio cia mũthamaki, na thuutha ũcio ũcooke gwĩka ndungata ciaku o ta ũrĩa ũkuona kwagĩrĩire.”
english: Then let our faces be examined before you, and the face of the youths who eat of the king’s delicacies; and as you see, deal with your servants.”

kikuyu: “Nĩniinĩte ndũrĩrĩ; ciĩgitĩro ciao cia hinya nĩ momore. Nacio barabara ciao ndũmĩte itiganĩrio, gũtirĩ mũndũ ũracigerera. Matũũra mao manene nĩmanange; gũtirĩ mũndũ o na ũmwe ũtigarĩte, gũtirĩ o na ũmwe.
english: I have cut off nations. Their battlements are desolate. I have made their streets waste, so that no one passes by. Their cities are destroyed, so that there is no man, so that there is no inhabitant.

kikuyu: “Ũyũ nĩguo ũgaatuĩka mũhaka wa bũrũri ũcio: Mũhaka wa mwena wa gathigathini ũkoima Iria Rĩrĩa Inene ũgereire njĩra ya Hethiloni, ũhĩtũkĩre Lebo-Hamathu, nginya Zedadi,
english: “This shall be the border of the land: “On the north side, from the great sea, by the way of Hethlon, to the entrance of Zedad;

kikuyu: o na ningĩ matukũ-inĩ ma wathani wa Jehoiakimu mũrũ wa Josia, mũthamaki wa Juda, o nginya mweri wa gatano, mwaka-inĩ wa ikũmi na ũmwe wa wathani wa Zedekia mũrũ wa Josia, mũthamaki wa Juda, rĩrĩa andũ a Jerusalemu maatahirwo. Gwĩtwo kwa Jeremia
english: It came also in the days of Jehoiakim the son of Josiah, king of Judah, to the end of the eleventh year of Zedekiah, the son of Josiah, king of Judah, to the carrying away of Jerusalem captive in the fifth month.

kikuyu: No ithe akĩrega, akiuga atĩrĩ, “Nĩnjũũĩ ũguo mũrũ wakwa, nĩnjũũĩ. O nake nĩagatuĩka rũrĩrĩ, na nĩakaneneha. No rĩrĩ, mũrũ wa nyina ũrĩa mũnini nĩakaneneha kũmũkĩra, nacio njiaro ciake ituĩke gĩkundi kĩa ndũrĩrĩ.”
english: His father refused, and said, “I know, my son, I know. He also will become a people, and he also will be great. However, his younger brother will be greater than he, and his offspring will become a multitude of nations.”

kikuyu: Ningĩ akĩndehe itoonyero-inĩ rĩa nja ĩyo. Ngĩrora ngĩona irima rũthingo-inĩ.
english: He brought me to the door of the court; and when I looked, behold, a hole in the wall.

kikuyu: a rĩrĩ, mwaka-inĩ wa keerĩ wa ũthamaki wake-rĩ, Nebukadinezaru nĩarootire irooto; agĩtangĩka meciiria na akĩũrwo nĩ toro.
english: n the second year of the reign of Nebuchadnezzar, Nebuchadnezzar  The word translated “Lord” is “Adonai.”  The Hebrew word rendered “God” is “אֱֹלהִ ֑ים” (Elohim).  or, seed

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo athuuri acio a itũũra rĩake nĩmageta mũndũ ũcio mamwarĩrie. Mũndũ ũcio angĩgwatĩria kuuga atĩrĩ, “Ndirenda kũmũhikia,”
english: Then the elders of his city shall call him, and speak to him. If he stands and says, “I don’t want to take her,”

kikuyu: Akĩeheria ngai cia kũngĩ, o na akĩeheria mũhianano ũrĩa warĩ thĩinĩ wa hekarũ ya Jehova, o na igongona ciothe iria aakĩte karĩma-inĩ ka hekarũ na kũu Jerusalemu, na agĩcite na kũu nja ya itũũra inene.
english: He took away the foreign gods and the idol out of Yahweh’s house, and all the altars that he had built in the mountain of Yahweh’s house and in Jerusalem, and cast them out of the city.

kikuyu: Jehova ekuuga atĩrĩ: “Nĩ ũndũ wa mehia matatũ ma Juda, o na mana-rĩ, niĩ ndikahũndũra mangʼũrĩ makwa. Tondũ nĩmaregete watho wa Jehova, na makaaga kũrũmia irĩra ciake cia watho wa kũrũmĩrĩrwo, tondũ nĩmahĩtithĩtio nĩ ngai cia maheeni, ngai iria maithe mao ma tene maarũmagĩrĩra.
english: Yahweh says: “For three transgressions of Judah, yes, for four, I will not turn away its punishment, because they have rejected Yahweh’s law, and have not kept his statutes, and their lies have led them astray, after which their fathers walked;

kikuyu: “Makaarĩĩaga no matikahũũna, makaahũũraga ũmaraya no matikaingĩha, tondũ nĩmatiganĩirie Jehova nĩguo meheane
english: They will eat, and not have enough. They will play the prostitute, and will not increase; because they have abandoned listening to Yahweh.

kikuyu: Mĩaka ĩĩrĩ yathira, hĩndĩ ĩrĩa amuri a ngʼondu guoya a Abisalomu maarĩ kũu Baali-Hazoru hakuhĩ na mũhaka wa Efiraimu-rĩ, agĩĩta ariũ a mũthamaki othe mathiĩ kuo.
english: After two full years, Absalom had sheep shearers in Baal Hazor, which is beside Ephraim; and Absalom invited all the king’s sons.

kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, Ayahudi amwe arĩa maathiiaga makĩingataga ngoma thũku makĩgeria kũhũthĩra rĩĩtwa rĩa Mwathani Jesũ harĩ andũ arĩa maarĩ na ndaimono. Moigaga atĩrĩ, “Thĩinĩ wa rĩĩtwa rĩa Jesũ, ũrĩa ũhunjagio nĩ Paũlũ, ndagwatha uume.”
english: But some of the itinerant Jews, exorcists, took on themselves to invoke over those who had the evil spirits the name of the Lord Jesus, saying, “We adjure you by Jesus whom Paul preaches.”

kikuyu: Kuuma mũhĩrĩga wa Nafitali maarĩ andũ 53,400.
english: those who were counted of them, of the tribe of Naphtali, were fifty-three thousand four hundred.

kikuyu: ‘Mũndũ angĩaga kũruta ũira rĩrĩa etĩtwo arute ũira igũrũ rĩa ũndũ oonete kana akaigua, mũndũ ũcio nĩehĩtie, na nĩagacookererwo nĩ wĩhia ũcio wake.
english: ‘If anyone sins, in that he hears a public adjuration to testify, he being a witness, whether he has seen or known, if he doesn’t report it, then he shall bear his iniquity.

kikuyu: “ ‘Nao andũ nĩmakaheagwo wĩra wa gũtheragia bũrũri ũcio. Amwe magaathiĩ bũrũri ũcio kũndũ guothe, na mongererwo angĩ a gũthikaga ciimba iria igaakorwo itigaire kũu. Mĩeri ĩyo mũgwanja yathira-rĩ, nĩguo makaambĩrĩria gũcaria matigari macio.
english: “ ‘ “They will set apart men of continual employment who will pass through the land. Those who pass through will go with those who bury those who remain on the surface of the land, to cleanse it. After the end of seven months they will search.

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo Maandĩko marĩa matheru moigĩte atĩrĩ, “Ndũkanohe ndegwa kanua rĩrĩa ĩkũranga ngano ĩkĩmĩhũũra,” na ningĩ nĩmoigĩte atĩrĩ, “Mũruti wĩra nĩagĩrĩirwo nĩkũheo mũcaara wake.”
english: For the Scripture says, “You shall not muzzle the ox when it treads out the grain.”* And, “The laborer is worthy of his wages.”*

kikuyu: Andũ arĩa magaatigario nĩ thigari iria ciikagia mĩguĩ, o acio njamba cia ita cia Kedari-rĩ, magaakorwo marĩ o anini.” Jehova Ngai wa Isiraeli nĩwe warĩtie ũhoro ũcio.
english: and the residue of the number of the archers, the mighty men of the children of Kedar, will be few; for Yahweh, the God of Israel, has spoken it.”

kikuyu: Nake Jesũ akĩmooria atĩrĩ, “Na inyuĩ mũthũkagia watho wa Ngai nĩ ũndũ wa irĩra cianyu nĩkĩ?
english: He answered them, “Why do you also disobey the commandment of God because of your tradition?

kikuyu: “Ta thaa thita ndĩ hakuhĩ gũkinya Dameski-rĩ, o rĩmwe gũkĩgĩa ũtheri mũnene woimire igũrũ, naguo ũkĩnjarĩra mĩena yothe.
english: “As I made my journey and came close to Damascus, about noon suddenly a great light shone around me from the sky.

kikuyu: Ngai nĩwe watũhonokirie na agĩtwĩtĩra mũtũũrĩre mũtheru, ti tondũ wa ũndũ twĩkĩte, no nĩ ũndũ wa ũrĩa aatanyĩte gwĩka na nĩ ũndũ wa wega wake. Twaheirwo wega ũyũ thĩinĩ wa Kristũ Jesũ mbere ya kĩambĩrĩria gĩa thĩ,
english: who saved us and called us with a holy calling, not according to our works, but according to his own purpose and grace, which was given to us in Christ Jesus before times eternal,

kikuyu: Nake Jusufu akĩĩra Firaũni atĩrĩ, “Irooto cia Firaũni cierĩ no kĩroto kĩmwe. Ngai nĩaguũrĩirie Firaũni ũrĩa akiriĩ gwĩka.
english: Joseph said to Pharaoh, “The dream of Pharaoh is one. What God is about to do he has declared to Pharaoh.

kikuyu: O na mbura ya mbembe ĩngĩgũithia mĩtĩ ya mũtitũ thĩ, narĩo itũũra inene rĩaraganio biũ-rĩ,
english: though hail flattens the forest, and the city is leveled completely.

kikuyu: Wee Jehova, maiguithie guoya, ũreke ndũrĩrĩ imenye atĩ cio no andũ.
english: Put them in fear, Yahweh. Let the nations know that they are only men. Selah.

kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, maitho maku mangĩkorwo nĩ mathũku, mwĩrĩ waku wothe ũrĩiyũragwo nĩ nduma. Naguo ũtheri ũrĩa ũrĩ thĩinĩ waku ũngĩkorwo nĩ nduma-rĩ, nduma ĩyo kaĩ nĩ nene-ĩ!
english: But if your eye is evil, your whole body will be full of darkness. If therefore the light that is in you is darkness, how great is the darkness!

kikuyu: Kĩgũũta kiugaga, “Nja kũrĩ na mũrũũthi!” Kana gĩkoiga atĩrĩ, “Nĩngũũragĩrwo njĩra-inĩ!”
english: The sluggard says, “There is a lion outside! I will be killed in the streets!”

kikuyu: Maaĩ ma kũu Nimurimu nĩmahũĩte, nayo nyeki nĩĩhoohete; namo mahuti mothe maigũ nĩmomĩte, na gũtirĩ kĩndũ kĩĩruru gĩtigaire.
english: For the waters of Nimrim will be desolate; for the grass has withered away, the tender grass fails, there is no green thing.

kikuyu: “No rĩrĩ, o nĩ mũhũũyũ ũrĩa ũrerete igũrũ rĩa maaĩ; igai rĩao bũrũri-inĩ nĩ irume, na nĩ ũndũ ũcio gũtirĩ mũndũ ũthiiaga mĩgũnda-inĩ ya mĩthabibũ.
english: “They are foam on the surface of the waters. Their portion is cursed in the earth. They don’t turn into the way of the vineyards.

kikuyu: (Ningĩ nĩ haarĩ mũndũ ũngĩ wetagwo Uria mũrũ wa Shemaia, wa kuuma Kiriathu-Jearimu, nake aarathire ũhoro akĩgwetaga rĩĩtwa rĩa Jehova; aarathire o maũndũ ta maya megiĩ itũũra rĩĩrĩ inene na bũrũri ũyũ, o ta ũguo Jeremia aarathĩte.
english: There was also a man who prophesied in Yahweh’s name, Uriah the son of Shemaiah of Kiriath Jearim; and he prophesied against this city and against this land according to all the words of Jeremiah.

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ wa nyũmba ya Jehova Ngai witũ, caragia ũgaacĩru waku.
english: For the sake of the house of Yahweh our God, I will seek your good.

kikuyu: Kũu Moabi nĩnganiina arĩa othe marutagĩra maruta kũndũ kũrĩa gũtũũgĩru, na magacinĩra ngai ciao ũbumba,” ũguo nĩguo Jehova ekuuga.
english: Moreover I will cause to cease in Moab,” says Yahweh, “him who offers in the high place, and him who burns incense to his

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ wa ikũũma rĩaku, Wee Ngai wa Jakubu, mbarathi o ũndũ ũmwe na ngaari cia ita igwĩte thĩ ikaaga kwĩigua.
english: At your rebuke, God of Jacob, both chariot and horse are cast into a dead sleep.

kikuyu: Musa akĩona atĩ andũ nĩmakararĩtie, na atĩ Harũni nĩamarekereirie meke ũrĩa mekwenda, nginya magaatuĩka andũ a gũthekagĩrĩrwo nĩ thũ ciao.
english: When Moses saw that the people were out of control, (for Aaron had let them lose control, causing derision among their enemies),

kikuyu: Gĩkundi gĩa keerĩ kĩa aini gĩgĩthiĩ kĩerekeire mwena wa ũmotho. Na niĩ ngĩmarũmĩrĩra kũu rũthingo igũrũ hamwe na nuthu ya andũ, tũkĩhĩtũkĩra Mũthiringo wa Mariiko ma Mwaki o nginya Rũthingo-inĩ Rwariĩ,
english: The other company of those who gave thanks went to meet them, and I after them, with the half of the people on the wall above the tower of the furnaces, even to the wide wall,

kikuyu: “Rĩu ngoro yakwa nĩĩratangĩka, na niĩ ngũkiuga atĩa? Njuge, ‘Baba honokia harĩ ihinda rĩĩrĩ’? Aca, gĩtũmi gĩkĩ nĩkĩo gĩtũmĩte nginyie ihinda rĩĩrĩ.
english: “Now my soul is troubled. What shall I say? ‘Father, save me from this time’? But I came to this time for this cause.

kikuyu: Nake mũraika ũngĩ, ũrĩa warĩ na rũgĩo rwa thahabu, agĩũka akĩrũgama kĩgongona-inĩ. Nake akĩheo ũbumba mũingĩ nĩgeetha aũrute igongona hamwe na mahooya ma arĩa othe aamũre hau igũrũ rĩa kĩgongona gĩa thahabu kĩrĩa kĩrĩ mbere ya gĩtĩ kĩa ũnene.
english: Another angel came and stood over the altar, having a golden censer. Much incense was given to him, that he should add it to the prayers of all the saints on the golden altar which was before the throne.

kikuyu: Ningĩ Jesũ agĩcooka agĩthiĩ na arutwo ake handũ heetagwo Gethisemane, akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Ikarai haha, na niĩ thiĩ haarĩa ngahooe.”
english: Then Jesus came with them to a place called Gethsemane, and said to his disciples, “Sit here, while I go there and pray.”

kikuyu: Kwaga ũguo, mwarĩa mwahũũna, na mwaka nyũmba njega na mũcitũũre,
english: lest, when you have eaten and are full, and have built fine houses and lived in them;

kikuyu: “O na kũrĩ we, wee Juda, nĩwathĩrĩirio ihinda rĩa magetha. “O hĩndĩ ĩrĩa ndaacookeria andũ akwa maũndũ ma kũmaguna,
english: “Also, Judah, there is a harvest appointed for you, when I restore the fortunes of my people.

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa mũndũ ũmwe wao onire ũguo, akĩĩra Joabu atĩrĩ, “Nĩndona Abisalomu acunjuurĩte mũtĩ-inĩ wa mũgandi.”
english: A certain man saw it, and told Joab, and said, “Behold, I saw Absalom hanging in an oak.”

kikuyu: Ningĩ andũ othe magĩũka na makĩringĩrĩria Daudi arĩe irio gũtanatuka, no Daudi akĩĩhĩta na mwĩhĩtwa, akiuga atĩrĩ, “Ngai arothũũra, o na anjĩke ũũru makĩria, ingĩcama irio kana kĩndũ kĩngĩ riũa rĩtanathũa!”
english: All the people came to urge David to eat bread while it was yet day; but David swore, saying, “God do so to me, and more also, if I taste bread or anything else, until the sun goes down.”

kikuyu: Tondũ nĩmandirikĩte, na magacinĩra ngai ingĩ ũbumba, na makaandakaria nĩ ũndũ wa mĩhianano yothe ĩrĩa mathondekete na moko mao, marakara makwa nĩmakanĩire kũndũ gũkũ, na
english: Because they have forsaken me and have burned incense to other gods, that they might provoke me to anger with all the work of their hands, therefore my wrath shall be kindled against this place, and it will not be quenched.’ ”

kikuyu: Ningĩ kiugo kĩa Jehova gĩkĩnginyĩrĩra, ngĩĩrwo atĩrĩ:
english: Yahweh’s word came to me, saying,

kikuyu: No ithe angĩmũkaania aigua ũhoro wa mĩĩhĩtwa ĩyo, hatirĩ o na kĩmwe kĩa mĩĩhĩtwa ĩyo yake, kana ciĩranĩro icio ehingĩte nacio we mwene, igatũũra; nake Jehova nĩakamũrekera nĩ ũndũ ithe nĩamũgirĩtie eke ũndũ ũcio.
english: But if her father forbids her in the day that he hears, none of her vows or of her pledges with which she has bound her soul, shall stand. Yahweh will forgive her, because her father has forbidden her.

kikuyu: Petero akĩmakiria na guoko na akĩmataarĩria ũrĩa Mwathani aamũrutĩte njeera. Akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Ĩrai Jakubu na ariũ na aarĩ a Ithe witũ ũhoro ũcio,” agĩcooka agĩthiĩ kũndũ kũngĩ.
english: But he, beckoning to them with his hand to be silent, declared to them how the Lord had brought him out of the prison. He said, “Tell these things to James and to the brothers.” Then he departed and went to another place.

kikuyu: Tondũ nĩ niĩ ndĩmwĩhĩirie, nĩngũkirĩrĩria mangʼũrĩ ma Jehova nginya rĩrĩa we akaanjiirĩrĩra, na onanie kĩhooto gĩakwa. Nĩakanduta nduma-inĩ, andehe ũtheri-inĩ, na niĩ nĩngeyonera ũthingu wake.
english: I will bear the indignation of Yahweh, because I have sinned against him, until he pleads my case and executes judgment for me. He will bring me out to the light. I will see his righteousness.

kikuyu: Ndacookagia rĩrĩa andũ maakaya nĩ ũndũ wa mwĩtũũgĩrio wa andũ arĩa aaganu.
english: There they cry, but no one answers, because of the pride of evil men.

kikuyu: Nake Jesũ akĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Niĩ njarĩirie andũ othe a thĩ itarĩ ũndũ ngũhitha, na ngarutana hĩndĩ ciothe thunagogi-inĩ o na hekarũ-inĩ, kũrĩa Ayahudi othe macemanagia. Ndirĩ ũndũ njaragia na hitho.
english: Jesus answered him, “I spoke openly to the world. I always taught in synagogues and in the temple, where the Jews always meet. I said nothing in secret.

kikuyu: ĩndĩ ĩyo Pilato akĩoya Jesũ, akĩmũhũũrithia iboko.
english: o Pilate then took Jesus and flogged him.

kikuyu: “Oya tũhiga twĩrĩ twa onigithi ũkurure marĩĩtwa ma ariũ a Isiraeli igũrũ rĩatuo
english: You shall take two onyx stones, and engrave on them the names of the children of Israel.

kikuyu: Nayo mbura ĩkiura, njũũĩ ikĩiyũra, naruo rũhuho rũkĩhurutana nacio ikĩhũũra nyũmba ĩyo na nditi, nayo nyũmba ĩyo ĩkĩmomoka na kũgũa kũnene.”
english: The rain came down, the floods came, and the winds blew and beat on that house; and it fell—and its fall was great.”

kikuyu: Rĩrĩa Joramu onire Jehu, akĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Jehu, ũũkĩte na thayũ?” Nake Jehu agĩcookia atĩrĩ, “Kũngĩgĩa na thayũ atĩa, ũhooi wa mĩhianano wothe na ũrogi wa nyũkwa, Jezebeli, ũingĩhĩte ũũ?”
english: When Joram saw Jehu, he said, “Is it peace, Jehu?” He answered, “What peace, so long as the prostitution of your mother Jezebel and her witchcraft abound?”

kikuyu: Tondũ ũcio arutwo arĩa angĩ makĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Nĩtuonete Mwathani.” Nowe akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Itoonete irema cia mĩcumarĩ moko-inĩ make, na ndoonyie kĩara gĩakwa hau irema-inĩ cia mĩcumarĩ, na njooke ndoonyie guoko mbaru-inĩ ciake, ndingĩĩtĩkia ũhoro ũcio.”
english: The other disciples therefore said to him, “We have seen the Lord!” But he said to them, “Unless I see in his hands the print of the nails, put my finger into the print of the nails, and put my hand into his side, I will not believe.”

kikuyu: Cookereria andũ arĩa manjambagia ũũru wao; Wee maniine nĩ ũndũ wa wĩhokeku waku.
english: He will repay the evil to my enemies. Destroy them in your truth.

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo andũ magana matandatũ a kuuma mũhĩrĩga wa Dani, meeohete indo cia mbaara, makiumagara kuuma Zora na Eshitaoli.
english: The family of the Danites set out from Zorah and Eshtaol with six hundred men armed with weapons of war.

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ya kũgetha yakuhĩrĩria-rĩ, agĩtũma ndungata ciake kũrĩ akombori acio nĩguo ikanengerwo maciaro make.
english: When the season for the fruit came near, he sent his servants to the farmers to receive his fruit.

kikuyu: nĩrĩrai mũkenete mũrĩ mbere ya Jehova, inyuĩ andũ othe a thĩ.
english: hout for joy to Yahweh, all you lands!

kikuyu: na ũkoroku, na rũmena, na maheeni, na ũũra-thoni, na ũiru, na njambanio, na mwĩtĩĩo, o na ũrimũ.
english: covetings, wickedness, deceit, lustful desires, an evil eye, blasphemy, pride, and foolishness.

kikuyu: Mũthenya wa ĩtatũ kuuma Jakubu oora, Labani akĩĩrwo atĩ Jakubu nĩorĩte.
english: Laban was told on the third day that Jacob had fled.

kikuyu: Mũndũ ũcio mũregi watho nĩagooka kũringana na wĩra wa Shaitani ũrĩa wonekaga thĩinĩ wa mĩthemba yothe ya ciama cia maheeni, na marũũri, na morirũ,
english: even he whose coming is according to the working of Satan with all power and signs and lying wonders,

kikuyu: Agĩcooka akĩona mũtumia mũthĩĩni, warĩ wa ndigwa, agĩĩkĩra tũthendi twĩrĩ o ho thĩinĩ.
english: He saw a certain poor widow casting in two small brass coins.†

kikuyu: Nake mũndũ-wa-nja ũcio akĩheo mathagu meerĩ ma nderi ĩrĩa nene nĩgeetha ombũke athiĩ werũ-inĩ handũ harĩa aathondekeirwo, harĩa aarĩ amenyererwo kwa ihinda, nakwamahindananuthuyaihinda, harĩaatangĩakinyĩrirwonĩnyokaĩyo.
english: Two wings of the great eagle were given to the woman, that she might fly into the wilderness to her place, so that she might be nourished for a time, times, and half a time, from the face of the serpent.

kikuyu: inyuĩ na inyuĩ mũkĩre muume kũrĩa mũmooheirie, mũtharĩkĩre itũũra rĩu inene na mũrĩtunyane. Jehova Ngai wanyu nĩekũrĩneana moko-inĩ manyu.
english: and you shall rise up from the ambush, and take possession of the city; for Yahweh your God will deliver it into your hand.

kikuyu: o nginya rĩrĩa ngooka ndĩmũtware bũrũri ũhaana ta wanyu, bũrũri ũrĩ ngano na ndibei ya mũhihano, bũrũri ũrĩ irio na mĩgũnda ya mĩthabibũ.
english: until I come and take you away to a land like your own land, a land of grain and new wine, a land of bread and vineyards.

kikuyu: Ningĩ mũthenya wa kenda wa mweri wa ĩna wa mwaka-inĩ wa ikũmi na ũmwe wa wathani wa Zedekia, nĩguo rũthingo rwa itũũra rĩu inene rwatũrĩkirio.
english: In the eleventh year of Zedekiah, in the fourth month, the ninth day of the month, a breach was made in the city.

kikuyu: Nake mũraika wa Jehova akĩhutia nyama na mĩgate ĩyo ĩtaarĩ na ndawa ya kũimbia na mũthia wa rũthanju rwake. Mwaki ũgĩakana uumĩte ihiga-inĩ, ũgĩcina nyama icio o hamwe na mĩgate ĩyo. Nake mũraika wa Jehova akĩbuĩria na ndaacookire kuonwo.
english: Then Yahweh’s angel stretched out the end of the staff that was in his hand, and touched the meat and the unleavened cakes; and fire went up out of the rock and consumed the meat and the unleavened cakes. Then Yahweh’s angel departed out of his sight.

kikuyu: Matiigana kũũria atĩrĩ: ‘Jehova arĩ ha, ũrĩa watũrutire bũrũri wa Misiri, agĩtũgereria werũ-inĩ ũtaarĩ kĩndũ, tũkĩgerera bũrũri warĩ wa werũ na iharũrũka, bũrũri ũtoiraga mbura, na warĩ nduma ta ya gĩkuũ, bũrũri ũtaageragĩrwo na ũtaatũũragwo nĩ mũndũ?’
english: They didn’t say, ‘Where is Yahweh who brought us up out of the land of Egypt, who led us through the wilderness, through a land of deserts and of pits, through a land of drought and of the shadow of death, through a land that no one passed through, and where no man lived?’

kikuyu: Mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai acooke ehĩtithie mũtumia ũcio, amwĩre atĩrĩ, “Angĩkorwo hatirĩ mũndũ ũngĩ ũkomete nawe, na ndũhĩngĩcanĩtie mĩthiĩre yaku ũgethaahia hĩndĩ ĩrĩa ũrĩ na mũthuuri-rĩ, maaĩ maya marũrũ marĩa marehaga kĩrumi maroaga gũgwĩka ũũru.
english: The priest shall cause her to take an oath and shall tell the woman, “If no man has lain with you, and if you haven’t gone aside to uncleanness, being under your husband’s authority, be free from this water of bitterness that brings a curse.

kikuyu: Mũndũ o wothe ũngĩagire gũkinya Jerusalemu mĩthenya ĩtatũ ĩtanathira kũringana na itua rĩa anene na athuuri, atunywo indo ciake ciothe, nake we mwene aingatwo, ehere kĩũngano-inĩ kĩa andũ arĩa maatahĩtwo.
english: and that whoever didn’t come within three days, according to the counsel of the princes and the elders, all his possessions should be forfeited, and he himself separated from the assembly of the captivity.

kikuyu: ũguo noguo mũndũ akomaga na ndacookaga gũũkĩra rĩngĩ; o nginya rĩrĩa igũrũ rĩgaathira-rĩ, andũ matirĩ hĩndĩ magookĩra kana maarahũrwo mookĩre.
english: so man lies down and doesn’t rise. Until the heavens are no more, they will not awake, nor be roused out of their sleep.

kikuyu: Gũtirĩ mũndũ ũngĩndunya guo, no niĩ ngũũruta na kwĩyendera. Ndĩ na ũhoti wa kũũruta, o na ũhoti wa kũwoya rĩngĩ. Watho ũyũ ndaamũkĩrire kuuma kũrĩ Baba.”
english: No one takes it away from me, but I lay it down by myself. I have power to lay it down, and I have power to take it again. I received this commandment from my Father.”

kikuyu: Nao andũ acio maatũmĩtwo magĩcooka rĩngĩ makiuga atĩrĩ, “Beni-Hadadi ekuuga ũũ: ‘Ndaatũ- manire ngĩenda betha ciaku, na thahabu, na atumia aku, o na ciana ciaku.
english: The messengers came again and said, “Ben Hadad says, ‘I sent indeed to you, saying, “You shall deliver me your silver, your gold, your wives, and your children;

kikuyu: na Shemaia, na Joiaribu, na Jedaia,
english: Shemaiah, Joiarib, Jedaiah,

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Daudi akĩĩra Joabu na anene a mbũtũ cia ita atĩrĩ: “Thiĩi mũgatare andũ a Isiraeli kuuma Birishiba nginya Dani, mũcooke mũndehere ũhoro nĩguo menye ũrĩa maigana.”
english: David said to Joab and to the princes of the people, “Go, count Israel from Beersheba even to Dan; and bring me word, that I may know how many there are.”

kikuyu: Maya nĩmo marĩĩtwa ma ciana iria aaciarĩire kũu Jerusalemu:
english: These are the names of those who were born to him in Jerusalem: Shammua, Shobab, Nathan, Solomon,

kikuyu: Maithe manyu ma tene maarĩĩaga mana marĩ werũ-inĩ, na no maakuire.
english: Your fathers ate the manna in the wilderness and they died.

kikuyu: Mũtikanakuue mũrigo mũũrute nyũmba-inĩ cianyu, kana mũrute wĩra o na ũrĩkũ mũthenya wa Thabatũ, no rĩrĩ, mũthenya wa Thabatũ mũwamũrage, o ta ũrĩa ndaathire maithe manyu mekage.
english: Don’t carry a burden out of your houses on the Sabbath day. Don’t do any work, but make the Sabbath day holy, as I commanded your fathers.

kikuyu: Gĩa na ũũgĩ, gĩa na kũmenya maũndũ; ndũkanariganĩrwo nĩ ciugo ciakwa, kana wahũke kuuma harĩ cio.
english: Get wisdom. Get understanding. Don’t forget, and don’t deviate from the words of my mouth.

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa ndeera mũndũ mwaganu atĩrĩ, ‘Wee mũndũ ũyũ mwaganu, ti-itherũ nĩũgũkua,’ nawe wage kũmũringĩrĩria atigane na mĩthiĩre ĩyo yake-rĩ, mũndũ ũcio mwaganu nĩagaakua tondũ wa wĩhia wake, no wee nĩwe ngooria thakame yake.
english: When I tell the wicked, ‘O wicked man, you will surely die,’ and you don’t speak to warn the wicked from his way, that wicked man will die in his iniquity, but I will require his blood at your hand.

kikuyu: No Afarisai makiuga atĩrĩ, “Aingataga ndaimono na hinya wa mũnene wa ndaimono.” Aruti Wĩra nĩ Anini
english: But the Pharisees said, “By the prince of the demons, he casts out demons.”

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio-rĩ, ikaragai mwĩiguĩte tondũ nĩndamwĩra maũndũ mothe mbere ya ihinda rĩtanakinya.
english: But you watch. “Behold, I have told you all things beforehand.

kikuyu: ‘Mũthenya wa ira nĩndĩronire thakame ya Nabothu na thakame ya ariũ ake, nĩguo Jehova ekuuga, na ti-itherũ nĩngatũma ũmĩrĩhĩre mũgũnda-inĩ o ũyũ, nĩguo Jehova ekuuga.’ Rĩu kĩmuoe, ũmũikie mũgũnda-inĩ ũcio, kũringana na kiugo kĩa Jehova.”
english: ‘Surely I have seen yesterday the blood of Naboth, and the blood of his sons,’ says Yahweh; ‘and I will repay you in this plot of ground,’ says Yahweh. Now therefore take and cast him onto the plot of ground, according to Yahweh’s word.”

kikuyu: na Uria ũrĩa Mũhiti, na Zabadi mũrũ wa Ahalai,
english: Uriah the Hittite, Zabad the son of Ahlai,

kikuyu: wĩra wake nĩũkoneka ũrĩa ũtariĩ, tondũ Mũthenya ũcio nĩũgaatũma woneke ũtheri-inĩ. Wĩra ũcio ũkaaguũrio na mwaki, nĩgũkorwo wĩra wa o mũndũ nĩũkagerio na mwaki ũcio.
english: each man’s work will be revealed. For the Day will declare it, because it is revealed in fire; and the fire itself will test what sort of work each man’s work is.

kikuyu: Nake Joshua akĩiga mahiga marĩa ikũmi na meerĩ maarutĩtwo Rũũĩ rwa Jorodani kũu Giligali.
english: Joshua set up those twelve stones, which they took out of the Jordan, in Gilgal.

kikuyu: Ũũ nĩguo Ngai oonanirie wendo wake harĩ ithuĩ: Nĩatũmire Mũriũ wake, o we wa mũmwe, gũkũ thĩ nĩgeetha tũtũũre muoyo nĩ ũndũ wake.
english: By this God’s love was revealed in us, that God has sent his only born† Son into the world that we might live through him.

kikuyu: Magũrũ maitũ marũgamĩte ihingo-inĩ ciaku, wee Jerusalemu.
english: Our feet are standing within your gates, Jerusalem!

kikuyu: Rĩrĩa Gideoni aiguire ũhoro wa kĩroto kĩu na ũtaũri wakĩo, akĩhooya Ngai. Agĩcooka nginya kambĩ-inĩ ya Isiraeli akĩmeeta akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Ũkĩrai! Jehova nĩaneanĩte kambĩ ya Amidiani moko- inĩ manyu.”
english: It was so, when Gideon heard the telling of the dream and its interpretation, that he worshiped. Then he returned into the camp of Israel and said, “Arise, for Yahweh has delivered the army of Midian into your hand!”

kikuyu: Mwathiĩ- rĩ, hunjagiai ndũmĩrĩri ĩno: ‘Ũthamaki wa igũrũ nĩũkuhĩrĩirie.’
english: As you go, preach, saying, ‘The Kingdom of Heaven is at hand!’

kikuyu: Mũnene ũcio wa thigari cia mũthamaki nĩakuuire mbakũri iria nene, na Ngĩo, na mbakũri cia kũminjaminjĩria maaĩ, na nyũngũ, na mĩtĩ ya matawa, na thaani, o na mbakũri iria ciahũthagĩrwo na kũruta maruta ma kũnyuuo, iria ciothe ciathondeketwo na thahabu ĩrĩa therie kana betha.
english: The captain of the guard took away the cups, the fire pans, the basins, the pots, the lamp stands, the spoons, and the bowls; that which was of gold, as gold, and that which was of silver, as silver.

kikuyu: Thuutha wa kũhũũrwo mũno magĩikio njeera, na mũrangĩri wa njeera agĩathwo amarangĩre wega.
english: When they had laid many stripes on them, they threw them into prison, charging the jailer to keep them safely.

kikuyu: Ũyũ nĩguo ũhoro ũrĩa Jehova aarĩtie wa kũmũũkĩrĩra: “ ‘Mũirĩtu Gathirange, Mwarĩ wa Zayuni, nĩakũmenete na agakũnyarara. Mwarĩ wa Jerusalemu arainia mũtwe rĩrĩa wee ũroora.
english: This is the word that Yahweh has spoken concerning him: ‘The virgin daughter of Zion has despised you and ridiculed you. The daughter of Jerusalem has shaken her head at you.

kikuyu: Acio nĩo maatarirwo nĩ Musa na Eleazaru ũrĩa mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai rĩrĩa maatarĩire andũ a Isiraeli werũ-inĩ wa Moabi hũgũrũrũ-inĩ cia Rũũĩ rwa Jorodani, mũrĩmo ũyũ wa Jeriko.
english: These are those who were counted by Moses and Eleazar the priest, who counted the children of Israel in the plains of Moab by the Jordan at Jericho.

kikuyu: Niĩ nĩndamũcookeria ũhoro ũcio ũmũthĩ, no rĩrĩ, mũtiathĩkĩire Jehova Ngai wanyu harĩ maũndũ macio mothe aandũmĩte ndĩmwĩre.
english: I have declared it to you today; but you have not obeyed Yahweh your God’s voice in anything for which he has sent me to you.

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ wa kũmũtũgĩria ũguo-rĩ, andũ othe, na ndũrĩrĩ ciothe, na andũ a mĩario yothe nĩmainainaga marĩ mbere yake na makamwĩtigĩra. Andũ arĩa mũthamaki angĩendire mooragwo nĩamooragithagia; arĩa angĩendire kũhonokia mĩoyo yao, nĩamahonokagia; arĩa angĩendire gũtũũgĩria, nĩamatũgagĩria; nao arĩa angĩendire kũnyiihia, nĩamanyiihagia.
english: Because of the greatness that he gave him, all the peoples, nations, and languages trembled and feared before him. He killed whom he wanted to, and he kept alive whom he wanted to. He raised up whom he wanted to, and he put down whom he wanted to.

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo Ngai na ũndũ wa ũũgĩ wake nĩonire atĩ andũ a thĩ ĩno na ũũgĩ wao matikaamũmenya, akĩona kwagĩrĩire ahũthĩre maũndũ ma ũrimũ marĩa tũhunjagia nĩgeetha ahonokie arĩa mamwĩtĩkagia.
english: For seeing that in the wisdom of God, the world through its wisdom didn’t know God, it was God’s good pleasure through the foolishness of the preaching to save those who believe.

kikuyu: na ithuĩ nĩtũgũtema mĩgogo yothe ĩrĩa ũbataire kuuma Lebanoni, na tũmĩikũrũkie tũmĩohanĩtie, ĩgereirio iria-inĩ nginya Jopa. Nawe ũgĩcooke ũmĩoe ũmĩtware nginya Jerusalemu.”
english: and we will cut wood out of Lebanon, as much as you need. We will bring it to you in rafts by sea to Joppa; then you shall carry it up to Jerusalem.”

kikuyu: Andũ arĩa athingu nĩmagakena maarĩhĩrio, rĩrĩa magathambia nyarĩrĩ ciao thakame-inĩ ya arĩa aaganu.
english: The righteous shall rejoice when he sees the vengeance. He shall wash his feet in the blood of the wicked,

kikuyu: wa gatatũ wagũĩrĩire Zakuri, na ariũ ake, na andũ a nyũmba yao, maarĩ andũ 12;
english: the third to Zaccur, his sons and his brothers, twelve;

kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, mũthenya wa ikũmi na ĩtatũ wa mweri wa mbere, aandĩki-marũa a mũthamaki magĩĩtwo. Makĩandĩka ũrĩa wothe Hamani aathanĩte, na mwandĩkĩre wa o bũrũri, na rũthiomi rwa o rũrĩrĩ, makĩandĩkĩra anene a mũthamaki, na abarũthi a mabũrũri a kũndũ na kũndũ, na andũ arĩa maarĩ igweta a ndũrĩrĩ mwanya. Marũa macio maandĩkirwo na rĩĩtwa rĩa Mũthamaki Ahasuerusu we mwene, na magĩĩkĩrwo mũhũũri wa gĩcũhĩ gĩake mwene.
english: Then the king’s scribes were called in on the first month, on the thirteenth day of the month; and all that Haman commanded was written to the king’s local governors, and to the governors who were over every province, and to the princes of every people, to every province according to its writing, and to every people in their language. It was written in the name of King Ahasuerus, and it was sealed with the king’s ring.

kikuyu: (o na harĩa ithe ateekire ũndũ o na ũmwe wa mo): “We nĩarĩĩaga irio iria irutĩirwo mahooero-inĩ marĩa marĩ irĩma-igũrũ. Nĩathũkagia mũtumia wa mũndũ ũrĩa ũngĩ.
english: or who does not do any of those things but has eaten at the mountain shrines and defiled his neighbor’s wife,

kikuyu: audi nĩatũmanĩire anene othe a Isiraeli moongane Jerusalemu, nao nĩo: anene arĩa maarũgamĩrĩire mĩhĩrĩga, na anene a ikundi iria ciatungatagĩra mũthamaki, na anene a ikundi cia o ngiri ngiri na anene a ikundi cia o igana igana, na anene arĩa maarũgamĩrĩire indo ciothe na mahiũ ma mũthamaki na ma ariũ ake o na anene a nyũmba ya ũthamaki, na andũ arĩa maarĩ njamba, na andũ othe arĩa maarĩ ũrũme wa kũrũa mbaara.
english: avid assembled all the princes of Israel, the princes of the tribes, the captains of the companies who served the king by division, the captains of thousands, the captains of hundreds, and the rulers over all the substance and possessions of the king and of his sons, with the officers and the mighty men, even all the mighty men of valor, to Jerusalem.

kikuyu: Makinya make mamũikagia wabu-inĩ, nake akoorũraga thĩinĩ wa marima mayo.
english: For he is cast into a net by his own feet, and he wanders into its mesh.

kikuyu: Nuhu aarĩ mũrĩmi, na nĩahaandire mũgũnda wa mĩthabibũ.
english: Noah began to be a farmer, and planted a vineyard.

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo rũthingo rwa itũũra rĩu inene rũkĩmomorwo, nayo mbũtũ yothe ya ita ĩkĩũra ũtukũ ĩgereire kĩhingo-inĩ kĩrĩa kĩarĩ gatagatĩ ga thingo cierĩ hakuhĩ na mũgũnda wa mũthamaki, o na gũtuĩka andũ a Babuloni nĩmarigiicĩirie itũũra rĩu inene. Nayo ĩkĩũra ĩrorete Araba,
english: Then a breach was made in the city, and all the men of war fled by night by the way of the gate between the two walls, which was by the king’s garden (now the Chaldeans were against the city around it); and the king went by the way of the Arabah.

kikuyu: Nacio njiaro cia Dani kũringana na mbarĩ ciao ciarĩ ici: kuuma kũrĩ Shuhamu nĩ mbarĩ ya Ashuhamu. Icio nĩcio ciarĩ mbarĩ ya Dani.
english: These are the sons of Dan after their families: of Shuham, the family of the Shuhamites. These are the families of Dan after their families.

kikuyu: nĩngaririkana kĩrĩkanĩro gĩakwa na Jakubu, na kĩrĩkanĩro gĩakwa na Isaaka, na kĩrĩkanĩro gĩakwa na Iburahĩmu, na ndirikane bũrũri ũcio.
english: then I will remember my covenant with Jacob, my covenant with Isaac, and also my covenant with Abraham; and I will remember the land.

kikuyu: Ũhaataga andũ, ũkameeheria, magakoma toro wa gĩkuũ; andũ mahaana ta nyeki ĩrĩa ĩringũkaga rũciinĩ:
english: You sweep them away as they sleep. In the morning they sprout like new grass.

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio akĩmoonia, nao makĩũraga andũ a itũũra rĩu inene na rũhiũ rwa njora, no makĩhonokia mũndũ ũcio na nyũmba yake yothe.
english: He showed them the entrance into the city, and they struck the city with the edge of the sword; but they let the man and all his family go.

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo andũ a Isiraeli matigacooka kũhĩtia njĩra mehere harĩ niĩ, kana macooke gwĩthaahia na mehia macio mao mothe. Nĩmagatuĩka andũ akwa, na niĩ nduĩke Ngai wao, ũguo nĩguo Mwathani Jehova ekuuga.’ ” Ituĩro rĩa Ciira Rĩtangĩehererwo
english: that the house of Israel may no more go astray from me, neither defile themselves any more with all their transgressions; but that they may be my people, and I may be their God,” says the Lord Yahweh.’ ”

kikuyu: ĩndĩ ĩyo anene othe a mbũtũ cia ita, hamwe na Johanani mũrũ wa Karea, na Jezania mũrũ wa Hoshaia, na andũ othe kuuma ũrĩa mũnini nginya ũrĩa mũnene, magĩthiĩ harĩ
english: hen all the captains of the forces, and Johanan the son of Kareah, and Jezaniah the son of Hoshaiah, and all the people from the least even to the greatest, came near,

kikuyu: No andũ acio matikoragwo makĩmenya atĩ arĩa akuũ marĩ kũu, na atĩ ageni ake marĩ o kũrĩa kũriku mbĩrĩra-inĩ. 10 Thimo cia Solomoni
english: But he doesn’t know that the departed spirits are there, that her guests are in the depths of Sheol.†

kikuyu: tĩrĩrĩ, ndũkanakũũme mũndũ mũkũrũ, kaba ũmũthaithage taarĩ thoguo, nao andũ arĩa ethĩ ta marĩ ariũ a thoguo,
english: on’t rebuke an older man, but exhort him as a father; the younger men as brothers;

kikuyu: Nake Jesũ aigua ũguo akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Andũ arĩa agima mĩĩrĩ ti o mabataragio nĩ ndagĩtarĩ, no nĩ arĩa arũaru. Ndiokire gwĩta andũ arĩa athingu, no nĩ arĩa ehia.” Jesũ Kũũrio Ũhoro wa Kwĩhinga kũrĩa Irio
english: When Jesus heard it, he said to them, “Those who are healthy have no need for a physician, but those who are sick. I came not to call the righteous, but sinners to repentance.”

kikuyu: Ihinda rĩa gũcakaya rĩathira-rĩ, Daudi akiuga areehwo gwake nyũmba, nake agĩtuĩka mũtumia wake, na akĩmũciarĩra kahĩĩ. No rĩrĩ, ũndũ ũcio Daudi ekĩte nĩwarakaririe Jehova.
english: When the mourning was past, David sent and took her home to his house, and she became his wife and bore him a son. But the thing that David had done displeased Yahweh.

kikuyu: Na rĩrĩa Hushai ookire, Abisalomu akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Ahithofeli nĩaheanĩte mataaro maya. Nĩ wega twĩke ũguo aroiga? Angĩkorwo ti ũguo-rĩ, tũhe woni waku.”
english: When Hushai had come to Absalom, Absalom spoke to him, saying, “Ahithophel has spoken like this. Shall we do what he says? If not, speak up.”

kikuyu: Jerusalemu nĩrĩkoretwo na athamaki a hinya magĩathana Mũrĩmo-wa- Farati wothe, na nĩmarĩhagwo igooti na maruta ma mũthamaki, o na igooti rĩa mĩrigo ĩgĩtoonya bũrũri-inĩ.
english: There have also been mighty kings over Jerusalem who have ruled over all the country beyond the River; and tribute, custom, and toll was paid to them.

kikuyu: Ningĩ Musa akĩĩra andũ a Isiraeli atĩrĩ, “Onei, Jehova nĩathuurĩte Bezaleli mũrũ wa Uri ũrĩa mũriũ wa Huri, wa mũhĩrĩga wa Juda,
english: Moses said to the children of Israel, “Behold, Yahweh has called by name Bezalel the son of Uri, the son of Hur, of the tribe of Judah.

kikuyu: huutha ũcio, Jesũ nĩaceerangire Galili, nĩ ũndũ ndendaga gũthiĩ Judea tondũ Ayahudi akuo nĩmamũcaragia mamũũrage.
english: fter these things, Jesus was walking in Galilee, for he wouldn’t walk in Judea, because the Jews sought to kill him.

kikuyu: Andũ arĩa maathagwo nĩ mwĩrĩ ũyũ wĩhagia matingĩhota gũkenia Ngai.
english: Those who are in the flesh can’t please God.  Exodus 20:17; Deuteronomy 5:21  NU omits “who don’t walk according to the flesh, but according to the Spirit”

kikuyu: Ngai cia Hamathu na Aripadi irĩ kũ? Ngai cia Sefarivaimu, na Hena na Iva irĩ kũ? Nĩihonoketie Samaria kuuma guoko-inĩ gwakwa?
english: Where are the gods of Hamath and of Arpad? Where are the gods of Sepharvaim, of Hena, and Ivvah? Have they delivered Samaria out of my hand?

kikuyu: Jehova anjĩkĩire maũndũ kũringana na ũthingu wakwa; andĩhĩte kũringana na ũtheru wa moko makwa.
english: Yahweh has rewarded me according to my righteousness. According to the cleanness of my hands, he has recompensed me.

kikuyu: Nake akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Reke hakorwo harĩ na kĩrĩĩko giitũ nawe, o ta ũrĩa haarĩ kĩrĩĩko gatagatĩ ka baba na thoguo. Ta kĩone, kĩheo kĩa betha na thahabu nĩkĩo ndagũtũmĩra. Rĩu tharia kĩrĩĩko kĩanyu na Baasha mũthamaki wa Isiraeli nĩguo atigane na niĩ.”
english: “Let there be a treaty between me and you, like that between my father and your father. Behold, I have sent to you a present of silver and gold. Go, break your treaty with Baasha king of Israel, that he may depart from me.”

kikuyu: Ũrĩ thĩinĩ wa ũnene waku thiĩ ũhaicĩte mbarathi, ũkĩhootanaga nĩ ũndũ wa ũhoro wa ma, na wa kwĩnyiihia, o na wa ũthingu; reke guoko gwaku kwa ũrĩo kuonanie ciĩko cia kũmakania.
english: In your majesty ride on victoriously on behalf of truth, humility, and righteousness. Let your right hand display awesome deeds.

kikuyu: usa na athuuri a Isiraeli nĩ maathire andũ, makĩmeera atĩrĩ: “Rũmiai maathani maya mothe ndĩramũhe ũmũthĩ.
english: oses and the elders of Israel commanded the people, saying, “Keep all the commandment which I command you today.

kikuyu: “Mũndũ angĩgũtha ndungata ya mũndũ mũrũme kana ya mũndũ-wa-nja riitho narĩo rĩthũke-rĩ, no nginya akaarekereria ndungata ĩyo ĩĩthiĩre, ũndũ ũcio ũtuĩke nĩguo irĩhi rĩa riitho.
english: “If a man strikes his servant’s eye, or his maid’s eye, and destroys it, he shall let him go free for his eye’s sake.

kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, Elumo ũcio mũragũri (amu ũguo nĩguo rĩĩtwa rĩake riugĩte) akĩmakararia na akĩgeria kũgarũra barũthi ũcio nĩgeetha ndagetĩkie.
english: But Elymas the sorcerer (for so is his name by interpretation) withstood them, seeking to turn the proconsul away from the faith.

kikuyu: Ndũkerirĩrie ũthaka wake ngoro-inĩ yaku, kana ũreke akũguucĩrĩrie na maitho make,
english: Don’t lust after her beauty in your heart, neither let her captivate you with her eyelids.

kikuyu: hĩndĩ ĩyo nĩ ũkaigua ũrĩ kũu igũrũ na ũtue itua. Ũtuithanie ndungata ciaku ciira, ũrĩa mũhĩtia atuĩrwo ciira na acookererwo nĩ mahĩtia make marĩa ekĩte. Nake ũrĩa ũtehĩtie ũmũtue ndehĩtie, na nĩ ũndũ ũcio wonanie atĩ ti mwĩhia.
english: then hear in heaven, and act, and judge your servants, condemning the wicked, to bring his way on his own head, and justifying the righteous, to give him according to his righteousness.

kikuyu: Hezekia nĩoorĩtie Isaia atĩrĩ, “Kĩmenyithia gĩgaakorwo kĩrĩ kĩrĩkũ kĩa atĩ Jehova nĩekũũhonia, na atĩ nĩngambata thiĩ hekarũ-inĩ ya Jehova mũthenya wa gatatũ kuuma ũmũthĩ?”
english: Hezekiah said to Isaiah, “What will be the sign that Yahweh will heal me, and that I will go up to Yahweh’s house the third day?”

kikuyu: hĩndĩ ĩyo ũrĩ o kũu igũrũ, gĩikaro-inĩ gĩaku, ũkaigua ihooya rĩao na mathaithana mao, na ũmatirĩrĩre ũhoro-inĩ ũcio wao.
english: then hear their prayer and their supplication in heaven, your dwelling place, and maintain their cause;

kikuyu: Mũcirutanĩrie na thenge ĩmwe ĩrĩ igongona rĩa kũhoroherio mehia, yongererwo harĩ igongona rĩa njino rĩa hĩndĩ ciothe, hamwe na iruta rĩa mũtu na iruta rĩa kũnyuuo.
english: and one male goat for a sin offering; in addition to the continual burnt

kikuyu: Nĩmakarĩa njaũ cia mahiũ maku, na marĩe maciaro ma bũrũri waku nginya makũniine. Matigagũtigĩria ngano, kana ndibei ya mũhihano, kana maguta, o na kana magũtigĩre njaũ o na ĩmwe ya ndũũru cia ngʼombe ciaku kana tũũri twa rũũru rwa mbũri ciaku, nginya ũthĩĩnĩke biũ.
english: They will eat the fruit of your livestock and the fruit of your ground, until you are destroyed. They also won’t leave you grain, new wine, oil, the

kikuyu: Kũngĩkorwo nĩnjĩtĩkĩrĩkĩte nĩ mũthamaki, na akorwo mũthamaki no etĩkĩre ihooya rĩakwa na aahingĩrie ũrĩa ndĩrooria-rĩ, mũthamaki na Hamani nĩmagooka rũciũ iruga-inĩ rĩrĩa ngaamarugithĩria. Hĩndĩ ĩyo nĩguo ngaacookia kĩũria kĩa mũthamaki.” Hamani Kũrakarĩra Moridekai
english: If I have found favor in the sight of the king, and if it pleases the king to grant my petition and to perform my request, let the king and Haman come to the banquet that I will prepare for them, and I will do tomorrow as the king has said.”

kikuyu: Nyũngũ o yothe ĩrĩ kũu Jerusalemu na Juda ĩkaamũrĩrwo Jehova Mwene-Hinya-Wothe, nao arĩa othe magookaga kũruta igongona-rĩ, makooyaga nyũngũ imwe cia cio makaruga nacio. Na mũthenya ũcio-rĩ, gũtigacooka kũgĩa na mwonjorithia thĩinĩ wa nyũmba ya Jehova Mwene-Hinya-Wothe.
english: Yes, every pot in Jerusalem and in Judah will be holy to Yahweh of Armies; and all those who sacrifice will come and take of them, and cook in them. In that day there will no longer be a Canaanite‡ in the house of Yahweh of Armies.

kikuyu: Tondũ wa thĩĩna ũrĩa ũkaareherwo nĩ thũ yaku hĩndĩ ya kũrigiicĩrio, nĩ ũkaarĩa maciaro ma nda, ũrĩe nyama cia ariũ na airĩtu arĩa Jehova Ngai waku akũheete.
english: You will eat the fruit of your own body, the flesh of your sons and of your daughters, whom Yahweh your God has given you, in the siege and in the distress with which your enemies will distress you.

kikuyu: Matu mangĩiyũra mbura, mamiuragia gũkũ thĩ. Mũtĩ ũngĩgũa na mwena wa gũthini, kana mwena wa gathigathini, o harĩa ũgũũaga, hau no ho ũikaraga.
english: If the clouds are full of rain, they empty themselves on the earth; and if a tree falls toward the south, or toward the north, in the place where the tree falls, there shall it be.

kikuyu: Niĩ ndatariĩ ta gatũrũme kahooreri koimagarĩtio gagathĩnjwo; ndiamenyaga atĩ nĩmathugundĩte kũnjĩka ũũru, makiugaga atĩrĩ, “Nĩtwanangei mũtĩ ũyũ na maciaro maguo; nĩtũmũniinei oime bũrũri-inĩ wa arĩa marĩ muoyo, nĩgeetha rĩĩtwa rĩake rĩtikanacooke kũririkanwo o rĩ, o rĩ.”
english: But I was like a gentle lamb that is led to the slaughter. I didn’t know that they had devised plans against me, saying, “Let’s destroy the tree with its fruit, and let’s cut him off from the land of the living, that his name may be no more remembered.”

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Daudi agĩĩka o ta ũrĩa Jehova aamwathĩte, akĩhũũra Afilisti amarutĩtie Gibeoni, o nginya Gezeri.
english: David did so, as Yahweh commanded him, and struck the Philistines all the way from Geba to Gezer.

kikuyu: “Mũkũũri nĩagooka Zayuni, na akinye kũrĩ andũ a Jakubu arĩa maherete mehia mao,” ũguo nĩguo Jehova ekuuga.
english: “A Redeemer will come to Zion, and to those who turn from disobedience in Jacob,” says Yahweh.

kikuyu: O rĩmwe kairĩtu kau gagĩũkĩra, gakĩambĩrĩria gũthiĩ (nako kaarĩ na ũkũrũ wa mĩaka ikũmi na ĩĩrĩ). Nao mona ũguo makĩgega mũno.
english: Immediately the girl rose up and walked, for she was twelve years old. They were amazed with great amazement.

kikuyu: “ ‘Mũndũ ũmwe thĩinĩ wa kĩrĩndĩ angĩĩhia atekwenda na eke ũndũ ũrĩa mũkananie thĩinĩ wa maathani mothe ma Jehova, nĩahĩtĩtie.
english: “ ‘If anyone of the common people sins unwittingly, in doing any of the things which Yahweh has commanded not to be done, and is guilty,

kikuyu: Kũu nĩkuo gwakomagwo nĩ ikundi nene cia andũ arĩa maarĩ arũaru, ta atumumu, na cionje, na arĩa maakuĩte ciĩga (meetereire maaĩ moinjugwo.
english: In these lay a great multitude of those who were sick, blind, lame, or paralyzed, waiting for the moving of the water;

kikuyu: Rĩrĩa ngwĩciiria ũrĩrĩ wakwa nĩguo ũkũũhooreria, na atĩ gĩtanda gĩakwa no kĩĩniinĩre gũteta-rĩ,
english: When I say, ‘My bed will comfort me. My couch will ease my complaint,’

kikuyu: O na rĩrĩ, o nĩmoonire atĩ niĩ nĩndehokeirwo wĩra wa kũhunjia Ũhoro-ũrĩa-Mwega kũrĩ andũ-a-Ndũrĩrĩ, o ta ũrĩa Petero eehokeirwo wĩra wa kũhunjĩria Ayahudi.
english: but to the contrary, when they saw that I had been entrusted with the Good News for the uncircumcised, even as Peter with the Good News for the circumcised—

kikuyu: na atĩ o ũmwe wanyu amenye gwatha mwĩrĩ wake mwene ũikarage ũrĩ mũtheru na akĩũtĩĩaga,
english: that each one of you know how to control his own body† in sanctification and honor,

kikuyu: Nĩngũmũrathima, na ti-itherũ nĩngũkũhe mwana wa kahĩĩ ũciarĩirwo nĩwe. Nĩngũmũrathima nĩguo atuĩke nyina wa ndũrĩrĩ; athamaki a ndũrĩrĩ makoima harĩwe.”
english: I will bless her, and moreover I will give you a son by her. Yes, I will bless her, and she will be a mother of nations. Kings of peoples will come from her.”

kikuyu: Githĩ wĩtigĩri Ngai waku tiguo wagĩrĩirwo gũtuĩka ũũmĩrĩru waku, nayo mĩthiĩre yaku ĩtarĩ ũcuuke-rĩ, githĩ tiyo mwĩhoko waku?
english: Isn’t your piety your confidence? Isn’t the integrity of your ways your hope?

kikuyu: “Wee Ngai wakwa, nĩũguũrĩirie ndungata yaku atĩ nĩũkamĩakĩra nyũmba. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio ndungata yaku nĩĩgĩĩte na ũmĩrĩru wa gũkũhooya.
english: For you, my God, have revealed to your servant that you will build him a house. Therefore your servant has found courage to pray before you.

kikuyu: Nao makĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Mũndũ ũcio arĩ ha?” Nake akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Niĩ ndiũũĩ.” Afarisai Gũtuĩria Ũhoro wa Kũhonio kwa Mũtumumu Ũcio
english: Then they asked him, “Where is he?” He said, “I don’t know.”

kikuyu: no makoragwo marĩ na ciĩko njega, iria ciagĩrĩire andũ-a-nja arĩa moimbũraga atĩ nĩ ahooi Ngai.
english: but with good works, which is appropriate for women professing godliness.

kikuyu: a ũhoro ũkoniĩ bũrũri wa Moabi: Jehova Mwene-Hinya-Wothe, Ngai wa Isiraeli ekuuga atĩrĩ: “Wũi-ĩiya, nĩ ũndũ Nebo nĩgũkwanangwo-ĩ. Kiriathaimu nĩgũgaconorithio na gũtunyanwo; kĩĩgitĩro kĩrĩa kĩrũmu nĩgĩgaconorithio na kĩharaganio.
english: f Moab. Yahweh of Armies, the God of Israel, says: “Woe to Nebo! For it is laid waste. Kiriathaim is disappointed. It is taken. Misgab† is put to shame

kikuyu: ningĩ matikae gũcambia mũndũ o na ũrĩkũ, no makorwo marĩ a thayũ na acaayanĩri, na monanagie mĩtugo ya ũhooreri harĩ andũ othe.
english: to speak evil of no one, not to be contentious, to be gentle, showing all humility toward all men.

kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa ũhaandaga maũndũ ma gũkenia merirĩria ma mwĩrĩ-rĩ, ũcio akaagetha kwanangĩka kũrĩa kuumanaga na maũndũ macio. No ũrĩa ũhaandaga maũndũ ma gũkenia Roho-rĩ, ũcio akaagetha muoyo wa tene na tene ũrĩa uumanaga na Roho.
english: For he who sows to his own flesh will from the flesh reap corruption. But he who sows to the Spirit will from the Spirit reap eternal life.

kikuyu: Githĩ Hezekia we mwene ndeheririe kũndũ kũrĩa gũtũũgĩru kwa ngai ĩyo, o na akĩeheria igongona ciayo, na akĩĩra Juda na Jerusalemu atĩrĩ, ‘No nginya mũhooere kĩgongona-inĩ kĩmwe na mũcinagĩre magongona ho’?
english: Hasn’t the same Hezekiah taken away his high places and his altars, and commanded Judah and Jerusalem, saying, ‘You shall worship before one altar, and you shall burn incense on it’?

kikuyu: “Wee na ariũ aku-rĩ, mũtikananyue ndibei kana kĩndũ o gĩothe kĩngĩtũma mũndũ arĩĩo rĩrĩa rĩothe mũrĩtoonyaga Hema-ya-Gũtũnganwo, nĩguo mũtigakue. Ũndũ ũcio nĩ ũtuĩke watho mwandĩke wa gũtũũra nginya njiarwa-inĩ iria igooka.
english: “You and your sons are not to drink wine or strong drink whenever you go into the Tent of Meeting, or you will die. This shall be a statute forever throughout your generations.

kikuyu: Tondũ inyuĩ mũkĩrĩ oogĩ-rĩ,
english: For you bear with the foolish gladly, being wise.

kikuyu: “ ‘Nĩ ũndũ ũcio, kwambĩrĩria mũthenya wa ikũmi na ĩtano wa mweri wa mũgwanja, mwarĩkia kũgetha irio bũrũri-inĩ, nĩ mũgaakũngũĩra gĩathĩ kĩu kĩa Jehova mĩthenya mũgwanja. Mũthenya wa mbere nĩ wa kũhurũka, na mũthenya wa ĩnana o naguo no wa kũhurũka.
english: “ ‘So on the fifteenth day of the seventh month, when you have gathered in the fruits of the land, you shall keep the feast of Yahweh seven days. On the first day shall be a solemn rest, and on the eighth day shall be a solemn rest.

kikuyu: Arĩhaga mũndũ kũringana na ũrĩa ekĩte; akamũrehere o kĩrĩa kĩringaine na mĩthiĩre yake.
english: For the work of a man he will give to him, and cause every man to find according to his ways.

kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa ũheaga athĩĩni ndarĩ kĩndũ akaaga, no mũndũ ũrĩa ũmahingagĩra maitho nĩarĩĩrumagwo kaingĩ.
english: When the wicked rise, men hide themselves; but when they perish, the righteous thrive.

kikuyu: Ciana iria ciaciarĩirwo bũrũri ũrĩa watahĩirwo, ikoiga ũkĩiguaga atĩrĩ, ‘Bũrũri ũyũ nĩ mũnini, ndũngĩtũigana, tũhe kũndũ kũngĩ gwa gũtũũra.’
english: The children of your bereavement will say in your ears, ‘This place is too small for me. Give me a place to live in.’

kikuyu: “Atĩrĩrĩ, wee itũũra rĩĩrĩ inene inyamarie, o wee ũthukathukĩtwo nĩ ihuhũkanio, na ũkaaga wa gũkũhooreria, nĩngagwaka na mahiga ma rangi wa mĩthemba mĩingĩ, nayo mĩthingi yaku ndĩmĩake na mahiga ma yakuti.
english: “You afflicted, tossed with storms, and not comforted, behold, I will set your stones in beautiful colors, and lay your foundations with sapphires.

kikuyu: Jehova anyiitĩte gĩkombe kĩiyũrĩte ndibei ĩrĩ na mũhũũyũ, ĩtukanĩtio na mahuti manungi wega; amĩonoragia, nao andũ othe aaganu a thĩ makamĩnyua, makainĩkĩrĩra nginya itata rĩa mũthia.
english: For in Yahweh’s hand there is a cup, full of foaming wine mixed with spices. He pours it out. Indeed the wicked of the earth drink and drink it to its very dregs.

kikuyu: andũ arĩa Jehova arathimĩte nĩmakagaya bũrũri, no arĩa arumĩte nĩmakaniinwo.
english: For such as are blessed by him shall inherit the land. Those who are cursed by him shall be cut off.

kikuyu: na Kaini, na Gibea, na Timina; mothe maarĩ matũũra ikũmi na tũtũũra twamo.
english: Kain, Gibeah, and Timnah; ten cities with their villages.

kikuyu: “Ĩra andũ a Isiraeli atĩrĩ, ‘Mũndũ o wothe ũngĩrehere Jehova iruta rĩa ũiguano, akaarehe gĩcunjĩ kĩarĩo kĩrĩ igongona rĩake mwene kũrĩ Jehova.
english: “Speak to the children of Israel, saying, ‘He who offers the sacrifice of his peace offerings to Yahweh shall bring his offering to Yahweh out of the sacrifice of his peace offerings.

kikuyu: Tondũ ũcio Balamu agĩcooka kũrĩ Balaki na akĩmũkora arũngiĩ hakuhĩ na iruta rĩake, marĩ hamwe na anene a Moabi. Balaki akĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Jehova oiga atĩa?”
english: He came to him, and behold, he was standing by his burnt offering, and the princes of Moab with him. Balak said to him, “What has Yahweh spoken?”

kikuyu: Na ningĩ ngĩona arĩa akuũ, anene na arĩa anini, marũgamĩte mbere ya gĩtĩ kĩu kĩa ũnene, namo mabuku makĩhingũrwo. Narĩo ibuku rĩngĩ rĩkĩhingũrwo, na nĩrĩo ibuku rĩa muoyo. Nao andũ arĩa akuũ magĩtuĩrwo ciira kũringana na ciĩko iria meekĩte, o ta ũrĩa kwaandĩkĩtwo mabuku-inĩ macio.
english: I saw the dead, the great and the small, standing before the throne, and they opened books. Another book was opened, which is the book of life. The dead were judged out of the things which were written in the books, according to their works.

kikuyu: Nake akĩũria Solomoni atĩrĩ, “Mũrũ wa baba, nĩ matũũra matariĩ atĩa maya ũũheete?” Akĩmatua Bũrũri wa Kabuli, na noguo metagwo o na ũmũthĩ.
english: He said, “What cities are these which you have given me, my brother?” He called them the land of Cabul† to this day.

kikuyu: “Ithe akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, ‘Mũrũ wakwa, wee tũtũũraga nawe hĩndĩ ciothe, na indo ciothe iria ndĩ nacio nĩ ciaku.
english: “He said to him, ‘Son, you are always with me, and all that is mine is yours.

kikuyu: Andũ acio nĩmacaririe maandĩko ma nyũmba ciao, no matiigana kũmona, nĩ ũndũ ũcio makĩeherio thiritũ-inĩ ya athĩnjĩri-Ngai, na magĩtuuo ta andũ maarĩ na thaahu.
english: These sought their place among those who were registered by genealogy, but they were not found; therefore they were deemed disqualified and removed from the priesthood.

kikuyu: kĩyo kĩrĩa ndĩ nakĩo kĩa nyũmba yaku nĩkĩo kĩrandĩa, irumi cia arĩa makũrumaga nĩ niĩ igũĩrĩire.
english: For the zeal of your house consumes me. The reproaches of those who reproach you have fallen on me.

kikuyu: No mbere ya maũndũ mothe-rĩ, no nginya mũmenye atĩ gũtirĩ ũhoro wanarathwo Maandĩko-inĩ Matheru ũngĩtaũrwo nĩ mũnabii na ũtaũri wake mwene.
english: knowing this first, that no prophecy of Scripture is of private interpretation.

kikuyu: Nao maarĩ na mĩthemba yothe ya nyamũ cia gĩthaka, o nyamũ kũringana na mũthemba wayo, na mahiũ mothe kũringana na mĩthemba yamo, na ciũmbe ciothe iria ithiiagĩra thĩ kũringana na mĩthemba yacio, na nyoni ciothe kũringana na mĩthemba yacio, na kĩndũ gĩothe kĩrĩ mathagu.
english: they, and every animal after its kind, all the livestock after their kind, every creeping thing that creeps on the earth after its kind, and every bird after its kind, every bird of every sort.

kikuyu: No angĩciara mwana wa kairĩtu, agaaikara arĩ na thaahu ciumia igĩrĩ, o ta ũrĩa aikaraga arĩ na thaahu ihinda rĩake rĩa mweri. Ningĩ no nginya eterere mĩthenya mĩrongo ĩtandatũ na ĩtandatũ nĩguo atherio thaahu wa kuura thakame.
english: But if she bears a female child, then she shall be unclean two weeks, as in her period; and she shall continue in the blood of purification sixty-six days.

kikuyu: niĩ na niĩ nĩngamũũkĩrĩra na marakara na niĩ mwene ngĩrĩrĩrie kũmũherithia maita mangĩ mũgwanja nĩ ũndũ wa mehia manyu.
english: then I will walk contrary to you in wrath. I will also chastise you seven times for your sins.

kikuyu: Kĩambĩrĩria kĩa wathani wa Ahasuerusu, ataarani acio magĩthitanga andũ a Juda na Jerusalemu.
english: In the reign of Ahasuerus, in the beginning of his reign, they wrote an accusation against the inhabitants of Judah and Jerusalem.

kikuyu: O acio nĩo Ngai oonire arĩ wega kũmenyithia hitho ya ũtonga ũrĩa ũrĩ riiri kũrĩ andũ-a-Ndũrĩrĩ, nayo hitho ĩyo nĩ atĩ Kristũ arĩ thĩinĩ wanyu, o we kĩĩrĩgĩrĩro gĩa gũkaagĩa na riiri.
english: to whom God was pleased to make known what are the riches of the glory of this mystery among the Gentiles, which is Christ in you, the hope of glory.

kikuyu: “We ti mũndũ ta niĩ atĩ nĩguo ndĩmũcookerie, nĩguo tũngʼethanĩre igooti-inĩ.
english: For he is not a man, as I am, that I should answer him, that we should come together in judgment.

kikuyu: Nao matiarĩ na twana tondũ Elizabethi aarĩ thaata; na eerĩ maarĩ akũrũ mũno.
english: But they had no child, because Elizabeth was barren, and they both were well advanced in years.

kikuyu: Mwahota gũkeĩĩra atĩrĩ, “Ũhoti witũ na hinya wa moko maitũ nĩcio ituoneire ũtonga ũyũ.”
english: and lest you say in your heart, “My power and the might of my hand has gotten me this wealth.”

kikuyu: Mwathani Jehova ekuuga atĩrĩ: Ti-itherũ o ta ũrĩa niĩ ndũũraga muoyo, tondũ rũũru rwakwa nĩ rwagĩte mũrĩithi, na nĩrũtahĩtwo rũgatuĩka irio cia nyamũ ciothe cia gĩthaka, na ningĩ tondũ arĩithi akwa matiigana gũcaria rũũru rwakwa, no nĩo ene meemenyereire handũ ha kũmenyerera rũũru rwakwa-rĩ,
english: “As I live,” says the Lord Yahweh, “surely because my sheep became a prey, and my sheep became food to all the animals of the field, because there was no shepherd, and my shepherds didn’t search for my sheep, but the shepherds fed themselves, and didn’t feed my sheep,

kikuyu: Arangĩri acio magĩcookia atĩrĩ, “Gũtirĩ mũndũ o na ũmwe ũrĩ waria ta ũrĩa mũndũ ũcio aragia.”
english: Which of you convicts me of sin? If I tell the truth, why do you not believe me?

kikuyu: Naguo Ũhoro-ũyũ-Mwega wa ũthamaki nĩũkahunjio thĩ yothe ũtuĩke ũira kũrĩ ndũrĩrĩ ciothe, na thuutha ũcio ithirĩro rĩkinye.
english: This Good News of the Kingdom will be preached in the whole world for a testimony to all the nations, and then the end will come.

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ wa ũrĩa watho wa mũthamaki warĩ wa na ihenya, atwari a marũa makiumagara o narua tondũ watho ũcio waheanirwo kuuma thĩinĩ wa nyũmba ya ũthamaki kũu gĩikaro kĩrĩa kĩarĩ kĩirigĩre gĩa Shushani. Mũthamaki na Hamani magĩikara thĩ kũnyua, no itũũra rĩu inene rĩa Shushani rĩkĩgagaya mũno.
english: The couriers went out in haste by the king’s commandment, and the decree was given out in the citadel of Susa. The king and Haman sat down to drink; but the city of Susa was perplexed.

kikuyu: Nĩamũtongoririe mũgĩtũĩkanĩria werũ ũcio mũnene na wa kũmakania, bũrũri ũcio mũngʼaru ũtarĩ maaĩ, na warĩ na nyoka njũru, o na tũngʼaurũ. Nĩamũrutĩire maaĩ ihiga-inĩ rĩa nyaigĩ.
english: who led you through the great and terrible wilderness, with venomous snakes and scorpions, and thirsty ground where there was no water; who poured water for you out of the rock of flint;

kikuyu: ‘Kuuma mũthenya ũrĩa ndaarutire andũ akwa a Isiraeli kuuma Misiri, ndirĩ ndathuura itũũra inene thĩinĩ wa mũhĩrĩga o na ũmwe wa Isiraeli, atĩ nĩguo njakĩrwo hekarũ ĩĩtanĩtio na Rĩĩtwa rĩakwa kuo, no nĩndathuurire Daudi athamakĩre andũ akwa Isiraeli.’
english: ‘Since the day that I brought my people Israel out of Egypt, I chose no city out of all the tribes of Israel to build a house, that my name might be there; but I chose David to be over my people Israel.’

kikuyu: Musa agĩtoonyia kĩgongona gĩa thahabu thĩinĩ wa Hema-ya-Gũtũnganwo hau mbere ya gĩtam- baya gĩa gũcuurio,
english: He put the golden altar in the Tent of Meeting before the veil;

kikuyu: No Maandĩko moigaga atĩ andũ a thĩ nĩ mohetwo nĩ mehia, nĩgeetha kĩĩranĩro kĩrĩa kĩheanagwo nĩ ũndũ wa gwĩtĩkia Jesũ Kristũ kĩheagwo o arĩa metĩkĩtie.
english: But the Scripture imprisoned all things under sin, that the promise by faith in Jesus Christ might be given to those who believe.

kikuyu: “Nowe agĩcookia atĩrĩ, ‘Ngũmwĩra atĩrĩ na ma, niĩ ndimũũĩ.’
english: But he answered, ‘Most certainly I tell you, I don’t know you.’

kikuyu: Nake mũndũ ũcio akiumagara agĩthiĩ, na aakinya, akĩĩra Daudi maũndũ mothe marĩa Joabu aamũtũmĩte akoige.
english: So the messenger went, and came and showed David all that Joab had sent him for.

kikuyu: Nake akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Ikiai wabu mwena wa ũrĩo wa marikabu na nĩmũkũgwatia.” Rĩrĩa meekire ũguo, makĩremwo nĩ kũguucia wabu ũcio nĩ ũrĩa thamaki icio ciarĩ nyingĩ.
english: He said to them, “Cast the net on the right side of the boat, and you will find some.” They cast it therefore, and now they weren’t able to draw it in for the multitude of fish.

kikuyu: Jehoashu akĩhurũka hamwe na maithe make, agĩthikwo Samaria hamwe na athamaki a Isiraeli. Nake mũriũ Jeroboamu agĩtuĩka mũthamaki ithenya rĩake.
english: Jehoash slept with his fathers, and was buried in Samaria with the kings of Israel; and Jeroboam his son reigned in his place.

kikuyu: Mwarĩ wa nyũkwa ũrĩa mũkũrũ aarĩ Samaria ũrĩa watũũraga mwena waku wa gathigathini marĩ na airĩtu ake; nake mwarĩ wa nyũkwa ũrĩa mũnini aarĩ Sodomu, ũrĩa watũũragamwena waku wa gũthini hamwena airĩtu ake.
english: Your elder sister is Samaria, who dwells at your left hand, she and her daughters; and your younger sister, who dwells at your right hand, is Sodom with her daughters.

kikuyu: Nake Jesũ akĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Ngũkwĩra na ma, ũmũthĩ nĩũrĩkorwo hamwe na niĩ ikenero.” Gũkua kwa Jesũ
english: Jesus said to him, “Assuredly I tell you, today you will be with me in Paradise.”

kikuyu: Nĩakarehera mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai irĩhi, ũguo nĩ kuuga arute kĩndũ gĩake gĩa kũrutĩrwo Jehova nĩ ũndũ wa mahĩtia make, arute ndũrũme kuuma rũũru-inĩ, ndũrũme ĩtarĩ na kaũũgũ na ya thogora ũrĩa wagĩrĩire.
english: He shall bring his trespass offering to Yahweh: a ram without defect from the flock, according to your estimation, for a trespass offering, to the priest.

kikuyu: Thĩinĩ wa mabũrũri mothe kũrĩa marũa na watho wa mũthamaki wakinyaga kwagĩaga na gũcakaya kũnene, na kwĩhinga kũrĩa irio, na kũrĩra na kũgirĩka kũnene kũrĩ Ayahudi. Aingĩ ao magĩkoma thĩ mehumbĩte nguo cia ikũnia na mehurĩirie mũhu.
english: In every province, wherever the king’s commandment and his decree came, there was great mourning among the Jews, and fasting, and weeping, and wailing; and many lay in sackcloth and ashes.

kikuyu: Niĩ nĩ niĩ ndaarutire andũ a Efiraimu gwĩtwara na magũrũ,
english: Yet I taught Ephraim to walk. I took them by their arms, but they didn’t know that I healed them.

kikuyu: Andũ nĩmagatũũrania hamwe kũu Juda o na matũũra-inĩ makuo mothe, arĩmi o na andũ arĩa Morũũraga na ndũũru ciao cia mbũri.
english: Judah and all its cities will dwell therein together, the farmers, and those who go about with flocks.

kikuyu: Ngĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Wee-rĩ, wathiĩ kũ?” Nake akĩnjookeria atĩrĩ, “Ndathiĩ gũthima itũũra rĩa Jerusalemu, nĩguo menye wariĩ o na ũraihu warĩo.”
english: 2 1 I lifted up my eyes, and saw, and behold, a man with a measuring line in his hand. 2 Then I asked, “Where are you going?” He said to me, “To measure Jerusalem, to see what is its width and what is its length.”

kikuyu: Mũhaka wao wa mwena wa gũthini wambĩrĩire gĩcongoco-inĩ kĩa mwena wa gũthini kĩa Iria rĩa Cumbĩ,
english: Their south border was from the uttermost part of the Salt Sea, from the bay that looks southward;

kikuyu: nduĩkĩte ta mũndũ ũtaiguaga, ũrĩa kanua gake gatarĩ ũndũ kangĩhota gũcookia.
english: Yes, I am as a man who doesn’t hear, in whose mouth are no reproofs.

kikuyu: Ndungata cia hekarũ ciatũũraga kĩrĩma-inĩ kĩa Ofeli, marũgamĩrĩirwo nĩ Ziha na Gishipa.
english: But the temple servants lived in Ophel; and Ziha and Gishpa were over the temple servants.

kikuyu: Ta thikĩrĩriai, inyuĩ andũ a ngoro nyũmũ, o inyuĩ mũraihanĩrĩirie na ũhoro wa ũthingu.
english: Listen to me, you stubborn-hearted, who are far from righteousness!

kikuyu: Nake Shafani ũcio mwandĩki akĩĩra mũthamaki atĩrĩ, “Hilikia ũrĩa mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai nĩanengera ibuku.” Nake Shafani agĩthoma ibuku rĩu arĩ mbere ya mũthamaki.
english: Shaphan the scribe told the king, saying, “Hilkiah the priest has delivered me a book.” Shaphan read from it to the king.

kikuyu: Ũhoro ũcio Daudi oigire nĩwaiguirwo, ũkĩmenyithio Saũlũ, nake Saũlũ agĩtũmana ageetwo. * 17:4 Goliathũ aarĩ wa rũciaro rwa Anaki, tondũ Joshua ndaamaniinire othe. † 17:4 nĩ ta mita 3 ‡ 17:5 nĩ ta kilo 57 § 17:7
english: When the words were heard which David spoke, they rehearsed them before Saul; and he sent for him.

kikuyu: nĩgũkorwo Jehova nĩwe ũgaatuĩka mwĩhoko waku, na amenyagĩrĩre kũgũrũ gwaku gũtikanyiitwo nĩ mũtego.
english: for Yahweh will be your confidence, and will keep your foot from being taken.

kikuyu: oirĩirie andũ mana ta mbura marĩe, na akĩmahe ngano ya kũu igũrũ.
english: He rained down manna on them to eat, and gave them food from the sky.

kikuyu: Maigua ũguo makĩgeria kũmũnyiita, no gũtirĩ o na ũmwe wamũhutirie, tondũ ihinda rĩake rĩtiarĩ ikinyu.
english: They sought therefore to take him; but no one laid a hand on him, because his hour had not yet come.

kikuyu: Jeroboamu nĩaakire mahooero kũndũ kũrĩa gũtũũgĩru, na agĩthuura athĩnjĩri-ngai a kuuma kũrĩ andũ a mĩthemba yothe, o na gũtuĩka matiarĩ Alawii.*
english: He made houses of high places, and made priests from among all the people, who were not of the sons of Levi.

kikuyu: Mwathani Jehova ekuuga atĩrĩ, Niĩ mwene nĩ niĩ ngerĩithagĩria ngʼondu ciakwa, na ndũme ciarahage kũu.
english: I myself will be the shepherd of my sheep, and I will cause them to lie down,” says the Lord Yahweh.

kikuyu: Rĩrĩa Jakubu aiguire atĩ mwarĩ Dina nĩarĩkĩtie gũthũkio-rĩ, na tondũ ariũ ake maarĩ gĩthaka-inĩ na mahiũ make, agĩkira na ũhoro ũcio o nginya rĩrĩa mainũkire.
english: Now Jacob heard that he had defiled Dinah, his daughter; and his sons were with his livestock in the field. Jacob held his peace until they came.

kikuyu: Nake mũraika wa Jehova agĩatha Gadi eere Daudi aambate athiĩ agaakĩre Jehova kĩgongona o kũu kĩhuhĩro-inĩ kĩa ngano kĩa Arauna ũrĩa Mũjebusi.
english: Then Yahweh’s angel commanded Gad to tell David that David should go up and raise an altar to Yahweh on the threshing floor of Ornan the Jebusite.

kikuyu: Nao magĩũkĩra o hĩndĩ ĩyo magĩcooka Jerusalemu. Kũu magĩkora arutwo arĩa ikũmi na ũmwe, hamwe na arĩa maarĩ nao monganĩte,
english: They rose up that very hour, returned to Jerusalem, and found the eleven gathered together, and those who were with them,

kikuyu: “Njiguĩrai tha, inyuĩ arata akwa, iguai tha, nĩgũkorwo guoko kwa Ngai nĩkũngũthĩte.
english: “Have pity on me. Have pity on me, you my friends, for the hand of God has touched me.
