kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, iguai ndũmĩrĩri ya Jehova, inyuĩAyahudiothearĩamũtũũragabũrũriwaMisiri: Jehovaekuugaatĩrĩ, ‘Nĩndĩhĩtĩtenarĩĩtwarĩakwa inene, ngoiga gũtirĩ mũndũ o na ũmwe wa kuuma Juda ũtũũraga handũ o hothe thĩinĩ wa bũrũri wa Misiri ũgaacooka kũgweta rĩĩtwa rĩakwa kana ehĩte akiugaga atĩrĩ, “Ti-itherũ o ta ũrĩa Mwathani Jehova atũũraga muoyo.”
english: “Therefore hear Yahweh’s word, all Judah who dwell in the land of Egypt: ‘Behold, I have sworn by my great name,’ says Yahweh, ‘that my name will no more be named in the mouth of any man of Judah in all the land of Egypt, saying, “As the Lord Yahweh lives.”

kikuyu: Rĩrĩa akerubi acio marũgama, o namo makarũgama o ũguo; na rĩrĩa akerubi mombũka, namo makombũkania nao, tondũ roho wa ciũmbe icio irĩ muoyo warĩ thĩinĩ wamo.
english: When they stood, these stood. When they mounted up, these mounted up with them; for the spirit of the living creature was in them.

kikuyu: Nao makĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Ũkwenda tũgacihaarĩrie kũ?”
english: They said to him, “Where do you want us to prepare?”

kikuyu: Nĩanjirigĩire na rũthingo nĩguo ndikae kũũra; mĩnyororo yakwa nĩamĩritũhĩtie mũno.
english: He has walled me about, so that I can’t go out. He has made my chain heavy.

kikuyu: Rehoboamu nĩathuurire Abija mũrũ wa Maaka atuĩke mũriũ mũnene wa ariũ a ithe acio angĩ nĩgeetha akaamũtua mũthamaki.
english: Rehoboam appointed Abijah the son of Maacah to be chief, the prince among his brothers, for he intended to make him king.

kikuyu: Matukũ-inĩ marĩa Jesũ aatũũraga gũkũ thĩ-rĩ, nĩahooyaga na agathaitha Ngai, agĩkayaga mũno agĩitaga maithori kũrĩ ũcio ũngĩahotire kũmũhonokia kuuma gĩkuũ-inĩ, na nĩaiguagwo nĩ ũndũ wa kwĩnyiihia gwake.
english: He, in the days of his flesh, having offered up prayers and petitions with strong crying and tears to him who was able to save him from death, and having been heard for his godly fear,

kikuyu: Nake Daudi akĩiga ũhoro ũcio ngoro-inĩ na agĩĩtigĩra Akishi mũthamaki wa Gathu mũno.
english: David laid up these words in his heart, and was very afraid of Achish the king of Gath.

kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, mũndũ ũrĩa mwaganu Ngai ekũmũũria atĩrĩ: “Ũrĩ na kĩhooto kĩrĩkũ gĩa kuuga mawatho makwa, kana kwaria ũhoro wa kĩrĩkanĩro gĩakwa?
english: But to the wicked God says, “What right do you have to declare my statutes, that you have taken my covenant on your lips,

kikuyu: Tũũrai mũrũmĩtie kĩrĩra kĩa watho wakwa wa kũrũmĩrĩrwo, na mawatho makwa, nĩgũkorwo mũndũ ũrĩa ũrĩĩmathĩkagĩra nĩarĩtũũrio muoyo nĩmo. Niĩ nĩ niĩ Jehova.
english: You shall therefore keep my statutes and my ordinances, which if a man does, he shall live in them. I am Yahweh.

kikuyu: Musa na Harũni magĩcookio kũrĩ Firaũni. Nake akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Thiĩi mũkahooe Jehova Ngai wanyu; no rĩrĩ, nĩ arĩkũ megũthiĩ?”
english: Moses and Aaron were brought again to Pharaoh, and he said to them, “Go, serve Yahweh your God; but who are those who will go?”

kikuyu: Mũthenya wa kana makĩũngana Gĩtuamba-inĩ kĩa Beraka, kũrĩa maagooceire Jehova. Kĩu nĩkĩo gĩtũmaga gwĩtwo Gĩtuamba kĩa Beraka nginya ũmũthĩ.
english: On the fourth day, they assembled themselves in Beracah§ Valley, for there they blessed Yahweh. Therefore the name of that place was called “Beracah Valley” to this day.

kikuyu: Agĩcooka agĩikũrũka, agĩthiĩ Kaperinaumu, itũũra rĩarĩ Galili, na mũthenya wa Thabatũ akĩambĩrĩria kũruta andũ.
english: He came down to Capernaum, a city of Galilee. He was teaching them on the Sabbath day,

kikuyu: Ũrimũ ũkenagia mũndũ ũrĩa ũtooĩ gũkũũrana maũndũ, no mũndũ ũrĩa mũkuũku nĩarũngagĩrĩria mĩthiĩre yake.
english: Folly is joy to one who is void of wisdom, but a man of understanding keeps his way straight.

kikuyu: Agĩcooka agĩtura makerubi meerĩ ma thahabu akĩmekĩra mĩthia- inĩ ya gĩtĩ kĩu gĩa tha.
english: He made two cherubim of gold. He made them of beaten work, at the two ends of the mercy seat:

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio, nĩ kwagĩrĩire mĩhaano ya indo iria irĩ igũrũ ĩtheragio na magongona macio, no indo cia igũrũ cio nyene itheragio na magongona mega kũrĩ macio.
english: It was necessary therefore that the copies of the things in the heavens should be cleansed with these, but the heavenly things themselves with better sacrifices than these.

kikuyu: Kuuma hĩndĩ ĩyo Jesũ akĩambĩrĩria kũhunjia ũhoro akiugaga atĩrĩ, “Mwĩrirei, nĩgũkorwo ũthamaki wa igũrũ nĩũkuhĩrĩirie.” Jesũ gwĩta Arutwo ake a Mbere
english: From that time, Jesus began to preach, and to say, “Repent! For the Kingdom of Heaven is at hand.”

kikuyu: Nake Simoni Petero akĩheneria mũrutwo ũcio, akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Ta mũũrie araaria ũhoro wa ũ.”
english: Simon Peter therefore beckoned to him, and said to him, “Tell us who it is of whom he speaks.”

kikuyu: Musa akĩrakarĩra anene a mbũtũ icio cia ita na atongoria a mbũtũ cia o ngiri, ngiri, na atongoria a mbũtũ cia o igana, igana, arĩa moimĩte mbaara-inĩ.
english: Moses was angry with the officers of the army, the captains of thousands and the captains of hundreds, who came from the service of the war.

kikuyu: nĩathuurirwo na njĩra ya gũcuukĩrwo mĩtĩ, kũringana na mũtugo wa ũthĩnjĩri-Ngai, athiĩ thĩinĩ wa hekarũ ya Mwathani agacine ũbumba.
english: according to the custom of the priest’s office, his lot was to enter into the temple of the Lord and burn incense.

kikuyu: “Mũndũ ũrĩa ũtarĩ hamwe na niĩ, ũcio nĩ thũ yakwa, na ũrĩa ũtacookanagĩrĩria hamwe na niĩ, ũcio nĩ kũhurunja ahurunjaga.
english: “He who is not with me is against me. He who doesn’t gather with me scatters.

kikuyu: Nake Ayubu aigua-ũhoro ũcio, akĩrũgama, agĩtembũranga nguo yake ya igũrũ, na akĩenjwo mũtwe. Agĩcooka akĩĩgũithia thĩ, akĩhooya Ngai,
english: Then Job arose, and tore his robe, and shaved his head, and fell down on the ground, and worshiped.

kikuyu: mũnene wa mũhĩrĩga wa Zebuluni aarĩ Ishimaia mũrũ wa Obadia; mũnene wa mũhĩrĩga wa Nafitali aarĩ Jeremothu mũrũ wa Azirieli;
english: of Zebulun, Ishmaiah the son of Obadiah; of Naphtali, Jeremoth the son of Azriel;

kikuyu: Ririkana kũrĩa uumĩte ũkagũa! Wĩrire ũcooke wĩke maũndũ marĩa wekaga mbere. Waga kwĩrira, nĩngũũka kũrĩ we na njeherie mũtĩ waku wa kũigĩrĩra tawa kuuma handũ haguo.
english: Remember therefore from where you have fallen, and repent and do the first works; or else I am coming to you swiftly, and will move your lamp stand out of its place, unless you repent.

kikuyu: Bũrũri ũcio ũrĩ ndwere-inĩ cia iria, o kũu Akerethi matũũraga, gũgaatuĩka kũndũ gwa gwakwo ithũnũ cia arĩithi, na ciugũ cia ngʼondu.
english: The sea coast will be pastures, with cottages for shepherds and folds for flocks.

kikuyu: O na korwo ndihĩtĩtie-rĩ, ndingĩhota kũmũcookeria ũndũ; ũrĩa ingĩĩka no gũthaitha ingĩthaitha Mũnjiirithia anjiguĩre tha.
english: Though I were righteous, yet I wouldn’t answer him. I would make supplication to my judge.

kikuyu: “Tuĩkanĩriai mĩgũnda yao ya mĩthabibũ mũmĩharaganie, no mũtikamĩanange biũ. Hũrũrai honge ciayo, nĩgũkorwo andũ aya ti a Jehova.
english: “Go up on her walls, and destroy, but don’t make a full end. Take away her branches, for they are not Yahweh’s.

kikuyu: Joabu mũrũ wa Zeruia na andũ a Daudi makiumagara, magĩtũngana nao karia- inĩ ka Gibeoni. Gĩkundi kĩmwe gĩgĩikara thĩ mwena ũmwe wa Karia kau na gĩkundi kĩu kĩngĩ gĩgĩikara mwena ũcio ũngĩ.
english: Joab the son of Zeruiah and David’s servants went out, and met them by the pool of Gibeon; and they sat down, the one on the one side of the pool and the other on the other side of the pool.

kikuyu: “Nĩ ũndũ ũcio nĩngũmũciirithia o rĩngĩ,” ũguo nĩguo Jehova ekuuga. “Ningĩ nĩngaciirithia ciana cia ciana cianyu.
english: “Therefore I will yet contend with you,” says Yahweh, “and I will contend with your children’s children.

kikuyu: 210 Gũcookerithia 21:6 20 Gũthiĩ Mbaara-inĩ 1 Rĩrĩa mũgaathiĩ kũrũa na thũ cianyu na muone irĩ na mbarathi na ngaari cia ita na mbũtũ cia ita nyingĩ gũkĩra cianyu, mũtikanaciĩtigĩre, nĩ ũndũ Jehova Ngai wanyu, ũrĩa wamũrutire bũrũri wa Misiri, agaakorwo hamwe na inyuĩ.
english: hen you go out to battle against your enemies, and see horses, chariots, and a people more numerous than you, you shall not be afraid of them; for Yahweh your God, who brought you up out of the land of Egypt, is with you.

kikuyu: Mũthamaki wa Ashuri agĩtũma mwathi ũrĩa mũnene wa mbũtũ ciothe cia ita, na mũrũgamĩrĩri wa anene arĩa angĩ, na mwathi wa mbũtũ cia mbaara, marĩ na mbũtũ nene ya ita kuuma Lakishi
english: The king of Assyria sent Tartan, Rabsaris, and Rabshakeh from Lachish to King Hezekiah with a great army to Jerusalem. They went up and came to Jerusalem. When they had come up, they came and stood by the conduit of the upper pool, which is in the highway of the fuller’s field.

kikuyu: na nĩamũiyũrĩtie na Roho wa Ngai, na ũũgĩ, na ũhoti, na ũmenyo wa mawĩra mothe ma ũbundi
english: He has filled him with the Spirit of God, in wisdom, in understanding, in knowledge, and in all kinds of workmanship;

kikuyu: Mwene-Hinya-Wothe nĩwe ũ, atĩ nĩguo tũmũtungatagĩre? Tũkũgunĩka nakĩ tũngĩmũhooya?’
english: What is the Almighty, that we should serve him? What profit should we have, if we pray to him?’

kikuyu: naĩkĩambĩrĩriekũhũũrandungata iria ingĩ, na kũrĩa na kũnyua hamwe na arĩĩu.
english: and begins to beat his fellow servants, and eat and drink with the drunkards,

kikuyu: Othe makĩrĩa makĩhũũna, nao arutwo makĩiyũria ciondo ikũmi na igĩrĩ cia cienyũ iria ciatigarire. Petero Kuumbũra Kristũ
english: They ate and were all filled. They gathered up twelve baskets of broken pieces that were left over.

kikuyu: ũtuĩkanĩirie mwena wa gũthini ũrorete Mwanya wa Akirabimu, ũthiĩte na mbere o nginya Zini, na ũikũrũke na mwena wa gũthini wa Kadeshi-Barinea. Ningĩ ũcooke ũkinye Hazoru-Adari, na uumĩrĩre Azimoni,
english: Your border shall turn about southward of the ascent of Akrabbim, and pass along to Zin; and it shall pass southward of Kadesh Barnea; and it shall go from there to Hazar Addar, and pass along to Azmon.

kikuyu: Nĩwamaheire bũrũri ũyũ werĩire maithe mao na mwĩhĩtwa atĩ nĩũkamahe, bũrũri ũrĩ bũthi wa iria na ũũkĩ.
english: and gave them this land, which you swore to their fathers to give them, a land flowing with milk and honey.

kikuyu: Mũtikanaheenio: Ngai ndangĩnyũrũrio. Kĩrĩa mũndũ ahaandaga no kĩo agethaga.
english: Don’t be deceived. God is not mocked, for whatever a man sows, that he will also reap.

kikuyu: No Sara akĩona atĩ mũriũ wa Hagari ũrĩa Mũmisiri aaciarĩire Iburahĩmu aarĩ na kĩnyũrũri.
english: Sarah saw the son of Hagar the Egyptian, whom she had borne to Abraham, mocking.

kikuyu: Ningĩ agĩkoma na mũtumia wake rĩngĩ, nake akĩgĩa nda agĩciara kahĩĩ. Nake agĩgatua Beria, tondũ nĩ gwakoretwo na mũtino thĩinĩ wa nyũmba yake.
english: He went in to his wife, and she conceived and bore a son, and he named him Beriah,† because there was trouble with his house.

kikuyu: mũtigetue anene a andũ arĩa mwĩhokeirwo, no mũtuĩke cionereria njega kũrĩ rũũru rũu.
english: not as lording it over those entrusted to you, but making yourselves examples to the flock.

kikuyu: Ariũ a ithe nĩmamũiguĩrĩire ũiru, no ithe agĩikara agĩĩciiragia ũhoro wa irooto icio. Jusufu kwendio nĩ Ariũ a Ithe
english: His brothers envied him, but his father kept this saying in mind.

kikuyu: Nĩmagĩte kwĩhokeka harĩ Jehova; nao magaciara ciana itarĩ na ũngai. Na rĩrĩ, ciathĩ ciao cia Karũgamo ka Mweri nĩikaamameria, o hamwe na mĩgũnda yao.
english: They are unfaithful to Yahweh; for they have borne illegitimate children. Now the new moon will devour them with their fields.

kikuyu: Ningĩ ndũmĩrĩri ya Jehova nĩyanginyĩrire o rĩngĩ ngĩũrio atĩrĩ: “Nĩ kĩĩ kĩngĩ ũrona?” Na niĩ ngĩcookia atĩrĩ, “Ndĩrona nyũngũ ĩgũtherũka, ĩinamĩte kuuma mwena wa gathigathini.”
english: Yahweh’s word came to me the second time, saying, “What do you see?” I said, “I see a boiling cauldron; and it is tipping away from the north.”

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo gũtirĩ mũndũ ũngĩaka mũthingi ũngĩ tiga ũrĩa ũrĩkĩtie gwakwo, na nĩguo Jesũ Kristũ.
english: For no one can lay any other foundation than that which has been laid, which is Jesus Christ.

kikuyu: Athuuri na andũ othe makĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Tiga kũmũthikĩrĩria, na ndũgetĩkĩre maũndũ macio mothe arenda.”
english: All the elders and all the people said to him, “Don’t listen, and don’t consent.”

kikuyu: Mũthenya ũcio gĩthima nĩgĩgathikũrio nĩ ũndũ wa nyũmba ya Daudi o na nĩ ũndũ wa andũ arĩa matũũraga Jerusalemu, gĩthima gĩa kũmatheria mehia o na thaahu.”
english: In that day there will be a fountain opened to David’s house and to the inhabitants of Jerusalem, for sin and for uncleanness.

kikuyu: Ambatai mũthiĩ bũrũri ũcio ũrĩ bũthi wa iria na ũũkĩ. No niĩ ndigathiĩ na inyuĩ, tondũ mũrĩ andũ momĩtie ngingo na ndahota kũmũniinĩra njĩra-inĩ.”
english: Go to a land flowing with milk and honey; but I will not go up among you, for you are a stiff-necked people, lest I consume you on the way.”

kikuyu: Nao aciarithania acio magĩcookeria Firaũni atĩrĩ, “Atumia a Ahibirania matihaana ta atumia a Misiri; o nĩ ahiũ magĩaga ciana aciarithania matanakinya.”
english: The midwives said to Pharaoh, “Because the Hebrew women aren’t like the Egyptian women; for they are vigorous and give birth before the midwife comes to them.”

kikuyu: thamaki 11:1 375 1 Athamaki 11:25 11 Atumia a Solomoni 1 Mũthamaki Solomoni nĩendire andũ-a-nja aingĩ a kũndũ kũngĩ o hamwe na mwarĩ wa Firaũni, nĩahikirie atumia a Amoabi, na a Amoni, na a Aedomu, na a Asidoni, na a Ahiti.
english: ow King Solomon loved many foreign women, together with the daughter of Pharaoh: women of the Moabites, Ammonites, Edomites, Sidonians, and Hittites,

kikuyu: Ĩtĩkĩra tha ciaku inginyĩre nĩguo ndũũre muoyo, nĩgũkorwo watho waku nĩguo ngenagĩra.
english: Let your tender mercies come to me, that I may live; for your law is my delight.

kikuyu: Anabii nĩ a tũhũ ta rũhuho, nakĩo kiugo kĩa Jehova gĩtirĩ thĩinĩ wao; nĩ ũndũ ũcio-rĩ, ũguo moigĩte nĩguo mageekwo.”
english: The prophets will become wind, and the word is not in them. Thus it will be done to them.”

kikuyu: “Na rĩrĩ, nũũ wanyu ũngĩĩtio mũgate nĩ mwana wake amũhe ihiga?
english: Or who is there among you who, if his son asks him for bread, will give him a stone?

kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, ũndũ ũrĩa tũgwĩka Gibea nĩ ũyũ: Tũkwambata kuo tũmookĩrĩre o ta ũrĩa ũcuuki wa mĩtĩ ũgũtuonia.
english: But now this is the thing which we will do to Gibeah: we will go up against it by lot;

kikuyu: Rĩrĩa Saũlũ aamenyire atĩ Jehova aarĩ hamwe na Daudi, na atĩ mwarĩ Mikali nĩendeete Daudi rĩ,
english: Saul saw and knew that Yahweh was with David; and Michal, Saul’s daughter, loved him.

kikuyu: Hamwe na ariũ arĩa eerĩ a Jusufu arĩa aaciarĩire Misiri, andũ a nyũmba ya Jakubu arĩa maathiire Misiri maarĩ mĩrongo mũgwanja.
english: The sons of Joseph, who were born to him in Egypt, were two souls. All the souls of the house of Jacob, who came into Egypt, were seventy.

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio mbĩrĩra nĩĩnenehetie thuti nĩ ũndũ wao, na ĩgaathamia kanua kayo gatarĩ gĩturi; kũu nĩkuo gũkaaharũrũka andũ ao arĩa marĩ igweta na ikundi cia andũ, hamwe na andũ arĩa manyuuaga makanegena, o na arĩĩu.
english: Therefore Sheol‡ has enlarged its desire, and opened its mouth without measure; and their glory, their multitude, their pomp, and he who rejoices among them, descend into it.

kikuyu: “ ‘Matandĩko mothe marĩa mũndũ ũcio ũroira angĩikarĩra akuuĩtwo nĩ nyamũ, nĩmakanyiitwo nĩ thaahu,
english: “ ‘Whatever saddle he who has the discharge rides on shall be unclean.

kikuyu: Ta rorai muone! Mũndũ ũcio aroka ta matu, ngaari ciake cia ita irooka ta kĩhuhũkanio, nacio mbarathi ciake ihanyũkaga gũkĩra nderi. Ĩiya witũ-ĩ! Tũrĩ aniine biũ.
english: Behold, he will come up as clouds, and his chariots will be as the whirlwind. His horses are swifter than eagles. Woe to us! For we are ruined.

kikuyu: Mũrũgamĩrĩri wa gĩkundi kĩa mweri wa keerĩ aarĩ Dodai ũrĩa Mũahohi; mũtongoria wa gĩkundi gĩake aarĩ Mikilothu. Gĩkundi gĩake kĩarĩ na andũ 24,000.
english: Over the division of the second month was Dodai the Ahohite and his division, and Mikloth the ruler; and in his division were twenty-four thousand.

kikuyu: Hatarĩ nganja gũtingĩaga kũgĩa na ngarari gatagatĩ-inĩ kanyu cia kuonania arĩa anyu metĩkĩrĩkĩte nĩ Ngai.
english: For there also must be factions among you, that those who are approved may be revealed among you.

kikuyu: No matindiri makuo na njabacabi ciakuo itikagĩra; igaatigwo irĩ cia cumbĩ.
english: But its swamps and marshes will not be healed. They will be given up to salt.

kikuyu: Wee nĩwe ũtũhotithagia kũhoota thũ ciitũ; nĩ ũndũ wa rĩĩtwa rĩaku tũrangagĩrĩria arĩa marĩ muku na ithuĩ.
english: Through you, we will push down our adversaries. Through your name, we will tread down those who rise up against us.

kikuyu: Ũtonga wa andũ arĩa oogĩ nĩguo thũmbĩ yao, no ũrimũ wa andũ akĩĩgu ũciaraga ũrimũ.
english: The crown of the wise is their riches, but the folly of fools crowns them with folly.

kikuyu: Ningĩ no nginya athambie nyama cia nda na mathagiro na maaĩ nake mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai acicinĩre ciothe hau kĩgongona-inĩ. Nĩ iruta rĩa njino, iruta rĩa gũcinwo na mwaki, rĩrĩ na mũtararĩko mwega wa gũkenia Jehova.
english: but he shall wash its innards and its legs with water. The priest shall burn all of it on the altar, for a burnt offering, an offering made by fire, of a pleasant aroma to Yahweh.

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo ndagwatha ũmũthĩ wendage Jehova Ngai waku, na ũthiiage na njĩra ciake, na ũmenyagĩrĩre maathani make, na irĩra cia watho wa kũrũmĩrĩrwo, na mawatho; hĩndĩ ĩyo nĩũgatũũra muoyo na ũingĩhe, nake Jehova Ngai waku nĩagakũrathima ũrĩ bũrũri-inĩ ũrĩa ũratoonya ũwĩgwatĩre.
english: For I command you today to love Yahweh your God, to walk in his ways and to keep his commandments, his statutes, and his ordinances, that you may live and multiply, and that Yahweh your God may bless you in the land where you go in to possess it.

kikuyu: Na tondũ ũcio-rĩ, mũndũ ũrĩa waragia na rũthiomi rũgeni nĩahooyage nĩguo ahote gũtaũra ũrĩa aroiga.
english: Therefore let him who speaks in another language pray that he may interpret.

kikuyu: Nake Firaũni agĩtuga Aburamu nĩ ũndũ wa Sarai, na Aburamu akĩgĩa na mbũri na ngʼombe, na ndigiri cia njamba na cia nga, ndungata cia arũme na cia airĩtu, na ngamĩĩra.
english: He dealt well with Abram for her sake. He had sheep, cattle, male donkeys, male servants, female servants, female donkeys, and camels.

kikuyu: “Rĩrĩa andũ anyu magaakũũria atĩrĩ, ‘Kaĩ ũtangĩtwĩra gĩtũmi kĩa maũndũ maya ũreka?’
english: “When the children of your people speak to you, saying, ‘Won’t you show us what you mean by these?’

kikuyu: Ti atĩ nĩ kĩheo ndĩracaria kuuma kũrĩ inyuĩ, no ndĩracaria kĩrĩa kĩngĩigwo mũthiithũ-inĩ wanyu.
english: Not that I seek for the gift, but I seek for the fruit that increases to your account.

kikuyu: Honokia, nĩgũkorwo ndĩ waku, na nĩĩmaathĩte mawatho maku.
english: I am yours. Save me, for I have sought your precepts.

kikuyu: tĩrĩrĩ, Beli nĩĩinamĩrĩire, nayo Nebo ĩkahocerera; mĩhianano yacio ya kũhooywo ĩkuuagwo nĩ nyamũ cia mũtitũ o na ngʼombe. Mĩhianano ĩyo mĩacũhie ĩkuuagwo kũndũ na kũndũ nĩĩtuĩkĩte mĩrigo mĩritũ, mĩrigo ya kũnogia nyamũ iria imĩkuuaga.
english: el bows down. Nebo stoops. Their idols are carried by animals, and on the livestock. The things that you carried around are heavy loads, a burden for the weary.

kikuyu: magĩĩkagĩra arutwo hinya na kũmomĩrĩria nĩgeetha matũũre marũmĩtie wĩtĩkio. Makameeraga atĩrĩ, “No nginya ithuĩ tũgerere mathĩĩna-inĩ maingĩ nĩguo tũtoonye ũthamaki-inĩ wa Ngai.”
english: strengthening the souls of the disciples, exhorting them to continue in the faith, and that through many afflictions we must enter into God’s Kingdom.

kikuyu: Hwaĩ-inĩ aathiiaga agatoonyakuo, narũciinĩagacookamwenaũngĩwanyũmbaĩyoyatũũragwonĩandũ-a-nja, nakuokũu akamenyagĩrĩrwo nĩ Shaashagazu, ndungata ĩrĩa yarĩ hakũre ya mũthamaki, ĩrĩa yehokeirwo thuriya cia mũthamaki. Ndangĩacookire rĩngĩ kũrĩ mũthamaki tiga o mũthamaki akenirio nĩwe amũtũmanĩre na rĩĩtwa rĩake.
english: In the evening she went, and on the next day she returned into the second women’s house, to the custody of Shaashgaz, the king’s eunuch, who kept the concubines. She came in to the king no more, unless the king delighted in her, and she was called by name.

kikuyu: Githĩ ũcio wanyũũmbire kũu nda ya maitũ to we wamoombire? Githĩ tiwe watũthondekire tũrĩ kũu nda cia aa maitũ?
english: Didn’t he who made me in the womb make him? Didn’t one fashion us in the womb?

kikuyu: mũthĩnjĩri- Ngai nĩagaathana areherwo nyoni igĩrĩ irĩ muoyo na itarĩ na thaahu, na kamũtĩ ka mũtarakwa, na rũrigi rwa rangi wa gakarakũ, na mũthobi irehwo nĩ ũndũ wa mũndũ ũcio ũgũtherio.
english: then the priest shall command them to take for him who is to be cleansed two living clean birds, cedar wood, scarlet, and hyssop.

kikuyu: Ariũ a Esaũ arĩa aaciarĩirwo nĩ Oholibama mũtumia wake mwarĩ wa Ana ũrĩa warĩ mwarĩ wa Zibeoni: nĩ Jeushu, na Jalamu, na Kora.
english: These were the sons of Oholibamah, the daughter of Anah, the daughter of Zibeon, Esau’s wife: she bore to Esau Jeush, Jalam, and Korah.

kikuyu: Kũngĩkĩhaana ũguo-rĩ, mwene iruga ũrĩa ũmwĩtĩte inyuĩ eerĩ no ooke, akwĩre atĩrĩ, ‘Eherera mũndũ ũyũ gĩtĩ kĩu.’ Hĩndĩ ĩyo, nĩũgaaikara thuutha biũ ũconokete.
english: and he who invited both of you would come and tell you, ‘Make room for this person.’ Then you would begin, with shame, to take the lowest place.

kikuyu: Thuutha wa maũndũ macio ngĩcooka ngĩrora, na hau mbere yakwa-rĩ, ngĩona kĩrĩndĩ kĩnene gĩtangĩtarĩka nĩ mũndũ, kĩa andũ moimĩte ndũrĩrĩ-inĩ ciothe, na maarĩ a mĩhĩrĩga yothe, na iruka ciothe, na thiomi ciothe, marũgamĩte mbere ya gĩtĩ kĩa ũnene, na mbere ya Gatũrũme. Nao meehumbĩte nguo ndaaya cia rangi mwerũ, na makanyiita makĩndũ na moko.
english: After these things I looked, and behold, a great multitude which no man could count, out of every nation and of all tribes, peoples, and languages, standing before the throne and before the Lamb, dressed in white robes, with palm branches in their hands.

kikuyu: Hĩa icio inya ciamerire ithenya rĩa rũhĩa rũu ruoinĩkire irũgamĩrĩire mothamaki mana marĩa makoima thĩinĩ wa bũrũri wake, no matigaakorwo marĩ na ũhoti ta wake.
english: As for that which was broken, in the place where four stood up, four kingdoms will stand up out of the nation, but not with his power.

kikuyu: Magĩcooka magĩthondeka mĩrĩnga ĩngĩ ĩĩrĩ ya thahabu na makĩmĩnyiitithania na mwena wa magũrũ-inĩ ma ndaamĩ cia kĩande mwena-inĩ wa mbere wa ebodi, hakuhĩ na rũtumo, igũrũ hanini wa mũcibi wa ebodi.
english: They made two more rings of gold, and put them on the two shoulder straps of the ephod underneath, in its front, close by its coupling, above the skillfully woven band of the ephod.

kikuyu: no nĩyo ndũmĩrĩri ya Jehova ĩkoniĩ Isiraeli. Jehova, ũrĩa watambũrũkirie igũrũ, na akĩhaanda gĩtina gĩa thĩ ĩno, o na akĩũmba roho thĩinĩ wa mũndũ, ekuuga ũũ:
english: revelation of Yahweh’s word concerning Israel: Yahweh, who stretches out the heavens and lays the foundation of the earth, and forms the spirit of man within him says:

kikuyu: Isaia akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Ĩrai mwathi wanyu atĩrĩ, ‘Ũũ nĩguo Jehova ekuuga: Tiga gwĩtigĩra nĩ ũndũ wa ũrĩa ũiguĩte ciugo icio atungati a mũthamaki wa Ashuri manumĩte nacio.
english: Isaiah said to them, “Tell your master this: ‘Yahweh says, “Don’t be afraid of the words that you have heard, with which the servants of the king of Assyria have blasphemed me.

kikuyu: Rĩrĩa Samũeli aakinyire harĩ we, Saũlũ akiuga atĩrĩ, “Jehova arokũrathima! Nĩhingĩtie mawatho ma Jehova.”
english: Samuel came to Saul; and Saul said to him, “You are blessed by Yahweh! I have performed the commandment of Yahweh.”

kikuyu: Rĩrĩa Afarisai moonire ũguo, makĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Ta rora! Arutwo aku nĩmareeka ũrĩa watho ũtetĩkĩrĩtie gwĩkwo mũthenya wa Thabatũ.”
english: But the Pharisees, when they saw it, said to him, “Behold, your disciples do what is not lawful to do on the Sabbath.”

kikuyu: nĩ ũndũ ũcio Daudi na nyũmba yothe ya Isiraeli makĩambatia ithandũkũ rĩa Jehova makĩanagĩrĩra na makĩhuhaga tũrumbeta.
english: So David and all the house of Israel brought up Yahweh’s ark with shouting and with the sound of the trumpet.

kikuyu: Nĩndaheire Isiraeli ũcio wahutatĩire marũa ma gũtigana nake, ngĩmũingata nĩ ũndũ wa ũtharia ũcio wake wothe. No rĩrĩ, nĩndonire atĩ o na Juda, mwarĩ wa nyina ũcio ũtaarĩ mwĩhokeku, ndaarĩ na guoya; o nake nĩoimagarire, agĩthiĩ gũtharia.
english: I saw when, for this very cause, that backsliding Israel had committed adultery, I had put her away and given her a certificate of divorce, yet treacherous Judah, her sister, had no fear, but she also went and played the prostitute.

kikuyu: Wee nĩwambararĩtie rũhĩa rwakwa o ta rwa mbogo; ndĩĩmũitĩrĩrie maguta marĩa mega mũno.
english: But you have exalted my horn like that of the wild ox. I am anointed with fresh oil.

kikuyu: ingĩkorwo ndanarĩa maciaro maguo iteekũrĩha, kana ngoraga ngoro cia ene guo-rĩ,
english: if I have eaten its fruits without money, or have caused its owners to lose their life,

kikuyu: Wamaheire Roho waku mwega amarutage. Ndwagire kũmahe mana marĩe, kana ũkĩaga kũmahe maaĩ maanyoota.
english: You gave also your good Spirit to instruct them, and didn’t withhold your manna from their mouth, and gave them water for their thirst.

kikuyu: Mũthamaki ũcio agĩũkĩra marĩ hamwe na barũthi, na Berinike, na andũ arĩa maikarĩte hamwe nao.
english: The king rose up with the governor and Bernice, and those who sat with them.

kikuyu: niĩ mwene nĩngamwĩonera na maitho, niĩ mwene, ti mũndũ ũngĩ. Ĩ ngoro yakwa ndĩkĩrĩ na wendo mũnene!
english: whom I, even I, will see on my side. My eyes will see, and not as a stranger. “My heart is consumed within me.

kikuyu: Simoni Petero marĩ na mũrutwo ũngĩ makĩrũmĩrĩra Jesũ. Na tondũ mũrutwo ũcio nĩoĩo nĩ mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai ũrĩa mũnene, agĩtwarana na Jesũ kũu nja ya mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai ũcio mũnene.
english: Simon Peter followed Jesus, as did another disciple. Now that disciple was known to the high priest, and entered in with Jesus into the court of the high priest;

kikuyu: Mũthamaki aatwekithagĩria indo icio kũrĩa gwathondekagĩrwo indo cia rĩũmba kũu werũ-inĩ wa Jorodani gatagatĩ ga Sukothu na Zarethani.
english: The king cast them in the plain of the Jordan, in the clay ground between Succoth and Zarethan.

kikuyu: Nĩaakire tũtũũra na akĩgĩa na ndũũru nene cia ngʼondu na cia ngʼombe, nĩgũkorwo Ngai nĩamũheete ũtonga mũnene.
english: Moreover he provided for himself cities, and possessions of flocks and herds in abundance; for God had given him abundant possessions.

kikuyu: a rĩrĩ, Daudi akĩũngania andũ arĩa maarĩ nake, na agĩthuura anene a gwatha thigari ngiri ngiri na a gwatha thigari igana igana.
english: avid counted the people who were with him, and set captains of thousands and captains of hundreds over them.

kikuyu: Jehova Mwene-Hinya-Wothe arĩ na mũthenya aigĩte wa gũũkĩrĩra andũ arĩa othe etĩĩi na arĩa meĩkagĩrĩra, na arĩa othe metũũgagĩria (nao othe nĩmakanyiihio),
english: For there will be a day of Yahweh of Armies for all that is proud and arrogant, and for all that is lifted up, and it shall be brought low—

kikuyu: Nacio mbũtũ ciake cia thigari ciothe iria ikoora nĩikooragwo na rũhiũ rwa njora, namo matigari mao mahurunjĩrwo huho-inĩ ciothe. Hĩndĩ ĩyo nĩmũkamenya atĩ niĩ Jehova nĩ niĩ njarĩtie.
english: All his fugitives in all his bands will fall by the sword, and those who remain will be scattered toward every wind. Then you will know that I, Yahweh, have spoken it.’

kikuyu: Matũũra mothe ma mbarĩ cia Agerishoni maarĩ ikũmi na matatũ hamwe na ithaka ciamo cia ũrĩithio.
english: All the cities of the Gershonites according to their families were thirteen cities with their pasture lands.

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio, ithuothe nĩtũiguanĩire tũthuure andũ amwe tũmatũme kũrĩ inyuĩ marĩ na arata aitũ endwa, Baranaba na Paũlũ,
english: it seemed good to us, having come to one accord, to choose out men and send them to you with our beloved Barnabas and Paul,

kikuyu: Nake Jehova akĩnjĩĩra atĩrĩ, “Ndũkamwĩtigĩre, nĩ ũndũ nĩndĩmũneanĩte hamwe na mbũtũ ciake ciothe cia ita na bũrũri wake moko-inĩ maku. Mwĩke o ũrĩa wekire Sihoni mũthamaki wa Aamori, ũrĩa waathanaga Heshiboni.”
english: Yahweh said to me, “Don’t fear him; for I have delivered him, with all his people and his land, into your hand. You shall do to him as you did to Sihon king of the Amorites, who lived at Heshbon.”

kikuyu: Mũkonyo waku nĩ ta kaihũri ga gĩthiũrũrĩ karĩa gataagaga ndibei ningie. Njohero yaku ĩhaana ta kĩhumbu kĩa ngano ngonyore, gĩthiũrũrũkĩirio nĩ itoka.
english: Your body is like a round goblet, no mixed wine is wanting. Your waist is like a heap of wheat, set about with lilies.

kikuyu: Harũni nĩakaruta thenge ĩrĩa ĩkaagũĩrwo nĩ mũtĩ wa mwena wa Jehova, amĩrute ĩrĩ igongona rĩa kũhoroherio mehia.
english: Aaron shall present the goat on which the lot fell for Yahweh, and offer him for a sin offering.

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo bũrũri ũcio nĩũgatiganĩrio nĩo, naguo nĩũgakenera Thabatũ ciaguo rĩrĩa ũgaakira ihooru ũtarĩ nao. Nĩmakarĩhio mehia mao nĩ ũndũ nĩmaregire mawatho makwa na magĩthũũra kĩrĩra gĩakwa kĩa watho.
english: The land also will be left by them, and will enjoy its Sabbaths while it lies desolate without them; and they will accept the punishment of their iniquity because they rejected my ordinances, and their soul abhorred my statutes.

kikuyu: Isiraeli nĩmehĩtie; nĩmathũkĩtie kĩrĩkanĩro gĩakwa kĩrĩa ndamaathire marũmagĩrĩre. Nĩmoete indo imwe iria ciamũrĩtwo cia kũniinwo; nĩmaiyĩte, na makaheenania, o na magaciiganĩria hamwe na indo ciao o ene.
english: Israel has sinned. Yes, they have even transgressed my covenant which I commanded them. Yes, they have even taken some of the devoted things, and have also stolen, and also deceived. They have even put it among their own stuff.

kikuyu: makĩmũkururia, makĩmumia nja ya itũũra, na makĩambĩrĩria kũmũhũũra na mahiga nyuguto. Nao, aira makĩiga nguo ciao magũrũ-inĩ ma mwanake wetagwo Saũlũ.
english: They threw him out of the city and stoned him. The witnesses placed their garments at the feet of a young man named Saul.

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo andũ ooru nĩmakaniinwo, no arĩa mehokete Jehova nĩmakagaya bũrũri.
english: For evildoers shall be cut off, but those who wait for Yahweh shall inherit the land.

kikuyu: Nineve kũhaana ta ndia, na maaĩ makuo nĩkũhwa marahwa. Kũraanĩrĩrwo, gũkeerwo atĩrĩ, “Ta rũgamai! Ta rũgamai!” No gũtirĩ mũndũ ũngĩĩhũgũra.
english: But Nineveh has been from of old like a pool of water, yet they flee away. “Stop! Stop!” they cry, but no one looks back.

kikuyu: Andũ othe makĩgega, makĩũrania atĩrĩ, “Ũyũ no akorwo arĩ we Mũrũ wa Daudi?”
english: All the multitudes were amazed, and said, “Can this be the son of David?”

kikuyu: Nyũngũ nĩ ya gũthererio betha, na kĩrugutĩro nĩ gĩa gũthererio thahabu, no Jehova nĩwe ũgeragia ngoro cia andũ.
english: The refining pot is for silver, and the furnace for gold, but Yahweh tests the hearts.

kikuyu: Bethueli nĩwe watuĩkire ithe wa Rebeka. Acio nĩo ariũ arĩa anana Milika aaciarĩire Nahoru, mũrũ wa nyina na Iburahĩmu.
english: Bethuel became the father of Rebekah. These eight Milcah bore to Nahor, Abraham’s brother.

kikuyu: Nyina agĩthiĩ, akĩmũkomia ũrĩrĩ-inĩ wa mũndũ wa Ngai, agĩcooka akĩhinga mũrango, agĩthiĩ.
english: She went up and laid him on the man of God’s bed, and shut the door on him, and went out.

kikuyu: Atumi a nguo nĩmagakorwo na kĩeha, na arĩa othe maheagwo mĩcaara nĩmakaigua ũũru ngoro.
english: The pillars will be broken in pieces. All those who work for hire will be grieved in soul.

kikuyu: “ ‘Nĩ ũndũ wa ũguo-rĩ, nĩngũrehere nyũmba ya Jeroboamu mwanangĩko. Nĩngwehereria Jeroboamu mwana o wothe wa kahĩĩ thĩinĩ wa Isiraeli, arĩ ngombo kana atarĩ ngombo. Nĩngacina nyũmba ya Jeroboamu o ta ũrĩa mũndũ acinaga mai nginya magathira.
english: therefore, behold, I will bring evil on the house of Jeroboam, and will cut off from Jeroboam everyone who urinates on a wall,† he who is shut up and he who is left at large in Israel, and will utterly sweep away the house of Jeroboam, as a man sweeps away dung until it is all gone.

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo, nyina wa Jesũ na ariũ a nyina na Jesũ magĩũka. Makĩrũgama nja, magĩtũma mũndũ atoonye akamwĩte.
english: His mother and his brothers came, and standing outside, they sent to him, calling him.

kikuyu: Iria imata na ũũkĩ nĩcio gakarĩĩaga rĩrĩa gakaamenya kũrega ũũru na gwĩthuurĩra maũndũ marĩa mega.
english: He shall eat butter and honey when he knows to refuse the evil and choose the good.

kikuyu: Ningĩ akĩoya kahiũ, agĩtambũrũkia guoko gwake, oorage mũriũ.
english: Abraham stretched out his hand, and took the knife to kill his son.

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Hagari agĩciarĩra Aburamu mwana wa kahĩĩ, nake Aburamu agĩtua kaana kau aaciarĩirwo Ishumaeli.
english: Hagar bore a son for Abram. Abram called the name of his son, whom Hagar bore, Ishmael.

kikuyu: Thĩ ĩhaanaga ta rĩũmba rĩhũrĩtwo mũhũũri;
english: It is changed as clay under the seal, and presented as a garment.

kikuyu: rĩu no taguo o nao matuĩkĩte aremi, nĩgeetha o nao rĩu maiguĩrwo tha nĩ ũndũ wa ũrĩa Ngai amũiguĩrĩire tha.
english: even so these also have now been disobedient, that by the mercy shown to you they may also obtain mercy.

kikuyu: Ngʼombe na nduba ikaarĩĩaga hamwe, natuo tũcaũ twacio tũgaakomanagia hamwe, naguo mũrũũthi ũkaarĩĩaga nyeki ta ndegwa.
english: The cow and the bear will graze. Their young ones will lie down together. The lion will eat straw like the ox.

kikuyu: nĩndĩĩririkanaga kĩrĩkanĩro gĩakwa gatagatĩ kanyu na niĩ, na gatagatĩ ga ciũmbe cia mĩthemba yothe iria itũũraga muoyo. Gũtirĩ hĩndĩ ĩngĩ maaĩ magatuĩka mũiyũro wa kũniina mĩoyo yothe.
english: I will remember my covenant, which is between me and you and every living creature of all flesh, and the waters will no more become a flood to destroy all flesh.

kikuyu: Naguo ũhoro ũcio ũgĩthĩĩnia Iburahĩmu mũno tondũ wakoniĩ mũriũ.
english: The thing was very grievous in Abraham’s sight on account of his son.

kikuyu: Nĩmagooce rĩĩtwa rĩake makĩrũgarũgaga, na mamũinĩre na tũhembe, na kĩnanda kĩa mũgeeto.
english: Let them praise his name in the dance! Let them sing praises to him with tambourine and harp!

kikuyu: Marũgamagia athamaki matarĩ na rũtha rwakwa, magathuura anene itametĩkĩrĩtie. Methondekagĩra mĩhianano na betha na thahabu ciao, nayo ĩgatuĩka ya kũmaniina.
english: They have set up kings, but not by me. They have made princes, and I didn’t approve. Of their silver and their gold they have made themselves idols, that they may be cut off.

kikuyu: Ĩkagai maũndũ mothe mũtekũnuguna na mũtegũkararania,
english: Do all things without complaining and arguing,

kikuyu: “Nĩ ũndũ ũcio ĩra andũ a nyũmba ya Isiraeli atĩrĩ, ‘Mwathani Jehova ekuuga atĩrĩ: Mwĩrirei! Garũrũkai mũtigane na mĩhianano ĩyo yanyu, na mũregane na maũndũ marĩa mothe mwĩkaga marĩ magigi!
english: “Therefore tell the house of Israel, ‘The Lord Yahweh says: “Return, and turn yourselves from your idols! Turn away your faces from all your abominations.

kikuyu: aĩ mũrĩ na haaro-ĩ! inyuĩ mũikaraga mwĩgangarĩte mũrĩ kũu Zayuni, na inyuĩ mũiguaga mũrĩ agitĩre mũrĩ kĩrĩma-igũrũ gĩa Samaria;* o inyuĩ mũrĩ igweta thĩinĩ wa rũrĩrĩ rũrĩa rũrĩ mbere ya iria ingĩ, kũrĩa andũ a Isiraeli mathiiaga.
english: oe to those who are at ease in Zion, and to those who are secure on the mountain of Samaria, the notable men of the chief of the nations, to whom the house of Israel come!

kikuyu: Ũguonĩguongoonaniaũneneonaũtheruwakwa, nandũmemenyekemaitho-inĩmandũrĩrĩnyingĩ. Hĩndĩ ĩyo nĩmakamenya atĩ niĩ nĩ niĩ Jehova.’
english: I will magnify myself and sanctify myself, and I will make myself known in the eyes of many nations. Then they will know that I am Yahweh.” ’

kikuyu: Gũtirĩ mũtumia wa ndigwa wagĩrĩire kwandĩkwo mũtaratara-inĩ wa atumia a ndigwa atakinyĩtie mĩaka mĩrongo ĩtandatũ, na akorwo aarĩ mwĩhokeku kũrĩ mũthuuri-we,
english: Let no one be enrolled as a widow under sixty years old, having been the wife of one man,

kikuyu: Rĩu niĩ nĩmenyete atĩ Jehova nĩ mũnene gũkĩra ngai iria ingĩ ciothe, nĩgũkorwo ũguo nĩguo eekire arĩa maahatĩrĩirie Isiraeli metĩĩte.”
english: Now I know that Yahweh is greater than all gods because of the way that they treated people arrogantly.”

kikuyu: Nĩmatunyanire matũũra maarĩ mairigĩre na thingo cia hinya, na mĩgũnda mĩnoru, makĩĩgwatĩra nyũmba ciaiyũrĩte indo njega cia mĩthemba yothe, na ithima iria cienjetwo, na mĩgũnda ya mĩthabibũ, na mĩgũnda ya mĩtamaiyũ, na mĩtĩ mĩingĩ mũno ya matunda. Makĩrĩa, nginya makĩhũũna na makĩagĩra mĩĩrĩ; magĩkenera wega waku mũingĩ.
english: They took fortified cities and a rich land, and possessed houses full of all good things, cisterns dug out, vineyards, olive groves, and fruit trees in abundance. So they ate, were filled, became fat, and delighted themselves in your great goodness.

kikuyu: No nĩngũgũkaĩra ũndeithie, Wee Jehova; rũciinĩ mahooya makwa mokaga harĩwe.
english: But to you, Yahweh, I have cried. In the morning, my prayer comes before you.

kikuyu: Kĩheo kĩheanĩtwo na hitho nĩkĩhooreragia marakara, na ihaki ihithe nguo-inĩ rĩhooreragia mangʼũrĩ manene.
english: A gift in secret pacifies anger, and a bribe in the cloak, strong wrath.

kikuyu: Thondekai tũmĩhiano twa mahũha na twa tũbĩa iria irathũkia bũrũri, na mũtĩĩe ngai ya Isiraeli. No gũkorwo nĩakeheria guoko gwake kuume igũrũ rĩanyu, o na igũrũ rĩa ngai cianyu, o na bũrũri wanyu.
english: Therefore you shall make images of your tumors and images of your mice that mar the land; and you shall give glory to the God of Israel. Perhaps he will release his hand from you, from your gods, and from your land.

kikuyu: Aburamu agĩtuĩkanĩria bũrũri ũcio agĩkinya handũ haarĩ na mũtĩ mũnene wa More kũu Shekemu. Hĩndĩ ĩyo Akaanani nĩo maatũũraga bũrũri ũcio.
english: Abram passed through the land to the place of Shechem, to the oak of Moreh. At that time, Canaanites were in the land.

kikuyu: Matũũra macio nĩmo maya: Bezeri kũu werũ-inĩ ũrĩa mwaraganu, nĩ ũndũ wa andũ a mũhĩrĩga wa Rubeni; na Ramothu kũu Gileadi, nĩ ũndũ wa andũ a mũhĩrĩga wa Gadi; na Golani kũu Bashani, nĩ ũndũ wa mũhĩrĩga wa Manase. Kĩambĩrĩria gĩa Kũrutana Watho
english: Bezer in the wilderness, in the plain country, for the Reubenites; and Ramoth in Gilead for the Gadites; and Golan in Bashan for the Manassites.

kikuyu: Na rĩrĩa Jesũ na arutwo ake moimaga Jeriko-rĩ, andũ aingĩ makĩmũrũmĩrĩra.
english: As they went out from Jericho, a great multitude followed him.

kikuyu: “Ningĩ ũthondeke mĩgamba ya mbaũ cia mũgaa: ĩtano yayo nĩ ũndũ wa mbaũ cia mwena ũmwe wa hema ĩyo nyamũre,
english: “You shall make bars of acacia wood: five for the boards of the one side of the tabernacle,

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa yothe mũrũ wa Jesii egũtũũra muoyo gũkũ thĩ, wee ndũkehaanda kana ũthamaki waku wĩhaande. Rĩu mũtũmanĩre oke kũrĩ niĩ, tondũ no nginya akue!”
english: For as long as the son of Jesse lives on the earth, you will not be established, nor will your kingdom. Therefore now send and bring him to me, for he shall surely die!”

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo nĩmakamenya atĩ niĩ nĩ niĩ Jehova, rĩrĩa ngaacina bũrũri wa Misiri na mwaki, nao arĩa othe maamateithagia mahehenjwo.
english: They will know that I am Yahweh when I have set a fire in Egypt, and all her helpers are destroyed.

kikuyu: Harĩ o ndegwa ĩmwe haaragĩriai iruta rĩa mũtu gĩcunjĩ gĩa ithatũ gĩa * 28:5 nĩ ta kilo 2 † 28:5 nĩ ta lita 1 ‡ 28:7 nĩ ta lita 4 § 28:9 nĩ ta kilo inya na nuthu (4:5) * 28:12 nĩ ta kilo ithathatũ
english: with their meal offering, fine flour mixed with oil. You shall offer three tenths for a bull, and two tenths for the ram.

kikuyu: “Mbogo ya njamba-rĩ, yetĩkĩra gũgũtungata? No ĩkindĩrie mũharatĩ-inĩ waku ũtukũ?
english: “Will the wild ox be content to serve you? Or will he stay by your feeding trough?

kikuyu: Ningĩ Ngai akĩĩra Nuhu hamwe na ariũ ake atĩrĩ,
english: God spoke to Noah and to his sons with him, saying,

kikuyu: ũtakaheo maita maingĩ ma kĩrĩa atigĩte ihinda-inĩ rĩĩrĩ tũrĩ, na ihinda rĩrĩa rĩgooka aheo muoyo wa tene na tene.” Jesũ kwaria Ũhoro wa Gĩkuũ Gĩake Rĩngĩ
english: who will not receive many times more in this time, and in the world to come, eternal life.”

kikuyu: Namo marakara ma Jehova makĩrĩrĩmbũkĩra Musa, akĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Ĩ mũrũ wa nyũkwa Harũni, ũrĩa Mũlawii-rĩ, ndangĩaria? Nĩnjũũĩ no aarie wega. Arĩ njĩra-inĩ agĩũka gũgũtũnga, na nĩegũkena akuona.
english: Yahweh’s anger burned against Moses, and he said, “What about Aaron, your brother, the Levite? I know that he can speak well. Also, behold, he is coming out to meet you. When he sees you, he will be glad in his heart.

kikuyu: Mũthuuri nĩagĩrĩirwo nĩ kũhingagĩria mũtumia wake bata wake wa kĩhiko, o nake mũtumia ahingagĩrie mũthuuriwe bata wake.
english: Let the husband give his wife the affection owed her,† and likewise also the wife her husband.

kikuyu: Nĩngũtũma mũraika mbere yanyu na nĩngaingata Akaanani, na Aamori, na Ahiti, na Aperizi, na Ahivi, na Ajebusi.
english: I will send an angel before you; and I will drive out the Canaanite, the Amorite, and the Hittite, and the Perizzite, the Hivite, and the Jebusite.

kikuyu: O na ningĩ agĩciara kahĩĩ kangĩ, agĩgatua Shela. Aagaciarĩire kũu Chezibu.
english: She yet again bore a son, and named him Shelah. He was at Chezib when she bore him.

kikuyu: Moko make mahaana ta thanju cia thahabu, itheecereirwo tũhiga-inĩ tũrĩa twĩtagwo thumarati; mwĩrĩ wake ũhaana ta mũguongo ũrĩa mũnyorokie, ũgemetio na yakuti ĩrĩa theru.
english: His hands are like rings of gold set with beryl. His body is like ivory work overlaid with sapphires.

kikuyu: na rũciinĩ nĩmũkona riiri wa Jehova, tondũ nĩaiguĩte ũrĩa mũmũtetetie. Ithuĩ tũkĩrĩ a, atĩ nĩguo mũtũtetie?”
english: In the morning, you shall see Yahweh’s glory; because he hears your murmurings against Yahweh. Who are we, that you murmur against us?”

kikuyu: Kuuma mũberethi ũrĩa ũrĩ gĩtina-inĩ nginya mũthia-inĩ wa mũhuro kĩrĩ na mĩkono ĩĩrĩ§ kũraiha na igũrũ, na wariĩ wa mũkono ũmwe, na kuuma mũthia-inĩ ũrĩa mũnini nginya mũthia- inĩ ũrĩa mũnenanene, kĩrĩ na mĩkono ĩna* kũraiha na igũrũ, na wariĩ wa mũkono ũmwe.
english: From the bottom on the ground to the lower ledge shall be two cubits, and the width one cubit; and from the lesser ledge to the greater ledge shall be four cubits, and the width a cubit.

kikuyu: Nacio ciĩraga ooni-maũndũ atĩrĩ, “Mũtigatuonere maũndũ mangĩ!” na ikeera anabii atĩrĩ, “Tigai gũtũrathĩra maũndũ ma ũhoro wa ma! Twĩragei maũndũ marĩa mangĩtũkenia, na mũtũrathagĩre maũndũ ma maheeni.
english: who tell the seers, “Don’t see!” and the prophets, “Don’t prophesy to us right things. Tell us pleasant things. Prophesy deceits.

kikuyu: na mũthamaki wa Makeda, ũmwe, na mũthamaki wa Betheli, ũmwe,
english: the king of Makkedah, one; the king of Bethel, one;

kikuyu: nĩ cia gũtũma ũrĩa ũtarĩ na ũũgĩ agĩe na ũmenyo, na cia gũtũma mũndũ mwĩthĩ agĩe na ũmenyo na ũgereri:
english: to give prudence to the simple, knowledge and discretion to the young man—

kikuyu: Nĩnyenjete ithima mabũrũri ma kũngĩ na nganyua maaĩ kuo. Nĩhũithĩtie tũrũũĩ twa Misiri na makinya ma magũrũ makwa.’
english: I have dug and drunk water, and with the sole of my feet I will dry up all the rivers of Egypt.”

kikuyu: Nake Jesũ akĩmũkaania na hinya na akĩmwĩra athiĩ,
english: He strictly warned him and immediately sent him out,

kikuyu: Akĩmooria atĩrĩ, “Kaĩ mwĩtĩkĩrĩtie andũ a nja othe matũũre muoyo?
english: Moses said to them, “Have you saved all the women alive?

kikuyu: Mĩrumbĩ ya ngigĩ nĩĩkegwatĩra mĩtĩ yaku yothe na ĩrĩe irio cia bũrũri waku.
english: Locusts will consume all of your trees and the fruit of your ground.

kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, nĩ kwarĩ na maruta ma njino maingĩ mũno, hamwe na maguta ma maruta ma ũiguano, o na maruta ma indo cia kũnyuuo marĩa maarutanagĩrio na maruta ma njino.
english: Also the burnt offerings were in abundance, with the fat of the peace offerings and with the drink offerings for every burnt offering. So the service of Yahweh’s house was set in order.

kikuyu: No Petero agĩtigwo etereire hau nja ya kĩhingo. Nake mũrutwo ũcio ũngĩ, woĩo nĩ mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai ũrĩa mũnene, agĩcooka, akĩaria na mũirĩtu ũrĩa warĩ kĩhingo-inĩ agĩtoonyia Petero.
english: but Peter was standing at the door outside. So the other disciple, who was known to the high priest, went out and spoke to her who kept the door, and brought in Peter.

kikuyu: Makĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Wĩkaga maũndũ maya na ũhoti ũrĩkũ? Na nũũ wakũheire ũhoti wa gwĩka maũndũ maya?”
english: and they began saying to him, “By what authority do you do these things? Or who gave you this authority to do these things?”

kikuyu: Jehova nĩagatũma kĩrathimo kũu makũmbĩ-inĩ maku o na ũndũ-inĩ o wothe ũngĩka na moko maku. Jehova Ngai waku nĩagakũrathimĩra bũrũri-inĩ ũcio arakũhe.
english: Yahweh will command the blessing on you in your barns, and in all that you put your hand to. He will bless you in the land which Yahweh your God gives you.

kikuyu: Heni ciake imũrĩkaga thĩ yothe; thĩ ĩcionaga ĩkainaina.
english: His lightning lights up the world. The earth sees, and trembles.

kikuyu: Ndũgetigĩre mwanangĩko wa narua, kana kĩhuhũkanio kĩrĩa gĩkoragĩrĩra andũ arĩa aaganu,
english: Don’t be afraid of sudden fear, neither of the desolation of the wicked, when it comes;

kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, hĩndĩ ĩrĩa Herode aarĩ mũthamaki wa Judea, nĩ kwarĩ mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai wetagwo Zakaria, warĩ wa gĩkundi kĩa athĩnjĩri-Ngai kĩa Abija; mũtumia wake eetagwo Elizabethi na aarĩ wa rũciaro rwa Harũni.
english: There was in the days of Herod, the king of Judea, a certain priest named Zacharias, of the priestly division of Abijah. He had a wife of the daughters of Aaron, and her name was Elizabeth.

kikuyu: Ngai nĩatuĩte o mbere atĩ nĩtũgaatuĩka ciana ciake nĩ ũndũ wa Jesũ Kristũ, kũringana na ũrĩa eendete na ũrĩa oonire arĩ wega,
english: having predestined us for adoption as children through Jesus Christ to himself, according to the good pleasure of his desire,

kikuyu: Nao magĩĩtigĩra mũno na makĩũrania atĩrĩ, “Kaĩ mũndũ ũyũ akĩrĩ ũũ? Tondũ o na rũhuho na makũmbĩ ma iria nĩiramwathĩkĩra!” 5 Kũhonio kwa Mũndũ warĩ na Ndaimono
english: They were greatly afraid and said to one another, “Who then is this, that even the wind and the sea obey him?”

kikuyu: rutithania wĩra na ithuĩ, tũkorwo na mũhuko ũmwe ithuothe”;
english: You shall cast your lot among us. We’ll all have one purse”—

kikuyu: Ngũmwĩra atĩrĩ, ũtukũ ũcio andũ eerĩ nĩmagakorwo makomete ũrĩrĩ ũmwe, nake ũmwe wao akuuo, na ũrĩa ũngĩ atigwo.
english: I tell you, in that night there will be two people in one bed. One will be taken and the other will be left.

kikuyu: Maakaire mateithio, no hatiarĩ na wa kũmahonokia, magĩkaĩra Jehova no ndaametĩkire.
english: They looked, but there was no one to save; even to Yahweh, but he didn’t answer them.

kikuyu: “Rĩrĩa ingĩhinga igũrũ nayo mbura yaage kuura, kana njathe ngigĩ cianange bũrũri kana ndũme mũthiro kũrĩ andũ akwa,
english: “If I shut up the sky so that there is no rain, or if I command the locust to devour the land, or if I send pestilence among my people,

kikuyu: Mũthenya ũcio gũtigakorwo na ũtheri kana heho o na kana mbaa.
english: It will happen in that day that there will not be light, cold, or frost.

kikuyu: “Atĩrĩrĩ, ihinda rĩa ciumia mĩrongo mũgwanja nĩrĩo rĩtuĩrĩirwo andũ aku, na rĩgatuĩrwo itũũra rĩaku itheru, nĩgeetha hagĩe na ihinda rĩa kũniina ũmaramari, na rĩa gũtigana na mehia, na rĩa kũhooroheria waganu, nĩguo ũhoro wa ũthingu ũrĩa ũtathiraga ũgĩe, nakĩo kĩoneki na ũrathi ciĩkĩrwo mũhũũri, nake ũrĩa mũtheru mũno aitĩrĩrio maguta.
english: “Seventy weeks are decreed on your people and on your holy city, to finish disobedience, to make an end of sins, to make reconciliation for iniquity, to bring in everlasting righteousness, to seal up vision and prophecy, and to anoint the most holy.

kikuyu: Niĩ-rĩ, o niĩ nĩ niĩ Jehova, na gũtirĩ mũhonokia ũngĩ tiga niĩ.
english: I myself am Yahweh. Besides me, there is no savior.

kikuyu: Kũrĩa gũtarĩ ndegwa mĩharatĩ ĩkoragwo ĩrĩ mĩtheru, no maciaro maingĩ moimanaga na hinya wa ndegwa.
english: Where no oxen are, the crib is clean, but much increase is by the strength of the ox.

kikuyu: Hadadezeri aarehithĩtie Asuriata kuuma mũrĩmo wa Rũũĩ rwa Farati; magĩthiĩ nginya Helamu marĩ hamwe, na Shobaki mũnene wa mbũtũ ya ita ya Hedadezeri, amatongoretie.
english: Hadadezer sent and brought out the Syrians who were beyond the River; and they came to Helam, with Shobach the captain of the army of Hadadezer at their head.

kikuyu: “Ihinda rĩa magetha nĩrĩhĩtũku, nayo hĩndĩ ya riũa ĩgathira, na tũtihonoketio.”
english: “The harvest is past. The summer has ended, and we are not saved.”

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio mũthamaki akĩruta gĩcũhĩ kĩa mũhũũri wake kuuma kĩara-inĩ gĩake, agĩkĩnengera Hamani mũrũ wa Hamedatha, ũrĩa Mũagagi, ũcio thũ ya Ayahudi.†
english: The king took his ring from his hand, and gave it to Haman the son of Hammedatha the Agagite, the Jews’ enemy.

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Jonathani agĩĩta Daudi akĩmwĩra ũhoro ũcio maaragia wothe. Akĩmũtwara kũrĩ Saũlũ, nake Daudi agĩikara na Saũlũ o ta mbere.
english: Jonathan called David, and Jonathan showed him all those things. Then Jonathan brought David to Saul, and he was in his presence as before.

kikuyu: wa ikũmi na ĩna wagũĩrĩire Matithia, na ariũ ake, na andũ a nyũmba yao, maarĩ andũ 12;
english: for the fourteenth, Mattithiah, his sons and his brothers, twelve;

kikuyu: Thuutha wa rũciaro rũu ruothe gũkua, gũkĩgĩa na rũciaro rũngĩ rũtooĩ Jehova kana rũkamenya maũndũ marĩa eekĩire Isiraeli.
english: After all that generation were gathered to their fathers, another generation arose after them who didn’t know Yahweh, nor the work which he had done for Israel.

kikuyu: Ũmarutage ciana ciaku na kĩyo. Aragia ũhoro wamo rĩrĩa ũikarĩte gwaku mũciĩ na rĩrĩa ũgũthiĩ na njĩra, o na rĩrĩa ũkomete na rĩrĩa wokĩra.
english: and you shall teach them diligently to your children, and shall talk of them when you sit in your house, and when you walk by the way, and when you lie down, and when you rise up.

kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, Jehoashu mũthamaki wa Isiraeli agĩcookeria Amazia mũthamaki wa Juda atĩrĩ: “Mahiũ ma nyeki-inĩ ma kũu Lebanoni nĩmatũmĩire mũtarakwa ndũmĩrĩri o kũu Lebanoni makĩwĩra atĩrĩ, ‘Heana mwarĩguo ahikio nĩ mũrũ wakwa.’ Hĩndĩ ĩyo nyamũ ya gĩthaka kũu Lebanoni ĩgĩũka ĩkĩrangĩrĩria mahiũ macio ma nyeki-inĩ na makinya mayo.
english: Jehoash the king of Israel sent to Amaziah king of Judah, saying, “The thistle that was in Lebanon sent to the cedar that was in Lebanon, saying, ‘Give your daughter to my son as wife.’ Then a wild animal that was in Lebanon passed by, and trampled down the thistle.

kikuyu: Watũmire atigie o hanini aiganane nawe,
english: For you have made him a little lower than the angels,† and crowned him with glory and honor.

kikuyu: Nao andũ a itũũra othe makĩũngana hau mũrango-inĩ wa nyũmba,
english: All the city was gathered together at the door.

kikuyu: Nĩngacirehera arĩithi a gũcirĩithagia, na itigacooka gwĩtigĩra kana kũmaka, na gũtirĩ o na ĩmwe yacio ĩkoora,” ũguo nĩguo Jehova ekuuga.
english: I will set up shepherds over them who will feed them. They will no longer be afraid or dismayed, neither will any be lacking,” says Yahweh.

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo matiathikĩrĩirie ndũmĩrĩri ciakwa, ndũmĩrĩri iria ndaamatũmĩire kaingĩ na tũnua twa ndungata ciakwa cia anabii”, ũguo nĩguo Jehova ekuuga “O na inyuĩ arĩa mwatahirwo-rĩ, mũtiigana gũthikĩrĩria,” ũguo nĩguo Jehova ekuuga.
english: because they have not listened to my words,” says Yahweh, “with which I sent to them my servants the prophets, rising up early and sending them; but you would not hear,” says Yahweh.

kikuyu: “Na rĩrĩ, ta thikĩrĩria, wee wendete ikeno, na ũikaraga ũtarĩ ũgwati ũngĩĩtigĩra, na ũkeĩraga atĩrĩ, ‘Niĩ no niĩ, na gũtirĩ ũngĩ tiga niĩ. Ndirĩ hĩndĩ ngaatuĩka mũtumia wa ndigwa, kana menye ũhoro wa gũkuĩrwo nĩ ciana.’
english: “Now therefore hear this, you who are given to pleasures, who sit securely, who say in your heart, ‘I am, and there is no one else besides me. I won’t sit as a widow, neither will I know the loss of children.’

kikuyu: Na rĩrĩa Stefano mũira waku aaitirwo thakame, ndaarũngiĩ ho ngĩĩtĩkĩra ũndũ ũcio wĩkwo, na ngaikaria nguo cia andũ arĩa maamũũragire.’
english: When the blood of Stephen, your witness, was shed, I also was standing by, consenting to his death, and guarding the cloaks of those who killed him.’

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Iburahĩmu akĩrehe ngʼondu na ngʼombe akĩhe Abimeleku, nao andũ acio eerĩ makĩgĩa na kĩrĩkanĩro.
english: Abraham took sheep and cattle, and gave them to Abimelech. Those two made a covenant.

kikuyu: Mũtikanathaahie bũrũri ũrĩa mũtũũraga na kũrĩa niĩ ndũũraga, nĩ ũndũ niĩ Jehova ndũũraga gatagatĩ ka andũ a Isiraeli.’ ”
english: You shall not defile the land which you inhabit, where I dwell; for I, Yahweh, dwell among the children of Israel.’ ”

kikuyu: no nyina akĩaria, akiuga atĩrĩ, “Aca! Gegwĩtwo Johana.”
english: His mother answered, “Not so; but he will be called John.”

kikuyu: Nao makũgeithie na makũhe mĩgate ĩĩrĩ, ĩrĩa ũrĩamũkĩra kuuma kũrĩ o.
english: They will greet you and give you two loaves of bread, which you shall receive from their hand.

kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, mũndũ ũrĩa ũrutaga ndegwa igongona ahaana o ta ũrĩa ũragaga mũndũ, nake ũrĩa ũrutaga gatũrũme, ahaana o ta mũndũ ũrĩa uunaga ngui ngingo; mũndũ ũrĩa ũrutaga iruta rĩa mũtu, ahaana o ta mũndũ ũrĩa ũrutaga thakame ya ngũrwe, nake ũrĩa ũcinaga ũbumba wa kĩririkano, ahaana o ta mũndũ ũrĩa ũhooyaga mũhianano. Andũ acio nĩmethuurĩire njĩra ciao o ene, na ngoro ciao igakenagio nĩ maũndũ marĩa marĩ magigi;
english: He who kills an ox is as he who kills a man; he who sacrifices a lamb, as he who breaks a dog’s neck; he who offers an offering, as he who offers pig’s blood; he who burns frankincense, as he who blesses an idol. Yes, they have chosen their own ways, and their soul delights in their abominations.

kikuyu: Ariũ a ithe magĩcooka magĩthiĩ kũrĩ we, makĩĩgũithia thĩ mbere yake, makiuga atĩrĩ, “Tũrĩ ngombo ciaku.”
english: His brothers also went and fell down before his face; and they said, “Behold, we are your servants.”

kikuyu: No ũhote gũtheecanga rũũa rwayo na mĩcengi, kana ũtheecange mũtwe wayo na mĩcengi ĩrĩa ĩtegaga thamaki?
english: Can you fill his skin with barbed irons, or his head with fish spears?

kikuyu: No Harũni na njiaro ciake nĩo maarutaga magongona kĩgongona-inĩ kĩa igongona rĩa njino, na kĩgongona-inĩ gĩa gũcinĩrwo ũbumba, na wĩra wothe warutagwo ũkonainie na Handũ-hau-Hatheru-Mũno, nĩ ũndũ wa kũhoroheria andũ a Isiraeli mehia, kũringana na ũrĩa Musa ndungata ya Ngai yaathanĩte.
english: But Aaron and his sons offered on the altar of burnt offering, and on the altar of incense, for all the work of the most holy place, and to make atonement for Israel, according to all that Moses the servant of God had commanded.

kikuyu: na andũ a Rama na Geba maarĩ 621
english: The men of Ramah and Geba: six hundred twenty-one.

kikuyu: Rũciinĩ tene riũa rĩkĩratha-rĩ, ũthiĩ ũtharĩkĩre itũũra inene. Na rĩrĩa Gaali na andũ ake mariumĩra magũtharĩkĩre-rĩ, ĩka ũrĩa wothe guoko gwaku kũngĩhota gwĩka.”
english: It shall be that in the morning, as soon as the sun is up, you shall rise early and rush on the city. Behold, when he and the people who are with him come out against you, then may you do to them as you shall find occasion.”

kikuyu: andũ a Efiraimu, njamba cia ita irĩ ũrũme na irĩ ngumo mĩhĩrĩga-inĩ yao, maarĩ 20,800;
english: Of the children of Ephraim: twenty thousand eight hundred, mighty men of valor, famous men in their fathers’ houses.

kikuyu: Ngũmwĩra atĩrĩ, hũthagĩrai ũtonga wa gũkũ thĩ na gũthondeka ũrata na andũ, nĩguo rĩrĩa ũtonga ũcio ũgaathira, mũkaamũkĩrwo ciikaro-inĩ iria cia tene na tene.
english: I tell you, make for yourselves friends by means of unrighteous mammon, so that when you fail, they may receive you into the eternal tents.

kikuyu: na aarĩkia gũtharia watho ũrĩa warĩ mwandĩke hamwe na mũtabarĩre waguo, o ũcio watũthitangaga na watũire ũreganĩte na ithuĩ-rĩ; akĩweheria na ũndũ wa kũũhũrĩra mĩcumarĩ mũtharaba-inĩ.
english: wiping out the handwriting in ordinances which was against us. He has taken it out of the way, nailing it to the cross.

kikuyu: Na rĩrĩa aahuraga mbeũ icio, imwe ikĩgũa mũkĩra-inĩ wa njĩra, nacio nyoni igĩũka igĩcirĩa.
english: As he sowed, some seed fell by the road, and the birds† came and devoured it.

kikuyu: Rĩrĩa ũtarakĩendetie- rĩ, githĩ gĩtirarĩ gĩaku? Na thuutha wa gũkĩendia-rĩ, githĩ mbeeca itirakĩrĩ o ciaku wĩke nacio o ũrĩa ũkwenda? Ũreciiririe gwĩka ũguo nĩkĩ? Ti andũ ũheenetie, no nĩ Ngai.”
english: While you kept it, didn’t it remain your own? After it was sold, wasn’t it in your power? How is it that you have conceived this thing in your heart? You haven’t lied to men, but to God.”

kikuyu: Igongona rĩu mũrĩrĩĩage o mũthenya ũcio rĩarutwo, kana mũthenya ũyũ ũngĩ; kĩndũ o gĩothe kĩa rĩo kĩngĩtigara gĩkinyie mũthenya wa gatatũ, no nginya gĩcinwo kĩhĩe biũ.
english: It shall be eaten the same day you offer it, and on the next day. If anything remains until the third day, it shall be burned with fire.

kikuyu: Ndirikanaga, Wee Jehova, hĩndĩ ĩrĩa ũgwĩka andũ aku wega, ũndeithagie rĩrĩa ũkũmahonokia,
english: Remember me, Yahweh, with the favor that you show to your people. Visit me with your salvation,

kikuyu: Mũndũ mũtaana egunaga we mwene, no mũndũ ũtarĩ tha erehagĩra thĩĩna we mwene.
english: The merciful man does good to his own soul, but he who is cruel troubles his own flesh.

kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa wathanaga angĩthikĩrĩria maheeni-rĩ, anene ake othe matuĩkaga andũ aaganu.
english: If a ruler listens to lies, all of his officials are wicked.

kikuyu: na Barikosi, na Sisera, na Tema,
english: the children of Barkos, the children of Sisera, the children of Temah,

kikuyu: “Na niĩ ngĩũria atĩrĩ, ‘Wee nĩ we ũ, Mwathani?’ “Nake akĩnjookeria atĩrĩ, ‘Nĩ niĩ Jesũ wa Nazarethi, ũrĩa ũranyariira.’
english: I answered, ‘Who are you, Lord?’ He said to me, ‘I am Jesus of Nazareth, whom you persecute.’

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa andũ acio maageririo na magerio manene-rĩ, gĩkeno kĩao kĩnene na ũthĩĩni wao mũkĩru nĩciatũmire maingĩhĩrwo nĩ ũtaana mũnene.
english: how in a severe ordeal of affliction, the abundance of their joy and their deep  Leviticus 26:12; Jeremiah 32:38; Ezekiel 37:27  Isaiah 52:11; Ezekiel 20:34,41  2 Samuel 7:14; 7:8

kikuyu: Nao Alawii: arĩa maarĩ a njiaro cia Jeshua na Kadimieli (iria cioimĩte harĩ ciana cia Hodavia) maarĩ 74.
english: The Levites: the children of Jeshua and Kadmiel, of the children of Hodaviah, seventy-four.

kikuyu: makĩĩra Mũthamaki Nebukadinezaru atĩrĩ, “Wee mũthamaki ũrotũũra tene na tene!
english: They answered Nebuchadnezzar the king, “O king, live for ever!

kikuyu: Hĩ! Kaĩ wee njata ya kĩwariĩ nĩũgwĩte ũkoima igũrũ-ĩ, o wee mũrũ wa rũciinĩ kĩroko gwakĩa! Ũharũrũkĩtio ũgaikio gũkũ thĩ, o wee ũrĩa hĩndĩ ĩmwe wahinyagĩrĩria ndũrĩrĩ!
english: How you have fallen from heaven, shining one, son of the dawn! How you are cut down to the ground, who laid the nations low!

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo magĩĩtĩkia ciĩranĩro ciake, na makĩmũinĩra, makĩmũgooca.
english: Then they believed his words. They sang his praise.

kikuyu: Na rĩrĩa maanyuuaga ndibei-rĩ, mũthamaki akĩũria Esiteri o ro rĩngĩ atĩrĩ, “Ihooya rĩaku nĩ rĩrĩkũ? Nĩũkũhingĩrio. Na nĩ kĩĩ ũrooria ũheo? O na wahooya ũheo nuthu ya kũrĩa guothe thamakaga-rĩ, nĩũkũheo.”
english: The king said to Esther at the banquet of wine, “What is your petition? It shall be granted you. What is your request? Even to the half of the kingdom it shall be performed.”

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo Zedekia mũrũ wa Kenaana agĩkuhĩrĩria na akĩgũtha Mikaya rũhĩ rwa ũthiũ. Akĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Kaĩ roho wa Jehova aragereire kũ rĩrĩa aroimire kũrĩ niĩ, nĩguo aarie nawe?”
english: Then Zedekiah the son of Chenaanah came near and struck Micaiah on the cheek, and said, “Which way did Yahweh’s Spirit go from me to speak to you?”

kikuyu: Nake Nathanieli akĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Kũrĩ kĩndũ kĩega kĩngiuma Nazarethi?” Nake Filipu akĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Ũka ũkoone.”
english: Nathanael said to him, “Can any good thing come out of Nazareth?” Philip said to him, “Come and see.”

kikuyu: Kuuma kũrĩ njiaro cia Kohathu, Urieli nĩwe warĩ mũtongoria, na andũ a nyũmba yao 120;
english: of the sons of Kohath, Uriel the chief, and his brothers one hundred twenty;

kikuyu: nĩgeetha kuuma irathĩro rĩa riũa nginya ithũĩro rĩarĩo, andũ mamenye atĩ gũtirĩ ũngĩ tiga niĩ. Niĩ nĩ niĩ Jehova, na gũtirĩ ũngĩ.
english: that they may know from the rising of the sun, and from the west, that there is no one besides me. I am Yahweh, and there is no one else.

kikuyu: Mũthenya wa ĩtatũ-rĩ, hagĩũka mũndũ oimĩte kambĩ-inĩ ya Saũlũ, arĩ na nguo ndembũkangu na rũkũngũ mũtwe. Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa aakinyire harĩ Daudi, akĩĩgũithia thĩ nĩguo amũhe gĩtĩĩo.
english: on the third day, behold,† a man came out of the camp from Saul, with his clothes torn and earth on his head. When he came to David, he fell to the earth and showed respect.

kikuyu: O na tũhĩĩ tũrĩa tũnini nĩtũũnyararĩte, rĩrĩa ndatuumĩrĩra no gũũthekerera tũũthekagĩrĩra.
english: Even young children despise me. If I arise, they speak against me.

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Jefitha akĩũrĩra ariũ a ithe, agĩthiĩ gũtũũra bũrũri wa Tobu, kũrĩa gĩkundi kĩa andũ matarĩ kĩene kĩamũrigiicĩirie na gĩkĩmũrũmĩrĩra.
english: Then Jephthah fled from his brothers and lived in the land of Tob. Outlaws joined up with Jephthah, and they went out with him.

kikuyu: Maũndũ- inĩ marĩa mothe ndeekire, nĩndamuonirie atĩ no nginya tũteithagie andũ arĩa matarĩ hinya, na ũndũ wa kũruta wĩra mũritũ ta ũcio, tũkĩririkanaga ciugo iria ciaririo nĩ Mwathani Jesũ we mwene rĩrĩa oigire atĩrĩ, ‘Kũheana nĩ kũrĩ irathimo nyingĩ gũkĩra kũheo.’ ”
english: In all things I gave you an example, that so laboring you ought to help the weak, and to remember the words of the Lord Jesus, that he himself said, ‘It is more blessed to give than to receive.’ ”

kikuyu: Ageetete ũta wake o ta thũ; guoko gwake kwa ũrĩo nĩ kwĩhaarĩirie. Arĩa othe maatũire makenagia maitho-rĩ, amooragĩte o ta marĩ thũ; hema ya Mwarĩ wa Zayuni amĩitĩrĩirie mangʼũrĩ make mahaana ta mwaki.
english: He has bent his bow like an enemy. He has stood with his right hand as an adversary.

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ wa ũhoro wakwa-rĩ, nĩmũgatwarwo mbere ya aathani na athamaki nĩgeetha mũtuĩke aira kũrĩ o na kũrĩ andũ-a-Ndũrĩrĩ.
english: Yes, and you will be brought before governors and kings for my sake, for a testimony to them and to the nations.

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Daudi agĩthiĩ Baali-Perazimu, na agĩtooreria Afilisti kuo. Akiuga atĩrĩ, “O ta ũrĩa maaĩ moinaga, noguo Jehova aharaganĩtie thũ ciakwa mbere yakwa.” Nĩ ũndũ ũcio handũ hau hagĩĩtwo Baali-Perazimu.
english: David came to Baal Perazim, and David struck them there. Then he said, “Yahweh has broken my enemies before me, like the breach of waters.” Therefore he called the name of that place Baal Perazim.†

kikuyu: Jehova nĩarĩkũgitagĩra kuuma ũũru-inĩ wothe: nĩarĩĩmenyagĩrĩra muoyo waku;
english: Yahweh will keep you from all evil. He will keep your soul.

kikuyu: Agĩcooka akĩhũgũkĩra arutwo ake, akĩmeera marĩ oiki atĩrĩ, “Kũrathimwo nĩ maitho marĩa marona kĩrĩa mũrona.
english: Turning to the disciples, he said privately, “Blessed are the eyes which see the things that you see,

kikuyu: Nao makĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Arutwo a Johana nĩ meehingaga kũrĩa irio kaingĩ kaingĩ makahooya, o nao arutwo a Afarisai nĩmekaga o ũguo, no aku marĩĩaga na makanyua hĩndĩ ciothe.”
english: They said to him, “Why do John’s disciples often fast and pray, likewise also the disciples of the Pharisees, but yours eat and drink?”

kikuyu: Indo iria aarutire ciarĩ thaani ĩmwe ya betha ya ũritũ wa cekeri igana rĩa mĩrongo ĩtatũ, na mbakũri ĩmwe ya betha ya kũminjaminjĩria ya ũritũ wa cekeri mĩrongo mũgwanja, cierĩ kũringana na gĩthimo gĩa cekeri ya harĩa haamũre, na o ĩmwe ĩiyũrĩtio mũtu ũrĩa mũhinyu mũno ũtukanĩtio
english: gave his offering: one silver platter, the weight of which was one hundred thirty shekels, one silver bowl of seventy shekels, according to the shekel of the sanctuary, both of them full of fine flour mixed with oil for a meal offering;

kikuyu: Nake ithe Zakaria akĩiyũrwo nĩ Roho Mũtheru, akĩratha ũhoro, akiuga atĩrĩ,
english: His father Zacharias was filled with the Holy Spirit, and prophesied, saying,

kikuyu: Ũgacooka ũkarigiicũka ũrorete na mwena wa irathĩro, ũgakinya Bethi-Dagoni, ũkahutia Zebuluni na Gĩtuamba kĩa Ifita-Eli, ũgacooka ũkerekera mwena wa gathigathini ũgakinya Bethi-Emeki na Neieli, ũhĩtũkĩire Kabuli mwena wa ũmotho.
english: It turned toward the sunrise to Beth Dagon, and reached to Zebulun, and to the valley of Iphtah El northward to Beth Emek and Neiel. It went out to Cabul on the left hand,

kikuyu: Na niĩ ngĩona araika mũgwanja arĩa marũgamaga mbere ya Ngai, nao makĩheo tũrumbeta mũgwanja.*
english: I saw the seven angels who stand before God, and seven trumpets were given to them.

kikuyu: Maũndũ macio mothe maraaria ũhoro wa indo iria ithiraga bata ciarĩkia kũhũthĩrwo, tondũ icio no irĩra na wathani na ũrutani wa andũ.
english: (all of which perish with use), according to the precepts and doctrines of men?

kikuyu: Josia akĩrehe athĩnjĩri-Ngai othe kuuma matũũra-inĩ mothe ma Juda, na agĩtharia kũndũ kũrĩa gũtũũgĩru, kuuma Geba nginya Birishiba kũrĩa athĩnjĩri-ngai maacinagĩra ũbumba, akĩmomora igongona iria ciarĩ ihingo-inĩ, o na itoonyero-inĩ rĩa Kĩhingo kĩa Joshua, mũnene wa itũũra rĩrĩa inene, kĩrĩa kĩarĩ mwena wa ũmotho wa kĩhingo gĩa itũũra rĩu inene.
english: He brought all the priests out of the cities of Judah, and defiled the high places where the priests had burned incense, from Geba to Beersheba; and he broke down the high places of the gates that were at the entrance of the gate of Joshua the governor of the city, which were on a man’s left hand at the gate of the city.

kikuyu: ngũheana taranda ngiri ithatũ* cia thahabu, o thahabu ĩrĩa ya Ofiri, na taranda ngiri mũgwanja† cia betha ĩrĩa therie nĩ ũndũ wa kũgemia thingo cia nyũmba,
english: even three thousand talents of gold,† of the gold of Ophir, and seven thousand talents‡ of refined silver, with which to overlay the walls of the houses;

kikuyu: Samusoni akiuga atĩrĩ, “Reke ngue hamwe na Afilisti!” Agĩcooka agĩcitindĩka na hinya wake wothe, nayo hekarũ ĩkĩgwĩra aathani acio hamwe na andũ othe arĩa maarĩ thĩinĩ wayo. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio akĩũraga andũ aingĩ agĩkua gũkĩra arĩa ooragire arĩ muoyo.
english: Samson said, “Let me die with the Philistines!” He bowed himself with all his might; and the house fell on the lords, and on all the people who were in it. So the dead that he killed at his death were more than those who he killed in his life.

kikuyu: orwo Jehova ndaakoretwo arĩ mwena witũ; Isiraeli nĩmakiuge ũguo:
english: f it had not been Yahweh who was on our side, let Israel now say,

kikuyu: Na o na mbere ya maguta gũcinwo-rĩ, ndungata ya mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai nĩyokaga na ĩkeera mũndũ ũrĩa ũraruta igongona atĩrĩ, “He mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai nyama imwe ahĩĩhie; ndegwĩtĩkĩra nyama nduge kuuma kũrĩ we, tiga o nyama njĩthĩ.”*
english: Yes, before they burned the fat, the priest’s servant came, and said to the man who sacrificed, “Give meat to roast for the priest; for he will not accept boiled meat from you, but raw.”

kikuyu: Andũ aku nĩo magaacookereria kũrĩa kwaanangĩtwo o tene, na maakĩrĩre mĩthingi ĩrĩa ya tene mũno; nawe ũgeetagwo Mũcookereria wa Thingo iria Momoku, na wĩtagwo Mũcookereria wa Njĩra cia Itũũro.
english: Those who will be of you will build the old waste places. You will raise up the foundations of many generations. You will be called Repairer of the Breach, Restorer of Paths with Dwellings.

kikuyu: Nĩ tondũ wanyu-rĩ, Zayuni gũgaaciimbwo ta mũgũnda, Jerusalemu gũtuĩke hĩba ya mahiga, nakĩo kĩrĩma kĩrĩa gĩakĩtwo hekarũ gĩtuĩke kĩhumbu kĩmereirwo nĩ ihinga.
english: Therefore Zion for your sake will be plowed like a field, and Jerusalem will become heaps of rubble, and the mountain of the temple like the high places of a forest.

kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, hau nĩ haarĩ na mũtumia watũire oiraga thakame mĩaka ikũmi na ĩĩrĩ.
english: A certain woman who had a discharge of blood for twelve years,

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo nĩamwĩriragĩria inyuothe, na nĩaratangĩka tondũ nĩmwaiguĩte atĩ aarĩ mũrũaru.
english: since he longed for you all, and was very troubled because you had heard that he was sick.

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio mũngĩkorwo mũtarĩ ehokeku na ũtonga wa gũkũ thĩ-rĩ, nũũ ũngĩmwĩhokera ũtonga ũrĩa wa ma?
english: If therefore you have not been faithful in the unrighteous mammon, who will commit to your trust the true riches?

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo niĩ ndaruithirio ndĩ na ũkũrũ wa thikũ inyanya, na ndĩ wa rũciaro rwa andũ a Isiraeli, na wa mũhĩrĩga wa Benjamini, na ndĩ Mũhibirania waciarĩtwo nĩ Ahibirania. Ha ũhoro ũkoniĩ watho-rĩ, ndĩ Mũfarisai;
english: circumcised the eighth day, of the stock of Israel, of the tribe of Benjamin, a Hebrew of Hebrews; concerning the law, a Pharisee;

kikuyu: Mwena wa gũthini bũrũri warĩ wa Efiraimu, naguo mwena wa gathigathini warĩ wa Manase. Bũrũri wa Manase wakinyĩte iria-inĩ, na ũkahakana na bũrũri wa Asheri mwena wa gathigathini, na Isakaru mwena wa irathĩro.
english: Southward it was Ephraim’s, and northward it was Manasseh’s, and the sea was his border. They reached to Asher on the north, and to Issachar on the east.

kikuyu: ya gũcora mĩcoro ya ũũgĩ indo-inĩ cia thahabu, na betha na gĩcango, na
english: to devise skillful works, to work in gold, and in silver, and in bronze,

kikuyu: Nake akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Gũtirĩ na thĩĩna, tigai gwĩtigĩra. Ngai wanyu, o we Ngai wa ithe wanyu, nĩwe wamũheire kĩgĩĩna makũnia-inĩ manyu; betha cianyu nĩndacĩamũkĩire.” Agĩcooka akĩmarehera Simeoni.
english: He said, “Peace be to you. Don’t be afraid. Your God, and the God of your father, has given you treasure in your sacks. I received your money.” He brought Simeon out to them.

kikuyu: Nayo mbũtũ ya ita ya Babuloni ĩkĩnyiita Mũthamaki Zedekia. Makĩmũtwara kũrĩ mũthamaki wa Babuloni ũrĩa warĩ kũu Ribila, bũrũri-inĩ wa Hamathu, na kũu nĩkuo aamũtuĩrĩire ciira.
english: Then they took the king, and carried him up to the king of Babylon to Riblah in the land of Hamath; and he pronounced judgment on him.

kikuyu: “O ta ũrĩa wĩtanaga mũthenya wa gĩathĩ-rĩ, noguo ũnjĩtĩire maũndũ ma kũnguoyohia, makandigiicĩria mĩena yothe o taarĩ mũthenya wa gĩathĩ. Mũthenya wa marakara ma Jehova-rĩ,
english: “You have called, as in the day of a solemn assembly, my terrors on every side. There was no one that escaped or remained in the day of Yahweh’s anger. My enemy has consumed those whom

kikuyu: Nĩ harĩ ũmwe wĩyumĩrĩtie thĩinĩ waku wee Nineve, ũrĩa ũthugundaga ũũru wa gũũkĩrĩra Jehova, na akaheanaga irĩra cia waganu.
english: One has gone out of you who devises evil against Yahweh, who counsels wickedness.

kikuyu: Nĩmarutaga wĩra wa gũtungatĩra Ngai wao, na wĩra wa gwĩtheria, o ta ũrĩa aini na aikaria a ihingo meekaga, kũringana na ũrĩa gwathanĩtwo nĩ Daudi na mũriũ Solomoni.
english: They performed the duty of their God and the duty of the purification, and so did the singers and the gatekeepers, according to the commandment of David and of Solomon his son.

kikuyu: “Rĩrĩa andũ aku a Isiraeli mahootwo nĩ thũ nĩ ũndũ nĩmakũhĩtĩirie, nao magũcookerere na moimbũre rĩĩtwa rĩaku, na mahooe magĩgũthaithaga marĩ hekarũ-inĩ ĩno-rĩ,
english: “When your people Israel are struck down before the enemy because they have sinned against you, if they turn again to you and confess your name, and pray and make supplication to you in this house,

kikuyu: Ciũra icio ikũrũgĩrĩre na irũgĩrĩre andũ aku o na anene aku.’ ”
english: The frogs shall come up both on you, and on your people, and on all your servants.” ’ ”

kikuyu: na Shaalabini, na Aijaloni, na Ithila,
english: Shaalabbin, Aijalon, Ithlah,

kikuyu: O nawe mwĩmenyerere, tondũ nĩakararirie ndũmĩrĩri iitũ na hinya.
english: Beware of him, for he greatly opposed our words.

kikuyu: Mĩtaro ya maaĩ nĩĩkanunga; tũrũũĩ twa Misiri nĩtũkaahũa tũũme biũ. Namo marura na mathanjĩ nĩmakahooha,
english: The rivers will become foul. The streams of Egypt will be diminished and dried up. The reeds and flags will wither away.

kikuyu: “Mũndũ mũrũme kana mũndũ-wa-nja angĩkorwo na kĩronda mũtwe kana kĩreru-rĩ,
english: “When a man or woman has a plague on the head or on the beard,

kikuyu: na cia Harimu ciarĩ 1,017.
english: The children of Harim: one thousand seventeen.

kikuyu: Ngwa yaku yaiguĩkanire kĩhuhũkanio-inĩ, rũheni rwaku rũkĩmũrĩka thĩ yothe, nayo thĩ ĩkĩinaina na ĩgĩthingitha.
english: The voice of your thunder was in the whirlwind. The lightnings lit up the world. The earth trembled and shook.

kikuyu: O na ningĩ magĩthondeka maguta matheru ma gũitanĩrĩrio na ũbumba mũnungi wega ũrĩa mũtherie, wĩra wa mũthondeki mũũgĩ wa maguta.
english: He made the holy anointing oil and the pure incense of sweet spices, after the art of the perfumer.

kikuyu: “Ndagũthaitha, amba ũgerie ndungata ciaku handũ ha mĩthenya ikũmi: Ndũgaatũhe kĩndũ o nakĩ tiga o nyeni cia kũrĩa na maaĩ ma kũnyua.
english: “Test your servants, I beg you, ten days; and let them give us vegetables to eat and water to drink.

kikuyu: na Elamu aarĩ wa gatano, na Jehohanani aarĩ wa gatandatũ, na Eliehoenai aarĩ wa mũgwanja.
english: Elam the fifth, Jehohanan the sixth, and Eliehoenai the seventh.

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Gaali agĩtongoria atũũri a Shekemu makĩhũũrana na Abimeleku.
english: Gaal went out before the men of Shechem, and fought with Abimelech.

kikuyu: Nake Amasa eegaragaragia thakame- inĩ yake arĩ njĩra gatagatĩ, nake mũndũ ũcio akĩona atĩ mbũtũ ciothe cia ita nĩciarũgamaga hau. Rĩrĩa
english: Amasa lay wallowing in his blood in the middle of the highway. When the man saw that all the people stood still, he carried Amasa out of the highway into the field, and cast a garment over him when he saw that everyone who came by him stood still.

kikuyu: Nake nĩahotaga gũkirĩrĩria arĩa matarĩ na ũmenyo o na arĩa maturuurĩte, kuona atĩ o nake nĩarĩ ũhũthũ wake.
english: The high priest can deal gently with those who are ignorant and going astray, because he himself is also surrounded with weakness.

kikuyu: Rĩngĩkũrĩra handũ hatarĩ maaĩ rĩũmaga narua gũkĩra nyeki,
english: While it is yet in its greenness, not cut down, it withers before any other reed.

kikuyu: Mũisiraeli ũcio woragirwo marĩ na mũndũ-wa-nja Mũmidiani eetagwo Zimuri mũrũ wa Salu, mũtongoria wa nyũmba ya Asimeoni.
english: Now the name of the man of Israel that was slain, who was slain with the Midianite woman, was Zimri, the son of Salu, a prince of a fathers’ house among the Simeonites.

kikuyu: Atĩrĩrĩ inyuĩ andũ a ndũrĩrĩ, ugĩrĩriai mbugĩrĩrio ya mbaara, na no mũgũthetherwo! Ta thikĩrĩriai inyuĩ mabũrũri ma kũraya. Haarĩriai mbaara, na no mũgũthetherwo! Haarĩriai mbaara, na no mũgũthetherwo!
english: Make an uproar, you peoples, and be broken in pieces! Listen, all you from far countries: dress for battle, and be shattered! Dress for battle, and be shattered!

kikuyu: Mathaai Jehova hĩndĩ ĩrĩa angĩoneka; mũkaĩrei hĩndĩ ĩrĩa arĩ hakuhĩ.
english: Seek Yahweh while he may be found. Call on him while he is near.

kikuyu: Tũnua twacio tũtiaragia ũhoro ũngĩĩhokwo; ngoro ciacio ciyũrĩte mwanangĩko. Mĩmero yacio nĩ ta mbĩrĩra ngunũre; ciaragia maheeni na rũrĩmĩ rwacio.
english: For there is no faithfulness in their mouth. Their heart is destruction. Their throat is an open tomb. They flatter with their tongue.

kikuyu: “Wee nĩwe ũheaga mbarathi hinya wayo, kana ũkamĩhumba ngingo na mũreera?
english: “Have you given the horse might? Have you clothed his neck with a quivering mane?

kikuyu: Nao matigacooka kũruta wĩra wa tũhũ, o na kana maciare ciana ciathĩrĩirio mũtino; nĩgũkorwo magaatuĩka andũ arathime nĩ Jehova, o hamwe na njiaro ciao.
english: They will not labor in vain nor give birth for calamity; for they are the offspring of Yahweh’s blessed and their descendants with them.

kikuyu: Maaĩ magĩcookana, magĩthika ngaari cia ita na andũ arĩa mathiiaga mahaicĩte mbarathi; nĩo mbũtũ yothe ya Firaũni ĩrĩa yarũmĩrĩire andũ a Isiraeli iria-inĩ. Gũtirĩ o na ũmwe wao wahonokire.
english: The waters returned, and covered the chariots and the horsemen, even all Pharaoh’s army that went in after them into the sea. There remained not so much as one of them.

kikuyu: Nĩamenyithirie Musa njĩra ciake, nacio ciĩko ciake agĩcimenyithia andũ a Isiraeli:
english: He made known his ways to Moses, his deeds to the children of Israel.

kikuyu: Maarĩkia kwamũkĩra hema-rĩ, maithe maitũ, matongoretio nĩ Joshua magĩũka nayo rĩrĩa menyiitĩire bũrũri kuuma kũrĩ ndũrĩrĩ iria Ngai aarutũrũrire ciehere mbere yao. Nayo ĩgĩtũũra kũu bũrũri ũcio o nginya hĩndĩ ya Daudi,
english: which also our fathers, in their turn, brought in with Joshua when they entered into the possession of the nations whom God drove out before the face of our fathers to the days of David,

kikuyu: “ ‘Na rĩrĩ, mũhaka wanyu wa mwena wa irathĩro, ũkoima Hazoru-Enani ũkinye Shefamu.
english: “ ‘You shall mark out your east border from Hazar Enan to Shepham.

kikuyu: Na andũ gĩkundi kĩnene mũno makĩara nguo ciao njĩra-inĩ, nao arĩa angĩ magĩtua mathĩgĩ ma mĩtĩ makĩmaara njĩra-inĩ.
english: A very great multitude spread their clothes on the road. Others cut branches from the trees and spread them on the road.

kikuyu: “Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Jehova ekuuga ũũ igũrũ rĩa mũthamaki wa Ashuri: “Ndagatoonya itũũra rĩĩrĩ inene, kana arathũkie mũguĩ kuo. Ndagooka mbere ya rĩo na ngo,
english: “Therefore Yahweh says concerning the king of Assyria, ‘He will not come to this city, nor shoot an arrow there. He will not come before it with shield, nor cast up a mound against it.

kikuyu: Ariũ a Saũlũ maarĩ Jonathani, na Ishivi, na Maliki-Shua. Mwarĩ wake ũrĩa mũkũrũ eetagwo Merabu, na ũrĩa mũnini eetagwo Mikali.
english: Now the sons of Saul were Jonathan, Ishvi, and Malchishua; and the names of his two daughters were these: the name of the firstborn Merab, and the name of the younger Michal.

kikuyu: Nake Joabu agĩtiga gũtengʼeria Abineri, akĩhũndũka agĩcookanĩrĩria andũ ake othe. Andũ ikũmi na kenda a Daudi makĩoneka matiarĩ ho, Asaheli atatarĩtwo.
english: Joab returned from following Abner; and when he had gathered all the people together, nineteen men of David’s and Asahel were missing.

kikuyu: Ngũrongoragia na ngoro yakwa yothe; ndũkanareke njũrũũre ndigane na maathani maku.
english: With my whole heart I have sought you. Don’t let me wander from your commandments.

kikuyu: Nũũ waciirĩire atĩ itũũra rĩa Turo rĩũkĩrĩrwo, o itũũra rĩu rĩheanaga tanji cia ũthamaki, o rĩo onjorithia a rĩo arĩ ariũ a athamaki, na endia a rĩo marĩ igweta thĩ yothe?
english: Who has planned this against Tyre, the giver of crowns, whose merchants are princes, whose traders are the honorable of the earth?

kikuyu: Nake Jesũ akĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Kwandĩkĩtwo atĩa Watho-inĩ? Wee-rĩ, kaĩ ũthomaga atĩa?”
english: He said to him, “What is written in the law? How do you read it?”

kikuyu: na mũthamaki wa Jaramuthu, ũmwe, na mũthamaki wa Lakishi, ũmwe,
english: the king of Jarmuth, one; the king of Lachish, one;

kikuyu: ya Joiaribu, aarĩ Matenai; ya Jedaia, aarĩ Uzi;
english: of Joiarib, Mattenai; of Jedaiah, Uzzi;

kikuyu: Makĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Ũũ nĩguo Hezekia ekuuga: Mũthenya wa ũmũthĩ nĩ mũthenya wa mĩnyamaro, na ikũũmana, na gĩconoko, o ta ihinda rĩa ciana rĩa gũciarwo rĩakinya na hinya wa gũciara ũkaaga.
english: They said to him, “Hezekiah says, ‘Today is a day of trouble, of rebuke, and of rejection; for the children have come to the point of birth, and there is no strength to deliver them.
