kikuyu: na itambaya cia gũcuurio iria cia kũirigĩra nja ĩrĩa ĩthiũrũrũkĩirie Hema-ĩyo-Nyamũre na kĩgongona, na gĩtambaya gĩa gũcuurio gĩa itoonyero-inĩ, na mĩhĩndo na indo ciothe itũmagĩrwo ũtungata-inĩ wayo. Agerishoni acio nĩo marĩrutaga wĩra wothe ũrĩa wagĩrĩire kũrutwo na indo icio.
english: the hangings of the court, the screen for the door of the gate of the court which is by the tabernacle and around the altar, their cords, and all the instruments of their service, and whatever shall be done with them. They shall serve in there.

kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, tũma andũ mathiĩ Jopa magĩĩre mũndũ wĩtagwo Simoni, na nowe wĩtagwo Petero.
english: Now send men to Joppa, and get Simon, who is also called Peter.

kikuyu: Mehia marĩa meekĩirwo Peori matitũiganĩte? Nginya ũmũthĩ ũyũ tũtirĩ twetheria mehia macio, o na gũtuĩka mũthiro nĩwokire igũrũ rĩa kĩrĩndĩ kĩa Jehova!
english: Is the iniquity of Peor too little for us, from which we have not cleansed ourselves to this day, although there came a plague on the congregation of Yahweh,

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio mũthamaki ndaigana gũthikĩrĩria andũ acio, tondũ maũndũ macio meekĩkire moimĩte kũrĩ Jehova, nĩguo kiugo kĩhingio kĩrĩa Jehova eerire Jeroboamu mũrũ wa Nebati na kanua ka Ahija ũrĩa Mũshiloni.
english: So the king didn’t listen to the people; for it was a thing brought about from Yahweh, that he might establish his word, which Yahweh spoke by Ahijah the Shilonite to Jeroboam the son of Nebat.

kikuyu: Angĩ maagereire iria-inĩ marĩ marikabu-inĩ; maathiiaga wonjoria kũu maria-inĩ manene.
english: Those who go down to the sea in ships, who do business in great waters,

kikuyu: Mĩbango ya ngoro ya mũndũ nĩ mĩingĩ, no muoroto wa Jehova noguo ũkaahinga.
english: There are many plans in a man’s heart, but Yahweh’s counsel will prevail.

kikuyu: tondũ nĩmamũtuire thũ ciao rĩrĩa maamũheenirie ũhoro-inĩ wa kũhoera kũu Peori na wa mwarĩ wa nyina wao Kozibi, mwarĩ wa mũtongoria Mũmidiani, mũndũ-wa-nja ũrĩa woragirwo rĩrĩa mũthiro wokire nĩ ũndũ wa Peori.”
english: for they harassed you with their wiles, wherein they have deceived you in the matter of Peor, and in the incident regarding Cozbi, the daughter of the prince of Midian, their sister, who was slain on the day of the plague in the matter of Peor.”

kikuyu: Mũthuuri wakwa ndarĩ mũciĩ; nĩathiĩte rũgendo rũraihu.
english: For my husband isn’t at home. He has gone on a long journey.

kikuyu: Nĩndĩraikũrũkire mũgũnda wa mĩkombokombo, ngarore tũmĩtĩ tũrĩa tũmerete kũu gĩtuamba-inĩ, nĩguo nyone kana mĩthabibũ nĩĩthundũrĩte, o na kana mĩkomamanga nĩĩrutĩte kĩro.
english: I went down into the nut tree grove, to see the green plants of the valley, to see whether the vine budded, and the pomegranates were in flower.

kikuyu: Mĩthiĩre ya mũndũ kĩmaramari no ya waganu, athugundaga o maũndũ mooru ma kũniinithia andũ arĩa athĩĩni na ũhoro wa maheeni, o na rĩrĩa mathaithana ma mũbatari marĩ ma kĩhooto.
english: The ways of the scoundrel are evil. He devises wicked plans to destroy the humble with lying words, even when the needy speaks right.

kikuyu: O na agĩthima mwena wa gũthini; mũigana waho warĩ mĩkono magana matano ũthimĩtwo na rũthanju rũu rwa gũthima.
english: He measured on the south side five hundred reeds with the measuring reed.

kikuyu: Mathiiaga magĩkĩragĩrĩria kũgĩa na hinya, nginya o mũndũ o mũndũ agakinya mbere ya Ngai kũu Zayuni.
english: They go from strength to strength. Every one of them appears before God in Zion.

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo thiritũ ya anabii a kũu Betheli magĩũka kũrĩ Elisha, makĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Nĩũmenyete atĩ Jehova nĩekuoya mwathi waku amweherie harĩwe ũmũthĩ?” Nake Elisha akĩmacookeria atĩrĩ, “Ĩĩ nĩnjũũĩ, no mũtikaarie ũhoro ũcio.”
english: The sons of the prophets who were at Bethel came out to Elisha, and said to him, “Do you know that Yahweh will take away your master from over you today?” He said, “Yes, I know it. Hold your peace.”

kikuyu: Ningĩ Solomoni agĩaka Handũ-harĩa-Hatheru-Mũno, ũraihu waho waringaine na wariĩ wa hekarũ, mĩkono mĩrongo ĩĩrĩ kũraiha na mĩkono mĩrongo ĩĩrĩ kwarama. Akĩgemia thĩinĩ waho na taranda 600§ cia thahabu ĩrĩa njega mũno.
english: He made the most holy place. Its length, according to the width of the house, was twenty cubits, and its width twenty cubits; and he overlaid it with fine gold, amounting to six hundred talents.‡

kikuyu: Aakorwo nĩ mũndũ wa mĩaka mĩrongo ĩtandatũ kana makĩrĩa, ũgaatua irĩhi rĩa mũndũ mũrũme rĩtuĩke nĩ cekeri ikũmi na ithano,§ na rĩa mũndũ-wa-nja nĩ cekeri ikũmi.*
english: If the person is from sixty years old and upward; if he is a male, then your valuation shall be fifteen shekels, and for a female ten shekels.

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio mwaka-inĩ wa kenda wa ũthamaki wa Zedekia, mũthenya wa ikũmi wa mweri wa ikũmi-rĩ, Nebukadinezaru mũthamaki wa Babuloni agĩthiĩ kũhũũrana na Jerusalemu arĩ na ita rĩake rĩothe, nao makĩamba hema nja ya itũũra rĩu inene, na magĩaka ihumbu cia tĩĩri mĩena yothe yarĩo cia kũmateithia gũtharĩkĩra itũũra rĩu.
english: In the ninth year of his reign, in the tenth month, in the tenth day of the month, Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon came, he and all his army, against Jerusalem, and encamped against it; and they built forts against it round about.

kikuyu: Jehova ekuuga atĩrĩ: “Ta rorai muone, nĩ harĩ na ita rĩroka riumĩte bũrũri wa mwena wa gathigathini; rũrĩrĩ rũnene nĩ rwarahũrĩtwo kuuma ituri-inĩ cia thĩ.
english: Yahweh says, “Behold, a people comes from the north country. A great nation will be stirred up from the uttermost parts of the earth.

kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, o rũrĩrĩ rũgĩĩthondekera ngai ciaruo matũũra-inĩ mothe marĩa maatũũraga, magĩciiga mahooero-inĩ marĩa maathondeketwo nĩ andũ a Samaria kũndũ kũrĩa gũtũũgĩru.
english: However every nation made gods of their own, and put them in the houses of the high places which the Samaritans had made, every nation in their cities in which they lived.

kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, nĩ kwagĩire ngʼaragu bũrũri ũcio, nake Aburamu agĩikũrũka athiĩ Misiri agaikare kuo kwa ihinda, tondũ ngʼaragu nĩ yarĩ nene mũno.†
english: There was a famine in the land. Abram went down into Egypt to live as a foreigner there, for the famine was severe in the land.

kikuyu: Jusufu agĩkĩigithia ngano nyingĩ mũno, o ta mũthanga wa iria-inĩ; yarĩ nyingĩ mũno, o nginya agĩtiga kũiga maandĩko ma mũigana wayo tondũ ndĩngĩathimĩkire.
english: Joseph laid up grain as the sand of the sea, very much, until he stopped counting, for it was without number.

kikuyu: Magĩcooka makĩĩrana atĩrĩ, “Rĩu tũtireka wega. Mũthenya ũyũ nĩ wa kũheana ũhoro mwega, na ithuĩ no gũkira tũkirĩte naguo. Tũngĩeterera nginya gũkĩe, no tũherithio. Rekei tũthiĩ o ro rĩu, tũkaheane ũhoro ũyũ nyũmba-inĩ ya mũthamaki.”
english: Then they said to one another, “We aren’t doing right. Today is a day of good news, and we keep silent. If we wait until the morning light, punishment will overtake us. Now therefore come, let’s go and tell the king’s household.”

kikuyu: No rĩu no nginya mwĩaũre maũndũ maya mothe, namo nĩ, marakara, na mangʼũrĩ, na rũmena, na njambanio, na kwariamaũndũmathoninakanua.
english: but now you must put them all away: anger, wrath, malice, slander, and shameful speaking out of your mouth.

kikuyu: Matũũra marĩa maarĩ mũthia wa gũthini ma mũhĩrĩga wa Juda thĩinĩ wa Negevu kwerekera mũhaka wa Edomu maarĩ maya: Kabizeeli, na Ederi, na Jaguri,
english: The farthest cities of the tribe of the children of Judah toward the border of Edom in the South were Kabzeel, Eder, Jagur,

kikuyu: “Andũ arĩa othe matarĩ na ũũgĩ nĩmoke gũkũ!” Ningĩ akeera andũ arĩa maagĩĩte ũtaũku atĩrĩ,
english: “Whoever is simple, let him turn in here!” As for him who is void of understanding, she says to him,

kikuyu: No amwe ao magĩthiĩ kũrĩ Afarisai, makĩmeera ũrĩa Jesũ ekĩte.
english: But some of them went away to the Pharisees and told them the things which Jesus had done.

kikuyu: ake Zofaru ũrĩa Mũnaamathi agĩcookia atĩrĩ:
english: hen Zophar the Naamathite answered,

kikuyu: Ũtukũ ũmwe-rĩ, o ũmwe witũ akĩroota kĩroto, na o kĩroto kĩarĩ na ũtaũri wakĩo mwanya.
english: We dreamed a dream in one night, he and I. Each man dreamed according to the interpretation of his dream.

kikuyu: ake Joshua mũrũ wa Nuni agĩtũma athigaani eerĩ kuuma Shitimu na hitho; akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Thiĩi mũbaare bũrũri ũcio, na makĩria Jeriko.”* Nĩ ũndũ ũcio magĩthiĩ, magĩtoonya nyũmba ya mũmaraya wetagwo Rahabu, magĩikara kuo. * 1:2 Hĩndĩ ĩno andũ a Isiraeli maarĩ Shitimu (2:1), werũ-inĩ wa Moabi (Gũcook 34:3) gũkuhĩ na Rũũĩ rwa Jorodani. * 2:1 Jeriko rĩarĩ itũũra rĩa tene, na rĩairigĩtwo na thingo cia hinya. Rĩarĩ ta kilomita 8 mwena wa ithũĩro wa Rũũĩ rwa Jorodani.
english: oshua the son of Nun secretly sent two men out of Shittim as spies, saying, “Go, view the land, including Jericho.” They went and came into the house of a prostitute whose name was Rahab, and slept there.  “Yahweh” is God’s proper Name, sometimes rendered “LORD” (all caps) in other translations.  The Hebrew word rendered “God” is “אֱֹלהִ ֑ים” (Elohim).  “Behold”, from “הִ ͏נֵּה”, means look at, take notice, observe, see, or gaze at. It is often used as an interjection.

kikuyu: Thũ nĩũcikinyĩirie mwanangĩko ũtathiraga, nĩwanangĩte matũũra macio manene; o na kũririkanwo kwao nĩgũthirĩte.
english: The enemy is overtaken by endless ruin. The very memory of the cities which you have overthrown has perished.

kikuyu: Ĩĩ-ni, o na nĩngũkũũria, wee mũrata wakwa wa ma, wee tũrutithanagia wĩra ta tuohetwo icooki rĩmwe, teithagia atumia acio, arĩa maarũĩrĩire Ũhoro-ũrĩa-Mwega marĩ hamwe na niĩ, o na Klementi na andũ arĩa angĩ marutithanagia wĩra hamwe na niĩ, arĩa marĩĩtwa mao mandĩkĩtwo ibuku-inĩ rĩa muoyo.
english: Yes, I beg you also, true partner, help these women, for they labored with me in the Good News with Clement also, and the rest of my fellow workers, whose names are in the book of life.

kikuyu: Ningĩ Firaũni akĩĩra Jusufu atĩrĩ, “Nĩ niĩ Firaũni, no rĩrĩ, hatarĩ na rũtha rwaku, gũtirĩ mũndũ ũgeeka ũndũ atarĩ na rũtha rwaku gũkũ Misiri guothe.”
english: Pharaoh said to Joseph, “I am Pharaoh. Without you, no man shall lift up his hand or his foot in all the land of Egypt.”

kikuyu: Akomaga ũtukũ na agokĩra mũthenya, nacio mbeũ ikamera na igakũra, nowe ndamenyaga ũrĩa ikũraga.
english: and should sleep and rise night and day, and the seed should spring up and grow, though he doesn’t know how.

kikuyu: Nĩ haarĩ na atumia meeroragĩra marĩ haraaya. Ũmwe wao aarĩ Mariamu Mũmagidali, na Mariamu nyina wa Jakubu ũrĩa warĩ Mũnini na wa Jose, na Salome.
english: There were also women watching from afar, among whom were both Mary Magdalene and Mary the mother of James the less and of Joses, and Salome;

kikuyu: o na mũngĩgaatũma ĩkũre mũthenya o ro ũcio mũkaamĩhaanda, o na rũciinĩ rũrũ rũngĩ mũtũme ĩrute kĩro-rĩ, gũtirĩ magetha mũkoona mũthenya ũcio wa mũrimũ na ruo rũtangĩthira.
english: In the day of your planting, you hedge it in. In the morning, you make your seed blossom, but the harvest flees away in the day of grief and of desperate sorrow.

kikuyu: Kaĩ nyiihĩtie agũrũki atĩ nĩguo mũndehere mũndũ ũyũ haha eke maũndũ ta maya mbere yakwa? Mũndũ ũyũ no nginya oke nyũmba yakwa?”
english: Do I lack madmen, that you have brought this fellow to play the madman in my presence? Should this fellow come into my house?”

kikuyu: “Nĩngamarehithĩria mĩthemba ĩna ya aniinani mamookĩrĩre.” ũguo nĩguo Jehova ekuuga, “Rũhiũ rwa njora rũgooka rũũrage andũ, namo magui mamakururie, nacio nyoni cia rĩera-inĩ, o na nyamũ cia gũkũ thĩ imatambuure, imaniine.
english: “I will appoint over them four kinds,” says Yahweh: “the sword to kill, the dogs to tear, the birds of the sky, and the animals of the earth, to devour and to destroy.

kikuyu: Naho hau hakiumĩra nyamũ inya ciarĩ nene kuuma iria-inĩ rĩu, na gũtirĩ yahanaga ĩrĩa ĩngĩ.
english: Four great animals came up from the sea, different from one another.

kikuyu: Musa agĩĩta Mishaeli na Elizafani, ariũ a Uzieli, ithe mũnini wa Harũni, akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Ũkai haha, muoye ciimba cia ariũ a ithe wanyu, mũciumie nja ya kambĩ haraihu na itoonyero rĩa handũ-harĩa- haamũre.”
english: Moses called Mishael and Elzaphan, the sons of Uzziel the uncle of Aaron, and said to them, “Draw near, carry your brothers from before the sanctuary out of the camp.”

kikuyu: Nao andũ othe nĩmathiiaga hekarũ-inĩ rũciinĩ tene kũmũthikĩrĩria.
english: All the people came early in the morning to him in the temple to hear him.

kikuyu: O rũciinĩ mũndũ o mũndũ nĩoyaga ĩrĩa abatairio nĩyo, narĩo riũa rĩahiũha ĩgatweka.
english: They gathered it morning by morning, everyone according to his eating. When the sun grew hot, it melted.

kikuyu: Ũhoro ũcio nĩ wa kwĩhokeka. Na ngũkĩenda waragie maũndũ maya ũgwatĩirie, nĩgeetha andũ arĩa mehokete Ngai memenyerie kwĩrutĩra na kĩyo gwĩkaga maũndũ mega. Maũndũ maya nĩ magĩrĩru kũna, na nĩ marĩ uumithio harĩ mũndũ o wothe.
english: This saying is faithful, and concerning these things I desire that you insist confidently, so that those who have believed God may be careful to maintain good works. These things are good and profitable to men;

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio ihinda rĩu Mũthamaki Joramu, akiuma Samaria na agĩcookanĩrĩria andũ a Isiraeli othe.
english: King Jehoram went out of Samaria at that time, and mustered all Israel.

kikuyu: na Elishama, na Beeliada, na Elifeleti. Daudi Kũhoota Afilisti
english: Elishama, Beeliada, and Eliphelet.

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo ũmwe wa anabii agĩthiĩ mũgũnda gwetha nyeni, agĩkora rũũngũ rwa gĩthaka. Agĩtua maciaro maruo, akĩmaiyũria ruuno-inĩ rwa nguo yake ya igũrũ. Acooka akĩmatinangĩria nyũngũ-inĩ ĩyo yarugaga irio, o na gũtuĩka gũtirĩ mũndũ wamenyire ciarĩ kĩĩ.
english: One went out into the field to gather herbs, and found a wild vine, and gathered a lap full of wild gourds from it, and came and cut them up into the pot of stew; for they didn’t recognize them.

kikuyu: “Gũtirĩ mũndũ ũgwatagia tawa agacooka akaũiga handũ ũtangĩoneka kana akaũiga rungu rwa irebe, no aũigaga handũ igũrũ nĩguo arĩa megũtoonya nyũmba mathererwo nĩ ũtheri.
english: “No one, when he has lit a lamp, puts it in a cellar or under a basket, but on a stand, that those who come in may see the light.

kikuyu: Nĩ niĩ ndaamũtharire kuuma guoko-inĩ kwa andũ a Misiri, na kuuma guoko-inĩ kwa arĩa othe maamũhinyagĩrĩria. Ngĩmarutũrũra mbere yanyu na ngĩmũhe bũrũri wao.
english: I delivered you out of the hand of the Egyptians and out of the hand of all who oppressed you, and drove them out from before you, and gave you their land.

kikuyu: Rĩrĩa Belishazaru aanyuuaga ndibei yake, agĩathana ikombe cia thahabu na cia betha, iria ithe Nebukadinezaru aatahĩte akaruta hekarũ-inĩ Jerusalemu, irehwo nĩgeetha mũthamaki, na andũ ake arĩa maarĩ igweta, na atumia ake na thuriya ciake manyue nacio.
english: Belshazzar, while he tasted the wine, commanded that the golden and silver vessels which Nebuchadnezzar his father had taken out of the temple which was in Jerusalem be brought to him, that the king and his lords, his wives and his concubines, might drink from them.

kikuyu: Ũthingu waku nĩ wa gũtũũra tene na tene, naguo watho waku nĩ wa ma.
english: Your righteousness is an everlasting righteousness. Your law is truth.

kikuyu: O na gũtuĩka haana ta mondo ya rũũa ya ndibei ĩigĩtwo ndogo-inĩ,* ndiriganagĩrwo nĩ kĩrĩra kĩa watho waku.
english: For I have become like a wineskin in the smoke. I don’t forget your statutes.

kikuyu: o nginya rĩrĩa Ũcio-Ũtũire-ho-kuuma-o-Tene ookire na agĩtuĩra andũ acio atheru a Ũrĩa-ũrĩ-Igũrũ-Mũno ciira wa kĩhooto, narĩo ihinda rĩgĩkinya makĩĩgwatĩra ũthamaki.
english: until the Ancient of Days came, and judgment was given to the saints of the Most High, and the time came that the saints possessed the kingdom.

kikuyu: Kwĩĩndigĩra gũtigakũmakie, kana guoko gwakwa gũkũritũhĩre.
english: Behold, my terror will not make you afraid, neither will my pressure be heavy on you.

kikuyu: naguo ũhoro ũcio aarĩtie wa kĩama kana wa ũrirũ wĩkĩke-rĩ, acooke oige atĩrĩ, “Nĩtũrũmĩrĩrei ngai ingĩ (ngai iria mũtooĩ) mũreke tũcihooe,”
english: and the sign or the wonder comes to pass, of which he spoke to you, saying, “Let’s go after other gods” (which you have not known) “and let’s serve them,”

kikuyu: Nĩnjũũĩ mĩnyamaro yaku na ũthĩĩni waku, no ũrĩ gĩtonga! Nĩnjũũĩ ũrĩa ũrumagwo nĩ arĩa moigaga atĩ nĩ Ayahudi no ti Ayahudi, no nĩ thunagogi ya Shaitani.
english: “I know your works, oppression, and your poverty (but you are rich), and the blasphemy of those who say they are Jews, and they are not, but are a synagogue of Satan.

kikuyu: Mũthenya o ro ũcio nĩguo Nuhu na ariũ ake, Shemu, na Hamu na Jafethu, hamwe na mũtumia wake na atumia a ariũ ake atatũ maatoonyire thabina.
english: In the same day Noah, and Shem, Ham, and Japheth—the sons of Noah—and Noah’s wife and the three wives of his sons with them, entered into the ship—

kikuyu: Rehagĩrai Jehova iruta rĩa gũcinwo na mwaki, igongona rĩa njino rĩa tũtegwa twĩrĩ, na ndũrũme ĩmwe na tũtũrũme mũgwanja o kamwe ka ũkũrũ wa mwaka ũmwe, ciothe itarĩ na kaũũgũ.
english: but you shall offer an offering made by fire, a burnt offering to Yahweh: two young bulls, one ram, and seven male lambs a year old. They shall be without defect,

kikuyu: na ingĩmũũria kĩũria, mũtingĩnjoke- ria.
english: and if I ask, you will in no way answer me or let me go.

kikuyu: Ngĩũria mũraika ũcio wanjaragĩria atĩrĩ, “Mwathi wakwa, ici nacio nĩ ndũũ?”
english: Then I asked the angel who talked with me, “What are these, my lord?”

kikuyu: Geria mũno o ũrĩa wahota ũũke kũrĩ niĩ na ihenya,
english: Be diligent to come to me soon,

kikuyu: ookeriai Jehova ngaatho nĩgũkorwo nĩ mwega; wendo wake nĩ wa tene na tene.
english: ive thanks to Yahweh, for he is good, for his loving kindness endures forever.

kikuyu: Ciarĩkia kũmũnyũrũria, cikĩmũruta nguo ĩyo, na cikĩmũhumba nguo ciake mwene. Cigĩcooka cikĩmumagaria cikamwambe mũtĩ-igũrũ. Kwambwo kwa Jesũ Mũtĩ-Igũrũ
english: When they had mocked him, they took the robe off him, and put his clothes on him, and led him away to crucify him.

kikuyu: Ariũ a Obedi-Edomu maarĩ: Shemaia, na nĩwe warĩ wa irigithathi, na Jehozabadu aarĩ wa keerĩ, na Joa aarĩ wa gatatũ, na Sakaru aarĩ wa kana, na Nethaneli aarĩ wa gatano,
english: Obed-Edom had sons: Shemaiah the firstborn, Jehozabad the second, Joah the third, Sacar the fourth, Nethanel the fifth,

kikuyu: Ta kĩĩgemie na riiri na ũkengi, na wĩhumbe gĩtĩĩo na ũnene.
english: “Now deck yourself with excellency and dignity. Array yourself with honor and majesty.

kikuyu: “ ‘Mũndũ angĩhikia mũtumia wa mũrũ wa nyina ũcio nĩ thaahu, nĩaconorithĩtie mũrũ wa nyina. Megũtũũra matarĩ magĩa mwana.
english: “ ‘If a man takes his brother’s wife, it is an impurity. He has uncovered his brother’s nakedness. They shall be childless.

kikuyu: No Jehova nĩagekĩra ngũũrani gatagatĩ ka ũhiũ wa andũ a Isiraeli na andũ a Misiri, nĩgeetha gũtikagĩe nyamũ ya Mũisiraeli ĩgũkua.’ ”
english: Yahweh will make a distinction between the livestock of Israel and the livestock of Egypt; and nothing shall die of all that belongs to the children of Israel.” ’ ”

kikuyu: No Abiatharu, mũrũ ũmwe wa Ahimeleku mũrũ wa Ahitubu, akĩhonoka, akĩũra, na akĩrũmĩrĩra Daudi.
english: One of the sons of Ahimelech the son of Ahitub, named Abiathar, escaped and fled after David.

kikuyu: “ ‘Nĩngatũma rĩĩtwa rĩakwa itheru rĩmenyeke gatagatĩ-inĩ ka andũ akwa a bũrũri wa Isiraeli. Ndigacooka kũreka rĩĩtwa rĩu rĩakwa itheru rĩthaahio, nacio ndũrĩrĩ nĩikamenya atĩ niĩ Jehova nĩ niĩ Ũrĩa Mũtheru thĩinĩ wa Isiraeli.
english: “ ‘ “I will make my holy name known among my people Israel. I won’t allow my holy name to be profaned any more. Then the nations will know that I am Yahweh, the Holy One in Israel.

kikuyu: Nake Saũlũ agĩtharĩkĩra Aamaleki amarutĩtie kuuma Havila nginya Shuri, o nginya mwena wa irathĩro wa Misiri.
english: Saul struck the Amalekites, from Havilah as you go to Shur, which is before Egypt.

kikuyu: Mũgeeka atĩa mũthenya wa ciira, rĩrĩa mũgaakorererwo nĩ mwanangĩko uumĩte kũraya? Mũkoorĩra kũrĩ ũ amũteithie? Ũtonga wanyu mũkaaũtiga kũ?
english: What will you do in the day of visitation, and in the desolation which will come from afar? To whom will you

kikuyu: Norĩrĩ, andũnĩmehumbenguociamakũniaonacionyamũnĩihumbwo makũnia. O mũndũ nĩakaĩre Ngai narua. Andũ nĩmatige mĩthiĩre yao mĩũru na ngũĩ ciao.
english: but let them be covered with sackcloth, both man and animal, and let them cry mightily to God. Yes, let them turn everyone from his evil way and from the violence that is in his hands.

kikuyu: ndakamũigua. Ningĩ nĩngatambũrũkia guoko gwakwa kũu bũrũri wa Misiri, na ndute ikundi cia andũ akwa a Isiraeli moime
english: But Pharaoh will not listen to you, so I will lay my hand on Egypt, and bring out my armies, my people the children of Israel, out of the land of Egypt by great judgments.

kikuyu: na Ibiharu, na Elishua, na Nefegu, na Jafia,
english: Ibhar, Elishua, Nepheg, Japhia,

kikuyu: Magĩcooka makĩoya mahĩndĩ macio mao na makĩmathika gĩtina-inĩ kĩa mũtĩ wa mũbinde kũu Jabeshi, na makĩĩhinga kũrĩa irio mĩthenya mũgwanja.
english: They took their bones and buried them under the tamarisk† tree in Jabesh, and fasted seven days.

kikuyu: “Na rĩrĩ, ndaarĩkia kũnengera Baruku wa Neria marũa macio ma kĩrĩkanĩro gĩa kuonania nũũ mwene gĩthaka, ngĩhooya Jehova ngiuga ũũ:
english: Now after I had delivered the deed of the purchase to Baruch the son of Neriah, I prayed to Yahweh, saying,

kikuyu: nĩgũkorwo nĩaririkanĩte kwĩnyiihia kwa ndungata yake. Kuuma rĩu njiarwa ciothe irĩĩnjĩtaga mũrathime,
english: for he has looked at the humble state of his servant. For behold, from now on, all generations will call me blessed.

kikuyu: iugo kĩa Jehova nĩkĩanginyĩrĩire, ngĩĩrwo atĩrĩ:
english: ahweh’s word came to me, saying,

kikuyu: Kuuma mũhĩrĩga wa Dani maarĩ andũ 62,700.
english: those who were counted of them, of the tribe of Dan, were sixty-two thousand seven hundred.

kikuyu: na harĩ o gatũrũme hagĩage na iruta rĩa mũtu gĩcunjĩ gĩa ikũmi kĩa eba ĩmwe‡ ya mũtu mũhinyu mũno ũtukanĩtio na maguta. Rĩrĩ nĩ igongona rĩa njino, rĩrĩ na mũtararĩko mwega, rĩrutĩirwo Jehova na ũndũ wa gũcinwo na mwaki.
english: and one tenth part of fine flour mixed with oil for a meal offering to every lamb, as a burnt offering of a pleasant aroma, an offering made by fire to Yahweh.

kikuyu: Nao makiuma Midiani magĩthiĩ Parani. Ningĩ makĩoya andũ angĩ kũu Parani, magĩthiĩ bũrũri wa Misiri, kũrĩ Firaũni mũthamaki wa Misiri, ũrĩa waheire Hadadi nyũmba, na gĩthaka, na akĩmũhe irio.
english: They arose out of Midian and came to Paran; and they took men with them out of Paran, and they came to Egypt, to Pharaoh king of Egypt, who gave him a house, and appointed him food, and gave him land.

kikuyu: “Ũrĩa wothe wendete ithe kana nyina kũngĩra ndagĩrĩire gũtuĩka wakwa; na ũrĩa wothe wendete mũriũ kana mwarĩ kũngĩra ndagĩrĩire gũtuĩka wakwa;
english: He who loves father or mother more than me is not worthy of me; and he who loves son or daughter more than me isn’t worthy of me.

kikuyu: Sharoni gũgaatuĩka ũrĩithio wa ndũũru cia mbũri, na Gĩtuamba-kĩa-Akori gĩtuĩke handũ ha kwarahagwo nĩ mahiũ, nĩ ũndũ wa andũ akwa arĩa matũire manjaragia.
english: Sharon will be a fold of flocks, and the valley of Achor a place for herds to lie down in, for my people who have sought me.

kikuyu: Moimĩte ndũrĩrĩ-inĩ iria Jehova eerĩte Isiraeli atĩrĩ, “Mũtikanahikanie nao, tondũ ti-itherũ nĩmakagarũra ngoro cianyu irũmĩrĩre ngai ciao.” No rĩrĩ, Solomoni agĩkĩrĩrĩria kũmeenda.
english: of the nations concerning which Yahweh said to the children of Israel, “You shall not go among them, neither shall they come among you, for surely they will turn away your heart after their gods.” Solomon joined to these in love.

kikuyu: tondũ Ũhoro-ũrĩa-Mwega tũhunjagia, tũtiamũreheire ũrĩ o ciugo theri, no twamũreheire ũrĩ na hinya, na ũrĩ na Roho Mũtheru, o na ũrĩ na ihooto itangĩthanganio. Ningĩ inyuĩ nĩmũũĩ ũrĩa twaikaraga na inyuĩ nĩ ũndũ wa kũmũguna.
english: and that our Good News came to you not in word only, but also in power, and in the Holy Spirit and with much assurance. You know what kind of men we showed ourselves to be among you for your sake.

kikuyu: Gideoni mũrũ wa Joashu aakuire arĩ mũkũrũ mũno, na agĩthikwo mbĩrĩra-inĩ ya ithe Joashu kũu Ofira ya Aabiezeri.
english: Gideon the son of Joash died in a good old age, and was buried in the tomb of Joash his father, in Ophrah of the Abiezrites.

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio akiumagara agĩthiĩ, akĩambĩrĩria kũhaara cairi kũu mĩgũnda-inĩ arĩ thuutha wa arĩa maagethaga. Na gũgĩkinya atĩrĩ, Ruthu ekorire akĩruta wĩra mũgũnda wa Boazu, ũcio warĩ wa mũhĩrĩga wa Elimeleku.
english: She went, and came and gleaned in the field after the reapers; and she happened to come to the portion of the field belonging to Boaz, who was of the family of Elimelech.

kikuyu: Kũu nĩkuo mĩhĩrĩga yambataga, o yo mĩhĩrĩga ya Jehova, ĩgathiĩ kũgooca rĩĩtwa rĩa Jehova kũringana na mawatho marĩa aheete Isiraeli.
english: where the tribes go up, even Yah’s tribes, according to an ordinance for Israel, to give thanks to Yahweh’s name.

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio kũngĩkorwo na mũndũ wanyu ũhĩtĩirio maũndũ-inĩ ta macio- rĩ, no mũthuure o na andũ matangĩtĩĩka kanitha-inĩ matuĩke a kũmũciirithia!
english: If then you have to judge things pertaining to this life, do you set them to judge who are of no account in the assembly?

kikuyu: Ehumbĩrire na nduma, agĩtua matu marĩa mairũ ma mbura ma kũu igũrũ hema yake ya kũmũthiũrũrũkĩria.
english: He made darkness a shelter around himself, gathering of waters, and thick clouds of the skies.

kikuyu: Mũndũ ũcio wehumbĩte nguo cia gatani, o ũcio warũgamĩte igũrũ rĩa maaĩ ma rũũĩ, akĩambararia guoko gwake kwa ũrĩo o na ningĩ kwa ũmotho amerekeirie na igũrũ, ngĩcooka ngĩigua ehĩta na rĩĩtwa rĩa ũrĩa ũtũũraga tene na tene, akiuga atĩrĩ, “Maũndũ macio megũikara ihinda rĩmwe, na mahinda meerĩ, na nuthu ya ihinda. Rĩrĩa hinya wa andũ arĩa atheru ũgaakorwo ũrĩ mũniine biũ-rĩ, nĩguo maũndũ macio mothe makaahingio.”
english: I heard the man clothed in linen, who was above the waters of the river, when he held up his right hand and his left hand to heaven, and swore by him who lives forever that it will be for a time, times, and a half; and when they have finished breaking in pieces the power of the holy people, all these things will be finished.

kikuyu: Wee ũiguaga mahooya-rĩ, andũ othe marĩũkaga harĩwe.
english: You who hear prayer, all men will come to you.

kikuyu: Jehoramu aarĩ wa mĩaka mĩrongo ĩtatũ na ĩĩrĩ rĩrĩa aatuĩkire mũthamaki. Nake agĩthamaka arĩ Jerusalemu mĩaka ĩnana.
english: Jehoram was thirty-two years old when he began to reign, and he reigned eight years in Jerusalem.

kikuyu: Magĩcooka magĩthondeka mĩtĩ ĩngĩ mĩraaya ya mũgaa na makĩmĩgemia na thahabu.
english: He made the poles of acacia wood, and overlaid them with gold.

kikuyu: “Nake nĩakamacookeria atĩrĩ, ‘Ngũmwĩra atĩrĩ na ma, ũndũ o wothe mũteekire ũmwe wa aya anini mũno, nĩ niĩ mũteekĩire.’
english: “Then he will answer them, saying, ‘Most certainly I tell you, because you didn’t do it to one of the least of these, you didn’t do it to me.’

kikuyu: Ngwĩtigĩra atĩ rĩrĩa ngooka kũu rĩngĩ, Ngai wakwa ahota gũkanjonorithia mbere yanyu, na niĩ njigue kĩeha nĩ ũndũ wa andũ aingĩ arĩa meehirie o mbere, na matiĩrirĩte waganu wa maũndũ marĩa mooru, na ma ũmaraya, na maũndũ ma ũũra-thoni marĩa matũũrĩte meekaga.
english: that again when I come my God would humble me before you, and I would mourn for many of those who have sinned before now, and not repented of the uncleanness, sexual immorality, and lustfulness which they committed.

kikuyu: Ariũ a Simeoni maarĩ: Jemueli, na Jamini, na Ohadi, na Jakini, na Zoharu, na Shauli mũrũ wa mũtumia ũrĩa Mũkaanani.
english: The sons of Simeon: Jemuel, Jamin, Ohad, Jachin, Zohar, and Shaul the son of a Canaanite woman.

kikuyu: Nginyagia ũmũthĩ andũ a Edomu matũũraga maremeire Juda. Andũ a Libina o nao makĩrega watho wa Juda o ihinda-inĩ rĩu.
english: So Edom revolted from under the hand of Judah to this day. Then Libnah revolted at the same time.

kikuyu: Musa arĩ kũu mwena wa irathĩro wa Rũũĩ rwa Jorodani bũrũri-inĩ wa Moabi, nĩambĩrĩirie gũtaarĩria ũhoro wa watho ũyũ, akiuga atĩrĩ:
english: Beyond the Jordan, in the land of Moab, Moses began to declare this law, saying,

kikuyu: Ngai eerire Iburahĩmu atĩrĩ: ‘Njiaro ciaku igaatũũra irĩ ngʼeni bũrũri ũtarĩ wacio, kũrĩa igaatuuo ngombo na inyariirwo ihinda rĩa mĩaka magana mana.’
english: God spoke in this way: that his offspring would live as aliens in a strange land, and that they would be enslaved and mistreated for four hundred years.

kikuyu: Gũtirĩ hĩndĩ ũtheri wa tawa ũgacooka kwara thĩinĩ waku o na rĩ. O naguo mũgambo wa mũhikania na wa mũhiki ndũrĩ hĩndĩ ũgaacooka kũiguuo thĩinĩ waku o na rĩ. Onjoria aku nĩo maarĩ andũ marĩ igweta gũkũ thĩ.
english: The light of a lamp will shine no more at all in you. The voice of the bridegroom and of the bride will be heard no more at all in you, for your merchants were the princes of the earth; for with your sorcery all the nations were deceived.

kikuyu: Rĩrĩa tũkũmũhoera tũrĩ mbere ya Ngai o we Ithe witũ-rĩ, nĩtũririkanaga tũtegũtigithĩria wĩra wanyu ũrĩa uumanĩte na gwĩtĩkia, na kwĩnogia kwanyu kũrĩa kũrehagwo nĩ wendani, o na gũkirĩrĩria kwanyu, kũrĩa kuumanĩte na kĩĩrĩgĩrĩro thĩinĩ wa Mwathani witũ Jesũ Kristũ.
english: remembering without ceasing your work of faith and labor of love and perseverance of hope in our Lord Jesus Christ, before our God and Father.

kikuyu: Paũlũ akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Ũbatithio wa Johana warĩ ũbatithio wa kwĩrira kwa mehia. Eeraga andũ metĩkie ũrĩa ũgũũka thuutha wake, nake nĩwe Jesũ.”
english: Paul said, “John indeed baptized with the baptism of repentance, saying to the people that they should believe in the one who would come after him, that is, in Christ Jesus.”†

kikuyu: Wee nĩũnjoheete, naguo ũndũ ũcio ũgatuĩka ũira; ũhĩnju ũyũ ndĩ naguo nĩwambararĩte ũkaanyumbũra ũrĩa ndariĩ.
english: You have shriveled me up. This is a witness against me. My leanness rises up against me. It testifies to my face.

kikuyu: Rĩrĩa Hamani onire atĩ Moridekai ndangĩmũturĩria maru kana amũtĩĩe, akĩngʼũrĩka mũno.
english: When Haman saw that Mordecai didn’t bow down nor pay him homage, Haman was full of wrath.

kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa ũhĩkaga gwĩka maũndũ ateciirĩtie wega, nĩakamenya maũndũ na ataũkĩrwo nĩmo, naruo rũrĩmĩ rũrĩa rũtondoiraga nĩrũkaaria o wega.
english: The heart of the rash will understand knowledge, and the tongue of the stammerers will be ready to speak plainly.

kikuyu: Mũthenya ũcio wa igongona rĩa Jehova-rĩ, nĩngaherithia anene, o na herithie ariũ a mũthamaki, hamwe na arĩa othe magaakorwo mehumbĩte nguo cia bũrũri ũngĩ.
english: It will happen in the day of Yahweh’s sacrifice that I will punish the princes, the king’s sons, and all those who are clothed with foreign clothing.

kikuyu: Andũ arĩa marĩ igweta maratũma ndungata ciao igatahe maaĩ; irathiĩ marima-inĩ ikaaga maaĩ. Iracooka na ndigithũ itarĩ kĩndũ; ikehumbĩra mĩtwe iconokete na ikuĩte ngoro.
english: Their nobles send their little ones to the waters. They come to the cisterns, and find no water. They return with their vessels empty. They are disappointed and confounded, and cover their heads.

kikuyu: kuuma mũhĩrĩga wa Simeoni, aarĩ Shafati mũrũ wa Hori;
english: Of the tribe of Simeon, Shaphat the son of Hori.

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Naamani agĩikũrũka, agĩĩtobokia Jorodani maita mũgwanja, o ta ũrĩa mũndũ wa Ngai aamwĩrĩte, naguo mwĩrĩ wake ũkĩhona, ũgĩthera ũkĩhaana ta wa kaana kanini.
english: Then went he down and dipped himself seven times in the Jordan, according to the saying of the man of God; and his flesh was restored like the flesh of a little child, and he was clean.

kikuyu: Akĩanĩrĩra, agĩũkĩrĩra kĩgongona kĩu na ndũmĩrĩri ya Jehova, agĩkĩĩra atĩrĩ, “Wee kĩgongona gĩkĩ, kĩgongona gĩkĩ! Jehova ekuuga ũũ, ‘Nyũmba ya Daudi nĩgũgaciarwo kaana ga kahĩĩ, nako gageetwo Josia. Nako nĩgakaruta athĩnjĩrĩ-Ngai a gũkũ gũtũũgĩru igongona haha igũrũ rĩaku, o acio marutagĩra magongona haha, na mahĩndĩ ma andũ nĩmagacinĩrwo igũrũ rĩaku.’ ”
english: He cried against the altar by Yahweh’s word, and said, “Altar! Altar! Yahweh says: ‘Behold, a son will be born to David’s house, Josiah by name. On you he will sacrifice the priests of the high places who burn incense on you, and they will burn men’s bones on you.’ ”

kikuyu: Akĩamũra ikũmi na eerĩ, akĩmatua atũmwo, nĩgeetha makoragwo nake, na nĩguo amatũmage makahunjie,
english: He appointed twelve, that they might be with him, and that he might send them out to preach

kikuyu: Maũndũ maya mekĩkire nĩgeetha ciugo iria Jesũ aarĩtie akĩonania mũthemba wa gĩkuũ kĩrĩa agaakua ihingio.
english: that the word of Jesus might be fulfilled, which he spoke, signifying by what kind of death he should die.

kikuyu: Ndũrĩrĩ nĩikoona ũthingu waku, nao athamaki othe mone riiri waku; nawe ũgeetagwo na rĩĩtwa rĩerũ, rĩrĩa rĩkaagwetwo na kanua ka Jehova.
english: The nations will see your righteousness, and all kings your glory. You will be called by a new name, which Yahweh’s mouth will name.

kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, mũndũ wa Ngai agĩũka kũrĩ we, akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Wee mũthamaki-rĩ, thigari ici cia kuuma Isiraeli itigatwarane nawe, nĩgũkorwo Jehova ndarĩ hamwe na Isiraeli, na ndarĩ hamwe na andũ o na arĩkũ a Efiraimu.
english: A man of God came to him, saying, “O king, don’t let the army of Israel go with you, for Yahweh is not with Israel, with all the children of Ephraim.

kikuyu: Tũnua twitũ twaiyũrire mĩtheko, nacio nĩmĩ ciitũ ikĩina nyĩmbo cia gĩkeno. Hĩndĩ ĩyo ndũrĩrĩ-inĩ gũkĩĩranwo atĩrĩ, “Jehova nĩekĩire andũ aya maũndũ manene.”
english: Then our mouth was filled with laughter, and our tongue with singing. Then they said among the nations, “Yahweh has done great things for them.”

kikuyu: Rĩu-rĩ, nĩ inyuĩ andũ-a-Ndũrĩrĩ ndĩraarĩria. Tondũ ndĩ mũtũmwo ũtũmĩtwo kũrĩ andũ-a-Ndũrĩrĩ- rĩ, ũtungata ũcio wakwa nĩndĩwĩkĩrĩire mũno,
english: For I speak to you who are Gentiles. Since then as I am an apostle to Gentiles, I glorify my ministry,

kikuyu: niĩ na niĩ nĩngamũthekerera hĩndĩ yanyu ya mwanangĩko, na ndĩmũnyũrũrie rĩrĩa mũgaakorererwo nĩ mũtino:
english: I also will laugh at your disaster. I will mock when calamity overtakes you,

kikuyu: Jesũ akĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Ngũkwĩra atĩrĩ, ti maita mũgwanja, no nĩ mĩrongo mũgwanja, maita mũgwanja.
english: Jesus said to him, “I don’t tell you until seven times, but, until seventy times seven.

kikuyu: No niĩ ngũmwĩra atĩrĩ, ũrĩa wothe ũkarakarĩra mũrũ wa ithe nĩagatuĩka wa gũtuĩrwo ciira. Na ningĩ-rĩ, mũndũ ũrĩa wothe wĩraga mũrũ wa ithe atĩrĩ,
english: But I tell you that everyone who is angry with his brother without a cause † will be in danger of the judgment. Whoever says to his brother, ‘Raca!’ ‡ will be in danger of the council. Whoever says, ‘You fool!’ will be in danger of the fire of Gehenna.§

kikuyu: Naguo mũthenya wa ĩna wakinya, makĩĩra mũtumia wa Samusoni atĩrĩ, “Heenereria mũthu- uriguo nĩguo atũtaũrĩre ndaĩ ĩyo, kana tũgũcine wee na nyũmba ya thoguo nginya mũkue. Anga mũtwĩtĩte gũkũ nĩguo mũtũtunye indo ciitũ?”
english: On the seventh day, they said to Samson’s wife, “Entice your husband, that he may declare to us the riddle, lest we burn you and your father’s house with fire. Have you called us to impoverish us? Isn’t that so?”

kikuyu: “O rĩngĩ ngũmwĩra atĩrĩ, andũ eerĩ anyu mangĩiguanĩra gũkũ thĩ ũndũ o wothe ũrĩa marahooya, nĩmakahingĩrio nĩ Baba ũrĩa ũrĩ igũrũ.
english: Again, assuredly I tell you, that if two of you will agree on earth concerning anything that they will ask, it will be done for them by my Father who is in heaven.

kikuyu: Nake mũthamaki akĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Angĩkorwo Jehova ndangĩgũteithia-rĩ, niĩ ingĩkũrutĩra ũteithio kũ? Nĩ kuuma kĩhuhĩro-inĩ kĩa ngano, kana nĩ kuuma kĩhihĩro-inĩ kĩa ndibei?”
english: He said, “If Yahweh doesn’t help you, where could I get help for you? From the threshing floor, or from the wine press?”

kikuyu: Nao athĩnjĩri-Ngai arĩa anene na arutani a watho maarũgamĩte hau, makĩmũthitanga magwatĩirie mũno.
english: The chief priests and the scribes stood, vehemently accusing him.

kikuyu: No mũthamaki wa Ashuri nĩamenyire atĩ Hoshea aarĩ mũkunyanĩri, nĩgũkorwo nĩatũmĩte andũ kũrĩ Soo mũthamaki wa Misiri, na nĩatigĩte kũrĩha igooti rĩa mũthamaki wa Ashuri ta ũrĩa ekaga mbere mwaka o mwaka. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Shalimaneseru akĩmũnyiita, na akĩmũhingĩra njeera.
english: The king of Assyria discovered a conspiracy in Hoshea; for he had sent messengers to So king of Egypt, and offered no tribute to the king of Assyria, as he had done year by year. Therefore the king of Assyria seized him, and bound him in prison.

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo mũndũ ũmwe akĩĩra Saũlũ atĩrĩ, “Atĩrĩrĩ, ta kĩone andũ aya nĩmehĩirie Jehova nĩ kũrĩa nyama irĩ na thakame.” Nake akiuga atĩrĩ, “Nĩmwagarara watho. Garagariai ihiga inene mũrĩrehe haha o rĩu.”
english: Then they told Saul, saying, “Behold, the people are sinning against Yahweh, in that they eat meat with the blood.” He said, “You have dealt treacherously. Roll a large stone to me today!”

kikuyu: Nĩmahooyaga Jehova, na no maatungatagĩra ngai ciao, kũringana na mĩtugo ya ndũrĩrĩ cia kũrĩa maarutĩtwo.
english: They feared Yahweh, and also served their own gods, after the ways of the nations from among whom they had been carried away.

kikuyu: Thuutha wa mĩrango yaku, na hingĩro-inĩ ciayo nĩkuo ũigĩte imenyithia cia gũkũririkania ũhoro wa mĩhianano ya andũ acio matooĩ Ngai. Nĩwahumbũririe gĩtanda gĩaku nĩ ũndũ wa kũndirika, ũkĩhaica igũrũ rĩakĩo, o na ũgĩkĩaramia makĩria; ũkĩrĩĩkanĩra na acio wendete itanda ciao, na ũgĩcooka ũkĩĩrorera njaga yao.
english: You have set up your memorial behind the doors and the posts, for you have exposed yourself to someone besides me, and have gone up. You have enlarged your bed and made a covenant for yourself with them. You loved what you saw on their bed.

kikuyu: Tondũ ũcio makĩanĩrĩra mũno, na magĩĩtemanga na hiũ cia njora na matimũ, nginya thakame ĩkĩambĩrĩria kũnyũrũrũka, tondũ ũcio nĩguo warĩ mũtugo wao.
english: They cried aloud, and cut themselves in their way with knives and lances until the blood gushed out on them.

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo aikia a mĩguĩ maraikĩria ndungata ciaku mĩguĩ marĩ rũthingo igũrũ, na andũ amwe a mũthamaki nĩmakuĩte. O na ningĩ ndungata yaku, Uria ũrĩa Mũhiti, nĩĩkuĩte.”
english: The shooters shot at your servants from off the wall; and some of the king’s servants are dead, and your servant Uriah the Hittite is also dead.”

kikuyu: Ningĩ mũgambo ũkiuma gĩtĩ-inĩ kĩu kĩa ũnene, ũkiuga atĩrĩ: “Goocai Ngai witũ, inyuĩ ndungata ciake inyuothe, o inyuĩ mũmwĩtigĩrĩte, arĩa anini na arĩa anene!”
english: A voice came from the throne, saying, “Give praise to our God, all you his servants, you who fear him, the small and the great!”

kikuyu: Wee Mũrungu, thuthuuria, ũmenye ngoro yakwa; ngeria ũmenye meciiria makwa.
english: Search me, God, and know my heart. Try me, and know my thoughts.

kikuyu: Guoko kwa Jehova kwarĩ igũrũ rĩakwa ndĩ o kũu, nake akĩnjĩĩra atĩrĩ, “Ũkĩra ũthiĩ werũ-inĩ ũrĩa mwaraganu, na kũu nĩkuo ngũkwarĩria.”
english: Yahweh’s hand was there on me; and he said to me, “Arise, go out into the plain, and I will talk with you there.”

kikuyu: Andũ a ndũrĩrĩ ciothe nĩmookaga gũthikĩrĩria ũũgĩ wa Solomoni matũmĩtwo nĩ athamaki othe a thĩ, arĩa maiguĩte ũhoro wa ũũgĩ wake.
english: People of all nations came to hear the wisdom of Solomon, sent by all kings of the earth who had heard of his wisdom.

kikuyu: Rĩrĩa Jesũ aatoonyire nyũmba ya Petero, akĩona atĩ nyina wa mũtumia wa Petero aarĩ mũrũaru mũrimũ wa kũhiũha mwĩrĩ, agakoma gĩtanda-inĩ.
english: When Jesus came into Peter’s house, he saw his wife’s mother lying sick with a fever.

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo Ngai nĩatuĩte andũ othe aremi nĩgeetha acooke amaiguĩre tha othe. Rwĩmbo rwa Kũgooca Ngai
english: For God has bound all to disobedience, that he might have mercy on all.

kikuyu: Gĩcigo kĩa mũigana wa ũraihu wa mĩkono 25,000, na wariĩ wa 10,000 gĩgaatuĩka kĩa Alawii, arĩa matungataga thĩinĩ wa hekarũ, gĩtuĩke igai rĩao rĩa gwakwo matũũra ma gũtũũrwo.
english: Twenty-five thousand cubits in length and ten thousand in width shall be for the Levites, the ministers of the house, as a possession for themselves, for twenty rooms.

kikuyu: mahooya-inĩ makwa hĩndĩ ciothe; na nĩndĩrahooya atĩ rĩu nĩ ũndũ wa kwenda kwa Ngai no hingũrĩrwo njĩra nĩguo njũke kũrĩ inyuĩ.
english: requesting, if by any means now at last I may be prospered by the will of God to come to you.

kikuyu: Rĩrĩa rĩothe ngũmũhoera inyuothe-rĩ, ndĩmũhooyagĩra hĩndĩ ciothe ngenete.
english: always in every request of mine on behalf of you all, making my requests with joy,

kikuyu: ikĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Akorwo nĩwe Mũthamaki wa Ayahudi, wĩhonokie.”
english: and saying, “If you are the King of the Jews, save yourself!”

kikuyu: 1153 Habakuku 3:14 3 Ihooya rĩa Habakuku 1 Rĩrĩ nĩ ihooya rĩa mũnabii Habakuku, rĩa kũinwo ta rwĩmbo.
english: prayer of Habakkuk, the prophet, set to victorious music.

kikuyu: No tondũ ũrĩ mũraru, ũkaaga kũhiũha na ũkaaga kũhora-rĩ, ngirie gũgũtua thĩ uume kanua gakwa.
english: So, because you are lukewarm, and neither hot nor cold, I will vomit you out of my mouth.

kikuyu: “Wee Jehova Ngai Mwene-Hinya-Wothe, Ngai wa Isiraeli, o wee ũikarĩire gĩtĩ kĩa ũnene gatagatĩ ka makerubi, Wee wiki nowe Ngai igũrũ rĩa mothamaki mothe ma thĩ. Nĩwe wombire igũrũ na thĩ.
english: “Yahweh of Armies, the God of Israel, who is enthroned among the cherubim, you are the God, even you alone, of all the kingdoms of the earth. You have made heaven and earth.

kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, hĩndĩ ya mathĩĩna mao nĩmacookereire Jehova, Ngai wa Isiraeli, makĩmũrongooria na makĩmuona.
english: But when in their distress they turned to Yahweh, the God of Israel, and sought him, he was found by them.

kikuyu: Atĩrĩrĩ, nĩngarahũra andũ a Amedi mamookĩrĩre, arĩa matarĩ bata wa betha, na matakenagio nĩ thahabu.
english: Behold, I will stir up the Medes against them, who will not value silver, and as for gold, they will not delight in it.

kikuyu: nĩũgaatũma ahoorere matukũ-inĩ ma thĩĩna, o nginya rĩrĩa mũndũ ũrĩa mwaganu akenjerwo irima.
english: that you may give him rest from the days of adversity, until the pit is dug for the wicked.

kikuyu: Rũhiũ rwa njora nĩrũkaniina ciana ciao njĩra-inĩ ya itũũra; kĩmako gĩkaamaiyũra marĩ kwao mĩciĩ. Aanake na airĩtu nĩmagathira, o hamwe na ngenge, na andũ arĩa marĩ na mbuĩ.
english: Outside the sword will bereave, and in the rooms, terror on both young man and virgin, the nursing infant with the gray-haired man.

kikuyu: na Kedemothu, na Mefaathu, hamwe na ũrĩithio wamo;
english: Kedemoth with its pasture lands, and Mephaath with its pasture lands;

kikuyu: Atongoria a Juda nĩndameerire mahaice rũthingo-igũrũ. Ningĩ ngĩhe ikundi igĩrĩ nene cia aini wĩra wa gũcookia ngaatho. Gĩkundi kĩmwe gĩgĩthiĩ mwena wa ũrĩo kĩrĩ rũthingo-igũrũ, kĩerekeire Kĩhingo gĩa Kĩara-inĩ.
english: Then I brought the princes of Judah up on the wall, and appointed two great companies who gave thanks and went in procession. One went on the right hand on the wall toward the dung gate;

kikuyu: Thikĩrĩriai kiugo kĩa Jehova, inyuĩ mwathanaga Sodomu;
english: Hear Yahweh’s word, you rulers of Sodom! Listen to the law of our God,§ you people of Gomorrah!

kikuyu: Rũrĩmĩ rwakwa nĩrũkĩine ũhoro wa kiugo gĩaku, nĩgũkorwo maathani maku mothe nĩ ma ũthingu.
english: Let my tongue sing of your word, for all your commandments are righteousness.

kikuyu: Arahũkai, inyuĩ arĩĩu aya mũrĩre! Girĩkai, inyuothe anyui a ndibei; girĩkai nĩ ũndũ wa ndibei ya mũhihano, nĩgũkorwo nĩĩhurĩtio kuuma tũnua-inĩ twanyu.
english: Wake up, you drunkards, and weep! Wail, all you drinkers of wine, because of the sweet wine, for it is cut off from your mouth.

kikuyu: Nake Daudi agĩĩciiria atĩrĩ, “Nĩngwĩka Hanuni mũrũ wa Nahashu maũndũ mega ma ũtugi tondũ ithe nĩanjĩkire maũndũ mega ma ũtugi.” Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Daudi agĩtũma andũ kũrĩ Hanuni makamũcakaithie nĩ ũndũ wa gũkuĩrwo nĩ ithe. Rĩrĩa andũ acio maatũmĩtwo nĩ Daudi maakinyire kũrĩ Hanuni o kũu bũrũri wa Amoni nĩguo mamũcakaithie-rĩ,
english: David said, “I will show kindness to Hanun the son of Nahash, because his father showed kindness to me.” So David sent messengers to comfort him concerning his father. David’s servants came into the land of the children of Ammon to Hanun to comfort him.

kikuyu: Mameeragĩra gũkũũrwo ũkombo-inĩ, o rĩrĩa o ene marĩ ngombo cia ũhoro wa kũbutha, nĩgũkorwo mũndũ nĩ ngombo ya ũndũ ũrĩa wothe ũmwathaga.
english: promising them liberty, while they themselves are bondservants of corruption; for a man is brought into bondage by whoever overcomes him.

kikuyu: Akĩrĩra mĩthenya ĩyo yothe mũgwanja ya iruga rĩu, na mũthenya wa mũgwanja Samusoni * 14:1 Timina rĩarĩ itũũra thĩinĩ wa Dani (Josh 19:43); Afilisti nĩo maikaraga kuo gũtanatunyanwo nĩ andũ a Isiraeli. † 14:5 Ihinda rĩĩrĩ nĩ gwakoragwo na mĩrũũthi mĩingĩ mwena wa gũthini wa Palestina.
english: She wept before him the seven days, while their feast lasted; and on the seventh day, he told her, because she pressed him severely; and she told the riddle to the children of her people.

kikuyu: Ũhinyanĩrĩria ũtũmaga mũndũ mũũgĩ akĩĩge, narĩo ihaki rĩgathũkia ngoro.
english: Surely extortion makes the wise man foolish; and a bribe destroys the understanding.

kikuyu: Rehagai igongona rĩa njino rĩna mũtararĩko mwega wa gũkenia Jehova rĩa gategwa kamwe na ndũrũme ĩmwe, na tũtũrũme mũgwanja o kamwe ka ũkũrũ wa mwaka ũmwe, ciothe itarĩ na kaũũgũ.
english: but you shall offer a burnt offering to Yahweh for a pleasant aroma: one young bull, one ram, seven male lambs a year old, all without defect;

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo Jesũ akĩreherwo twana nĩguo atũigĩrĩre moko na atũhooere. No arutwo magĩkũũma arĩa maatũrehete.
english: Then little children were brought to him that he should lay his hands on them and pray; and the disciples rebuked them.

kikuyu: No mũndũ wakwa ũrĩa mũthingu agaatũũra muoyo nĩ ũndũ wa gwĩtĩkia. Na angĩgacooka na thuutha, ngoro yakwa ndĩgakenio nĩwe.”
english: But the righteous one will live by faith. If he shrinks back, my soul has no pleasure in him.”*

kikuyu: Nake mũtumia ũcio akĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Noguo Mwathani, no o na ngui irĩ rungu rwa metha nĩ irĩĩaga rũitĩki rũrĩa rũitagwo nĩ ciana.”
english: But she answered him, “Yes, Lord. Yet even the dogs under the table eat the children’s crumbs.”

kikuyu: Maikarĩte metha- inĩ makĩrĩa akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Ngũmwĩra atĩrĩ na ma, ũmwe wanyu nĩekũngunyanĩra, ũmwe wa arĩa mararĩĩanĩra na niĩ.”
english: As they sat and were eating, Jesus said, “Most certainly I tell you, one of you will betray me—he who eats with me.”

kikuyu: nĩekũrathima arĩa metigĩrĩte Jehova, anini na anene o ũndũ ũmwe.
english: He will bless those who fear Yahweh, both small and great.

kikuyu: Andũ anyu nĩmakooragĩrwo gatagatĩ-inĩ kanyu, na inyuĩ nĩmũkamenya atĩ niĩ nĩ niĩ Jehova.
english: The slain will fall among you, and you will know that I am Yahweh.

kikuyu: Niĩ nĩmenyete wega biũ ndĩ thĩinĩ wa Mwathani Jesũ, atĩ gũtirĩ irio irĩ thaahu cio nyene. No rĩrĩ, mũndũ ũrĩa wonaga kĩndũ ta kĩrĩ thaahu-rĩ, ũcio harĩ we kĩndũ kĩu nĩ kĩrĩ thaahu.
english: I know and am persuaded in the Lord Jesus that nothing is unclean of itself; except that to him who considers anything to be unclean, to him it is unclean.

kikuyu: Ngoro ya mũndũ mũũgĩ nĩyo ĩtongoragia kanua gake, nayo mĩromo yake ĩtũũgagĩria ũtaaro.
english: The heart of the wise instructs his mouth, and adds learning to his lips.

kikuyu: Eeraga tharunji atĩrĩ, ‘Gwa thĩ,’ nayo mbura ya rũthuthuũ akamĩĩra atĩrĩ, ‘Tuĩka mbura nene.’
english: For he says to the snow, ‘Fall on the earth,’ likewise to the shower of rain, and to the showers of his mighty rain.

kikuyu: huutha wa Abimeleku, mũndũ wa mũhĩrĩga wa Isakaru wetagwo Tola mũrũ wa Pua, mũrũ wa Dodo, akĩarahũka ahonokie Isiraeli. Nake agĩtũũra Shamiru, kũu bũrũri ũrĩa ũrĩ irĩma wa Efiraimu.
english: fter Abimelech, Tola the son of Puah, the son of Dodo, a man of Issachar, arose to save Israel. He lived in Shamir in the hill country of Ephraim.

kikuyu: “Ningĩ nĩ kwĩrĩtwo atĩrĩ, ‘Ũrĩa wothe ũngĩtigana na mũtumia wake no nginya amũhe marũa ma kuonanianĩmatigananake.’
english: “It was also said, ‘Whoever shall put away his wife, let him give her a writing of divorce,’*

kikuyu: Ĩtũũraga kĩharũrũka-inĩ kĩa ihiga, na nĩ ho ĩraaraga; rũhĩa-inĩ rwa ihiga iraaya mũno nĩ ho mwĩgitio wayo.
english: On the cliff he dwells and makes his home, on the point of the cliff and the stronghold.

kikuyu: “Ndũkanethondekere mũhianano mũicũhie wa mũhianĩre wa kĩndũ o gĩothe kĩrĩ kũũrĩa igũrũ, kana gũkũ thĩ, kana kĩrĩ maaĩ-inĩ marĩa marĩ mũhuro wa thĩ.
english: “You shall not make a carved image for yourself—any likeness of what is in heaven above, or what is in the earth beneath, or that is in the water under the earth.

kikuyu: ũgitagĩra andũ arĩa matarĩ maithe na arĩa ahinyĩrĩrie, nĩgeetha mũndũ, ũrĩa ũrĩ o wa thĩ, ndagacooke gwĩkanĩra guoya.
english: to judge the fatherless and the oppressed, that man who is of the earth may terrify no more.

kikuyu: Amerari (Alawii arĩa angĩ) maagaĩirwo matũũra maya: Kuuma mũhĩrĩga wa Zebuluni makĩheo Jokineamu, na Karita, na Rimono, na Taboru, hamwe na ũrĩithio wamo;
english: To the rest of the Levites, the sons of Merari, were given, out of the tribe of Zebulun, Rimmono with its pasture lands, and Tabor with its pasture lands;

kikuyu: Nũũ ũkũnginyia itũũra rĩrĩa inene rĩirigĩre? Nũũ ũkũndongoria andware Edomu?
english: Who will bring me into the strong city? Who has led me to Edom?

kikuyu: “Ningĩ ngĩũria mwathi wakwa atĩrĩ, ‘Ĩ mũndũ-wa-nja ũcio angĩrega gũũka na niĩ?’
english: I asked my master, ‘What if the woman will not follow me?’

kikuyu: 252 Joshua 21:15 20 Matũũra ma Kũũrĩrwo 1 Nake Jehova akĩĩra Joshua atĩrĩ,
english: ahweh spoke to Joshua, saying,

kikuyu: Mwena wa gũthini nĩho ikundi cia kambĩ ya mũhĩrĩga wa Rubeni igaakorwo, harĩa bendera yao ĩrĩ. Mũtongoria wa andũ a Rubeni nĩ Elizuru mũrũ wa Shedeuru.
english: “On the south side shall be the standard of the camp of Reuben according to their divisions. The prince of the children of Reuben shall be Elizur the son of Shedeur.

kikuyu: nake akĩĩra arĩa maamũtungataga atĩrĩ, “Ũcio nĩ Johana Mũbatithania; nĩ ariũkĩte kuuma kũrĩ arĩa akuũ! Nĩkĩo arĩ na hinya mũnene ũguo wa kũringa ciama.”
english: and said to his servants, “This is John the Baptizer. He is risen from the dead. That is why these powers work in him.”

kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, rũrĩrĩ o na rũrĩkũ rũngĩkaaga kũnjigua-rĩ, nĩngarũmunya na mĩri na ndĩrũniine biũ,” ũguo nĩguo Jehova ekuuga.
english: But if they will not hear, then I will pluck up that nation, plucking up and destroying it,” says Yahweh.

kikuyu: Maya nĩmo marĩĩtwa ma ariũ a Esaũ: nĩ Elifazu, mũrũ wa Ada mũtumia wa Esaũ, na Reueli, mũrũ wa Basemathu mũtumia wa Esaũ.
english: these are the names of Esau’s sons: Eliphaz, the son of Adah, the wife of Esau; and Reuel, the son of Basemath, the wife of Esau.

kikuyu: Nake Rekabu na Baana, ariũ a Rimoni ũrĩa Mũbeerothi, magĩthiĩ marorete nyũmba-inĩ ya Ishi- Boshethu, na magĩkinya kuo rĩrĩa gũkoragwo kũrĩ ũrugarĩ mũthenya, rĩrĩa aahurũkĩte mĩaraho.
english: In Hebron he reigned over Judah seven years and six months, and in Jerusalem he reigned thirty-three years over all Israel and Judah.

kikuyu: Nake agĩcookia atĩrĩ, “Wee-rĩ, ndũũĩ ici nĩ ndũũ?” Na niĩ ngiuga atĩrĩ, “Aca, Mwathi wakwa, ndiũĩ.”
english: He answered me, “Don’t you know what these are?” I said, “No, my lord.”

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa Paũlũ aakinyire ngathĩ-inĩ kĩrĩndĩ kĩu gĩgĩthũka mũno o nginya Paũlũ agĩkuuo nĩ thigari.
english: When he came to the stairs, he was carried by the soldiers because of the violence of the crowd;

kikuyu: Nake Jehova agĩcooka akĩnjĩĩra atĩrĩ,
english: Yahweh spoke to me, saying,

kikuyu: O ta ũguo tũmwĩkĩte- rĩ, ndĩramwarĩria ta ciana ciakwa, o na inyuĩ hingũrai ngoro cianyu biũ. Mũtikoohanio na arĩa Matetĩkĩtie
english: Now in return—I speak as to my children—you also open your hearts.

kikuyu: Johana nĩoimbũraga ũhoro wake. Aanagĩrĩra akoiga atĩrĩ, “Ũyũ nĩwe ũrĩa ndaaragia ũhoro wake ngiuga atĩrĩ, ‘Ũrĩa ũgũũka thuutha wakwa nĩ mũnene kũngĩra, tondũ aarĩ mbere yakwa.’ ”
english: John testified about him. He cried out, saying, “This was he of whom I said, ‘He who comes after me has surpassed me, for he was before me.’ ”

kikuyu: Nĩngamũtambũrũkĩria wabu wakwa, nake agwatio nĩ mũtego wakwa. Nĩngamũrehe Babuloni ndĩmũciirithĩrie kuo, tondũ nĩagire kwĩhokeka harĩ niĩ.
english: I will spread my net on him, and he will be taken in my snare. I will bring him to Babylon, and will enter into judgment with him there for his trespass that he has trespassed against me.

kikuyu: O na njĩra cia gũtungata nĩ mĩthemba mĩthemba, no Mwathani ũrĩa ũtungatagwo no ũmwe.
english: There are various kinds of service, and the same Lord.

kikuyu: Arĩa othe matũũraga mabũrũri-inĩ marĩa marĩ ndwere-inĩ cia iria nĩmamakĩte nĩ ũndũ waku; athamaki ao mainainaga nĩ kũmaka, na magathita ithiithi nĩ guoya.
english: All the inhabitants of the islands are astonished at you, and their kings are horribly afraid. They are troubled in their face.

kikuyu: no ndutura yakwa, ũcio wakwa ũtarĩ ũcuuke-rĩ, gũtirĩ ũngĩ mahaanaine, nowe mũirĩtu wa nyina wiki, o we gĩtũnio kĩa ũcio mũmũciari. Airĩtu maamuonire makĩmwĩta mũrathime; nao atumia athamaki o na thuriya makĩmũkumia. Arata
english: My dove, my perfect one, is unique. She is her mother’s only daughter. She is the favorite one of her who bore her. The daughters saw her, and called her blessed. The queens and the concubines saw her, and they praised her.

kikuyu: Gatagatĩ-inĩ kao nĩmuonire mĩhianano yao ĩrĩ thaahu, na ngai cia kũhooywo cia mĩtĩ na cia mahiga, na cia betha na cia thahabu.
english: and you have seen their abominations and their idols of wood, stone, silver, and gold, which were among them);

kikuyu: Marohaana ta nyeki ĩrĩ nyũmba igũrũ, ĩrĩa ĩhoohaga ĩtanamera;*
english: Let them be as the grass on the housetops, which withers before it grows up,

kikuyu: Mweri-inĩ wa mũgwanja wa mwaka o ro ũcio-rĩ, Hanania ũcio mũnabii agĩkua.
english: So Hananiah the prophet died the same year in the seventh month.

kikuyu: Handũ ha kũigua-rĩ, akĩhũndũka agĩthiĩ nyũmba-inĩ yake ya ũthamaki, na ndaigana kũrũmbũiya ũhoro ũcio ngoro-inĩ yake.
english: Pharaoh turned and went into his house, and he didn’t even take this to heart.

kikuyu: Akĩmarora arakaire, na aiguĩte ũũru nĩ ũndũ wa ũremi wa ngoro ciao, akĩĩra mũndũ ũcio atĩrĩ, “Tambũrũkia guoko gwaku.” Nake agĩgũtambũrũkia, nakuo guoko gũkĩhona biũ.
english: When he had looked around at them with anger, being grieved at the hardening of their hearts, he said to the man, “Stretch out your hand.” He stretched it out, and his hand was restored as healthy as the other.

kikuyu: Rĩrĩa maiguire ciugo cia Mũfilisti ũcio, Saũlũ na andũ othe a Isiraeli makĩmaka, magĩĩtigĩra mũno.
english: When Saul and all Israel heard those words of the Philistine, they were dismayed and greatly afraid.

kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa waheire baba ũhoro ũcio arogwatwo nĩ kĩrumi, ũrĩa watũmire akene mũno, rĩrĩa aamwĩrire atĩrĩ, “Nĩũciarĩirwo mwana wa kahĩĩ!”
english: Cursed is the man who brought news to my father, saying, “A boy is born to you,” making him very glad.

kikuyu: Agaakuĩra kũu aatwarirwo arĩ mũgwate; ndakoona bũrũri ũyũ rĩngĩ.”
english: But he will die in the place where they have led him captive. He will see this land no more.”

kikuyu: Nĩũndũũcio-rĩ, mũndũũrĩaũhikagiamũirĩtuũrĩaorĩtienĩekaga ũrĩa kwagĩrĩire, no ũrĩa ũngĩaga kũmũhikia nĩekaga wega makĩria.
english: So then both he who gives his own virgin in marriage does well, and he who doesn’t give her in marriage does better.

kikuyu: Na aarĩkia kuuga ũguo, agĩikara kũu Galili.
english: Having said these things to them, he stayed in Galilee.

kikuyu: Nĩagatharĩkĩra kũndũ kũrĩa kũirigĩre na hinya mũno ateithĩrĩirio nĩ ngai ngʼeni, na nĩagatĩĩithia mũno andũ arĩa othe makamwĩnyiihĩria. Acio nĩo agaatua aathani maathage andũ aingĩ, na agayanie ithaka cia bũrũri ũcio, aciheane na thogora.
english: He will deal with the strongest fortresses by the help of a foreign god. He will increase with glory whoever acknowledges him. He will cause them to rule over many, and will divide the land for a price.

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio akĩgemia thĩinĩ guothe na thahabu. Ningĩ nĩagemirie kĩgongona kĩrĩa kĩarĩ thĩinĩ wa handũ-harĩa-haamũre na thahabu.
english: He overlaid the whole house with gold, until all the house was finished. He also overlaid the whole altar that belonged to the inner sanctuary with gold.

kikuyu: Nĩndĩrĩaragia ũhoro wa kĩrĩra gĩaku mbere ya athamaki na ndirĩconokaga,
english: I will also speak of your statutes before kings, and will not be disappointed.

kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, rũngĩkĩhuurũka atĩa, o rĩrĩa arĩ Jehova ũrwathĩte, o rĩrĩa arwĩrĩte rũtharĩkĩre Ashikeloni,* o na kũu ndwere-inĩ cia iria?”
english: “How can you be quiet, since Yahweh has given you a command? Against Ashkelon, and against the seashore, there he has appointed it.”

kikuyu: na maanyuuaga maaĩ o mamwe ma kĩĩroho; nĩgũkorwo maanyuire kuuma rwaro-inĩ rwa ihiga rwa kĩĩroho rũrĩa rwatwaranaga nao, naruo rwaro rũu rwarĩ Kristũ.
english: and all drank the same spiritual drink. For they drank of a spiritual rock that followed them, and the rock was Christ.

kikuyu: nĩngũkũrehithĩria andũ a kũngĩ magũũkĩrĩre, nacio nĩ ndũrĩrĩ iria itarĩ tha o na hanini; nĩigacomora hiũ ciacio cia njora ciũkĩrĩre ũthaka waku na ũũgĩ waku, na itheecange riiri waku mũcangararu.
english: therefore, behold, I will bring strangers on you, the terrible of the nations. They will draw their swords against the beauty of your wisdom. They will defile your brightness.

kikuyu: Maarĩkia gũthiĩ-rĩ, mũraika wa Mwathani akiumĩrĩra Jusufu kĩroto-inĩ, akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Ũkĩra woe kaana gaka na nyina mũũrĩre Misiri. Ikarai kũu nginya rĩrĩa ngaakwĩra, nĩgũkorwo Herode nĩegũcaria kaana kau akoorage.” * 1:17 Kristũ nĩ kuuga “Ũrĩa Mũitĩrĩrie maguta”; Kĩhibirania nĩ Mesia. * 2:4 Athĩnjĩri-Ngai maarĩ atongoria a mahooya ma Ayahudi, na nĩo maarutagĩra Ngai magongona. † 2:11 Ũbumba nĩ mahuti manungi wega macinagwo na mwaki. Manemane nĩ indo ithondekagwo na kĩndũ kĩhaana ta ũũkĩ wa gĩthaka, inungaga wega na nĩ cia goro mũno.
english: Now when they had departed, behold, an angel of the Lord appeared to Joseph in a dream, saying, “Arise and take the young child and his mother, and flee into Egypt, and stay there until I tell you, for Herod will seek the young child to destroy him.”

kikuyu: Angĩ nao makoiga atĩrĩ, “Ũyũ nĩ Elija.” Angĩ nao makoiga atĩrĩ, “Ũyũ nĩ mũnabii ta ũmwe wa arĩa a tene.”
english: But others said, “He is Elijah.” Others said, “He is a prophet, or like one of the prophets.”

kikuyu: O na ningĩ no harĩ Maandĩko mangĩ moigĩte atĩrĩ, “Makeerorera mũndũ ũrĩa maathecire.” Gũthikwo kwa Jesũ
english: Again another Scripture says, “They will look on him whom they pierced.”*

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa Azuba aakuire, Kalebu akĩhikia Efiratha, ũrĩa wamũciarĩire Huru.
english: Azubah died, and Caleb married Ephrath, who bore him Hur.

kikuyu: Hikaniai, mũciare aanake na airĩtu; carĩriai aanake anyu atumia mahikie, nao airĩtu anyu mũreke mahikio, nĩgeetha o nao maciare aanake na airĩtu. Ongererekai na mũingĩhe mũrĩ kũu; mũtikanyihanyiihe.
english: Take wives and father sons and daughters. Take wives for your sons, and give your daughters to husbands, that they may bear sons and daughters. Multiply there, and don’t be diminished.

kikuyu: Wee Ngai, matue nĩmahĩtĩtie! Reke magũithio nĩ mĩbango ĩyo yao ya ungumania. Maingate nĩ ũndũ wa mehia mao maingĩ,
english: Hold them guilty, God. Let them fall by their own counsels. Thrust them out in the multitude of their transgressions, for they have rebelled against you.

kikuyu: Rĩrĩa rĩothe hema ĩyo ĩgũthaamio, Alawii acio nĩo marĩmĩambũraga, na rĩrĩa rĩothe hema ĩyo ĩĩkwambwo-rĩ, Alawii acio nĩo marĩmĩambaga. Mũndũ ũngĩ o na ũrĩkũ angĩkamĩkuhĩrĩria nĩakooragwo.
english: When the tabernacle is to move, the Levites shall take it down; and when the tabernacle is to be set up, the Levites shall set it up. The stranger who comes near shall be put to death.

kikuyu: tĩrĩrĩ, wee Nineve, mũgũtharĩkĩri nĩokĩte gũgũtharĩkĩra. Rangĩra kĩĩgitĩro kĩa hinya, na ũgitĩre njĩra; wĩhotore, na wĩĩkĩre hinya mũno!
english: e who dashes in pieces has come up against you. Keep the fortress! Watch the way! Strengthen your waist! Fortify your power mightily!

kikuyu: Nake aarĩ wa rũciaro rwa Perezu, na nĩwe warĩ mũnene wa anene othe a mbũtũ cia ita mweri wa mbere.
english: He was of the children of Perez, the chief of all the captains of the army for the first month.

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio ngĩiga andũ amwe na thuutha wa kũrĩa rũthingo rwarĩ thĩ mũno na gũtaarĩ na ũgitĩri; ngĩmaiga kũringana na nyũmba ciao marĩ na hiũ ciao cia njora, na matimũ, na mota.
english: Therefore I set guards in the lowest parts of the space behind the wall, in the open places. I set the people by family groups with their swords, their spears, and their bows.

kikuyu: Isaaka akĩaria, agĩĩta ithe Iburahĩmu atĩrĩ, “Baba!” Iburahĩmu akĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Niĩ ũyũ, mũrũ wakwa.” Isaaka akiuga atĩrĩ, “Ndĩrona tũrĩ na mwaki na ngũ! No rĩrĩ, gatũrũme ka igongona rĩa njino karĩ ha?”
english: Isaac spoke to Abraham his father, and said, “My father?” He said, “Here I am, my son.” He said, “Here is the fire and the wood, but where is the lamb for a burnt offering?”

kikuyu: Mũirĩtu ũcio agĩthiĩ na mbere na gwĩka ũguo mĩthenya mĩingĩ. Marigĩrĩrio-inĩ Paũlũ agĩthirĩka mũno nginya akĩhũgũkĩra roho ũcio akĩwĩĩra atĩrĩ, “Ndagwatha thĩinĩ wa rĩĩtwa rĩa Jesũ Kristũ uume thĩinĩ wake!” O hĩndĩ ĩyo roho ũcio ũkĩmuuma.
english: She was doing this for many days. But Paul, becoming greatly annoyed, turned and said to the spirit, “I command you in the name of Jesus Christ to come out of her!” It came out that very hour.

kikuyu: Ũũ nĩguo ngoretwo ngĩĩka: nĩnjathĩkagĩra mawatho maku. Kwĩrutĩra nĩ ũndũ wa Watho wa Jehova
english: This is my way, that I keep your precepts. HETH

kikuyu: Nĩ ngai ĩrĩkũ harĩ ngai ciothe cia mabũrũri macio yanahonokia bũrũri wayo kuuma kũrĩ niĩ? Gwakĩhoteka atĩa Jehova ahonokie Jerusalemu kuuma guoko-inĩ gwakwa?”
english: Who are they among all the gods of the countries, that have delivered their country out of my hand, that Yahweh should deliver Jerusalem out of my hand?’ ”

kikuyu: “Na rĩrĩ, andũ arĩa meamũrĩte na magetheria nĩguo mathiĩ marũmĩrĩire ũrĩa ũrĩ gatagatĩ nĩguo makinyemagongona-inĩmarĩamarutagĩrwomĩgũnda-inĩ, oaciomarĩĩaganyamaciangũrwenambĩa, na indo ingĩ irĩ thaahu, magaathiranĩra hamwe,” ũguo nĩguo Jehova ekuuga.
english: “Those who sanctify themselves and purify themselves to go to the gardens, following one in the middle, eating pig’s meat, abominable things, and the mouse, they shall come to an end together,” says Yahweh.

kikuyu: Magĩcaria ũrĩa mangĩmũnyiita, no nĩmetigagĩra kĩrĩndĩ kĩu, tondũ andũ nĩmeetĩkĩtie atĩ aarĩ mũnabii.
english: When they sought to seize him, they feared the multitudes, because they considered him to be a prophet.
